user_story: As the system, I want to push updated workspace files and image upgrades to idle tenant containers every hour so that tenants always run the latest config without disrupting active sessions.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/cron/apply-pending-configs/. QStash-verified (401 if invalid). Finds active non-hibernated tenants with pending_config_version > config_version and last_message_at older than 15 minutes. Batch 1 (immediate): enqueues apply_single_tenant_config per eligible tenant + seed_cron_jobs per active non-hibernated entitled tenant not in stale-image list. Batch 2 (delayed 30s): enqueues apply_single_tenant_image for active non-hibernated tenants on stale OPENCLAW_IMAGE_TAG — skips tenants with an imminent cron via _cron_active_or_imminent check. Returns JSON with config_enqueued, image_enqueued, cron_seed_enqueued, image_skipped_imminent_cron counts. Scheduled every 60 minutes at :00 UTC.
inventory_notes: Image bumps are delayed 30s to ensure config writes (to Azure File Share) land before the container reads openclaw.json on restart. Imminent-cron guard prevents interrupting a scheduled agent turn mid-flight.

user_story: As the system, I want to suspend tenants whose trial has ended or who lack an active paid subscription so that unentitled accounts stop consuming LLM budget.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/cron/expire-trials/. QStash-verified (401 if invalid). Finds active tenants without: a Stripe subscription, an unexpired trial (is_trial=True and trial_ends_at in future), or is_budget_exempt=True. For each: calls suspend_tenant_crons (disables crons via gateway), sets tenant.is_trial=False and status=SUSPENDED, hibernates container. Returns {updated, already_hibernated, crons_disabled, hibernated}. Scheduled daily at 02:00 UTC.
dependencies: Stripe (subscription check), QStash, Azure Container Apps, OpenClaw container (suspend crons via gateway)
inventory_notes: The _unentitled_active_tenants query intentionally does NOT filter on is_trial=True to catch ghost tenants (is_trial=False, active status, no sub). budget_exempt tenants (canary, internal) are excluded. Order: disable crons while gateway reachable → flip status → hibernate.

feature: Bump all pending configs (CI deploy hook)
user_story: As CI/CD, I want to mark all active tenants for a config update after deploy so that new workspace files propagate to every tenant on their next idle cycle.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/cron/bump-all-pending-configs/. Auth: X-Deploy-Secret header must match DEPLOY_SECRET (401 if missing/wrong). POST method required (405 otherwise). Tenants without a delivery channel (no telegram_chat_id and no line_user_id) older than 24h are reset to config_version=0 and pending_config_version=0. Active tenants with a container and a delivery channel (or created <24h ago) have pending_config_version incremented by 1. Returns {queued: N}.
inventory_notes: 24h grace period for new tenants allows them to link Telegram/LINE before being treated as no-channel. No-channel tenants older than 24h are reset to version 0 rather than incremented.

feature: Register system QStash cron schedules (CI deploy hook)
user_story: As CI/CD, I want to register or update QStash scheduled cron entries after every deploy so that the platform keeps the correct schedule without manual intervention.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/cron/register-system-crons/. Auth: X-Deploy-Secret header (401 if wrong, 400 if base_url missing, 503 if QSTASH_TOKEN not configured). Fetches existing QStash schedules via API. For each entry in SYSTEM_CRONS (25 schedules): skips if destination+cron_expr unchanged, deletes+recreates if cron expression changed, creates if new. Returns {registered, updated, skipped, failed} lists.
inventory_notes: Idempotent — existing schedules with unchanged cron expression are skipped. SYSTEM_CRONS list includes 25 entries ranging from per-minute (reap-stuck-inbound-messages) to weekly (snapshot-gravity-weekly).
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

user_story: As the system, I want to refresh system cron rows from seed for all active tenants so that system crons always reflect current prompts and schedules.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/cron/force-reseed-crons/. Accepts either QStash signature OR X-Deploy-Secret header. Calls refresh_system_cron_rows_from_seed for each entitled active tenant. Returns {tenants, total_touched, total_errors, details}. Now a thin shim over the postgres-canonical seed refresh — the legacy gateway cron.list+cron.add path was removed. Signal-driven reconcile pushes new state to OpenClaw runtime.
inventory_notes: Deprecated — the refresh now operates on Postgres CronJob rows; signal handler debounces push to OpenClaw SQLite.

user_story: As CI/CD, I want to refresh system cron rows from seed for all active tenants after deploy so that prompt changes propagate to the fleet immediately.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/cron/run-update-cron-prompts/. Auth: X-Deploy-Secret (401 if wrong). POST required. Sets RLS service role. Iterates all active tenants with containers. Calls refresh_system_cron_rows_from_seed per tenant. Returns {results: [{tenant, created, updated, preserved_custom} | {tenant, status, error}], total_touched}.
inventory_notes: Signal fires post_save on each updated CronJob row, triggering debounced reconcile to push to OpenClaw container.

user_story: As an operator, I want to remove duplicate cron jobs from tenant containers so that the assistant does not fire redundant messages.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/cron/dedup-crons/. Accepts QStash signature or X-Deploy-Secret. Without ?tenant=UUID: fans out dedup_cron_jobs task per active tenant via QStash batch publish. With ?tenant=UUID: runs dedup_tenant_cron_jobs synchronously for that tenant and returns {tenant, kept, deleted, errors, duplicates_found}. Only targets active tenants with container_id and container_fqdn. Returns 404 if single tenant not found/inactive.
inventory_notes: Single-tenant sync mode intended for debugging. Fleet fan-out is async via QStash.
error_detail: Confirmed. In the fleet fan-out branch of dedup_crons (apps/cron/views.py:1344-1350), the response hardcodes "failed": 0. When publish_batch raises (e.g. QStash outage, since the prod batch_json path re-raises on any error at publish.py:208-210), the except block sets enqueued = 0 (views.py:1346-1348) and the endpoint returns {"enqueued": 0, "failed": 0}. That payload is indistinguishable from the legitimate "no active tenants matched" case (the empty-tasks early return at publish.py:155-156 also yields enqueued=0), so an operator reads it as "nothing needed processing" rather than "every dedup task was silently dropped." The sibling broadcast endpoint immediately above (views.py:1284-1291) handles this correctly: failed = len(batch_tasks) - enqueued. The dedup endpoint is simply missing that line. Additionally, in the sync-fallback path (QStash unconfigured, dev/test), publish_batch returns a partial count < len(tasks) when some tasks throw (publish.py:176-185); the hardcoded failed:0 masks those partial failures too. Impact is operator-facing telemetry only (admin/QStash-triggered cron maintenance endpoint), not stored-data corruption — hence low severity.

user_story: As an operator, I want to send a one-off message prompt to every tenant's AI assistant so that I can notify all subscribers of platform changes.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/cron/broadcast-message/. Accepts QStash signature or X-Deploy-Secret. Requires JSON body with {message: string}. message is required (400 if empty). Generates idempotency key from sha256 of message (or caller-supplied key). Enqueues broadcast_single_tenant QStash task per entitled active tenant with a container. Per-tenant deduplication key prevents double-delivery on retries. Returns {enqueued, failed}.
inventory_notes: Each tenant task is idempotency-keyed as {broadcast_key}-{tenant_id[:8]}.

user_story: As the system, I want to periodically check all tenant container health and alert the operator's personal agent when failures are detected so that production issues are surfaced promptly.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/cron/run-health-check/. Accepts QStash signature or X-Deploy-Secret. Runs check_all_tenants_health(). If unhealthy tenants exist, sends alert to MJ's personal OpenClaw gateway (ADMIN_OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_URL) via httpx POST to /v1/chat/completions with CF-Access headers. Rate-limited: cache_key=health_alert_sent, 30-minute cooldown. Cooldown set on delivered or undeliverable (transient skips cooldown so next tick retries). Returns {total, healthy, unhealthy, alerted?, alert_status?, details?}.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres, OpenClaw container (per-tenant health), Cloudflare Access (admin gateway), personal OpenClaw agent
inventory_notes: 3xx from admin gateway means CF-Access rejected the service token. 4xx/5xx distinguish undeliverable (sets cooldown) vs transient (leaves cooldown unset for retry). Alert capped at 10 unhealthy details in message.

user_story: As an operator, I want to query the health of all tenant containers on demand so that I can check platform status without waiting for the scheduled health check.
expected_behaviour: GET or POST /api/cron/admin-health/. Auth: X-Deploy-Secret (401 if wrong). Does NOT send alerts — read-only pull mechanism. Runs check_all_tenants_health(). Returns {total, healthy, unhealthy, config_drift, results: [{...per-tenant details}]}.
inventory_notes: Both GET and POST are accepted (uncommon for a read endpoint). No cooldown applied.

user_story: As the system, I want to restart a specific tenant's OpenClaw container via QStash so that container issues can be remediated without direct Azure CLI access.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/cron/restart-tenant-container/. QStash-verified (401 if invalid). Requires tenant_id in JSON body or POST data. Returns 400 if missing. Looks up active tenant by id (404 if not found or not active). Returns 400 if no container_id. Calls restart_container_app. Returns {restarted: true, container: container_id}.

feature: Atomic fleet bump (OpenClaw version + config + image)
user_story: As an operator, I want to atomically bump every tenant's OpenClaw version, config, and container image so that schema-crossing releases propagate consistently without partial-update windows.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/cron/rollout-atomic-bump/. Auth: X-Deploy-Secret. Rejects image_tag=latest (400). Concurrency-locked via Django cache key (409 if another bump in flight). Reads oc_version (default OPENCLAW_CURRENT_VERSION), image_tag (default OPENCLAW_IMAGE_TAG), optional tenant_id filter, dry_run flag. Skips tenants already at target version AND image_tag. Enqueues bump_openclaw_atomic_per_tenant QStash task per eligible tenant. Returns {queued, oc_version, image_tag, tenant_ids, dry_run}.
inventory_notes: Lock TTL is 30 minutes. Idempotent on (oc_version, image_tag) pair — partial version match alone is not enough. Per-tenant tasks use bump_openclaw_version_for_tenant which snapshots the file before write and restores on failure.
error_detail: Confirmed real, low-impact. The lock TTL is 30 min, commented "covers slowest fleet rollout" (views.py:1682), implying intent to hold it for the rollout. But it is released in the finally block (views.py:1813-1815) right after publish_batch returns (line 1797). publish_batch is a near-instant QStash fan-out enqueue, not the rollout itself; the per-tenant bumps run later in bump_openclaw_atomic_per_tenant_task (tasks.py:33-60), each ~30s across the fleet. So the lock is held for milliseconds, not the 30-min window. A second rollout_atomic_bump call arriving after the first HTTP response re-acquires the lock (cache.add succeeds) and re-runs the eligibility query, whose idempotency exclude (line 1779) reads DB state that is only written at task execution time. Tenants whose first task has not run yet are still "eligible" and get duplicate tasks enqueued. Backstop: the per-task guard at tasks.py:53-54 re-checks DB state at execution time, so if the first task already finished, the duplicate no-ops. A genuine double-write only occurs when both deliveries for one tenant execute before either writes DB state; even then bump_openclaw_version_for_tenant snapshots-before-write and restores-on-failure, so the repeated write is idempotent in effect (redundant container/config churn, no corruption). This is an operator/CI-only endpoint gated by X-Deploy-Secret (manual or workflow_dispatch), not user-facing, and a concurrent double-trigger is uncommon, hence low severity. The lock semantics do not match the documented 30-min intent, but the per-task idempotency backstop keeps real-world harm to redundant work.

user_story: As an operator, I want to see the current OpenClaw version and image tag for every active tenant so that I can verify a fleet rollout succeeded.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/cron/atomic-bump-status/. Auth: X-Deploy-Secret (401 if wrong, 503 if DEPLOY_SECRET unconfigured). Returns {tenants: [{tenant_id, oc_version, image_tag, hibernated}], count: N} for all active tenants with containers. Read-only — no side effects.
inventory_notes: Caller filters response against target values to find drift. No server-side target filtering.

user_story: As an operator, I want to push the current OpenClaw image tag to all active tenant containers so that the fleet upgrades to a new image build.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/cron/rollout-byo-image-bump/. Auth: X-Deploy-Secret. Wraps bump_all_tenant_images management command. Optional {include_hibernated: true} to also bump hibernated tenants. Returns {ok, include_hibernated, stdout_tail} on success, or {ok: false, error, stdout_tail, stderr_tail} on failure.
inventory_notes: One-shot operation intended for manual use or workflow_dispatch CI. Routine image rollouts use apply_pending_configs cron instead.

user_story: As an operator, I want to re-render and push AGENTS.md to all active tenant file shares so that persona changes propagate fleet-wide immediately.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/cron/rollout-byo-persona-refresh/. Auth: X-Deploy-Secret. Wraps refresh_persona_agents_md management command. Optional {include_hibernated: true}. Returns {ok, include_hibernated, stdout_tail} on success, or {ok: false, error, ...} on failure.
inventory_notes: Same one-shot semantics as rollout-byo-image-bump.

user_story: As CI/CD, I want to schedule Fuel/Gravity welcome crons for tenants who missed them so that feature onboarding flows complete even if prior attempts failed.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/cron/backfill-welcomes/. Auth: X-Deploy-Secret. Iterates all active tenants with containers. For fuel_enabled tenants: calls _schedule_fuel_welcome. For finance_active tenants: calls _schedule_finance_welcome. Both schedulers are idempotent (check gateway for existing _fuel:welcome/_finance:welcome before scheduling). Returns {tenants_walked, fuel: {status: count}, finance: {status: count}, statuses}.
inventory_notes: Per-feature schedulers handle replaced_stale/skipped_pending/skipped_already_delivered/failed outcomes. Exceptions per tenant are counted as failed without aborting the loop.

user_story: As CI/CD, I want to verify that the OpenClaw gateway cron tool responds correctly after deploy so that broken cron infrastructure is caught immediately.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/cron/verify-gateway-tools/. Auth: X-Deploy-Secret. Picks first active tenant with a container FQDN. Calls cron.list via invoke_gateway_tool. Returns {ok: true, tenant, cron_tool: available} on success. Returns {ok: false, tenant, error} with 500 on failure. Returns {ok: true, skipped: true} if no active tenants.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to signal Django when I finish booting so that the cron registry is rebuilt from Postgres truth immediately without waiting for the hourly reconcile.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/cron/runtime/<tenant_id>/container-started/. Auth: X-NBHD-Internal-Key + X-NBHD-Tenant-Id headers validated via validate_internal_runtime_request (401 on failure). Permission class AllowAny (auth done manually). If tenant.postgres_cron_canonical=False, returns {skipped, reason} with 200. Otherwise calls regenerate_tenant_crons synchronously. Returns {ok: true, ...reconcile_summary} with 200. Returns {error, detail} with 500 on regenerate failure.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres, OpenClaw container (called by startup script)
inventory_notes: Idempotent — safe to call repeatedly. Per-tenant internal key (Tenant.internal_api_key) or shared NBHD_INTERNAL_API_KEY. Sets RLS context to service_role after auth.

user_story: As the system, I want CronJob writes to automatically trigger a debounced reconcile push to the OpenClaw container so that Postgres stays in sync with the container's SQLite without manual intervention.
expected_behaviour: Fires on post_save and post_delete for CronJob. Only active when tenant.postgres_cron_canonical=True. Enqueues regenerate_tenant_crons QStash task with idempotency_key=regen-cron-{tenant_id} and delay_seconds=30. The debounce collapses rapid sequential edits (bulk-delete, drag-to-reschedule) into a single reconcile call. Errors in enqueue are caught and logged; the save still succeeds. The pre_save signal also fires on every typed-path CronJob save to re-derive CronJob.data from pattern+typed_payload (skips if pattern/payload/name/schedule unchanged).
inventory_notes: The pre_save data-derive signal is not disconnected by QuietCronSignalRunner in tests — it's a pure model-state derivation. The reconcile signals are disconnected by default in tests.

user_story: As a subscriber or agent, I want to create a cron job using a validated typed pattern so that the assistant's scheduled messages follow a predictable format the system can enforce.
expected_behaviour: Called via create_typed_cron(tenant, pattern, typed_payload, name, schedule, source). Validates: name non-empty, pattern in CronPattern.values, schedule shape (kind must be cron/every/at; required sub-fields per kind). Validates typed_payload against pattern's Pydantic schema. Creates CronJob row with creation_path=TYPED in an atomic transaction. For at-kind schedules (one-shots): managed=False, pushes to OpenClaw gateway immediately via cron.add and stamps gateway_job_id. For recurring: managed=True, signal triggers debounced reconcile. Raises TypedCronError on validation failure, CronNameConflictError on dup name.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres, OpenClaw container (gateway, for at-kind), QStash
inventory_notes: daily_briefing pattern is system-only — not accessible via agent's nbhd_cron_create_* tools. One-shot at-crons are pushed immediately because the reconciler explicitly skips kind:at jobs (OC auto-deletes on fire).

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to create a cron job with arbitrary unvalidated content via the console so that I have full control when standard patterns don't meet my needs.
expected_behaviour: Called via create_freeform_cron(tenant, name, data, user_confirmed_at, source). Requires user_confirmed_at non-null (TypedCronError if None; DB CHECK also enforces). Requires data.schedule to be a dict. Creates CronJob with creation_path=FREEFORM and pattern=None. For at-kind: managed=False, pushed immediately. For recurring: managed=True, signal-driven reconcile. Raises TypedCronError or CronNameConflictError. NEVER called from agent paths.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres, OpenClaw container (gateway, for at-kind), QStash
inventory_notes: The DB CHECK constraint cron_freeform_requires_user_confirmation is the backup enforcement if code bypasses the service layer.

feature: Typed cron outbound validation (enforcement plugin RPC)
user_story: As the OpenClaw enforcement plugin, I want to validate a cron turn's outbound message against its typed pattern contract so that the assistant cannot deviate from what the pattern guarantees (e.g., pure_reminder must echo verbatim text).
expected_behaviour: Called via validate_typed_cron_outbound(tenant_id, cron_name, content). Looks up CronJob with creation_path=TYPED by (tenant_id, cron_name). Returns {ok: True} for non-typed crons or unknown patterns (defensive pass-through). Calls handler.validate_outbound_message(content, payload). Returns {ok: True} on pass, {ok: False, reason, fallback_content} on fail. fallback_content is the handler's get_fallback_message().
inventory_notes: Called at message_sending hook time inside the OpenClaw container's enforcement plugin. Unknown pattern names return ok=True (don't block delivery on stale pattern name).

feature: Typed cron pattern context fetch (enforcement plugin RPC)
user_story: As the OpenClaw enforcement plugin, I want to retrieve a cron's pattern context before the agent turn starts so that it can inject the correct system-prompt constraints at fire time.
expected_behaviour: Called via fetch_cron_pattern_context(tenant_id, cron_name). Looks up CronJob with creation_path=TYPED. Returns None if not typed or not found. Otherwise validates typed_payload via handler, returns {pattern, typed_payload, name, prompt_injection}. prompt_injection is the handler's get_prompt_injection() output — appended to system prompt via appendSystemContext.
inventory_notes: Called by enforcement plugin's cron_changed/before_prompt_build hooks inside OpenClaw container.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to create a reminder that sends my exact text verbatim at the scheduled time so that I receive precise, unmodified reminders.
expected_behaviour: Pattern name: pure_reminder. Payload schema: {text: str, min_length=1, max_length=2000}. build_oc_data() sets sessionTarget=isolated, wakeMode=next-heartbeat, model=haiku-4.5, lightContext=True, toolsAllow=[nbhd_send_to_user], timeoutSeconds=30, delivery.mode=none. validate_outbound_message() checks that payload.text appears verbatim in the outbound message (exact match or substring). get_prompt_injection() appends a system-prompt block enforcing single-tool, verbatim-echo. get_fallback_message() returns a safe canned message on retry exhaustion.
dependencies: OpenClaw container (agent turn at fire time)
inventory_notes: Lowest-cost agent turn: haiku-4.5 + lightContext=true. Verbatim validation allows exact match or substring (narrow tolerance). toolsAllow is structural enforcement at the OC runtime level.

feature: Tenant cron suspension on entitlement loss
user_story: As the system, I want to disable all of a tenant's cron jobs when their trial expires or subscription lapses so that unentitled users stop receiving proactive messages.
expected_behaviour: Called via suspend_tenant_crons(tenant). Requires container_fqdn. Calls cron.list (includeDisabled=True) via gateway. Iterates jobs: skips already-disabled (increments already_disabled), calls cron.update with patch={enabled: False} for enabled ones. Returns {disabled, already_disabled, errors, job_names}. On gateway error listing crons, returns early with errors=1. On per-job error, increments errors count and continues.
inventory_notes: Jobs are disabled not deleted so they can be re-enabled if the user resubscribes. Called by expire_trials view and enforce_entitlement management command.

feature: Tenant cron resumption on subscription reactivation
user_story: As the system, I want to re-enable all of a tenant's cron jobs when they resubscribe so that proactive messaging resumes without the user needing to manually re-enable each task.
expected_behaviour: Called via resume_tenant_crons(tenant). Requires container_fqdn. Calls cron.list (includeDisabled=True). Iterates jobs: skips already-enabled, calls cron.update with patch={enabled: True} for disabled ones. Returns {enabled, already_enabled, errors, job_names}. On gateway error listing, returns early with errors=1.
inventory_notes: Re-enables all disabled jobs including both system and user-created crons.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see my scheduled tasks even when my container is hibernated or temporarily unavailable so that the dashboard does not show an error.
expected_behaviour: read_jobs_from_cache(tenant) returns the CronJob table rows as gateway-shape dicts. Falls back to Tenant.cron_jobs_snapshot JSONField if no CronJob rows exist. upsert_jobs_to_cache(tenant, jobs) writes a live cron.list response to CronJob rows (deduplicated by name, newest createdAt wins; stale rows removed). is_container_unavailable_error(exc) detects Azure container splash page via status code (404/502/503/504) or message substrings.
inventory_notes: Transitional — the Tenant.cron_jobs_snapshot JSONField fallback covers tenants that predate the CronJob table. On postgres_cron_canonical=True tenants, read_jobs_from_cache is not the primary read path.

user_story: As the system, I want a reliable client for calling OpenClaw gateway tools so that cron management operations are consistent across the codebase.
expected_behaviour: invoke_gateway_tool(tenant, tool, args) POSTs to https://{tenant.container_fqdn}/tools/invoke. Auth: per-tenant Tenant.internal_api_key or settings.NBHD_INTERNAL_API_KEY (last resort: Key Vault read). Raises GatewayError if no token found. Parses tool name as tool_name.action (e.g. cron.list → tool=cron, action=list). 45s timeout with one retry on Timeout. Returns result dict from gateway. Raises GatewayError on non-200 or ok=false gateway response. cron_exists() and cron_get() are thin wrappers. cron_remove() swallows not-found errors.
inventory_notes: 45s timeout with one retry was increased from 15s after a 22:00 UTC incident where cron.list timed out during an active agent turn. Per-tenant key takes precedence over shared key; KV is last resort.

user_story: As the system, I want to enqueue tasks asynchronously via QStash so that platform work is offloaded from web workers and retried on failure.
expected_behaviour: publish_task(task_name, *args, idempotency_key, delay_seconds, retries, **kwargs) publishes a single task to /api/cron/trigger/{task_name}/. Validates idempotency_key does not contain ':' or whitespace (ValueError otherwise). publish_batch(tasks, delay_seconds) sends multiple tasks in one QStash batch_json call. Both fall back to synchronous execution if QSTASH_TOKEN or API_BASE_URL are not configured (dev mode). Default retries=3. Per-process QStash client is cached (singleton per token) to avoid TLS re-handshake latency.
inventory_notes: idempotency_key maps to Upstash-Deduplication-Id header. QStash deduplicates messages with the same key within ~5 minutes. The singleton client avoids 150-400ms TLS overhead per publish call.

user_story: As the system, I want to verify that cron webhook requests genuinely originate from QStash so that the trigger endpoint cannot be called by unauthorized parties.
expected_behaviour: verify_qstash_signature(request) uses the official qstash.Receiver with QSTASH_CURRENT_SIGNING_KEY and QSTASH_NEXT_SIGNING_KEY. Returns False if Upstash-Signature header is missing, if QSTASH_CURRENT_SIGNING_KEY is not configured, or if verification throws. Returns True only on successful signature verification.
inventory_notes: NEXT_SIGNING_KEY falls back to CURRENT_SIGNING_KEY if not set. Used by all QStash-triggered endpoints in views.py.

feature: Register system QStash crons management command
user_story: As an operator, I want to register or update QStash schedules from the command line so that system crons can be seeded during initial setup or recovered after an outage.
expected_behaviour: python manage.py register_system_crons [--base-url URL] [--dry-run]. Requires QSTASH_TOKEN and base_url. Fetches existing QStash schedules. For each of 25 SYSTEM_CRONS entries: skips if cron_expr unchanged, deletes+recreates if cron_expr changed, creates if new. Dry-run prints intended changes without API calls. Outputs registered/updated/unchanged counts.
inventory_notes: The HTTP view /api/cron/register-system-crons/ wraps the same SYSTEM_CRONS list. This command is for manual/interactive use.
error_detail: Confirmed. In the --dry-run path, the new-entry (would-register) branch at lines 203-205 prints "[dry-run] would register: ..." then immediately continues WITHOUT incrementing the `registered` counter. The parallel dry-run update branch (lines 178-181) correctly increments `updated` before continuing. The final summary at line 217 reports "{registered} registered", so a --dry-run preview of a deploy that includes brand-new crons (ones not yet present in QStash) always reports "0 registered, X updated, Y unchanged" even when new crons would in fact be created. Impact is limited and cosmetic: this is an operator-only management command, and each would-be-registered cron is still printed individually on its own line, so the operator still sees every new cron in the per-line output. Only the summary tally undercounts. No functional effect on actual registration (the non-dry-run path increments correctly at line 213).

user_story: As an operator, I want to audit and suspend unentitled tenants from the command line so that I can run a one-shot recovery or inspect which accounts would be affected.
expected_behaviour: python manage.py enforce_entitlement [--apply] [--tenant UUID]. Default is dry-run. Prints matched unentitled active tenants with trial_ends_at, days_since, hibernation state. With --apply: calls _suspend_unentitled_tenant for each (disables crons, flips status=SUSPENDED, hibernates). Optional --tenant limits to a single account. Outputs suspended count, total crons disabled, containers hibernated.
dependencies: OpenClaw container (suspend_tenant_crons), Azure Container Apps (hibernate), Supabase/Postgres
inventory_notes: Reuses the same _unentitled_active_tenants() query as the expire_trials webhook, catching ghost tenants with is_trial=False.

user_story: As an operator, I want to delete and recreate system cron jobs with the correct tenant timezone so that scheduled tasks fire at the right local time after a timezone change.
expected_behaviour: python manage.py resync_cron_timezones [--dry-run] [--tenant UUID]. Iterates active tenants with container FQDNs. Fetches existing jobs via cron.list. Deletes jobs matching seed names (Morning Briefing, Evening Check-in, etc.). Recreates via build_cron_seed_jobs(tenant) which uses tenant.user.timezone. Dry-run prints intended changes. Reports ok/skip/error per tenant.
inventory_notes: Errors per tenant are caught and counted, not propagated. The command targets live gateway cron.list/cron.add/cron.remove directly (not postgres-canonical path).

feature: Backfill Telegram delivery targets management command
user_story: As an operator, I want to populate missing Telegram chat IDs on cron job delivery configs so that Telegram-channel crons reliably reach the subscriber.
expected_behaviour: python manage.py backfill_cron_targets. Targets active tenants with a container FQDN and a telegram_chat_id. For each: fetches cron.list, finds jobs with delivery.mode=announce, delivery.channel=telegram, and no delivery.to set. Updates each such job via cron.update with delivery.to=str(telegram_chat_id). Prints per-job/per-tenant results. Skips tenants with gateway errors.
inventory_notes: One-time migration for crons created before the auto-populate logic was added to the create view.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

feature: Backfill Postgres cron truth management command
user_story: As an operator, I want to seed the Postgres CronJob table from the container's current cron state so that I can safely enable the postgres-cron-canonical flow for a tenant.
expected_behaviour: python manage.py backfill_postgres_cron_truth --tenant UUID | --all [--dry-run] [--enable]. Reads gateway cron.list (falls back to Tenant.cron_jobs_snapshot if container unreachable). Augments with system seed jobs not already present. Deduplicates by name (newest createdAt wins). Calls upsert_from_gateway_jobs to atomically replace CronJob rows. With --enable (non-dry-run): sets Tenant.postgres_cron_canonical=True for backfilled tenants. Returns upserted/removed/enabled counts.
inventory_notes: Two-pass rollout: first --dry-run to inspect, then real run with --enable. --tenant and --all are mutually exclusive.

user_story: As the platform, I want a single catalog of all system QStash cron schedules so that CI can register them idempotently on every deploy.
expected_behaviour: SYSTEM_CRONS is a list of (name, cron_expr, path) tuples. Covers 25 schedules including: apply-pending-configs (hourly), reset-daily-counters (daily midnight), reset-monthly-counters (monthly), expire-trials (daily 02:00), hibernate-idle-tenants (hourly), reconcile-tenant-crons (hourly :05), reconcile-fuel-crons (hourly), ensure-at-cron-wakes (every 5 min), nightly-extraction (hourly), pii-arbiter (hourly :40), reap-stuck-inbound-messages (every minute), snapshot-gravity-weekly (Sunday 05:00), model-health-check (every 30 min), and more. All cron_exprs are UTC.
inventory_notes: Schedules with offsets (e.g. :10, :25, :40) are intentionally staggered to avoid collision on the same minute as other hourly tasks.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

feature: Typed cron pattern registry and handler dispatch
user_story: As the system, I want a registry of cron pattern handlers so that pattern-specific validation, OC data building, prompt injection, and outbound validation are dispatched to the correct handler.
expected_behaviour: register_handler(handler) registers a PatternHandler by handler.pattern (raises RuntimeError on duplicate pattern with different class). get_handler(pattern) looks up by name (raises KeyError for unknown pattern). Four patterns registered on import: pure_reminder, quote_user_intent, domain_summary, daily_briefing. Each handler implements: validate_payload (Pydantic), build_oc_data (OC job dict), get_tools_allow (toolsAllow list), get_prompt_injection (system-prompt appendage), validate_outbound_message (bool + reason), get_fallback_message.
inventory_notes: daily_briefing pattern is system-only — not available to agent nbhd_cron_create_* tools. Handlers are singletons registered at import time.

user_story: As CI/CD, I want to backfill lesson embeddings and rewrite lessons to be actionable from deploy-time hooks so that lesson quality improvements propagate without manual runs.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/cron/run-backfill-lesson-embeddings/. Auth: X-Deploy-Secret. Sets RLS service role. Finds approved Lesson rows with null embedding, calls process_approved_lesson per row. Returns {total, processed, errors}. POST /api/cron/run-rewrite-lessons-actionable/. Auth: X-Deploy-Secret. Sets RLS service role. Calls rewrite_lessons_actionable management command via call_command. Returns {ok, output_bytes}. POST /api/cron/run-reseed-lessons/. Auth: X-Deploy-Secret. Calls reseed_lessons management command. Returns {ok, output_bytes}.
inventory_notes: Lesson text is deliberately not logged to container logs (shared workspace concern). Output byte count is logged instead.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want a single endpoint that shows my tenant status, cumulative token usage, and connected integrations so that I can see my account health at a glance.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/dashboard/ requires IsAuthenticated (JWT). Looks up request.user.tenant; returns HTTP 404 {"detail": "No tenant found."} if no tenant exists. On success (HTTP 200) returns a JSON object with three top-level keys: "tenant" (id, status, model_tier, provisioned_at), "usage" (messages_today, messages_this_month, tokens_this_month, estimated_cost_this_month, monthly_token_budget from Tenant fields; plus all-time total_input_tokens/total_output_tokens/total_cost aggregated via SUM over UsageRecord), and "connections" (list of active Integration rows with provider, provider_email, connected_at). Response is cached per-tenant with a 30-second TTL under the "dashboard" cache tag; cache is bypassed when Redis is unhealthy (tag_v == 0) and an X-Cache: BYPASS header is set. Cached responses return X-Cache: HIT; misses set the cache and return X-Cache: MISS. Only 200 responses are stored in cache; 404s are never cached.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (UsageRecord, Integration, Tenant models), Upstash Redis (tenant_cache tag versioning via apps/common/cache.py)
inventory_notes: cost_estimate and total_cost are returned as strings (str(Decimal)); missing UsageRecord rows produce 0 totals via `or 0` guard. Connected integrations filter only Integration.Status.ACTIVE rows.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see my 50 most recent usage records so that I can audit which AI interactions consumed tokens and cost.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/dashboard/usage/ requires IsAuthenticated. Returns HTTP 404 if no tenant. On success (HTTP 200) returns {"results": [...]} with up to 50 UsageRecord rows ordered by -created_at. Each row includes: id (str UUID), event_type, input_tokens, output_tokens, model_used, cost_estimate (str Decimal), created_at. No pagination beyond the hard 50-row cap. This endpoint does NOT use the @tenant_cache decorator — it is uncached and always hits Postgres directly.
inventory_notes: Hard-capped at 50 records; no cursor/offset pagination. No cache layer, so high-frequency polling would hit Postgres each time.

feature: Horizons Aggregation (Goals + Mood + Momentum + Insights + Topic Signals)
user_story: As a subscriber, I want a single "Horizons" endpoint that aggregates my goals, pending extractions, weekly pulse, mood trend, 30-day momentum streak, assistant insights, and topic signal metadata so that the frontend can render a rich goal-and-wellbeing dashboard.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/dashboard/horizons/ requires IsAuthenticated. Returns HTTP 404 if no tenant. On success (HTTP 200) returns a JSON object with seven sections: (1) "goals" — union of typed Goal (status=ACTIVE) and legacy Document(kind=GOAL) rows, deduplicated via Goal.migrated_from_document; returns up to 20 items sorted by -updated_at, each with id, title, slug ("typed:<uuid>" for typed Goals), preview (first 200 chars of markdown), created_at, updated_at; inactive typed goals (ACHIEVED/ABANDONED) are excluded. (2) "pending_extractions" — up to 10 non-expired PendingExtraction rows of kind GOAL or TASK with status PENDING, ordered by -created_at. (3) "weekly_pulse" — up to 4 WeeklyReview rows (legacy model), ordered by -week_start, each with week_start, week_end, week_rating, top_win (first element of top_wins or null). (4) "weekly_documents" — up to 4 Document(kind=WEEKLY) rows, serialized via _serialize_weekly_document() which derives week_start/week_end from YYYY-MM-DD slug, strips markdown to a 180-char plain text preview, and includes full markdown. (5) "mood_trend" — all JournalEntry rows in the last 30 days with date, mood, energy fields. (6) "momentum" — 30-day array (today - 29d to today) where each entry has date, message_count (UsageRecord count for that date), has_journal (True if JournalEntry OR Document(kind=DAILY) exists for that date); plus "current_streak" (consecutive days with activity counting backwards from today). (7) "assistant_insights" — up to 20 AssistantInsight rows with status OPEN or CONFIRMED (REFUTED excluded), ordered by -created_at, with id, pillar, topic_slug, topic_display_name, statement, status, confidence, created_at, last_confirmed_at. (8) "topic_signals" — per-topic meta-state from apps/insights/signals.summarize_topic_signals(), covering topics with any engagement (snapshots, insights, goals, or voice prefs), returning sample_size, confirmed/refuted counts, has_goal flag, register_offset/register_scope. Response is cached per-tenant with a 60-second TTL under the "dashboard" cache tag; cache bypassed if Redis tag_v == 0.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (Goal, Document, PendingExtraction, WeeklyReview, JournalEntry, UsageRecord, AssistantInsight, TopicRegistry, PillarSnapshot, UserVoicePref models), Upstash Redis (tenant_cache), apps/insights/signals.summarize_topic_signals, apps/insights/baselines.TOPIC_EXTRACTORS
inventory_notes: Dual-read goal logic: typed Goals with migrated_from_document skip the legacy Document row to prevent duplication. The _derive_week_bounds fallback uses the Document's updated_at date to compute Monday of that week if the slug is not a parseable YYYY-MM-DD date. topic_signals does two passes: (1) FK-touched topics from insights/goals/prefs tables, (2) extractor-supported topics from PillarSnapshot payloads. REFUTED insights are intentionally excluded from "assistant_insights" in the user view but kept in DB for the assistant.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

feature: Dashboard Cache Invalidation via Signal Receivers
user_story: As the system, I want the per-tenant dashboard cache tag to be bumped immediately whenever a relevant model changes so that the frontend's optimistic UI mutations reflect in the next refetch instead of reverting.
expected_behaviour: Django post_save and post_delete signals on AssistantInsight, UserVoicePref, Goal, and Document all fire _bump_for(instance), which calls apps.common.cache.bump_tag(tenant_id, "dashboard"). This invalidates the cached responses for both DashboardView (TTL 30s) and HorizonsView (TTL 60s). The receiver swallows all exceptions so a Redis failure never propagates into the model save path. bump_tag already logs and returns 0 on Redis failures; the receiver adds a second try/except with logger.exception as belt-and-suspenders. Task model saves do NOT bump the tag (HorizonsView does not surface Task state). Receivers are registered in DashboardConfig.ready() via import of apps.dashboard.receivers.
dependencies: Upstash Redis (bump_tag via Django cache backend), apps/insights/models.AssistantInsight, apps/insights/models.UserVoicePref, apps/journal/models.Goal, apps/journal/models.Document
inventory_notes: bump_tag is a Redis INCR on a key like nbhd:tag:<tenant_id>:dashboard. Tag version 0 means Redis is unhealthy — the view bypasses cache entirely in that state. Document saves of ANY kind bump the tag (not just GOAL kind) because DashboardView also reads daily notes for the momentum streak.
error_detail: Real but lower-impact than claimed. HorizonsView.get is cached with @tenant_cache(ttl=60, tag="dashboard") and surfaces pending_extractions (apps/dashboard/views.py:192, 251-268). apps/dashboard/receivers.py registers bump-on-save receivers for AssistantInsight, UserVoicePref, Goal, and Document only — PendingExtraction and Task have NO receiver (the docstring at receivers.py:27-29 deliberately excludes Task). Two paths leave the dashboard cache tag un-bumped: (1) ExtractionDismissView writes only PendingExtraction.status=DISMISSED with no covered-model write (apps/journal/views.py:588-590); (2) the typed-task approve path Task.objects.create + pending.save (apps/router/extraction_callbacks.py:153-158), which only runs for tenants with experimental_typed_journal_lifecycle=True (default False, canary-gated). In both cases the dashboard tag version is not advanced, so a GET /horizons/ refetch returns the stale cached pending_extractions list for up to 60s. NOTE: approve-goal (typed Goal AND legacy Document) and legacy-task approve DO bump the tag via the Goal/Document receivers, so they are correctly covered. IMPACT IS TEMPERED by the frontend: PendingGoal (frontend/components/pending-goal.tsx:21-33) holds local resolved useState that hides the card on dismiss/approve success, so during the live session the user does not see a revert — the stale refetch is masked. The glitch only surfaces on a hard page reload within the 60s window (local state lost), where an already-resolved extraction transiently reappears. DB state is always correct; this is a cosmetic stale-cache flicker, not a data-integrity or functional defect — hence low, not medium.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want the /automations URL to take me to the correct page so that old bookmarks or links still work.
expected_behaviour: Visiting /automations triggers a client-side useEffect that calls router.replace('/settings/automations'). /settings/automations in turn immediately calls router.replace('/settings/cron-jobs'). Net effect: two consecutive client-side redirects land the user on /settings/cron-jobs. No content is rendered at either redirect step (both components return null). No API call is made.
inventory_notes: Two hops of router.replace occur before the final page renders. There is no loading state shown during either redirect — the page is blank momentarily.

feature: Scheduled Tasks page — initial load (skeleton / error / empty states)
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see my scheduled tasks when I open the Scheduled Tasks page so that I can manage them.
expected_behaviour: On mount, useCronJobsQuery fires GET /api/v1/cron-jobs/ (staleTime 60s). While loading, a SectionCardSkeleton with 5 placeholder lines is shown. If the request fails, a rose-coloured error card appears with the error message and a hint to run check_gateway_health. If the response returns zero jobs, a 'No scheduled tasks configured yet.' message is shown. If jobs exist, the 'Your Tasks' SectionCard renders the full list. The page also simultaneously fires GET /api/v1/tenants/me/ (useMeQuery) to obtain the user timezone, and GET /api/v1/integrations/ (useIntegrationsQuery) to determine connected providers.
inventory_notes: Error state additionally shows 'python manage.py check_gateway_health' as a hint, which is an internal operator command — visible to end users.
error_detail: Confirmed. In the subscriber-facing console page frontend/app/settings/cron-jobs/page.tsx, the "Your Tasks" error-state card (shown when the cron jobs query fails) renders a literal operator-internal CLI instruction: "If this persists, run: python manage.py check_gateway_health" (line 717). This is a "use client" component under app/settings/ in the Next.js subscriber dashboard, so the audience is end-user subscribers with no shell/server CLI access. The guidance is confusing and useless to them since they cannot run a Django management command. Impact is purely cosmetic/UX and only surfaces on the error branch; the adjacent text "Could not load scheduled tasks" still conveys the real problem, and no secret/sensitive data is exposed (the command name is not a credential). Hence low severity, not a security/data-integrity concern.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to set the time window when my assistant can proactively contact me so that I am not messaged outside my preferred hours.
expected_behaviour: WorkingHoursSection calls GET /api/v1/tenants/heartbeat/ on mount. While loading, a SectionCardSkeleton (2 lines) is shown. In view mode, the current time block label and range (e.g. 'Morning · 6:00 AM – 12:00 PM') plus the user's timezone are displayed, along with an Edit button. Clicking Edit enters an editing mode that initialises from server data (enabled boolean + start_hour integer). The user toggles On/Off and selects one of five preset blocks (Morning/Daytime/Afternoon/Evening/Night). Clicking Save calls PATCH /api/v1/tenants/heartbeat/ with { enabled, start_hour }. On success, the form closes and a 4-second success banner reads 'Saved! Changes take effect within 15 minutes.' On error, a rose banner reads 'Failed to save. Please try again.' Cancel discards changes. A PendingConfigChip is shown to indicate if a config push is outstanding.
inventory_notes: State is seeded from server data only when Edit is clicked (handleStartEdit), not when the component mounts — so if the query hasn't resolved yet when the user clicks Edit, it opens with default useState values (enabled=true, startHour=6). The feature_tips field is a separate concern handled by FeatureTipsSection but uses the same endpoint.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to control whether my assistant suggests new features so that I can keep our conversations focused.
expected_behaviour: FeatureTipsSection calls GET /api/v1/tenants/heartbeat/ (shared query with WorkingHoursSection, staleTime 5 min). While loading, a SectionCardSkeleton (1 line) is shown. The component renders a toggle switch styled as role='switch' aria-checked. The current description 'Your assistant may suggest one new feature per week' or 'won't suggest features unless you ask' is shown. Clicking the toggle fires PATCH /api/v1/tenants/heartbeat/ with { feature_tips: !enabled }. While pending, the toggle is disabled (opacity-50). On success, a 4-second banner confirms the new state. On error, 'Failed to save. Please try again.' appears for 4 seconds.
inventory_notes: Uses the same React Query key ['working-hours'] as WorkingHoursSection — updates to either component will cause both to reflect the new data via cache invalidation.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to open a form to create a new scheduled task so that I can configure recurring AI agent actions.
expected_behaviour: The Scheduled Tasks SectionCard shows an 'Add scheduled task' button when showCreate is false. Clicking it resets the create form to defaultCreateForm (uses me.timezone if loaded, else Intl browser TZ, else 'UTC') and sets showCreate=true, replacing the button with the full create form. Clicking Cancel or on successful submit sets showCreate=false and restores the 'Add scheduled task' button. No API call is made by the open/close action itself.
inventory_notes: If me data is not yet loaded when the button is clicked, timezone defaults to browser Intl or 'UTC'. Timezone default is fixed at form-open time and not reactively updated if the me query resolves later.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to pick from preset templates (Morning Briefing, News Digest, etc.) so that I can quickly configure a common automation without writing from scratch.
expected_behaviour: When the create form is open, a horizontally-scrollable (mobile) or wrapping (sm+) row of 6 pill buttons is shown: Morning Briefing, News Digest, Weather Report, Daily Schedule, Evening Recap, Weekly Review. Clicking any template calls handleApplyTemplate which sets createForm.name, createForm.message, createForm.expr, and createForm.foreground (all templates set foreground=true) to the template's values. The matching pill is visually highlighted (border-accent bg-accent/10 text-accent) based on name match. Selecting a template does not submit the form — the user still clicks Create. No API call is made.
inventory_notes: Template pill highlight relies on createForm.name === tpl.name exact match. If the user edits the Name field after applying a template, the pill deselects. The tz and deliveryMode fields are NOT overridden by the template — they retain their current form values.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want a guided schedule picker so that I can set up a cron schedule without knowing cron syntax.
expected_behaviour: The ScheduleBuilder component starts in easy mode when the initial expr is parseable by parseCronExpr. Easy mode shows a Frequency select (Every day / Weekdays / Weekends / Weekly / Monthly). Weekly shows day-of-week multi-select (Mon–Sun). Monthly shows a day-of-month select (1st–28th, no 29th–31st). Hour select (00–23) and Minute select (00, 15, 30, 45). State changes immediately rebuild the cron expression via buildCronFromState and propagate via onChange. A human-readable label ('Runs: Every day at 09:00') appears at the top. The builder also shows a toggle 'Use cron expression' to switch to advanced mode.
inventory_notes: Minute options are limited to :00, :15, :30, :45 in easy mode — arbitrary minutes require advanced mode. Monthly day range stops at 28 to avoid month-length issues. If the incoming expr is not parseable (e.g. a non-standard pattern), easy mode cannot be entered and an error message 'This expression uses a custom pattern not supported by the schedule builder.' is shown.
error_detail: Confirmed real. In frontend/components/schedule-builder.tsx the WEEKDAY_LABELS (lines 30-38) and WEEKDAY_NAMES (lines 40-48) arrays are ordered Monday=index0 ... Sunday=index6. For the weekly frequency the day-picker writes the array index directly into state.weekdays (lines 364-380), and buildCronFromState joins those indices into the 5th cron field unchanged (lines 119-123). But the resulting cron string is consumed with STANDARD cron weekday semantics (0=Sunday, 1=Monday ... 6=Saturday): croniter computes next-fire in apps/cron/gateway_client.py:243-254, and the expr is submitted verbatim from createForm.expr to the cron API / OpenClaw engine with no remapping (frontend/app/settings/cron-jobs/page.tsx:600-603). So picking Mon emits 0 which fires Sunday; Tue emits 1 which fires Monday; ... Sun emits 6 which fires Saturday. Every weekly task built via the easy builder fires one day earlier than intended. The bug is self-confirming because the hardcoded presets in the SAME file use correct standard convention: weekdays maps to 1-5 (line 116) and weekends maps to 0,6 (line 118), while only the weekly day-picker is off by one. formatHumanWeekdays (lines 209-215) and parseCronExpr (lines 173-189) use the same off-by-one arrays, so the job-list label (page.tsx:857) and round-trip edits stay internally consistent and the user never sees the mismatch in the UI -- they only find out when the task fires on the wrong day. User impact: silent wrong-day execution of every user-created weekly scheduled task.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to type a raw cron expression so that I can schedule tasks at exact intervals not covered by the easy builder.
expected_behaviour: Clicking 'Use cron expression' from easy mode switches to a monospace text input pre-filled with the current cron expression. Typing updates the expression immediately via onChange. A pill displays the current expression ('📋 Cron expression: …'). Clicking 'Use schedule builder' attempts to parse the current expression via parseCronExpr; if successful, switches back to easy mode populated with parsed values. If the expression is not parseable, shows 'This expression uses a custom pattern not supported by the schedule builder.' and stays in advanced mode.
inventory_notes: The cron input has no live validation — an invalid expression will be accepted in the form and only rejected server-side on submit. The expression field is marked required.

feature: Create scheduled task — Delivery mode selector
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to choose whether the task sends me a message or runs silently so that I control how the AI communicates its results.
expected_behaviour: A native <select> with two options: 'Announce (send message)' (value='announce') and 'Silent (no message)' (value='none'). Default is 'announce'. The selected value is stored in createForm.deliveryMode and submitted as delivery.mode in the create payload.
inventory_notes: The deliveryChannel field is also part of the form state but has no UI input in the create form — it defaults to 'telegram' in defaultCreateForm but is only included in the POST body when non-empty.

feature: Create scheduled task — Session mode selector (Foreground / Background)
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to choose whether a scheduled task reports back to my assistant after running so that background logging tasks do not clutter my conversation context.
expected_behaviour: SessionModeSelector renders two card buttons with role='radio' aria-checked. Foreground (default, foreground=true): 'Reports back to your assistant if it sends you a message. Best for: reminders, briefings, daily summaries.' Background (foreground=false): 'Runs silently. Never reports back to your assistant. Best for: logging, file updates, data collection.' The selected card gets border-accent + a glow shadow. The unselected card can be hovered. No API call until form submit.
inventory_notes: Under the 'universal isolation model' every cron runs in an isolated session; foreground/background only controls whether a Phase 2 summary is pushed back to the main session after the run.

feature: Create scheduled task — Prompt editor (TipTap rich text)
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to describe in plain language what my agent should do so that the task runs the right action.
expected_behaviour: PromptEditor is a TipTap-based rich text editor that serialises its content to/from plain text with embedded chip nodes (rendered as [Tag] tokens). Clicking the 'What should your agent do?' label calls createEditorRef.current?.focus(). The editor content is two-way-bound via onChange. On paste/type, the text is normalised via docToPlain (chip nodes serialised as [Tag]). No API call on typing.
inventory_notes: The placeholder hint references [Google] and [Weather] tag syntax. The editor value is trimmed before submission.

feature: Create scheduled task — Capability chips (tag insertion)
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to click capability chips (Web Search, Weather, Google, etc.) to insert tool tags into my prompt so that my agent knows which tools to use.
expected_behaviour: CapabilityChips renders three groups — Integrations (Google, Reddit), Skills (Daily Journal, Weekly Review, PKM), Built-in (Web Search, Weather, News, Memory). Connected integrations are determined by filtering useIntegrationsQuery data for status='active'. A chip is highlighted (border-accent bg-accent/10 text-accent) when its [Tag] is already present in the message string. Disconnected integration chips are disabled and show a 'connect' link pointing to /settings/integrations. Clicking an enabled chip calls editorRef.current?.insertChip(chip.tag) to insert [Tag] at the cursor position in the TipTap editor. No API call.
inventory_notes: Active detection uses message.includes('[Tag]') — a simple string check, not a TipTap node search. The title tooltip on a disabled chip says 'Connect [Tag] in Settings → Integrations'.

feature: Create scheduled task — Show/hide agent capabilities reference
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see a reference list of what my agent can do so that I can write an effective task prompt.
expected_behaviour: A small text toggle 'Show all capabilities ↗' / 'Hide' below the capability chips expands/collapses a panel listing 8 capabilities (Web Search, Weather, News, Message, Memory, Journal, Goals & Projects, Search Notes) with icon + description. State is local (showCapabilities boolean). No API call.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to submit the create form so that my new scheduled task is saved and begins running on schedule.
expected_behaviour: The Create button is disabled while createMutation.isPending and shows 'Creating…'. On submit (form onSubmit), handleCreate calls createMutation.mutateAsync with the full payload including name (required, trimmed), schedule { kind:'cron', expr, tz }, foreground, payload { kind:'agentTurn', message (trimmed) }, delivery { mode, optional channel }, enabled:true. The name field has the HTML required attribute. On success: form resets to defaultCreateForm, showCreate closes, the Create button briefly shows '✓ Created' in emerald for 2 seconds (SAVE_CLEAR_DELAY_MS), then returns to idle. On error: the button turns rose, an error message appears below it for 4 seconds (ERROR_CLEAR_DELAY_MS). The ['cron-jobs'] query is invalidated on success.
inventory_notes: Name is the only HTML-validated required field. There is no client-side validation for message length or cron expression validity — these are enforced server-side.
error_detail: Confirmed structurally. In handleCreate (page.tsx:357-359), on a successful create the code calls setShowCreate(false) BEFORE setSuccess(setCreateFeedback, ...). The Created emerald success state for the submit button is rendered only inside the form block, which is gated by showCreate===true (the form is the else branch of the !showCreate ternary at page.tsx:545-705; the success button text is at 682-687). Because the form is collapsed first, createFeedback.status flips to success while the button is already unmounted, so the Created affirmation never renders -- effectively dead code on the create path. There is also no toast on create success: the create mutation onSuccess (queries.ts:978-980) only invalidates the cron-jobs query, and handleCreate never calls showToast -- unlike the bulk-delete/bulk-enable paths which DO fire a confirmation toast (page.tsx:503,517), and unlike the edit path (handleUpdate, 396-397) which keeps its form open and shows its saved state. User impact is limited though: query invalidation refetches the list, so the newly created task does visibly appear in the Scheduled Tasks list below. So the create is not truly silent -- the user gets implicit confirmation via the new row -- but the explicit success affirmation the code clearly intends to show is lost and the form simply vanishes, inconsistent with the edit and bulk flows. Real but low-severity UX polish issue.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see each scheduled task's name, schedule, status, delivery channel, and session mode at a glance so that I can review my automations.
expected_behaviour: Each cron job is rendered as an <article> card showing: name (bold), human-readable schedule via cronToHuman(job.schedule.expr, job.schedule.tz) (e.g. 'Every day at 09:00 (Asia/Tokyo)'), status pill (active/paused), delivery channel badge (shown when mode !== 'none' and channel is set), foreground/background badge, and first 200 chars of the prompt (with date-context prefix stripped via stripPromptPrefix). Action buttons: Disable/Enable, Edit, Delete.
inventory_notes: jobIdentifier is job.jobId ?? job.name — legacy jobs without a jobId fall back to name. The prompt preview uses line-clamp-2 and is sliced to 200 chars before display.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to pause or resume a scheduled task so that I can temporarily stop it without deleting it.
expected_behaviour: Each job row shows a 'Disable' or 'Enable' button based on job.enabled. Clicking fires toggleMutation.mutate({ name: jobIdentifier, enabled: !job.enabled }), which calls POST /api/v1/cron-jobs/{nameOrId}/toggle/ with { enabled }. The mutation uses optimistic update: the ['cron-jobs'] cache is updated immediately before the request, with rollback on error. While toggleMutation.isPending (any job), the button is disabled (cursor-not-allowed opacity-45). There is no per-row pending state — all Enable/Disable buttons are disabled while any toggle is in flight.
inventory_notes: The optimistic update matches on job.jobId === jobId ?? name OR job.name === name — both conditions are checked. On error the cache is rolled back and the query invalidated.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to edit an existing scheduled task's schedule, message, delivery mode, and session mode so that I can update the automation without recreating it.
expected_behaviour: Clicking the Edit button on a job row calls handleStartEdit(job), which sets editingName to jobIdentifier and populates editForm from the job's current values (stripping date-context prefix from message). On desktop (isMobile !== true), an inline form appears inside the article card. It contains: TimezoneSelector, ScheduleBuilder, Delivery select, SessionModeSelector, and PromptEditor with CapabilityChips. Clicking Save calls handleUpdate which fires PATCH /api/v1/cron-jobs/{nameOrId}/ with the updated schedule, foreground, payload, and delivery. On success: form closes, Save button shows '✓ Saved' in emerald for 2 seconds. On error: rose button + error text for 4 seconds. Cancel clears editingName and resets feedback. Only one job can be in edit mode at a time.
inventory_notes: The edit form does NOT include a name field — job names cannot be changed via the UI. The update mutation resolves the ID via jobId ?? name. On both success and error, the ['cron-jobs'] query is invalidated.

user_story: As a subscriber on mobile, I want the edit form to open as a full-screen overlay so that I have enough room to type the task prompt.
expected_behaviour: When editingName is set and isMobile is true, the edit form is rendered via createPortal into document.body as a fixed full-screen overlay (z-[9999]). A sticky header bar at the top shows 'Edit Task', a Save button and a Cancel button. The scrollable body contains the same fields as the desktop inline form. When the overlay is open, document.body.style.overflow is set to 'hidden' and the app header is hidden (visibility: hidden) via querySelector. These are restored on cleanup. The Save action on mobile fires handleUpdate via an onClick that casts the event to a form event.
inventory_notes: Mobile is detected via window.matchMedia('(max-width: 767px)') with a change listener. The save button on mobile does not show the emerald/rose state feedback — it only shows '...' while pending.

feature: Cron job — single delete with inline confirmation
user_story: As a subscriber, I want a safety confirmation before deleting a scheduled task so that I don't accidentally remove it.
expected_behaviour: Clicking the 'Delete' button on a job row calls startSingleDeleteConfirm(jobId), which sets singleDeleteConfirm[jobId]=true and starts a 3-second auto-cancel timer (SINGLE_DELETE_TIMEOUT_MS). The row changes to a rose-tinted background. A 'Delete?' label and two 44px circular buttons appear: a ✓ confirm button and a ✕ cancel button. Clicking ✓ calls confirmSingleDelete(jobId) which fires DELETE /api/v1/cron-jobs/{nameOrId}/. While pending, the ✓ shows '…'. On success: the job disappears from the list (query invalidated) and is removed from selectedIds. On error: a rose error message appears below the buttons for 4 seconds, then resets. Clicking ✕ or the auto-3s timeout cancels and restores the normal Delete button.
inventory_notes: Per-row state is tracked in three separate Record<string, …> maps (singleDeleteConfirm, singleDeleteError, singleDeletePending) and a ref map of timers. On both success and error the ['cron-jobs'] query is invalidated.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to select multiple scheduled tasks at once so that I can bulk-delete or bulk-update them.
expected_behaviour: When there is more than one job, a 'Select all / Deselect all' checkbox row appears above the list. Each job row has its own 44px-target checkbox. Selecting one or more jobs reveals the bulk action bar (animated max-height transition). toggleSelectAll selects or deselects all jobs. toggleSelectOne adds/removes a single jobIdentifier from the selectedIds Set. The Clear button empties the set. Selection state is purely client-side.
inventory_notes: The 'select all' header row only appears when selectableJobs.length > 1. Selected rows get a different background (bg-surface-hover). The bulk bar is hidden when someSelected is false via max-h-0 CSS transition.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to delete multiple scheduled tasks at once so that I can clean up my automations efficiently.
expected_behaviour: With one or more jobs selected, the bulk bar shows 'Delete selected' (rose button). Clicking it sets bulkBarState to 'confirming', showing a red warning 'Delete N tasks? This cannot be undone.' with Confirm and Cancel buttons. Clicking Confirm fires bulkDeleteMutation.mutateAsync(ids) which calls POST /api/v1/cron-jobs/bulk-delete/ with { ids: string[] }. On success, selectedIds is cleared, bulkBarState returns to 'idle', and a Toast notification shows 'N tasks deleted' in green. On error, a rose error message appears in the bar and bulkBarState resets to 'idle'. Cancel resets to idle without deleting.
inventory_notes: The response body { deleted: number } is used for the toast. The bulk bar is aria-live='polite'. Confirm/Cancel buttons are disabled while bulkDeleteMutation.isPending.

feature: Cron job — bulk set foreground / background
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to change the session mode of multiple tasks at once so that I can quickly configure whether they report back to the assistant.
expected_behaviour: With jobs selected and bulkBarState='idle', the bulk bar shows 'Set foreground' and 'Set background' buttons. Clicking either fires bulkForegroundMutation.mutateAsync({ ids, foreground: true/false }) which calls POST /api/v1/cron-jobs/bulk-update-foreground/ with { ids, foreground }. While pending, both buttons show 'Updating…' and are disabled. On success, selectedIds is cleared and a Toast shows 'N tasks set to foreground/background'. On error, a Toast with the error message is shown. The ['cron-jobs'] query is invalidated on both success and error.
inventory_notes: No confirmation step before bulk foreground update — unlike bulk delete.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see my pending one-off reminders so that I know which scheduled reminders are queued up.
expected_behaviour: PendingRemindersSection fires GET /api/v1/cron-jobs/pending-at/ (staleTime 30s, refetchOnWindowFocus). While loading and no data is available, the component renders nothing (returns null). On error, an error card is shown. When jobs exist, each reminder shows: name, absolute fire time ('Today at 4:00 PM' / 'Tomorrow at…' / 'Wed, May 14 at 9:00 AM' in user's TZ), and a relative label ('in 20 min', 'in 3 hours', etc., refreshed every 30s via setInterval). A badge shows 'N of softCap' in neutral/amber/rose depending on proximity to the cap. A stale banner appears if the container was asleep when the fetch ran.
inventory_notes: The component is silent (null) on initial loading — no skeleton. Relative times auto-update client-side every 30s without re-fetching the server. Timezone falls back to browser Intl if userTz prop is undefined.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to cancel a pending one-off reminder so that it doesn't fire unexpectedly.
expected_behaviour: Each reminder row has a 'Cancel' button. Clicking it shows an inline confirmation: 'Confirm cancel' (rose) and 'Keep' buttons. A 3-second auto-timeout (CANCEL_CONFIRM_TIMEOUT_MS) reverts to the initial Cancel button if no action is taken. Clicking 'Confirm cancel' fires cancelMutation.mutate(job.name), calling DELETE /api/v1/cron-jobs/pending-at/{name}/. While pending, the button shows 'Cancelling…' and is disabled. On success, the ['pending-reminders'] query is invalidated and the reminder disappears. On error, a rose error message appears inline under the reminder name.
inventory_notes: The cancel confirmation timeout uses a single shared ref (cancelTimer), so only one reminder can be in confirming state at a time. The empty state shows a hint: 'Ask your assistant anytime — e.g. "remind me in 20 minutes" or "ping me at 4pm."'
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

feature: TimezoneSelector — timezone picker in create/edit forms
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to set the timezone for a scheduled task so that it fires at the correct local time in my location.
expected_behaviour: TimezoneSelector renders as a labelled select in the create and both edit forms. The value is initialised to the user's me.timezone (from useMeQuery) or the browser Intl TZ when the form opens. The subscriber can change the timezone via the select; the value is stored in form state (createForm.tz / editForm.tz) and sent as schedule.tz in the POST/PATCH body. No separate API call.
error_detail: Confirmed real. TimezoneSelector pairs a free-text search box (filter state) with a native select. The useEffect at lines 121-125 fires onChange(filteredTimezones[0]) whenever the currently-selected timezone is no longer in the filtered list. Because the filter re-applies on every keystroke, partial filter strings hide the current selection and commit an arbitrary alphabetically-first match into the parent form state (createForm.tz / editForm.tz). Crucially this transient is NOT reverted when the user clears the search: clearing makes all timezones visible again, so the guard at line 122 (currentValue not in filteredTimezones) becomes false and no re-selection occurs, so the silently-changed value persists. Real impact: a subscriber who starts typing to find a timezone but abandons the search (clears the box or submits/navigates) without explicitly clicking an option can save a wrong timezone, causing the scheduled task to fire at the wrong local time. Severity is low because (a) the select always displays currentValue so an attentive user can see the change, and (b) the dominant flow (type then click the matching option) ends with an explicit selection that overrides the transient. The footgun only bites the type-then-abandon path. It is intended-but-flawed behaviour: the comment at lines 119-120 states the sync intent but does not account for partial-typing transients.

feature: Settings navigation — Scheduled Tasks tab prefetch
user_story: As a subscriber, I want the Scheduled Tasks page to load quickly when I hover over the menu item so that navigation feels instant.
expected_behaviour: In SettingsLayout, hovering over the 'Scheduled Tasks' link (/settings/cron-jobs) on desktop triggers prefetchTabData which calls queryClient.prefetchQuery({ queryKey: ['cron-jobs'], queryFn: fetchCronJobs }). On mobile, the same prefetch fires on touchStart of the list item. This pre-warms the React Query cache before the route renders.
inventory_notes: Prefetch is fire-and-forget (void). The tab label in the sidebar is 'Scheduled Tasks' with a ClockIcon.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see a transient success or error toast after bulk operations so that I receive feedback without blocking the UI.
expected_behaviour: The useToast hook provides a [toast, showToast] pair. showToast(message, type) creates a toast with a unique id. The Toast component renders conditionally when toast is non-null at the top of the page. Toasts are used for: bulk delete success ('N tasks deleted', 'success'), bulk foreground update success, and error toasts from bulk foreground mutation errors. The toast auto-dismisses via the hook's internal mechanism. Per the component signature, the onDismiss callback is a no-op in the page (the hook manages state).
inventory_notes: Single-job delete and inline edit success/error do NOT use the Toast — they use inline button colour feedback instead.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

user_story: As a subscriber, I want the old /billing URL to still work so that I am not left on a dead page if I bookmarked it.
expected_behaviour: On mount, useEffect immediately calls router.replace('/settings/billing'). No content is ever rendered — the component returns null. The redirect is client-side only (static export); there is no server-side redirect.
inventory_notes: Same pattern as /usage → /settings/usage. Because it uses router.replace the back button will not return to /billing.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want the old /usage URL to still work so that I am not left on a dead page.
expected_behaviour: On mount, useEffect immediately calls router.replace('/settings/usage'). No content is rendered. Static-export client-side only.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see a loading placeholder while my billing data loads so that the page does not appear blank or broken.
expected_behaviour: While useTenantQuery isLoading is true the page renders a single SectionCardSkeleton with lines=5. Once data resolves it is replaced with the real billing UI.

feature: No-tenant empty state with onboarding CTA
user_story: As a subscriber whose tenant has not been provisioned yet, I want to see a clear prompt to complete onboarding so that I can get a subscription started.
expected_behaviour: When useTenantQuery returns no data (or an error), the billing SectionCard body shows a credit-card SVG, a 'No subscription yet' heading, and an onboarding CTA button. If the user has a telegram_chat_id the CTA links to '/settings/integrations' with label 'Review messaging setup'; otherwise it links to '/onboarding' with label 'Connect messaging'. A checkout error banner appears below if checkoutError is set.
inventory_notes: hasTenant = Boolean(tenant) && !error; so a network error also triggers this empty state rather than an explicit error UI.

user_story: As a subscriber on a free trial, I want to see how many days remain so that I know when I need to subscribe to keep my assistant.
expected_behaviour: When tenant.is_trial is true and trial_days_remaining > 0, a blue-tinted info banner renders inside the Billing SectionCard: 'You're on a free trial! N days remaining. Subscribe to keep your assistant.' When trial_days_remaining === 0 the same area renders a rose-tinted banner: 'Your free trial has ended. Subscribe to reactivate your assistant.'

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see my plan details and agent status at a glance so that I know whether my assistant is running.
expected_behaviour: A two-column grid renders: 'Plan' card ($12/mo with model summary from modelSummary()), 'Agent Status' card showing a StatusPill for tenant.status. When status is 'suspended' an explanatory paragraph appears below the pill. A full-width 'Subscription' card shows a StatusPill for active/pending based on tenant.has_active_subscription.
inventory_notes: Plan price is hardcoded as $12/mo in the JSX, not fetched from the API.

user_story: As a subscriber without an active subscription, I want to click a 'Subscribe' button so that I can be taken to Stripe Checkout to start my plan.
expected_behaviour: When tenant.has_active_subscription is false, a dashed CTA box renders with a 'Subscribe — $12/mo' button. On click it calls POST /api/v1/billing/checkout/ via useCheckoutMutation. While pending the button label becomes 'Redirecting...' and is disabled. On success window.location.assign(result.url) navigates the browser to Stripe Checkout. On error the JSON body.detail is parsed and displayed in a rose banner below; falls back to 'Checkout failed. Please try again.' The box styling varies: accent for active trial, rose for expired trial, amber for provisioned-but-never-trialed.
dependencies: GET /api/v1/tenants/me/, Stripe Checkout session creation on backend
inventory_notes: The same button also appears in the no-tenant empty state, so handleCheckout is reused in both branches.
error_detail: Confirmed as dead code. `checkoutError` is set only inside `handleCheckout` (lines 36-53), and `handleCheckout` is invoked only by the Subscribe button at line 136, which lives entirely within the `hasTenant && tenant` branch (lines 79-174). The no-tenant empty-state else branch (lines 175-206) renders a `checkoutError` banner at lines 200-204 but offers no control that can call `handleCheckout` — only a Link to onboarding/integrations (lines 187-192). Therefore in that branch `checkoutError` is permanently the empty string and the banner can never render. Real user impact is essentially nil: a no-tenant user has no way to start checkout from this view, so there is no error condition that would otherwise need surfacing (the QA framing that a user "encounters a backend issue and sees no error" is overstated — there is no checkout path to fail). The genuine defect is purely maintainability: unreachable JSX that misleads future maintainers into thinking both branches wire up checkout.

user_story: As an active subscriber, I want to open the Stripe billing portal so that I can manage my subscription, invoices, and payment method.
expected_behaviour: When tenant.has_active_subscription is true, an 'Open Stripe Portal' button appears. On click it calls POST /api/v1/billing/portal/. While pending the label becomes 'Opening...' and the button is disabled. On success window.location.assign(result.url) opens the Stripe Customer Portal. On error a JSON body.detail is parsed and displayed in an amber banner; falls back to 'Something went wrong. Please try again.'
dependencies: GET /api/v1/tenants/me/, Stripe Customer Portal on backend
inventory_notes: Portal button only renders when has_active_subscription is true; checkout CTA only when false — these are mutually exclusive.
