feature: Legal link footer on billing page
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to access the Terms, Privacy Policy, and Refund Policy from the billing page so that I can review them before subscribing.
expected_behaviour: A small text paragraph renders beneath the subscription controls (and also in the no-tenant empty state) with underlined links to /legal/terms, /legal/privacy, and /legal/refund. These are Next.js Link components navigating within the SPA.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see my current prepaid credit balance and monthly included usage so that I know how much budget I have left.
expected_behaviour: CreditsCard fetches GET /api/v1/billing/credits/ via useCreditsQuery (staleTime 30s). While loading it renders SectionCardSkeleton lines=4. On success a two-column grid shows: 'Prepaid balance' with data.purchased_credit (rolls over, never expires) and 'Included this month' with data.included_used / data.included_budget. When included_remaining is null, text reads 'Unlimited included usage'; otherwise it shows the remaining amount and notes prepaid credit is used once it runs out.
dependencies: GET /api/v1/tenants/me/ (CreditsCard only renders when hasTenant is true)
inventory_notes: If fetchCredits returns no data (null/undefined after load) the component returns null with no error shown.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to purchase a prepaid credit pack so that my assistant can keep running after my monthly included usage is spent.
expected_behaviour: The credit packs from data.packs are rendered as a 3-column grid of buttons showing pack.credit_display and pack.price_display. Clicking a pack calls POST /api/v1/billing/credits/checkout/ with {pack_id}. While pending all pack buttons are disabled. On success window.location.assign(url) redirects to Stripe Checkout. On error the JSON body.detail is parsed into a rose error banner; falls back to 'Couldn't start checkout. Please try again.'
inventory_notes: All packs share a single isPending flag from useCreditCheckoutMutation so all buttons disable simultaneously regardless of which was clicked.

user_story: As a subscriber who just completed a credit top-up, I want to see confirmation that my payment was received and my balance to update so that I know the credit was applied.
expected_behaviour: When the page mounts with ?topup=success in the URL, CreditsCard reads it via useState lazy initializer (window.location.search, not useSearchParams — avoids SSR/Suspense constraint). A green-tinted banner reads 'Payment received — your credit balance updates within a few seconds.' The credits query is invalidated immediately, then again at 2500ms and 6000ms via setTimeout to catch the webhook-delayed balance update.
inventory_notes: The comment in code explicitly notes 'never trust the redirect — just re-fetch'; the webhook may not have fired yet when Stripe redirects back.
error_detail: Confirmed. The topup value is captured once on mount from window.location.search (credits-card.tsx:18-20) and the URL is never cleaned — there is no router.replace or history.replaceState anywhere (grep for "topup" finds only reads in this one component). Stripe's credit checkout success_url is ${FRONTEND_URL}/settings/billing?topup=success (apps/billing/views.py:377). So after a top-up the user lands on that URL, sees the "Payment received — your credit balance updates within a few seconds." banner (lines 64-68), and the three re-fetch timers (immediate + 2500ms + 6000ms, lines 26-36) fire — all intended. The defect: if the user manually refreshes or revisits that exact URL later with no pending payment, the param persists, the banner re-displays, and the three credit re-fetches fire again. User impact is genuine but minor: the re-fetches are idempotent read-only GETs (no data harm), and the displayed balance is the real server value; only the reassurance banner is misleading on a stale reload. Realistically uncommon because users normally navigate away rather than refresh on this URL, and it isn't a URL one would bookmark.

user_story: As a subscriber who cancelled a top-up checkout, I want to see a message confirming no charge was made so that I am not confused.
expected_behaviour: When the page URL contains ?topup=cancelled a neutral grey-tinted banner renders: 'Checkout cancelled — no charge was made.' No query invalidation is triggered. The rest of the CreditsCard renders normally.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see a list of recent credit transactions so that I can understand what has been charged against my balance.
expected_behaviour: When data.recent_entries.length > 0, a 'Recent activity' section renders below the top-up packs. It shows up to 6 entries (sliced) in a bordered list. Each row shows entry.description or entry.kind (truncated) and entry.amount formatted with a +/- prefix in monospace. Negative amounts are rendered in ink-faint; positive in accent colour.
inventory_notes: Capped at 6 entries with no 'show more' control — entries beyond index 5 are silently dropped.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see my message counts and estimated cost at a glance so that I can understand how much of my plan I have consumed.
expected_behaviour: On load, all three queries (useTenantQuery, useUsageSummaryQuery, useTransparencyQuery) must resolve before content renders; while any of isLoading/summaryLoading/transparencyLoading is true the page renders three StatCardSkeleton placeholders inside a Usage SectionCard. Once resolved, three StatCards show: 'Messages Today' (tenant.messages_today), 'Messages This Month' (tenant.messages_this_month), and 'Estimated Cost' (usageSummary.total_cost ?? tenant.estimated_cost_this_month, 2dp, tone='signal'). When no tenant data, a 'No usage data available yet.' fallback text is shown.
inventory_notes: usageSummary staleTime is 30s; it will re-fetch more aggressively than the tenant query (5min staleTime).

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see a visual progress bar showing how much of my monthly budget I have spent so that I can track my usage at a glance.
expected_behaviour: Below the stat cards, an article renders a 'Monthly budget' row showing costUsed / costBudget in monospace. A gradient progress bar fills from accent to signal colour proportional to budgetPct (clamped 0–100%). Below the bar: '${budgetPct}% of monthly budget consumed. $${costRemaining} remaining this month.' or 'Nothing remaining this month.' When costBudget is 0 the bar renders at 0% with 0% text.
inventory_notes: costUsed falls back to tenant.estimated_cost_this_month if usageSummary.budget is absent. costBudget falls back to 0, making the bar invisible and budgetPct 0.

user_story: As a subscriber who has hit their monthly limit, I want to see a clear warning with a link to upgrade so that I know my assistant may be throttled.
expected_behaviour: When isOverQuota (costUsed >= costBudget && costBudget > 0) a signal-tinted panel renders between the stat cards and the budget bar: 'Monthly quota reached.' / 'You've used your full monthly budget. Upgrade your plan or wait until next month.' with a 'Go to Billing' link navigating to /settings/billing.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see which AI models consumed my tokens and at what cost so that I can understand where my usage is going.
expected_behaviour: A 'Usage by Model' SectionCard renders a scrollable table with columns: Model, Inputs, Outputs, Calls, Cost. Rows come from usageSummary.by_model, displaying entry.display_name or entry.model, input_tokens, output_tokens, count, and cost (4 decimal places). When modelBreakdown is empty, text reads 'No usage by model yet.'

feature: Usage history table with show more/less
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to browse individual usage records so that I can audit what API calls my assistant has made and their costs.
expected_behaviour: A 'Usage History' SectionCard fetches GET /api/v1/dashboard/usage/ via useUsageHistoryQuery (staleTime 60s). While loading it renders SectionCardSkeleton lines=5. Once resolved, a table shows Date, Event Type, Model, Tokens (input+output), and Cost (4dp) per record. Initially only the first 5 records (USAGE_HISTORY_PREVIEW) are shown. When total records exceed 5, a 'Show all N records' button appears; clicking it sets showAllUsage=true revealing all rows. A second click toggles back to 'Show less'. When results is empty the text 'No usage records yet.' renders.
inventory_notes: Dates are formatted with new Date(record.created_at).toLocaleDateString() — locale is the browser's default, no timezone normalization.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see exactly how my $12/mo is spent across AI model costs, infrastructure, and surplus so that I can trust where my money goes.
expected_behaviour: A 'Where Your Money Goes' SectionCard fetches GET /api/v1/billing/usage/transparency/ (staleTime 5min). Three StatCards show AI Model Usage (accent), Platform Infrastructure, and Community Impact (signal tone if donation enabled). A stacked horizontal bar visualises the split: accent (AI%), slate (platform%), emerald (donation% if >0). Below the bar, three colour-coded legend tiles echo the three buckets. An explanation string from transparency.explanation renders as prose. Infrastructure is labelled 'actual' (green badge) or 'estimate' (amber badge) based on infra_breakdown.source. A collapsible <details> 'Model pricing' table shows each model's input/output rate per 1M tokens. When transparency is null/undefined a 'Transparency data unavailable.' fallback renders.
inventory_notes: Infrastructure breakdown drill-down shows container, database_share, and storage_share separately. splitTotal is clamped to minimum 0.01 to prevent division-by-zero in percentage calculations.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to opt in to donating my monthly surplus to a food charity so that unused money from my subscription goes to a good cause.
expected_behaviour: An emerald-tinted article below the transparency breakdown has a toggle switch (role=switch, aria-checked). Current state comes from transparency.donation_enabled. Clicking the toggle calls PATCH /api/v1/billing/donation-preference/ with {donation_enabled: !current} via useDonationPreferenceMutation. The mutation applies an optimistic update to the ['usage-transparency'] cache, reverts on error, and invalidates on settle. While pending the toggle renders at 50% opacity. When donation is enabled a percentage selector appears with options [25, 50, 75, 100]; clicking one calls the same mutation with {donation_percentage: pct}. When surplus > 0 and mealsEstimate (floor(donationAmount / 0.25)) > 0, a green message shows '~N meals for children' with a Feeding America attribution link.
inventory_notes: Optimistic update merges only the changed field; the rest of the TransparencyData shape is preserved. No success toast is shown — the toggle state itself is the feedback.
error_detail: Confirmed. The donation-percentage mutation optimistic update (queries.ts:332-334) does a shallow merge of {...old, ...newData} where newData = { donation_percentage: pct }. It updates donation_percentage in the cached TransparencyData but leaves donation_amount stale. donation_amount is server-derived: surplus * (donation_percentage / 100) (usage_services.py:248), so a percentage change should change it. On the page, donationAmount (page.tsx:32) drives the Community Impact StatCard value (line 207), the cost-split legend amount (line 255), the split-bar widths (lines 36-39), and mealsEstimate = floor(donationAmount/0.25) feeding the ~N meals estimate (lines 42, 358). Because donationPercentage (line 34) updates optimistically but donationAmount does not, the UI is transiently inconsistent: the selected % button highlights and the StatCard hint "X% of $surplus surplus" (line 209) update instantly, while the dollar amount and meals count still show the OLD percentage until onSettled invalidate (queries.ts:343) refetches. The PATCH response (usage_views.py:104-109) returns only donation_enabled/donation_percentage, NOT donation_amount, so the refetch is genuinely required. User impact: for one network round-trip a user who picks e.g. 50% sees "50%" selected and "50% of surplus" but the impact dollars and meals still reflect the prior figure, which is momentarily misleading. No data lost; self-corrects on refetch. Minor UX-only inconsistency.

user_story: As a prospective or returning user clicking a campaign email link, I want to be redirected to the backend so that my promo code is verified and applied without manual entry.
expected_behaviour: GET /promo/redeem?code=&token= renders a loading screen ('Redeeming your offer…'). On mount useEffect reads code and token from useSearchParams. If either is missing it immediately does window.location.replace('/promo/redeemed?status=invalid'). Otherwise it constructs GET ${API_BASE}/api/v1/tenants/promos/redeem/?code=&token= and calls window.location.replace() to hand off to the backend. The backend verifies the HMAC and 302s to /promo/redeemed?status=<outcome>. The page is wrapped in a Suspense boundary; the fallback shows 'Loading…'
dependencies: Backend promo redemption endpoint with HMAC verification
inventory_notes: API_BASE falls back to 'http://localhost:8000' if NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL is not set. This is a hard full-page navigate (window.location.replace) not a Next.js router call, so the SPA state is discarded.

user_story: As a user who clicked a promo link, I want to see a clear confirmation or error message so that I know whether my trial extension was applied.
expected_behaviour: GET /promo/redeemed?status=<status> reads the status param via useSearchParams. Five status values are handled: 'success' (purple glyph, 'You're back on, for 14 days.' → CTA to /dashboard), 'already' (teal glyph, 'Already claimed.' → /dashboard), 'expired' (grey glyph, 'This offer has closed.' → /login), 'invalid' (rose glyph, 'We couldn't read that link.' → /login), 'active_subscription' (teal glyph, 'You're already covered.' → /dashboard). Unrecognised statuses fall back to the 'invalid' variant. Each variant renders a glyph icon, italic serif headline, body copy, and a min-h-[44px] CTA button. Wrapped in Suspense with 'Loading…' fallback.
inventory_notes: This page is accessible without authentication (onboarding shell). All five status variant CTA hrefs are hardcoded strings, not dynamic.

feature: Constellation Graph — Initial Load and Empty State
user_story: As a subscriber, I want my knowledge graph to load so I can see how my lessons relate to each other.
expected_behaviour: On mount, useConstellationQuery fires GET /api/v1/lessons/constellation/ (staleTime 5 min, requires isLoggedIn()). A centred 'Loading your constellation...' paragraph is shown during fetch. On network/API error, a rose-coloured panel renders the error message. On success with zero nodes, a full-canvas empty-state overlay reads 'Your constellation begins here / As you chat with your assistant, lessons will appear as nodes in your personal graph.' On success with data, the SVG graph is laid out client-side on a 2800x1900 virtual canvas with a radial-gradient starfield and a dot grid. If the backend returns nodes with no cluster data, a client-side tag-based clustering fallback assigns clusters automatically.
inventory_notes: affinity_edges defaults to [] if absent from the API response. Tag clustering fallback fires only when clusters.length===0 AND all nodes have cluster_id==null. Requires auth (isLoggedIn check in the query).

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to pan the graph canvas by dragging so I can explore all areas of the map.
expected_behaviour: Pointer-down on any canvas area that is not a node or a data-no-drag element starts a pan. Pointer movement translates the SVG world group via the pan state. Releasing with movement < 4px is treated as a tap: if a node is selected it deselects it; if in cluster isolation mode it exits that mode. Cursor shows grab/grabbing CSS states. Touch single-finger drag also pans. Both mouse and touch paths share the same pointerDown/pointerMove/pointerUp functions.
inventory_notes: Pan state is pure client state; no API call. The <section> has touchAction:none to prevent browser scroll interference.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to zoom the graph in and out so I can read individual nodes or see the whole map at once.
expected_behaviour: Mouse wheel (onWheel) adjusts zoom state via exponential factor clamped to [0.3, 3]. The +/- buttons in the toolbar increment/decrement zoom by fixed factors (x1.25 / x0.8). A percentage label shows current zoom (e.g. '85%'). On mobile/touch, a two-finger pinch gesture computes distance ratio and adjusts zoom in the same [0.3, 3] range. The SVG scale is derived as baseScale * zoom where baseScale fits the 2800x1900 world into the measured stage dimensions.
inventory_notes: Zoom controls (+/-/label) are hidden on mobile (hidden sm:flex). Mobile users must use pinch or the mobile Play entry button; the reset button is also desktop-only in the main toolbar.

feature: Constellation Graph — Node Click and Inspector Panel
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to click a graph node to see its full details so I can understand what the lesson or cluster represents.
expected_behaviour: Clicking a node (pointer-up with < 3px movement) sets selectedId. For Cluster nodes it also toggles cluster isolation mode. The Inspector panel slides in from the right (380-400px wide, full-height on mobile overlay) with an animation. It shows: node kind badge with glyph icon, the lesson text (in italics with quote marks) or cluster label, context paragraph for Lessons, a Cypher query panel, a property table (id/title/cluster_id/weight/source_type/source_ref/created_at/tags depending on kind), and a relationships list. Clicking 'x' or a backdrop tap deselects. On desktop, hovering a node also lights up its neighbourhood (focusAdj computed from visible edges).
inventory_notes: The Inspector is rendered as <aside> with absolute positioning on mobile (overlays canvas) and relative on md+ (pushes canvas left). The slide-in animation is defined via an inline <style jsx> keyframe.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to copy a Cypher query for the selected node so I can run it in a graph database.
expected_behaviour: Inside the Inspector panel, a pre-formatted Cypher query is shown for the selected node. A 'copy' button calls navigator.clipboard.writeText() with the query string. On success the button text switches to 'copied ✓' for 1400ms then reverts. The Cypher query is computed client-side by cypherPathFor() based on node kind (Lesson/Cluster/Evidence/Tag).
inventory_notes: Uses navigator.clipboard which may be unavailable in non-HTTPS contexts; no error handling if the API is absent.
error_detail: Confirmed at frontend/app/constellation/page.tsx:328. The copy button's onClick does `navigator.clipboard?.writeText(cypher); setCopied(true); ...` — the optional chaining means the write is skipped silently if `navigator.clipboard` is undefined, and `setCopied(true)` runs unconditionally with no `.then()`/`.catch()` on the (async) writeText promise. So the button flips to 'copied ✓' even when the copy never happened or the promise rejected. This diverges from the feature's documented expected_behaviour ('On success the button text switches to copied ✓'). However real-world impact is minimal: the frontend is an Azure Static Web App served over HTTPS, and local dev runs on localhost — both are secure contexts where `navigator.clipboard` is present and writeText succeeds in the overwhelming majority of cases. The remaining failure modes (promise rejection from an unfocused document or a rare permission denial) are edge cases; the copied content is a non-sensitive read-only Cypher string, and that exact string is rendered in the adjacent <pre> block (line 330) so a user can always select/copy it manually. Net: a real but low-severity optimistic-feedback gap — the indicator can lie in rare edge cases, with a visible manual fallback.

feature: Constellation Graph — Relationship Navigation (Jump to Neighbour)
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to click a related node in the Inspector's relationships list so I can jump directly to that node on the canvas.
expected_behaviour: The Inspector lists all graph edges touching the selected node (filtered to visible edges). Each entry shows direction arrow, relationship type, similarity score if SIMILAR_TO, a colour dot, and the neighbour's label. Clicking a neighbour button calls focusNode(id): it pans to the node's world position scaled by baseScale*1.4, sets zoom to 1.4, and sets selectedId to that node. The Inspector immediately re-renders for the new node.
inventory_notes: Jump only works if the node is in visibleNodes (not filtered out by kindFilter). If a neighbour is hidden by the kind filter, it still appears in the Inspector list but focusNode will pan to an empty position.
error_detail: Confirmed real. The Inspector Relationships list (neighbors) is computed from graphData.edges (ALL edges) with no visibility filter (page.tsx:565), rendering a clickable button per neighbour (page.tsx:340-345). By default kindFilter excludes Evidence and Tag (page.tsx:387 = Lesson, Cluster) and relFilter excludes EVIDENCED_BY and TAGGED_WITH (page.tsx:388 = IN_CLUSTER, SIMILAR_TO, REFINES). So selecting a Lesson that has tags or a source_ref shows TAGGED_WITH / EVIDENCED_BY neighbour buttons even though those kinds are hidden by default. Each button onClick = onJump(other.id) = focusNode (wired at page.tsx:733). focusNode (page.tsx:564): const p = positions[String(id)]; if (!p) return;. positions derives from baseLayout = layoutGraph(visibleNodes, ...) (page.tsx:430), and layoutGraph writes position entries only for the nodes handed to it (page.tsx:131-132). A hidden-kind node has no position entry, so focusNode returns immediately and does nothing - not even the setSelectedId after the early return. The user clicks a normal-looking relationship button and gets zero feedback. Reachable under default filters; no crash or data loss, hence medium.

feature: Constellation Graph — Delete Lesson from Graph
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to permanently delete a lesson from the constellation so I can remove incorrect or unwanted knowledge.
expected_behaviour: The Inspector shows a 'Remove from constellation' button only for Lesson nodes. Clicking it reveals a confirmation row with a 'Delete' button and a 'Cancel' button. Clicking 'Delete' calls useDeleteLessonMutation which sends DELETE /api/v1/lessons/{id}/. While in-flight both buttons are disabled and 'Delete' shows 'Deleting...'. On success, the mutation invalidates the ['constellation'] and ['pending-lessons'] query caches, which refetches and re-renders the graph without the deleted node. The Inspector closes (setSelectedId(null)). On error, the mutation raises and the buttons re-enable (but the error is not surfaced in the UI from what is visible in the code).
inventory_notes: Deletion is irreversible. No toast or error message is shown to the user on mutation failure — the modal simply stays open and re-enables buttons.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to toggle node types on/off using the legend so I can focus on specific parts of the graph.
expected_behaviour: A legend panel (bottom-left, desktop only: hidden sm:block) lists four node kinds (Lesson, Cluster, Evidence, Tag) each with a glyph icon, a colour label, and a count. Clicking a kind button toggles it in/out of kindFilter state. When a kind is off, its nodes disappear from the SVG and its label text greys out. The selectedId is reset to null on any kind toggle. The legend also lists five relationship types (IN_CLUSTER, SIMILAR_TO, EVIDENCED_BY, TAGGED_WITH, REFINES) with colour swatches; toggling a rel type hides/shows those edges.
inventory_notes: The legend is hidden on mobile (hidden sm:block). Initial default shows only Lesson and Cluster nodes visible; Evidence and Tag are off by default. Relationship defaults: IN_CLUSTER, SIMILAR_TO, REFINES on; EVIDENCED_BY and TAGGED_WITH off.
error_detail: Confirmed mechanism, but the claim overstates user impact. toggleKind (page.tsx:560) resets selectedId but never clears isolated. If a user is in cluster-isolation mode (entered by clicking a Cluster node, page.tsx:516) and then toggles the Cluster kind off via the legend, three things happen: (1) the cluster hub node is removed from the canvas because visibleNodes filters on kindFilter (page.tsx:412); (2) member lessons stay scoped to that cluster because isolated is still set and the isolation filter still runs (page.tsx:413-417); (3) the 'Focused' breadcrumb stays visible because it resolves isoNode from the full graphData.nodes set, not visibleNodes (page.tsx:633). So the user sees only the cluster's lessons with no hub node -- a genuine but mild inconsistency. The claim's 'stale breadcrumb with no way to understand the state' is inaccurate: the breadcrumb is a fully functional, labeled exit control -- it still shows the cluster's name (isoNode.constellation || isoNode.label, page.tsx:637) and clicking it clears isolation (page.tsx:634). Two more exits exist: 'Reset view' clears isolated (page.tsx:627) and clicking empty canvas clears it (page.tsx:512). The user can both understand which cluster they are focused on and exit easily. The scenario is also desktop-only: the legend toggle lives in a panel that is hidden sm:block (page.tsx:715), so phone users cannot trigger it. Real but low-severity edge case.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to click a constellation cluster node to isolate its members so I can see just that group without the rest of the graph.
expected_behaviour: Clicking a Cluster node sets both selectedId and isolated to that cluster's id. The visibleNodes list is filtered to show only that cluster node and the Lesson/Evidence/Tag nodes belonging to it. A breadcrumb pill appears below the header bar showing the cluster name and an X button to exit. Clicking the breadcrumb X clears isolated (restores the full filtered graph). Clicking on the empty canvas also clears isolation. While isolated, the Inspector shows the cluster's properties.
inventory_notes: Isolation only hides nodes client-side; no API call. If the cluster has no member nodes in the current kindFilter, the isolated view will appear empty except for the cluster hub.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to search for a node by name or text so I can quickly jump to a specific lesson or tag.
expected_behaviour: A search input is always visible in the header bar (min-width 200px, max 420px). Typing into it filters visibleNodes (up to 8 results) by matching label text, node.text, or id. Results appear in a dropdown below the input. Clicking a result calls focusNode() which pans to the node at zoom 1.4 and selects it, then clears the query. A 'clear' button appears when the query is non-empty; clicking it resets the query to ''. The search input has monospace font and a purple magnifier icon.
inventory_notes: Search only covers currently visible nodes (filtered by kindFilter and isolated). Nodes hidden by kind filter cannot be found via search.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to reset the node positions to the auto-computed layout so I can restore the graph after manually dragging nodes around.
expected_behaviour: A 'Relayout' button in the desktop toolbar (hidden on mobile) resets positions state to the current baseLayout (the memoized layoutGraph result). It does not re-fetch data. Any manual node positions the user has dragged are discarded.
inventory_notes: Relayout is only visible on sm+ screens (hidden sm:flex parent container). No API call.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to reset the viewport so I can return to the default zoom and position after navigating away.
expected_behaviour: A 'Reset view' button in the desktop toolbar sets pan to {x:0, y:0}, zoom to 0.85, selectedId to null, and isolated to null. This resets the viewport without changing node positions and without re-fetching data.
inventory_notes: Desktop only (same hidden sm:flex container as Relayout). Does not reset kindFilter or relFilter.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to drag individual nodes to rearrange them so I can organise the graph to my preference.
expected_behaviour: Pointer-down on a node element (data-graphnode attribute) starts a node-drag interaction. Moving the pointer more than 3px updates that node's entry in the positions state (world coordinates), repositioning it on the SVG. Releasing with < 3px movement is treated as a click/select instead. The drag offset preserves the grab point relative to the node centre. There is no API call; positions are ephemeral (reset by Relayout or page reload).
inventory_notes: Dragging any node disables the corresponding autopilot effect. Positions are not persisted server-side.

user_story: As a first-time visitor, I want to see a brief hint about how to use the graph so I know how to navigate it.
expected_behaviour: On first visit (localStorage key nbhd_constellation_hint_dismissed not set), a hint pill appears below the header bar on desktop (hidden on mobile) reading 'Similar thoughts sit near each other · hover to trace connections · click a cluster to focus'. It auto-dismisses after 8 seconds by setting the localStorage key and setting showHint to false. A manual X button also dismisses it immediately. Private-mode (localStorage unavailable) always shows the hint.
inventory_notes: Hint is desktop-only (hidden sm:flex). The localStorage key is nbhd_constellation_hint_dismissed. Auto-dismiss timer is cleared on component unmount.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see a badge on the constellation page when there are pending lessons waiting for review so I know to go curate them.
expected_behaviour: usePendingLessonsQuery fetches GET /api/v1/lessons/pending/ (staleTime 60s). If the returned array has length > 0, a purple pill link '# waiting' appears in the header stats row. Clicking it navigates to /constellation/pending. The badge is not shown if there are zero pending lessons.
inventory_notes: The badge count reflects the raw array length; no error state is surfaced if the pending lessons fetch fails (count silently stays at 0 via default = []).

user_story: As a subscriber who has opted into the beta, I want to see a Play button on the constellation page so I can enter the 3D galaxy game.
expected_behaviour: On mount, isPlayEnabled() checks NEXT_PUBLIC_CONSTELLATION_PLAY==='1' OR localStorage.nbhd_play_beta==='1'. If enabled, two Play buttons appear: one in the desktop toolbar (sm:flex area) and one in the mobile header (sm:hidden). Both link to /constellation/play. If play is not enabled, no button is shown and the play route redirects back to /constellation.
inventory_notes: The feature is dark by default (Phase 1 beta). The mobile Play button has min-h-[44px] for touch target. Phase 2 plans to replace this with a per-user settings toggle.

feature: Pending Lesson Queue — Load and Display
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to review lessons suggested by my assistant before they enter the constellation so I can curate my knowledge graph.
expected_behaviour: usePendingLessonsQuery fetches GET /api/v1/lessons/pending/. During load, a surface-coloured panel reads 'Loading pending lessons...' On success with zero items, a panel reads 'No pending lessons right now.' On success with items, cards are sorted newest-first by suggested_at and rendered: lesson text as heading, context or 'No context provided.' as body, suggestion timestamp via toLocaleString(), and tags as rounded pills. Each card has 'Approve' and 'Dismiss' buttons. A '← Back to constellation' link navigates to /constellation.
inventory_notes: Query errors are surfaced as a rose error panel above the list. No loading skeleton; shows a simple text placeholder.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to approve a pending lesson so it is added to my constellation knowledge graph.
expected_behaviour: Clicking 'Approve' on a card sets processing to that lesson id and starts a 220ms fade-out animation (scale-[0.99] opacity-0). useApproveLessonMutation sends PATCH /api/v1/lessons/{id}/approve/. On success, the ['pending-lessons'] and ['constellation'] query caches are invalidated (refetches both). Only one lesson can be processed at a time (both buttons disabled while processing === lesson.id showing 'Working...'). On error, an error string is set and displayed in the rose panel at the top. After success or error, processing is reset to null.
inventory_notes: The animation begins immediately (optimistic removal from view) before the PATCH resolves. If the PATCH fails, the card is visually gone but the error appears in the top banner. The list refetches correctly if the server rejects the approval.
error_detail: Confirmed by code reading. The 220ms fade-out (animateRemoval) is purely time-based and fully decoupled from the PATCH promise: the id is added to removingIds at call time and unconditionally cleared by a window.setTimeout(..., 220) regardless of mutation outcome (page.tsx:35-40, called at :45 and :57). On the success path this is clean — onSuccess invalidates ["pending-lessons"], the refetch drops the item from items, so clearing removingIds has nothing to reappear. On a PATCH failure, the mutations (useApproveLessonMutation/useDismissLessonMutation, queries.ts:1801-1820) have only onSuccess and no onError/optimistic revert, so the item stays in sortedItems; the timer still fires at 220ms and snaps the card back from opacity-0 to opacity-100 (article className, page.tsx:94-96), while the catch block sets the error banner rendered above the list (liveError, page.tsx:85). User-visible result, only on the error path: the card fades away, then flashes back to full opacity beneath a newly-appeared error banner. It is not data loss — the card returns fully actionable and processing resets in finally (page.tsx:49-51), so the user can retry; it is purely a cosmetic flash and only occurs on the uncommon request-failure path. Matches the QA verdict (ux, low).

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to dismiss a pending lesson so it is removed from my queue without being added to the constellation.
expected_behaviour: Clicking 'Dismiss' on a card sets processing and triggers the same 220ms fade-out animation. useDismissLessonMutation sends PATCH /api/v1/lessons/{id}/dismiss/. On success, only ['pending-lessons'] is invalidated (dismissed lessons do not enter the constellation so ['constellation'] is not invalidated). Behaviour on error and in-flight state is identical to Approve. The dismiss button is styled with rose colours (border-rose-border, bg-rose-bg, text-rose-text).
inventory_notes: Unlike Approve, Dismiss does not invalidate the constellation cache, so the graph is not refetched. Dismissal is immediate in the UI even before the server confirms.
error_detail: Both sub-claims are confirmed in frontend/app/constellation/pending/page.tsx. (1) Stale error banner: the `error` state (line 19) is only ever set in the catch branches of approve/dismiss (lines 48, 60) and is NEVER reset to empty — there is no setError("") on success or before the next attempt. `liveError` (line 25) is `error || queryError`, so once a single approve/dismiss fails, the rose banner (line 85) stays pinned at the top of the page indefinitely, even after the user successfully approves/dismisses other cards. The user is left staring at a failure message that no longer reflects reality, implying their successful action also failed. (2) Premature fade flicker: animateRemoval(lesson.id) is invoked BEFORE `await mutateAsync` (lines 45 and 57). The mutation hooks (queries.ts lines 1801-1819) only invalidate the pending-lessons query in onSuccess, so on a failure the list is NOT refetched and the card stays in `items`. The 220ms timer fades the card out (opacity-0/scale-0.99, lines 94-96) then clears removingIds restoring opacity-100, producing a brief disappear-then-reappear flicker on a failed operation. Both are genuine but low-impact (failure-path-only, cosmetic). The stale banner is the more meaningful of the two.

feature: Galaxy Play — Feature Gate and Redirect
user_story: As a subscriber, I want the play route to redirect me back to the constellation graph if the game is not enabled for my account so I am not shown a broken page.
expected_behaviour: On mount, the page calls isPlayEnabled(). If false, router.replace('/constellation') is called immediately and null is rendered. While the check is in-flight (allowed===null), null is also rendered. Only when allowed===true does the page proceed to fetch galaxy data and render the game. This prevents the Phaser bundle from being loaded for users without the beta flag.
inventory_notes: The flag check is intentionally deferred to useEffect (hydration-safe) per a comment in the code. The eslint rule react-hooks/exhaustive-deps is disabled for this specific setState-in-effect.

feature: Galaxy Play — Data Fetch Loading State
user_story: As a subscriber entering the play route, I want to see an animated loading screen while galaxy data is fetched so I know the page is working.
expected_behaviour: useGalaxyQuery fetches GET /api/v1/lessons/galaxy/ (staleTime 5 min). While isLoading, a full-screen FetchingScreen component renders a GalaxyLoader (orbiting ship animation, animated stars, respects prefers-reduced-motion) with headline 'Charting the galaxy...'. After 20 seconds (SLOW_MS), additional text appears with a 'Try again' button (calls refetch()) and a 'Back to constellation' link. The same loader is shown while the Phaser chunk is being imported (ChartingScreen). Both loaders auto-upgrade after 20s.
inventory_notes: The GalaxyLoader uses CSS animations suppressed when prefers-reduced-motion is active. The 20s timer is implemented via useElapsed hook using setTimeout.

feature: Galaxy Play — Data Fetch Error State
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see a clear error message if the galaxy data fails to load so I can retry or return to the graph.
expected_behaviour: If useGalaxyQuery returns an error and is not fetching, a full-screen error Screen shows 'Couldn't load your galaxy' with subtext 'The server may be waking up, or your connection dropped.' and two buttons: 'Try again' (calls refetch()) and 'Back to constellation'. While isFetching (retry in progress), only the subtext 'Retrying...' is shown with no action buttons. Phaser chunk import failures and mount-time Phaser errors are caught by the GameBoundary class error boundary, which shows 'Couldn't start the game' with a reload button.
inventory_notes: Errors thrown inside Phaser's render loop cannot be caught by GameBoundary (not a React component). These are caught by the useCapturedError window event listener and shown as an ErrorBanner at the bottom of the screen with Reload and Dismiss actions.

user_story: As a subscriber with no approved lessons, I want to see a message explaining why the game cannot start so I know what to do next.
expected_behaviour: If useGalaxyQuery returns data with data.stars.length === 0, a full-screen Screen shows 'Your galaxy is still forming' with subtext 'Approve a few lessons first, then come back and fly.' and a 'Back to constellation' link. No Phaser game is mounted.

feature: Galaxy Play — Game Mount and Phaser Scene
user_story: As a subscriber, I want the galaxy game to launch when data is available so I can fly through my knowledge represented as stars.
expected_behaviour: ConstellationGame is dynamic-imported (ssr:false) so Phaser lands in a lazy chunk separate from the main bundle. On mount, mountGalaxyGame creates a Phaser.Game instance on the canvasRef div and starts the GalaxyScene. The scene builds a semantic galaxy layout from the GalaxyData (PCA-based coordinates from the backend, mapped to world space), renders constellation nebula clouds, star glyphs by stage (proto/ignited/radiant/supernova) and cluster colour, similarity edges, parallax starfields, and a HUD showing total star count by stage. A WarpIn overlay fades from an accent glow on entry. An ExitLink fixed to the top-right corner navigates back to /constellation.
inventory_notes: The Phaser game is destroyed on component unmount via game.destroy(true). All scene teardown (waypoint tweens, meteor timers, DOM overlay reset) is handled in GalaxyScene.teardownCopilot via Phaser SHUTDOWN/DESTROY events.

user_story: As a subscriber on desktop, I want to fly my ship with WASD or arrow keys so I can navigate the galaxy.
expected_behaviour: W/up arrow: thrust forward. A/D or left/right arrows: rotate ship. S/down arrow: brake (multiplies drag by FEEL.brake=3.2). E: land on nearest star in dock radius. M: toggle map mode. Esc: close panel/exit map. The ship has arcade physics: acceleration 380, drag 95, max velocity 360, no gravity, world-bounded. Visual: the ship banks on turns (squash on Y axis), comet trail emits proportional to speed, camera leads velocity with zoom-out at full speed. All keyboard input is disabled while a note textarea or tutor input is focused.
inventory_notes: Keyboard controls require `input.keyboard` to be enabled. The note/tutor textarea focus handlers call setGameKeyboard(false) to suspend flight keys. The help overlay shows keyboard hints on non-coarse devices.

user_story: As a subscriber on mobile, I want to fly my ship by dragging on screen so I can navigate the galaxy without a keyboard.
expected_behaviour: Single-finger drag creates a virtual thumbstick: a ring drawn at the touch anchor and a knob at the drag position (clamped to radius 56). The stick's direction and throw magnitude (0..1) drive ship rotation and acceleration. A short tap (< 12px movement, < 320ms) on empty space sets an autopilot course to that world position with a ping animation; a tap near a star within dock radius lands on it. A 'Land here' button appears when a landable star is nearby. Two-finger pinch-zoom is handled in map mode.
inventory_notes: The help overlay swaps between keyboard and touch hints using CSS coarse pointer media query. The thumbstick is drawn each frame as screen-space graphics (scrollFactor 0).

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to tap a star or empty space to automatically fly there so I can navigate the galaxy without sustained steering.
expected_behaviour: A tap on a star that is outside the dock radius sets autopilot to {x, y, star}. The ship steers toward the target each frame via rotateToward, accelerating forward. A ping ring animation (beam-coloured ring expanding to alpha 0) confirms the course was set. When the ship reaches within DOCK_RADIUS (100 world units) of a star autopilot, it lands automatically. Tapping empty space sets a position autopilot with no star. Autopilot is cleared on any manual input (touchstart).
inventory_notes: Ping animation is suppressed when prefers-reduced-motion is active.

feature: Galaxy Play — Landing on a Star
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to land on a star to read the lesson it represents, so I can review and interact with my knowledge.
expected_behaviour: Landing is triggered by pressing E, tapping the 'Land here' button, or autopilot reaching dock radius. If prefers-reduced-motion is off, a docking tween glides the ship to a point just outside the star's surface (340ms Sine.Out), then fires a touchdown ripple in the cluster's colour. First-ever visits also burst 14 particles. After 140ms delay, openPanel() is called. The ship velocity is zeroed and the scene is paused (input suspended). A visited ring (light-blue stroke) is drawn around the star and its entry.visited flag set. The waypoint is cleared if this was the waypoint target.
inventory_notes: The landing animation includes a ship rotation tween to face the star the short angular way (Phaser.Math.Angle.Wrap). With reduced motion, openPanel() is called directly with no animation.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see a lesson's full details when I land on its star so I can revisit what I learned.
expected_behaviour: The landing panel (DOM overlay with role=dialog aria-modal=true) opens with class 'open'. It shows: stage badge (proto/ignited/radiant/supernova) with stage colour, cluster label, lesson text as h1, provenance line (context + source_ref if present), metadata line (source_type / connection_count / journal_count), tags as chips, any galaxy_note, a co-pilot line section (shows animated twinkling dots while loading, then fades in the AI line), a notes section (existing notes list + note composer). Action buttons: 'Show me where ->' (if co-pilot points to another star), 'Go deeper' (opens tutoring), 'Back to flight', 'Got it'. Panel is closed by those buttons, backdrop click, or Esc key.
inventory_notes: The panel is a DOM element queried by the Phaser scene (not a React component). Flight keyboard is disabled while the panel is open (paused=true). The reflectToken race guard prevents stale co-pilot lines from overwriting a newer landing.

feature: Galaxy Play — Co-pilot Reflection Line on Landing
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see a contextual AI-generated line when I land on a star so my assistant can connect what I'm revisiting to my recent flight path.
expected_behaviour: On openPanel(), requestCopilotLine() posts to POST /api/v1/lessons/galaxy/reflect/ with {star_id, recent_star_ids (last 8 stars visited), ship position, mode:'land'}. While in-flight, the co-pilot section shows twinkling dots (cg-loading-stars). On success, the line fades in with a CSS animation (cg-line-in). If the response includes a point (CopilotPoint), the 'Show me where ->' button appears. On fetch error, a fallback grounded line is shown quoting the lesson text. A reflectToken race guard ensures stale responses are discarded if the user has moved to a different star.
inventory_notes: The server always returns a line (source:'fallback' when LLM is unavailable). The frontend fallback fires only on a true network error.

feature: Galaxy Play — Co-pilot Waypoint ('Show me where')
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to follow a co-pilot pointer to another star so I can discover related knowledge the assistant is highlighting.
expected_behaviour: If the reflect API response includes a point with star_id, a 'Show me where ->' button appears in the landing panel. Clicking it closes the panel and calls setWaypoint(star_id). A pulsing beam-coloured ring is placed at the target star. A dashed heading line is drawn from the ship toward the target each frame. If the target is off-screen, a screen-edge arrow indicator (triangle + circle + truncated label) points toward it. If not in reduced-motion, a brief zoom-out reveal shows the target's region and then zooms back after 1800ms. The waypoint is cleared when the ship arrives within DOCK_RADIUS of the target or when a new waypoint is set.
inventory_notes: The waypoint display is suppressed during map mode. The minimap still shows the waypoint target.

feature: Galaxy Play — Star Notes (Add and View)
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to add free-text notes to a star when I land on it so I can record my personal context for that lesson.
expected_behaviour: The landing panel contains a notes section. On open, if the star's journal_count > 0, GET /api/v1/lessons/{id}/journal/ is fetched (notesToken race guard). Existing notes render as .cg-note-item divs with body text and localeDateString timestamp. A textarea (maxLength 2000, 2 rows) and a 'Save note' button compose a new note. Submitting (click button or Cmd/Ctrl+Enter) calls POST /api/v1/lessons/{id}/journal/create/ with {text, entry_type:'revisit'}. On success, the textarea clears, the new note is prepended to the list, and the button shows 'Saved ✓'. If the response includes star_stage and it is a promotion, the star visual is updated in-game. On failure, the button shows 'Couldn't save — try again' and re-enables. The textarea suspends flight keys while focused.
inventory_notes: A concurrency guard (savingNote flag) prevents double-saves from rapid clicks or double Cmd+Enter. Notes list is skipped (no fetch) if journal_count <= 0.

feature: Galaxy Play — Go Deeper Tutoring Session
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to open a 5-phase tutoring conversation for a star so my co-pilot can help me deepen my understanding of that lesson.
expected_behaviour: The 'Go deeper' button in the landing panel calls openTutor(). The landing panel closes and the tutoring overlay (role=dialog aria-modal=true) opens with class 'open'. The star text is shown as the session title. POST /api/v1/lessons/{id}/tutor/start/ is called; while awaiting, thinking dots appear in the thread. On success, the co-pilot's opening message is rendered in the thread, and a phase label + pip progress bar appear (e.g. 'restate · 1/5'). The user types a reply in a textarea (maxLength 5000, Cmd+Enter sends) and clicks 'Send'. Each reply POSTs to /api/v1/lessons/{id}/tutor/message/ with {session_id, message, action:'continue'}. Thinking dots show while awaiting. 'Skip phase' sends action:'skip'. On mastery_achieved or session_close, a closing message and stage growth notification appear and the composer is locked. 'End session' calls POST /api/v1/lessons/{id}/tutor/end/ and closes the overlay. A star that grows stage is promoted visually (size + glow + flash bloom).
inventory_notes: A 404 on tutorMessage is treated as session expiry: composer is locked and a 'timed out' message shown. tutorBusy flag prevents concurrent sends. Flight keys are suspended while tutor textarea is focused. End session fires tutorEnd even if the user closes mid-session.
error_detail: Confirmed, but minor. In openTutor() (galaxy-scene.ts:1434-1465), tutorSessionId is set to null at line 1439 and only assigned on success (line 1455). On a tutorStart failure (network/500), the catch (1459-1461) clears thinking dots and renders the copilot message "I couldn't start just now — let's try again in a moment." The finally clears tutorBusy. Critically, resetTutorControls() ran at line 1446 BEFORE the try, so Send and Skip are left ENABLED, and the error path does NOT call lockTutorComposer(). The user thus sees an enabled Send button plus copy inviting a retry, but tutorSend() (1467-1468) and tutorSkipPhase() (1479-1480) both guard on !this.tutorSessionId and silently return because the session id is still null — typing a reply and clicking Send does nothing, with no feedback. The only working escape is "End session" (line 795 -> closeTutor(true)), which also nulls openStarId, dismissing the landing panel; to actually retry, the user must close, re-select the star, and click "Go deeper" again. The defect is real: the copy promises a retry and the Send affordance looks active, but neither an in-overlay retry nor Send works in this state. Impact is limited — it only occurs on the uncommon start-failure path, there is no data loss, and a working escape exists — hence low rather than the verdict's medium.

user_story: As a subscriber drifting near a constellation, I want to receive an occasional ambient insight from my co-pilot so the galaxy feels alive.
expected_behaviour: Every game update tick, ambientTick() checks: ship speed < 45, dwell in a neighbourhood >= 4.2s, last ambient >= 75s ago, nearest star within 360 world units, and ship is inside a neighbourhood radius. If all conditions met, POST /api/v1/lessons/galaxy/reflect/ is called with mode:'ambient'. On success, the response line appears in a toast div (role=status, aria-live=polite) for 7 seconds then fades. The toast is never shown while a panel or encounter is active. On API failure the toast stays silent (best-effort).
inventory_notes: The ~75s rate limit (75000ms) prevents ambient toast spam. ambientInFlight flag prevents concurrent ambient requests. Toast timer is cleared on scene teardown.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to pause flight and pan around the full galaxy map so I can survey all stars and plan where to go next.
expected_behaviour: Pressing M or tapping the 'Map' button enters map mode. The ship stops and the camera detaches from following the ship. The camera zooms out to a survey level (42% of flightZoom). The 'Map' button text becomes 'Exit map'. A hint banner appears: 'Drag to look around · scroll/pinch to zoom · tap a star to set a course'. Flight help is hidden. In map mode, drag pans the camera, scroll/pinch adjusts zoom. Tapping near a star (within ~46px screen distance) selects it: a confirm popup shows the star name truncated to 44 chars with 'Fly here' and 'Cancel' buttons. 'Fly here' exits map mode and sets autopilot to that star. 'Cancel' deselects. Pressing M or 'Exit map' returns to normal flight, re-following the ship.
inventory_notes: Map mode cannot be entered while paused (panel/encounter open). The minimap continues to update in map mode. The waypoint in-world overlay is suppressed during map mode but the minimap marker is still shown.

user_story: As a subscriber flying near a neglected constellation, I want to be challenged by my 'shadow self' so I am prompted to revisit and strengthen that part of my knowledge.
expected_behaviour: On scene creation, detectGaps() analyses the galaxy data client-side for stale clusters (not visited in 14+ days), stuck proto stars (proto for 14+ days), and drifted radiant+ stars (not visited in 14+ days). Up to 2 Encounter objects are created at cluster centroids. When the ship enters within TRIGGER_RADIUS (260) of an encounter's centroid (and the encounter is armed, not resolved, not on cooldown, and the ship has previously left the radius), the nega-self ship spawns with a 360ms zoom-in and camera pull-back, the encounter overlay (role=dialog aria-modal=true) opens with a taunt line (built by buildTaunt()) and 2-4 reframe star choices (built by chooseReframes() from graph neighbours/cluster). The user clicks a reframe: outcome text appears, nega ship implodes and flies into the player, the affected cluster brightens. After 1200ms the encounter closes and flight resumes. 'Not today — slip past' button closes the encounter with a 30s cooldown.
inventory_notes: The encounter is fully client-side with no API call. Gap detection uses last_visited_at and last_tutored_at from the galaxy payload. The encounter is armed only after the ship exits the trigger radius first (prevents immediate spawn on scene load inside a neighbourhood). Max 2 encounters per session.

feature: Galaxy Play — Star Stage Visual Growth
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see a star grow visually when I deepen my understanding of it so I feel rewarded for engaging with my knowledge.
expected_behaviour: applyStarStage() is called when a note-save response or a tutor turn response returns a new star_stage that is a promotion from the current stage. promoteStar() tweens the star's core scale and glow scale to the new stage's values (Back.Out ease, 650ms). A one-shot white flash bloom fades over 700ms. For radiant+ stages, a continuous pulse tween is added to the glow. The star colour is updated to the cluster's colour tint. Stage only ever moves forward (no demotion). The visual change is permanent for the session but not re-fetched from the server until the galaxy is refetched.
inventory_notes: promoteStar() guards against a destroyed scene (this.destroyed check). Stage promotion is optimistic (applied immediately on the client from the API response).

user_story: As a subscriber flying the galaxy, I want a minimap so I always know where I am relative to the full galaxy.
expected_behaviour: A minimap is built as a Phaser Container in the top-left corner (scrollFactor 0). It shows each star as a coloured dot proportional to its world position, sized relative to the minimap. The current ship position is shown as a distinct marker. During map mode, the current camera view rectangle is drawn on the minimap. The minimap updates every frame. If a waypoint is set, the waypoint target appears on the minimap.
inventory_notes: The minimap container's size adapts to the scale.width and scale.height of the Phaser game. No interaction on the minimap; it is display-only.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see an error message if the galaxy game encounters a runtime error so I know something went wrong and can reload.
expected_behaviour: useCapturedError listens to window 'error' and 'unhandledrejection' events (capturing only the first). If an error is captured (e.g. Phaser throwing in its render loop, which React's GameBoundary cannot catch), an ErrorBanner renders fixed at the bottom of the screen: a rose panel with the error message, a 'Reload' button (calls window.location.reload()), and a 'Dismiss' button (hides the banner). The banner overlays whatever is currently rendered (game, loading screen, etc.).
inventory_notes: Only the first uncaught error is captured. The useCapturedError listeners are removed on unmount.

user_story: As a subscriber flying near a constellation, I want the cluster's nebula and stars to brighten as I approach so the galaxy feels spatially alive.
expected_behaviour: Each frame, updateNeighborhoods() computes the distance from the ship to each neighbourhood centroid. The cluster name text alpha transitions from 0.16 (far) to 0.62 (inside the neighbourhood). Each puff image in the nebula cloud brightens proportionally (up to 2.1x its base alpha). Stars within the neighbourhood have their glow alpha increased (up to 1.0) and core scale increased (up to 1.55x) as the ship approaches. This is pure visual/per-frame, no API calls.
inventory_notes: Proximity brightness is applied in addition to any pulsing/twinkling tweens on those objects (different properties). No reduced-motion guard on proximity; it is non-distracting brightness rather than animation.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want the Core page to indicate it is loading so that I do not accidentally trigger a billable compose before today's state is known.
expected_behaviour: On mount, phase is set to 'loading'. The BreathingOrb renders at 40% opacity (non-interactive). A 'Preparing your space…' status label is displayed below the orb with role='status'. The stats section renders 4 pulse-skeleton cards (aria-hidden). The library section renders 2 pulse-skeleton cards (aria-hidden). The session count label shows '—'. The orb click handler is a no-op in this phase. This state persists until fetchMeditations() resolves and setLoaded(true) fires.
inventory_notes: Phase intentionally starts as 'loading' (not 'invite') to prevent a fast tap from firing a billable compose before the library fetch resolves. The orb is visually muted but present so layout does not shift.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want my past meditations to load automatically so that the page reflects my actual history without any action on my part.
expected_behaviour: On mount, fetchMeditations() calls GET /api/v1/core/sessions/ (no query params, fetching ready sessions). The response is unwrapped from paginated DRF shape (data.results if not array). Sessions are stored in state and projected through the tenant's IANA timezone to produce the library. If the fetch throws, the error is silently swallowed and the existing sessions array (empty on first load) is kept. After fetch settles, setLoaded(true) fires and the phase transitions from 'loading' to either 'ready' (if a today session exists) or 'invite'.
entry_points: frontend/app/core/page.tsx:CorePage (loadLibrary useCallback, mount useEffect) ; frontend/lib/api.ts:fetchMeditations ; GET /api/v1/core/sessions/
inventory_notes: Network errors are silently ignored to keep the page functional. The function is also called after a compose completes to reconcile the optimistically-inserted session.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see a clear call-to-action to compose today's meditation so that I know how to start my session.
expected_behaviour: When the library loads and no meditation exists for today, phase transitions to 'invite'. The BreathingOrb shows a '+' (ComposeIcon) and its aria-label is 'Compose today's meditation'. Below the orb: eyebrow label 'Today · on demand', h2 'Compose today's sit', a descriptive paragraph, and a mono note that composition is in the background and replay is free. Clicking the orb calls compose().

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to press the orb to compose a personalized meditation so that my assistant generates a session tailored to my week.
expected_behaviour: Clicking the orb in 'invite' or 'failed' phase calls compose(). Phase immediately transitions to 'composing'. composeMeditation() POSTs to /api/v1/core/compose/. On success the API returns { meditation_id } and pollUntilDone(id) begins. On 403 the error is 'Core isn't enabled yet — turn it on in Settings → Integrations, then come back.' On 404 it is 'Your account isn't set up for Core yet. Please refresh, or contact support if this persists.' Any other error shows 'Couldn't start your meditation. Please try again in a moment.' Both error paths set phase to 'failed'.
entry_points: frontend/app/core/page.tsx:CorePage (compose callback, onOrb handler) ; frontend/lib/api.ts:composeMeditation ; POST /api/v1/core/compose/
inventory_notes: The API coalesces duplicate requests — if a compose is already in-flight it returns the existing session id rather than creating a second one. The orb is fully inert during 'loading' and 'composing' phases so repeated taps cannot fire extra requests.

feature: Composing in-progress state with rotating messages
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see calming progress messages while my meditation is being composed so that I know the process is active and I can step away.
expected_behaviour: While phase is 'composing', the orb shows a play/pause icon (not '+'), the eyebrow reads 'Composing' in signal colour, and the h2 cycles through four lyrical messages ('Drawing on your week…', 'Finding the words…', 'Placing the silences…', 'Almost there…') on a 3800ms interval with cross-fade. A mono note reads 'I'll message you when it's ready — feel free to step away'. If polling exceeds 4 minutes (POLL_TIMEOUT_MS), longRunning is set to true and the h2 and note change to 'Still composing…' and 'Taking a little longer than usual — I'll message you the moment it's ready.'
entry_points: frontend/app/core/page.tsx:CorePage (phase='composing' branch, msgIdx useEffect, longRunning state)
inventory_notes: The 3800ms interval was deliberately chosen over the earlier 700ms to avoid a 'flashing' feel on a mindfulness surface.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want the page to automatically detect when my meditation is ready so that I can begin listening without manually refreshing.
expected_behaviour: pollUntilDone(id) polls GET /api/v1/core/sessions/{id}/ every 3500ms. If session.status is 'ready' or 'delivered', the session is optimistically prepended to state, phase transitions to 'ready', showToast is set to true, and loadLibrary() is called to reconcile. If session.status is 'failed', phase transitions to 'failed' with the message 'Your meditation couldn't be composed just now. Please try again in a moment.' Network errors during polling are swallowed and polling continues. After 4 minutes total, polling stops and longRunning is set to true. The pollRef allows the loop to be cancelled on unmount.
inventory_notes: If polling times out (longRunning=true), the backend still sends a channel notification (Telegram/LINE) when rendering completes, so the user is not left without feedback.
error_detail: Confirmed, but narrow. The frontend poll loop only reacts to status=failed while it is actively polling (page.tsx:127-132). At the 4-minute POLL_TIMEOUT_MS it sets pollRef.active=false, calls setLongRunning(true), and returns WITHOUT scheduling another tick or changing phase (page.tsx:134-139) — phase stays composing and the UI shows "Still composing…". After that the page never polls again (no visibilitychange/focus re-poll handler exists), and onOrb is a hard no-op while phase===composing (page.tsx:180), so the orb offers no retry. If the backend marks the session failed AFTER the 4-minute timeout (plausible: a late transient render/persist failure or a tts_quota failure in the multi-minute TTS pipeline — apps/core/services.py:215-237 and 245-281), the web user is stranded on "Still composing…" with no in-page error and no retry path until a manual reload. The gap is sharpened by the backend: notify_meditation_ready is sent ONLY on status=ready (services.py:307-309); the _fail() path (services.py:314-317) sends NO channel ping on ANY channel. So the inventory-note mitigation ("backend sends a channel notification when rendering completes") holds only for the SUCCESS case — on failure-after-timeout there is zero out-of-band signal and the "Still composing…" copy actively misleads. For success-after-timeout the design degrades gracefully (channel ping + a reload surfaces the ready sit), so this is a low-frequency edge, not a broad break. The QA's "app-only user" emphasis is slightly off (this is the web page; web users get the toast/reload path on success), but the core claim — stranded composing screen, no in-page error/retry, and no failure notification on any channel — is verified by the code.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see today's meditation title and phase arc when it is ready so that I know what to expect before pressing play.
expected_behaviour: When phase is 'ready' and today is non-null, the hero section shows: eyebrow 'Today's sit', the meditation title as h2, duration + 'voice-guided' subtitle, the theme paragraph (if present), and a PhaseTimeline showing the 6-phase arc (Arrival, Breath, Body scan, Core practice, Integration, Closing) with segments proportional to their weights. The orb switches to a play icon and its aria-label is 'Begin meditation'. Clicking the orb calls play(today), setting current and playing=true.
inventory_notes: The PHASES constant in lib/core.ts is fixed (invariant; the backend enforces this arc in compose.py). Phase weights: Arrival=60, Breath=75, Body scan=150, Core practice=210, Integration=60, Closing=45. The 4th segment (Core practice) has a teal→purple gradient highlight.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to hover over phase segments to see their names so that I understand the structure of my meditation.
expected_behaviour: On sm+ screens, each segment in the PhaseTimeline shows a tooltip with the phase label on hover (opacity transitions from 0 to 100). Below the segments, phase labels are shown on sm+ screens in full. On mobile, only 'Arrival' and 'Closing' end-labels are shown with '· · ·' separators. Tooltip uses pointer-events-none so it cannot be interacted with. Below the timeline: '{total minutes} minutes · narration woven with stillness'.
inventory_notes: Tooltip is CSS-only (group-hover). No keyboard access for tooltips — they are decorative descriptions of the phase arc.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see a clear error and retry path when meditation composition fails so that I am not left with a broken UI.
expected_behaviour: When phase is 'failed', the hero section shows: eyebrow 'Couldn't compose' in rose-text colour, h2 'Let's try that again', and the error message string. For 403: the error directs to Settings → Integrations. For 404: account setup issue. For other errors / poll failure: generic message. A mono note reads 'Press the orb to retry'. The orb shows the '+' ComposeIcon again (compose=true). Clicking the orb calls compose(), resetting error/longRunning/showToast and re-entering the composing phase.
entry_points: frontend/app/core/page.tsx:CorePage (phase='failed' branch, compose callback error branches)
inventory_notes: Error messages differ by HTTP status code (403, 404, other). The poll failure error ('couldn't be composed') is distinct from the API call error. Both land in the same failed UI.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want an in-page notification when my meditation finishes composing so that I can immediately start listening without leaving the tab.
expected_behaviour: When the poll loop detects status='ready'/'delivered', showToast is set to true. The CoreToast slides in (animate-reveal) at top-right (fixed, z-60, top-[4.5rem]). It shows a mini orb icon, 'Your ten minutes is ready', the meditation title, and 'via Telegram' (default channel). Two actions: 'Play' button (calls onPlay: dismisses toast and calls play(today)) and dismiss button (X icon, calls onDismiss). Toast has role='status'. Dismissed via either button; never auto-dismisses.
inventory_notes: The toast mirrors the channel notification the backend sends (Telegram/LINE) when composition completes. The channel label is hardcoded to 'Telegram' (default prop) in all current usage — no dynamic channel name is passed from the page.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see all my past meditations in a grid so that I can browse and replay any session.
expected_behaviour: Below the hero, 'Your meditations' heading shows the session count (e.g. '3 sessions' or '1 session'). The MeditationLibrary renders a 1-column (mobile) / 2-column (sm+) grid of cards, staggered-revealed (120ms + 80ms * index). Each card shows: dateLabel (Today/Yesterday/Jun 1), duration in minutes, lyrical title, theme one-liner, and a play chip. Active card has a signal-coloured border ring. Cards animate reveal on initial paint. Empty state shows a dashed border panel: 'Your composed meditations will collect here. Press the orb above to make your first.' During loading, 2 pulse-skeleton cards are shown.
inventory_notes: Sessions are ordered newest-first (API orders by -date, -created_at per api.ts comment). The library array is the same data used for stats; no separate library fetch exists.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to click any library card to start playing that meditation so that I can replay past sessions.
expected_behaviour: Clicking a library card calls onPlay(m). If the clicked meditation's id matches the currently loaded current, playing is toggled (pause if playing, resume if paused). Otherwise current is set to the clicked meditation and playing is set to true. The card border changes to signal colour with a glow ring. The play chip inside the card switches to an animated Equalizer (4 bars animating at staggered 120ms intervals) when that card is both active and playing, or reverts to PlayGlyph otherwise. Clicking the card again while active toggles play/pause.
inventory_notes: The library cards are <button type='button'> elements. No keyboard interaction beyond standard button Enter/Space.

user_story: As a subscriber in the 'ready' state, I want to tap the orb to play or pause today's meditation so that I have a prominent, centered control.
expected_behaviour: When phase is 'ready', clicking the orb calls onOrb() → play(today). If today is already current, this toggles playing. If today is not current (user had selected a past session), it sets current=today and playing=true. The orb switches between a play triangle icon (aria-label 'Begin meditation') and a pause bars icon (aria-label 'Pause meditation') based on playing && todayActive.
entry_points: frontend/app/core/page.tsx:CorePage (onOrb, play functions, todayActive derived state) ; frontend/components/core/breathing-orb.tsx:BreathingOrb (playing prop)
inventory_notes: todayActive = current?.id === today?.id. The orb only reflects playing state when todayActive is true; selecting a past session from the library leaves the orb in a non-playing visual even if audio is running.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want a persistent floating player with a play/pause button so that I can control audio without scrolling.
expected_behaviour: When current is non-null, CoreAudioPlayer renders fixed at bottom-16 (mobile, above tab bar) / bottom-5 (lg+, desktop), z-40, spanning up to max-w-3xl. A circular button (aria-label 'Play' or 'Pause') with an orb-style gradient shows a pause icon when playing or a play icon when paused. Clicking it calls onTogglePlay() which toggles the playing state in the parent. The <audio> element responds: el.play() on playing=true, el.pause() on playing=false. At audio end, onTogglePlay is called (onEnded handler), reverting playing to false.
inventory_notes: preload='none' is set on the <audio> element so audio is not fetched until play() is called. The player uses a real <audio> element with an <source> tag for the audioUrl.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want a seekable progress bar in the audio player so that I can skip to any point in the meditation.
expected_behaviour: The scrubber div (role='slider', aria-label='Seek', tabIndex=0) shows elapsed/total time as 'M:SS / M:SS'. Before metadata loads, duration falls back to meditation.durationMin * 60. The progress bar fills left to right (gradient teal→purple). Clicking the bar calculates ratio from click x-position and calls seekToRatio(). A thumb dot appears on hover/focus. Keyboard controls (WCAG 2.1.1): ArrowRight/ArrowUp advance +5s, ArrowLeft/ArrowDown rewind -5s, Home jumps to start, End jumps to end. Seeking only functions after metadata has loaded (dur > 0). aria-valuenow, aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax, aria-valuetext are set for screen reader access.
inventory_notes: Seek is only possible after onLoadedMetadata fires. Before that, the bar shows visual progress based on metadata durationMin but clicking/keying is a no-op (dur guard). The scrubber thumb only appears on hover/focus-visible.

feature: Floating audio player — load error state
user_story: As a subscriber, I want a clear error message if audio fails to load so that I know to refresh rather than wait indefinitely.
expected_behaviour: If the <audio> element fires an error event, loadError is set to true. The progress scrubber and timestamp display are replaced by an inline error message (role='alert'): 'Couldn't load this audio. Please refresh and try again.' The play/pause button remains visible (play/pause state continues to be driven) but audio will not play. Switching to a different meditation resets loadError to false.
inventory_notes: Error state resets when meditation.id changes (render-phase state adjustment pattern, not useEffect).

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to close the floating player so that it does not obstruct the rest of the page.
expected_behaviour: The 'X' button (aria-label 'Close player') calls onClose() in the parent, which sets current=null and playing=false. This unmounts the CoreAudioPlayer (conditional render on current non-null) and pauses audio (the <audio> element is removed from DOM). The library card that was active reverts to showing PlayGlyph. The orb reverts to its non-playing state.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want the audio player to immediately switch to a newly selected track so that selecting a different meditation does not require closing and reopening the player.
expected_behaviour: When current changes (new meditation selected from library), the player resets: cur→0, dur→0, loadError→false (render-phase state adjustment). A useEffect triggers audioRef.current.load() to reload the audio element with the new source. A useEffect responds to playing state: calls el.play() or el.pause() including when meditation.id changes. The title, timestamp, and scrubber all update to reflect the new track.
inventory_notes: The render-phase state-adjustment pattern (if loadedId !== meditation.id) is used intentionally to avoid setState in an effect.

feature: Stats dashboard — sessions this week, total minutes, streak, last sat
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see my meditation statistics so that I can track my consistency over time.
expected_behaviour: Four stat cards are displayed in a 2-column (mobile) / 4-column (sm+) grid with staggered reveal animation. Cards: 'This week' (count of sessions in last 7 days), 'Total' (total minutes across all sessions), 'Streak' (consecutive days with ≥1 sit anchored at today or yesterday), 'Last sat' (relative date label of most recent session, '—' if none). All computed client-side from the library data using computeCoreStats(). During loading, pulse skeletons replace the cards (aria-hidden). Stats are recomputed reactively when sessions or timezone changes.
inventory_notes: Streak logic: starts at today's day-key; if today has no sit, rewinds one day before counting. Streak breaks if any calendar day (in tenant tz) has no sit. Total minutes uses durationMin (derived from duration_ms / 60000, minimum 1, defaults to 10 if no duration_ms).

user_story: As a subscriber, I want dates to display relative to my timezone so that 'Today' reflects my local calendar day, not UTC.
expected_behaviour: The tenant's IANA timezone is read from me.timezone (via useMeQuery). Fallback order: me.timezone → Intl.DateTimeFormat().resolvedOptions().timeZone → 'UTC'. All date comparisons (Today/Yesterday/week window/streak) use dayKeyInTz() which renders dates via en-CA locale in the tenant timezone. If tz resolves after the library loads (cache warm-up), the library is re-projected reactively via the useMemo on [sessions, tz]. The relative label 'Today'/'Yesterday' is recomputed on every render.
entry_points: frontend/app/core/page.tsx:CorePage (tz, library useMemo) ; frontend/lib/core.ts:dayKeyInTz, toMeditation, computeCoreStats, relativeDateLabel
inventory_notes: The comment in page.tsx explicitly documents that the old browser-tz behavior only bites before the cache warms. The streak and week window calculations in computeCoreStats use shiftDayKey() anchored at UTC noon to avoid DST edge cases.

feature: Breathing orb animation and reduced-motion safety
user_story: As a subscriber, I want the orb to animate calmly so that the mindfulness surface feels meditative, and I want the animation to respect my motion preferences.
expected_behaviour: The BreathingOrb renders three concentric expanding-ring spans (core-breathe-ring class) with staggered animation delays (0s, 2.3s, 4.6s) and a pulsing outer halo (core-breathe-glow). The orb body itself has a core-breathe class. All three CSS animation classes disable their animation under prefers-reduced-motion (per the comment in the component). On hover, the orb body scales to 1.03; on active, scales to 0.98. Focus-visible: ring-2 ring-signal/60 with a ring-offset-4 on dark bg.
inventory_notes: The core-breathe, core-breathe-ring, core-breathe-glow CSS classes must be defined in globals.css with @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) overrides. These classes are referenced but not defined in the component file itself.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see my personal finance dashboard load quickly so that I can check my debt and savings at a glance.
expected_behaviour: On mount, `useFinanceDashboardQuery` fetches `GET /api/v1/finance/dashboard/` (staleTime 30 s). While pending, the page renders three `StatCardSkeleton` components in a 3-column grid followed by a `SectionCardSkeleton` with 4 lines — no content is shown. On network error, a rose-coloured error banner appears with the message from the Error object or the fallback 'Failed to load Gravity. Please try again.' On success with data, the full dashboard renders. Data is never cached for more than 30 s; refetch happens automatically on stale.
inventory_notes: The query is enabled only when `isLoggedIn()` returns true. A `null` data state (successful fetch returning null) short-circuits to `return null` with no visible UI.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see a clear notice when Gravity is temporarily unavailable so that I understand my data is safe and the feature will return.
