expected_behaviour: After `useTenantQuery` resolves, if `tenant.gravity_available === false` the page renders a glass-card notice with heading 'Gravity is paused' and an explanatory paragraph — before any finance data is fetched. The Gravity nav tab is also hidden in the app-shell when `finance_enabled && gravity_available` is false, so the page is reachable only by direct URL in that state. No loading skeleton for this branch — the tenant query (staleTime 5 min) must resolve first.
inventory_notes: The nav tab is hidden by the app-shell when `tenant.finance_enabled && tenant.gravity_available` is false, but the route `/finance` itself is not server-guarded — a subscriber who bookmarks the URL can still reach it.

user_story: As a subscriber with no accounts set up, I want to see a helpful prompt so that I know how to add my first account via the assistant.
expected_behaviour: When the dashboard loads and `data.accounts.length === 0`, the page renders a glass-card with a coin SVG icon, heading 'No accounts yet', and onboarding copy instructing the subscriber to tell their assistant about debts/savings via natural language (e.g., 'I have a credit card with $5,000 at 22% APR'). There are no buttons or forms — account creation is chat-only. No KPI stats, charts, or account lists are rendered.
inventory_notes: Account creation is intentionally delegated to the AI assistant; there is no manual account creation form on this page.

feature: KPI Stats Row — Total Debt / Savings / Est. Payoff
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see my aggregate debt, savings, and estimated debt-free date at a glance so that I can gauge my financial health instantly.
expected_behaviour: Three `StatCard` components render in a responsive 3-column grid (1-col on mobile, 3-col on md+). 'Total Debt' shows `data.total_debt` formatted as `$X,XXX` with a hint showing the number of debt accounts. 'Savings' shows `data.total_savings` with a savings account count hint. 'Est. Payoff' shows the active plan's `payoff_date` formatted as 'Mon YYYY' with the strategy name as a hint; if no active plan exists, the value is an em-dash and the hint reads 'Ask your assistant to calculate'. All values are USD en-US formatted with no decimal places. Cards are hidden during loading (skeleton placeholders used instead).
dependencies: GET /api/v1/finance/dashboard/ — fields: total_debt, total_savings, debt_account_count, savings_account_count, active_plan
inventory_notes: Payoff date is parsed with `+ 'T00:00:00'` to avoid UTC-midnight off-by-one in negative-offset timezones.

user_story: As a subscriber with an active payoff plan, I want to see a visual chart of my projected debt payoff timeline so that I can track progress month by month.
expected_behaviour: Rendered only when `data.active_plan` is non-null. Lazy-loaded via `next/dynamic` with `ssr: false`; while the Recharts bundle loads, a `h-64 animate-pulse rounded-panel bg-surface` placeholder is shown. The chart is a Recharts `AreaChart` that merges: (1) projected balance from `active_plan.schedule_json` (dashed teal area), and (2) actual balances from `data.snapshots` (solid accent line with dots, shown only if snapshot data exists). Pre-plan historical snapshots before the plan start date are prepended. The X-axis shows up to ~6 date labels; Y-axis shows `$XK` shorthand. A custom `ChartTooltip` shows 'Projected'/'Actual' labels on hover. A `Legend` appears at top-right only when actual data exists. The chart is wrapped in a `role='img'` div with an accessible `aria-label`.
inventory_notes: Returns null early if `schedule_json` is empty. Recharts ~80 KB gzipped is only fetched when an active plan is present — intentional perf optimisation noted in source comments.

user_story: As a subscriber with an active payoff plan, I want to see which debt-payoff strategy I'm using and its key metrics so that I understand how my plan works.
expected_behaviour: Rendered only when `data.active_plan` is non-null, inside a `SectionCard` with title 'Strategy' on the right side of a 12-col grid (lg:col-span-4). The `StrategyComparison` component renders one `article` per plan in the `plans` array; when only `activePlan` is passed (no `allPlans`), a single card is rendered. The active plan card shows: strategy label (Snowball / Avalanche / Hybrid), a description sentence, payoff months, debt-free date, total interest (USD), and monthly budget. An 'Active' dot badge is shown on the active plan. If multiple plans exist, inactive plans are shown at 60% opacity. If a plan has higher total interest than the cheapest option, a '+$X vs best strategy' amber note is shown; the cheapest shows 'Lowest interest cost' in emerald. This component is purely read-only — no mutation is triggered.
inventory_notes: The `allPlans` prop is optional and not passed from the finance page (only `activePlan` is passed). The component accepts it for potential future multi-plan comparison but currently only ever shows a single plan card.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see all my active debt accounts in one section so that I can review each balance, APR, minimum payment, and payoff progress.
expected_behaviour: Rendered when `debtAccounts.length > 0` (accounts where `is_debt === true`). Shows a glass-card container titled 'Debt Accounts' with one `AccountCard` per debt account. Each card shows: emoji icon by account type, nickname, type label + APR (e.g., 'Credit Card · 22.0% APR'), optional 'Monthly' minimum payment, current balance (USD with 2 decimal places), and an archive button. A progress bar with `role='progressbar'` and `aria-valuenow` is rendered for debt accounts that have a `payoff_progress` value, showing % paid off and original balance. The archive icon button is visible at full opacity on mobile; on sm+ it is opacity-0 and appears on group hover or keyboard focus.
dependencies: GET /api/v1/finance/dashboard/ — field: accounts (filtered by is_debt: true)
inventory_notes: Account type labels and emojis for: credit_card, student_loan, personal_loan, mortgage, auto_loan, medical_debt, other_debt. Unknown types fall back to account_type string and 📄 emoji. Progress bar only renders for debt accounts (is_debt check), not savings.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see all my active savings accounts in one section so that I can track my savings balances.
expected_behaviour: Rendered when `savingsAccounts.length > 0` (accounts where `is_debt === false`). Shows a glass-card container titled 'Savings' with one `AccountCard` per savings account. Each card shows: emoji icon (💰 for savings, 🏦 for checking, 🛡️ for emergency_fund), nickname, type label, current balance. No progress bar is rendered for savings accounts (progress bar branch requires `isDebt === true`). The archive button behaves identically to debt accounts. Signal (teal) colour scheme is used for the icon background and progress bar colour (though the bar is hidden for savings).
dependencies: GET /api/v1/finance/dashboard/ — field: accounts (filtered by is_debt: false)
inventory_notes: Savings accounts with a `payoff_progress` value do not render the progress bar because the `isDebt && progress !== null` guard excludes them.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to archive an account I no longer track so that it is removed from my totals and payoff calculations without being permanently deleted.
expected_behaviour: Clicking the archive icon button on any `AccountCard` (either debt or savings) sets `confirmArchive` state to that `FinanceAccount`. This renders the `ConfirmArchiveDialog` as a fixed full-screen overlay (`z-50`, `role='dialog'`, `aria-modal='true'`). The dialog shows the account nickname in the heading, an explanation that it will be removed from Gravity totals but kept for records. Pressing Escape or clicking the backdrop dismisses the dialog (sets `confirmArchive` to null). Clicking 'Archive' fires `archiveMutation.mutate(account.id)` which calls `DELETE /api/v1/finance/accounts/{id}/`. While pending, the button shows 'Archiving…' and is disabled. On success, the dialog closes and both `finance-dashboard` and `finance-archived-accounts` query caches are invalidated. On error, a rose-coloured error message appears below the description text inside the dialog. No optimistic update.
dependencies: DELETE /api/v1/finance/accounts/{id}/ — maps to deleteFinanceAccount in api.ts (note: function is named delete but semantically archives, per UI copy)
inventory_notes: The archive action uses `DELETE` HTTP method via `deleteFinanceAccount`. The API appears to archive (not hard-delete) based on the unarchive endpoint and UI copy, but the frontend function name may be misleading. Escape key handler is added with `useEffect` on mount of the dialog. Error message falls back to 'Failed to archive. Please try again.' if the Error object is not an instance of Error.
error_detail: Confirmed. The finance page holds a single shared mutation instance (archiveMutation = useArchiveFinanceAccountMutation(), page.tsx:75) whose error state is never reset. The dialog's errorMessage is derived purely from archiveMutation.isError / archiveMutation.error (page.tsx:303-309). React Query v5 (@tanstack/react-query ^5.101.0) retains isError/error after a failed mutate until the next mutate call begins or reset() is called, and there is no reset() anywhere (grep found none in page.tsx or queries.ts). The cancel handler only does setConfirmArchive(null) (page.tsx:295), which unmounts the dialog but leaves mutation state untouched. So after: open dialog for account A, click Archive, request fails (isError=true, dialog stays open showing the rose-400 error at page.tsx:437-438), Cancel, then open dialog for account B (or even re-open A) — the freshly mounted ConfirmArchiveDialog immediately renders the stale error message from A's failed attempt before the user interacts. User impact: a confusing phantom error line on a just-opened dialog. It is non-blocking (clicking Archive re-triggers mutate and clears the error) and only surfaces after a prior archive failure, so impact is cosmetic/low.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to view and restore previously archived accounts so that I can reactivate them if my financial situation changes.
expected_behaviour: A collapsible section labelled 'Archived Accounts' is always present at the bottom of the dashboard (when there are active accounts). The toggle button has `aria-expanded` set to the `showArchived` boolean and shows ▾/▴ chevrons. Clicking it toggles `showArchived` state. When expanded for the first time, `useArchivedFinanceAccountsQuery(showArchived)` becomes enabled and fetches `GET /api/v1/finance/accounts/?archived=true` (staleTime 30 s). While loading, 'Loading…' text is shown. If the list is empty after load, 'No archived accounts.' is shown with the collapse button still visible. Each archived account shows its nickname, last balance, and a 'Restore' button. Clicking Restore fires `unarchiveMutation.mutate(account.id)` which calls `PATCH /api/v1/finance/accounts/{id}/` with `{is_active: true}`. While restoring a specific account, that account's button shows 'Restoring…' and is disabled. On success, both `finance-dashboard` and `finance-archived-accounts` caches are invalidated.
inventory_notes: The archived query is lazy — `enabled: isLoggedIn() && enabled` means it only fires when the user first expands the section. The section is rendered even when there are no active accounts (though it is hidden when expanded and empty after load). The `restoringId` tracks the specific account being restored by passing `unarchiveMutation.variables` cast to string.
error_detail: Confirmed real. The /finance dashboard endpoint returns only active accounts (apps/finance/views.py:203 filters is_active=True), so when a subscriber archives every account, data.accounts is empty so hasAccounts is false (page.tsx:126) and the empty-state early return fires (page.tsx:131-150), rendering "No accounts yet" and returning before the main block. ArchivedAccountsSection is only mounted in the main return (page.tsx:278), so the toggle/list/Restore UI never renders in that state. The archived data is NOT lost — it persists server-side and GET /api/v1/finance/accounts/?archived=true still returns it — but the web UI offers no path to reach the Restore button. The empty-state copy directs the user to "Tell your assistant about your debts and savings", i.e. create a NEW account, not restore; the assistant's finance services also resolve accounts only among is_active=True records (apps/finance/services.py:277-286), so there is no clean assistant-side unarchive either. Net user impact: after archiving all accounts, a subscriber is stranded — the only recovery is to create a brand-new active account (which re-renders the main block and makes the Archived section reappear) and then restore the old ones. Severity is medium rather than high because it requires the all-accounts-archived edge case, no data is lost, and a clumsy recovery path exists.

user_story: As a subscriber with multiple monthly snapshots, I want to see a bar chart of my debt and savings trends over time so that I can visualise my financial progress month by month.
expected_behaviour: Rendered only when `data.snapshots.length >= 2`. Lazy-loaded via `next/dynamic` with `ssr: false`; a pulse skeleton is shown while the Recharts chunk loads. The `ProgressChart` component renders a Recharts `BarChart` with two bar series: 'Debt' (rose) and 'Savings' (emerald). Snapshots are sorted oldest-first for left-to-right chronological display. The X-axis shows abbreviated month labels; Y-axis shows `$XK` shorthand. A custom `ChartTooltip` shows both values on hover. The chart is wrapped in `role='img'` with an `aria-label` stating the number of months shown. Both bars have rounded top corners (`radius=[4,4,0,0]`) and a 500 ms animation on first render.
dependencies: GET /api/v1/finance/dashboard/ — field: snapshots (requires >= 2 entries to render)
inventory_notes: Returns null if fewer than 2 snapshots. No loading state specific to this chart beyond the dynamic import skeleton.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see my total required monthly minimum payments in one number so that I know the minimum cash flow committed to debt service.
expected_behaviour: Rendered when `parseFloat(data.total_minimum_payments) > 0`. A small glass-card row at the bottom of the main content area (above the archived section) shows 'Total monthly minimums' as a muted label and the USD-formatted total as a mono-font value on the right. This is purely informational and read-only with no interaction.
inventory_notes: Hidden when total is zero or when data is loading/errored.

feature: Gravity Insights Strip — Recent Assistant Observations
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see my assistant's most recent finance-related insights on the Gravity page so that I can quickly act on them without navigating to Horizons.
expected_behaviour: The `GravityInsightsStrip` component queries `GET /api/v1/horizons/` (via `useHorizonsQuery`, staleTime 60 s). It filters `assistant_insights` to those where `pillar === 'gravity'` and `status === 'open'`, then slices to the first 3. If no qualifying insights exist, the component renders nothing (null). When insights are present, a glass-card strip appears with: a header showing 'Your assistant noticed' in accent mono text and '{N} new observation(s) waiting for your confirmation', up to 3 insight statement texts in a bullet list, and a 'View in Horizons →' link on desktop (hidden sm:inline-flex) and 'Confirm in Horizons →' link at the bottom on mobile (sm:hidden). Both links navigate to `/horizons`. Confirm/dismiss actions are not available inline — they must be performed on the Horizons page.
dependencies: GET /api/v1/horizons/ — HorizonsData.assistant_insights filtered by pillar='gravity' and status='open'
inventory_notes: The strip has no dedicated error or loading state — it simply renders nothing while horizons data is loading or if the query errors. The strip shows at most 3 insights regardless of how many open gravity insights exist.

feature: Obligations Status in Journal — Current Status Card
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see my recurring finance obligations and their payment status in the Journal page so that I can track what is paid, partial, or overdue without switching tabs.
expected_behaviour: The `CurrentStatusCard` component (rendered on the journal page, not `/finance`) fetches `GET /api/v1/journal/status/` via `useJournalStatusQuery` (staleTime 60 s). While loading, the card renders nothing (null). The obligations section appears when `obligations.length > 0`. Each obligation row shows: account nickname + minimum payment + due date (formatted as 'Mon D' in local timezone to avoid UTC off-by-one), and a status pill. Pill styles: 'Paid' (signal-faint/signal-text), 'Partial' (amber border/bg/text), 'Not yet paid' or 'Overdue' (rose border/bg/text). The 'Overdue' label overrides the status label when `ob.overdue === true` and status is not 'paid'. This section is entirely read-only; no finance mutations are triggered from here.
dependencies: GET /api/v1/journal/status/ — JournalStatus.obligations (JournalObligation[]); only rendered when finance is enabled and obligations are present
inventory_notes: The card only renders when at least one of obligations, open_tasks, or active_goals is non-empty. The `finance_enabled` field is on the JournalStatus response but is not used to conditionally hide the obligations section in this component — if obligations are returned, they will display. Date parsing uses `new Date(y, m-1, d)` (local) not `new Date(iso)` (UTC) to avoid off-by-one errors.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want a top-level Fuel page with tabs for Schedule, Calendar, History, and Progress so that I can navigate between different views of my fitness data.
expected_behaviour: On initial render the page shows a hero heading (hidden on mobile, replaced with a compact eyebrow + CTA row) and defaults to the 'Schedule' tab. Four tabs are rendered in a border-b tab strip: Schedule, Calendar (which fetches done-count via GET /api/v1/fuel/workouts/count/?status=done), History (shows done-count badge on sm+), and Progress. Clicking a tab switches content instantly (client-side state only; no navigation). The active tab is underlined with a 1.5px accent bar. The done-count badge only appears on sm+ viewports. No loading state for the tabs themselves.
inventory_notes: Tab state is local React state — refreshing the page always resets to 'schedule'. doneCount used solely for History badge display.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want a prominent 'Log workout' button at the top of the Fuel page so that I can quickly open the new-workout creation flow from anywhere on the page.
expected_behaviour: Two visually identical 'Log workout' buttons render: one in the mobile compact header (sm:hidden), one in the desktop hero (hidden sm:flex). Clicking either opens the NewWorkoutDialog with no preset date (generic quick-add). The button is a rounded-full accent-colored pill with a + icon, minimum 44px touch target. No loading or disabled state on this button itself.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see my fitness profile summary at the top of Fuel so that I can confirm the assistant knows my level, goals, and equipment.
expected_behaviour: On load a skeleton (SkelBar) renders while useFuelProfileQuery fetches GET /api/v1/fuel/profile/. If profile is null the card is not shown. If onboarding_status is 'pending', the card is suppressed (settings page handles messaging). If 'declined', a brief prose message renders. If 'in_progress', an accent-bordered prompt to continue chatting renders. If 'completed', a compact row shows fitness_level, days_per_week, goals, equipment, and limitations. No edit affordance on this card — edits happen via the assistant.
inventory_notes: Suppressed while 'pending'; settings page is the canonical onboarding surface.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want a 'This Week' summary strip above the tabs so that I can see at a glance how many sessions and minutes I've completed this week.
expected_behaviour: Fetches GET /api/v1/fuel/weekly-summary/ (useWeeklyVolumeQuery). A skeleton renders during load. If no sessions this week (totals.sessions === 0) or data is null, nothing renders. Otherwise shows session count, total minutes, and per-category colored dot + label + count chips. Read-only; no interactions.
inventory_notes: Hidden entirely when zero sessions — not shown as empty state.

feature: Today's Check-In Form (Weight, Sleep, Resting HR)
user_story: As a subscriber, I want a daily check-in card to log today's body weight, sleep hours/quality, and resting heart rate so that my assistant has current biometric data.
expected_behaviour: Loads data from GET /api/v1/fuel/body-weight/, GET /api/v1/fuel/sleep/, and GET /api/v1/fuel/resting-hr/ in parallel. Default date is today; user can pick a past date via a date popover (max=today). When both weight and sleep are logged for the selected date and it is today, the card collapses to a one-liner summary with an 'Edit' button. In expanded form, weight (decimal) and sleep hours (decimal) inputs are side-by-side; a quality dot-picker (1–5 dots) appears when hours are non-empty; resting HR is hidden by default behind a '+ Resting HR' text button. Submit is disabled unless at least one field is dirty (tolerance checks prevent re-saves of unchanged values). On submit, all dirty fields are mutated in parallel via POST or PATCH to their respective endpoints. Saving disables the button and shows 'Saving…'. Fields show 'Logged ✓' emerald badge when an entry exists for the selected date.
entry_points: frontend/components/fuel/today-checkin.tsx:TodayCheckIn ; GET /api/v1/fuel/body-weight/ ; POST /api/v1/fuel/body-weight/ ; PATCH /api/v1/fuel/body-weight/{id}/ ; GET /api/v1/fuel/sleep/ ; POST /api/v1/fuel/sleep/ ; PATCH /api/v1/fuel/sleep/{id}/ ; GET /api/v1/fuel/resting-hr/ ; POST /api/v1/fuel/resting-hr/ ; PATCH /api/v1/fuel/resting-hr/{id}/
inventory_notes: Weight stored as kg, displayed in user's preferred unit (kg/lbs). Quality dot toggle: clicking the active dot clears it. The 'forceEdit' state keeps the card expanded after a save if user clicked 'Edit' on the collapsed summary. Date picker has max=today to prevent future check-ins.
error_detail: Confirmed in part, with a correction to the claim's framing. handleSubmit fires all dirty field saves in parallel via Promise.allSettled then unconditionally calls setForceEdit(false) (today-checkin.tsx:192-193). The fuel mutations (useCreateBodyWeightMutation, useUpdateBodyWeightMutation, sleep, HR in lib/queries.ts:1415-1444) do NOT do optimistic cache writes; they only invalidateQueries on success, and they do NOT set meta.skipErrorToast, so each failed save falls through to the global mutation onError in providers.tsx:30-35 and emits a 3s toast. getErrorMessage can surface the backend detail/message so it is not strictly generic, but it never names which field failed. The genuine UX gap is the partial-failure-on-edit case: when both weight and sleep already exist for today (allLogged=true) and the user edits both, if one PATCH fails the cache keeps the stale value (no optimistic write) and the card collapses to its one-liner summary (collapsed = not forceEdit and allLogged and isToday, line 52), showing the updated field next to the stale one, signalled only by a transient toast. The claim's stronger wording (collapses unconditionally, split state with no indication) overstates the common case: collapse is gated by allLogged, so a FAILED first-time create leaves todayBW/todaySleep undefined, allLogged stays false, and the form does NOT collapse; the failed field then visibly lacks its Logged badge and stays editable and retryable. No data is lost in any case (server is source of truth and the cache reflects it). Net: a real but narrow medium-low UX issue (no per-field error state, and a partial edit-failure can collapse to a stale-looking summary signalled only by a transient toast), not a data-integrity bug.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see a banner when I have unsaved workout drafts from sessions that were deleted mid-edit so that I can recover my data.
expected_behaviour: Reads localStorage key 'nbhd_fuel_orphan_drafts_v1' via listOrphans(tenantId). Listens for both the window 'storage' event (cross-tab) and a custom 'nbhd:orphan-drafts-changed' event (same-tab stash). Renders nothing if no orphans. With orphans, renders an amber-bordered banner showing the first orphan's activity name and date. A 'Recover' button opens the OrphanRecoveryPanel for that draft. If >1 orphan, a <details> element expands to list all drafts with individual OPEN buttons.
inventory_notes: All data is localStorage-only; no backend fetch until the user opens a recovery panel.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want a guided recovery dialog for an orphaned draft so that I can choose to save it as a new workout, merge it with an existing same-day session, or discard it.
expected_behaviour: Opens as a bottom-sheet (mobile) / centered modal (sm+). Immediately fetches GET /api/v1/fuel/workouts/?date_from={date}&date_to={date} to check what's already on that day. Shows a draft summary with activity, metrics, and a read-only detail preview (WorkoutDetailReadOnly). Then routes to one of three cases: (1) 'save_as_new' — no same-day workout; offers 'Save as a new workout' (POST /api/v1/fuel/workouts/) or 'Discard'. (2) 'fill_in' — a planned same-day workout exists; offers 'Apply & mark complete' (PATCH /api/v1/fuel/workouts/{id}/ + marks done), 'Save as a separate workout', or 'Discard'. (3) 'potential_dup' — a completed same-day workout exists; offers 'Same — discard draft', 'Different — save as 2nd workout', or conditionally 'Merge my notes into the existing one' (only if draft has notes and existing doesn't). On success, discardOrphan() removes the stash from localStorage and emits a success toast.
inventory_notes: pickAction logic determines which case applies. The merge-notes path only appears when draft has notes AND existing record has no notes. Recovery uses draft.status to avoid silently downgrading a planned edit to done.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want a 7-day schedule view so that I can see all upcoming and today's planned sessions at a glance.
expected_behaviour: Fetches GET /api/v1/fuel/workouts/?window=7d (useScheduleWindowQuery). Shows a skeleton NextUpSkeleton while isPending and no cache. On desktop (sm+), renders 7 DayCard components in a responsive grid (2-up, 3-up, 4-up). On mobile, renders today as a DayCard + the other 6 days as compact DayRow components. Sessions within each day are sorted by scheduled_at (nulls last) then created_at. An empty day shows 'Rest day · tap to add' dashed button. A background 'syncing…' label appears when isLoading but data is already present.
inventory_notes: DayRow on mobile shows only the first session; multiple sessions are indicated by '+N more'. DayCard shows all sessions as SessionCard rows.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want a 'Next Up' banner highlighting my next planned session so that I can quickly complete or skip it.
expected_behaviour: Derived from the same 7-day schedule data — picks the chronologically first planned session. Renders a card with a left-border accent stripe in the category color. A relative-time label ('in 30 min', 'overdue', 'was Mon 9:00 AM') updates every 30 seconds via setInterval. Three actions: 'Complete' (POST /api/v1/fuel/workouts/{id}/complete/ → on success flashes a '✓ Done' green pill for 1.4s before the card transitions away), 'Skip' (window.prompt for optional reason → POST /api/v1/fuel/workouts/{id}/skip/), and 'View' (opens WorkoutDetail, desktop-only). The entire card is also a tap target to open WorkoutDetail, disabled during the post-complete flash. The skeleton (NextUpSkeleton) renders only while isPending and no cached data.
inventory_notes: The 'overdue' state is shown in amber text. justCompleted state prevents accidental re-open of a just-completed workout. Skip reason is captured via native window.prompt (not a custom modal).

feature: Schedule Tab — Session Card Quick Actions Menu
user_story: As a subscriber, I want a ⋯ menu on each session card so that I can quickly mark a session complete, skip it, or delete it without opening the full detail drawer.
expected_behaviour: Each SessionCard in the 7-day view has a ⋯ button (aria-label='Session actions', aria-expanded). Clicking opens a floating popover menu positioned below the button. A transparent click-away overlay closes it. For 'planned' sessions, 'Mark complete' (POST /api/v1/fuel/workouts/{id}/complete/) and 'Skip…' (window.prompt + POST /api/v1/fuel/workouts/{id}/skip/) items are shown. 'Delete' (with window.confirm) is always available. In-flight actions disable with opacity-50. The menu closes on action completion.
inventory_notes: Mark complete and Skip only appear for status='planned'. Done/skipped sessions only show Delete. Skip reason via native window.prompt; delete confirmation via window.confirm.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want a monthly calendar view showing workout dots on each day so that I can see my training patterns over a month.
expected_behaviour: Fetches GET /api/v1/fuel/calendar/?year={y}&month={m} (useFuelCalendarQuery). Renders a 7-column grid starting Monday. Out-of-month cells are dimmed and clickable. Today's cell has an accent border and 'TODAY' label. Each cell shows a workout count badge and up to 3 colored activity slivers (mobile: dots only; sm+: dots + truncated activity name). A rest day shows a grey dot + 'Rest' label (sm+). Clicking any cell calls onSelectDay which opens the DayDrawer. A 'THIS WEEK' stat (count + hours of done sessions in the past 7 days) is computed client-side. Prev/next month buttons (aria-label) navigate the cursor; a 'TODAY' text button resets to the current month. A color legend renders below the grid.
inventory_notes: Calendar data is also the backing store for the DayDrawer (deduped by React Query). week total counts only 'done' sessions. Overflow items beyond 3 per cell show '+N' label.

feature: Day Drawer — Open Day Detail Panel
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to click a calendar day and see all planned and completed sessions for that day in a side panel so that I can review and manage that day's activity.
expected_behaviour: Renders as a fixed full-screen overlay with a right-aligned panel (full width on mobile, 540px on sm+). Derives session data from the existing calendar cache (no new fetch). Shows a long-format date heading and a subtitle ('No activity', 'Rest day', or 'X done · Y planned'). Planned sessions are listed in a 'PLANNED' section, completed in a 'COMPLETED' section, each as WorkoutRow buttons. Clicking a WorkoutRow opens the WorkoutDetail drawer. An 'Add workout on {date}' dashed button opens NewWorkoutDialog with that date preset. A 'Mark as rest day' button (POST /api/v1/fuel/workouts/ with status='rest') only appears when no done sessions and no existing rest entry. If a rest entry exists, a 'Remove' button (DELETE /api/v1/fuel/workouts/{id}/) removes it. Prev/next day arrows navigate by ±1 day (client-side ISO date arithmetic). Clicking the backdrop closes.
inventory_notes: Day navigation updates the ISO date in the parent page state (setDayIso). The 'Mark as rest day' button is hidden once any 'done' workout exists to prevent marking a workout day as rest.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to pick a workout category when creating a new session so that the detail fields match my activity type.
expected_behaviour: Opens as a right-side panel (full width on mobile, 480px on sm+) with a 'NEW WORKOUT · PICK CATEGORY' header. Step 0 shows a 2-column grid of category buttons (strength, cardio, hiit, calisthenics, mobility, sport, other), each with a colored icon, label, and hint text. Clicking a category sets the category state and advances to step 1. A back arrow appears on step 1 to return to step 0. On a generic 'Log workout' open (no presetDate), if localStorage contains an unfinished draft (loadDraft), a 'Unfinished workout from X ago' restore prompt is shown with Resume / Start fresh buttons. On presetDate opens, restore prompt is suppressed.
inventory_notes: Autosave begins after category is selected. Dialog remounts each time it opens (key={open}) so state fully resets.

feature: New Workout Dialog — Activity Details & Create (Step 1)
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to enter an activity name, date, and status for a new workout so that I can log or plan a session quickly before filling in full details.
expected_behaviour: Step 1 shows: (1) an Activity text input with suggestion chips from CATEGORIES[cat].suggest; (2) if the user has templates for this category (GET /api/v1/fuel/templates/?category={cat}), a 'FROM TEMPLATE' section with buttons that pre-fill activity name and detail_json; (3) a date picker (defaults to presetDate or today); (4) a Done/Planned toggle (default: 'planned' if date is future, 'done' otherwise). 'Create workout' button calls POST /api/v1/fuel/workouts/ with default duration_minutes=45. On success: closes dialog, clears autosave draft, and immediately opens the WorkoutDetail drawer for the new workout via onCreated(result.id). Save button disabled and shows 'Creating…' while pending. On error, React Query's default error handling applies.
inventory_notes: Autosave is debounced 800ms and flushes on pagehide/visibilitychange. Activity field accepts free text; if empty, falls back to CATEGORIES[cat].label on create. default duration_minutes=45 is always submitted — user edits it in the WorkoutDetail drawer.

feature: Workout Detail Drawer — View & Edit Mode
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to open a full-detail panel for any workout so that I can view and edit its metrics, notes, category, date, duration, and RPE.
expected_behaviour: Fetches GET /api/v1/fuel/workouts/{id}/ (useWorkoutQuery, refetchOnMount=always). Shows a skeleton while loading. In view mode, shows activity name, category badge (PLANNED/DONE/SKIPPED), date, duration, RPE, category-specific detail section, and notes. An EDIT button in the header switches to edit mode. In edit mode, activity is an editable input; category shows a horizontal chip switcher; date, duration, RPE are editable fields; category-specific editors render. Save triggers PATCH /api/v1/fuel/workouts/{id}/ and exits edit mode. 'Mark complete' triggers POST /api/v1/fuel/workouts/{id}/complete/ (passes notes, RPE, duration from draft). Planned workouts open directly in edit mode. A stale-edit warning banner appears if the assistant wrote a newer fuel_version while the user was editing; a 'Refresh' button re-fetches. On save error, an alert banner shows the error message and keeps the drawer open with the draft intact.
dependencies: useWorkoutQuery, useUpdateWorkoutMutation, useCompleteWorkoutMutation, useFuelVersionQuery (polls every 30s), useAcquireEditLockMutation, useReleaseEditLockMutation, useMeQuery
inventory_notes: Edit lock: POST /api/v1/fuel/workouts/{id}/edit-lock/ is called on edit-mode entry and heartbeated every 30s; DELETE on exit. Lock is best-effort — missed release self-expires server-side. Autosave debounces dirty edits to localStorage every 800ms; flushes on pagehide/visibilitychange. A recover prompt appears on open if a saved draft differs from server state. A 'last_edited_by_user_at' footnote renders when a done workout was edited.

feature: Workout Detail Drawer — Autosave Draft Recovery
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to be offered my unsaved edits when I reopen a workout I was editing so that I don't lose work from a tab close or navigation away.
expected_behaviour: On WorkoutDetail open, after the server state loads, loadDraft(tenantId, workoutId) checks localStorage for a saved draft. If found and it differs from the server state (sameDraft check), a 'Unsaved changes from X ago' banner renders with 'Restore' and 'Discard' buttons. Restore: sets draft state to saved payload and enters edit mode. Discard: clears the localStorage entry. Autosave writes to localStorage at 800ms debounce while editing and dirty; clears the entry when draft matches server or on successful save.
inventory_notes: Draft recovery is skipped if the saved draft is identical to the server state (avoids prompting unnecessarily). The banner is suppressed while a restore prompt is showing (prevents clobbering).

user_story: As a subscriber, I want a clear message and safe close path when a workout I'm viewing has been deleted by my assistant so that I'm not stuck in a broken state.
expected_behaviour: If GET /api/v1/fuel/workouts/{id}/ returns 404, isNotFoundError triggers: (1) invalidates fuel-workouts, fuel-calendar, fuel-schedule queries; (2) stashes any meaningful in-progress draft to orphan-drafts localStorage (stashOrphan); (3) renders PhantomWorkoutRecovery panel with 'This session was removed' message and a 'Back to Fuel' button that calls onClose(). The OrphanDraftsBanner on the main page then surfaces the stash for recovery.
inventory_notes: Stash is a one-shot side-effect — stashedRef.current prevents double-stash. source field is 'phantom_404' or 'mutation_404' for debugging.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to log exercises with sets and reps (or holds or bodyweight) for a strength workout so that I can track my volume and estimated 1RM per lift.
expected_behaviour: In edit mode, shows an 'EXERCISES' section with '+ EXERCISE' button. Each exercise has a name input and a list of sets. Each set has a MetricToggle (WEIGHT / BODY / HOLD) to switch between weighted_reps, bodyweight_reps, and hold_time. Weighted: reps + weight (in user's unit, stored as kg). Bodyweight: reps only. Hold: hold_s (seconds). A trash button deletes individual sets or the whole exercise. '+ SET' adds a set defaulting to the last set's weight. Estimated 1RM is computed client-side (Epley formula) for weighted sets and shown as 'est 1RM X unit' if >0. Stored in detail_json.exercises. No server call per keystroke — all edits are staged in local draft state until 'Save changes' is clicked.
inventory_notes: NumericInput holds string during editing, commits on blur/Enter. PR flag is display-only (set by backend/assistant); not user-editable in this editor.
error_detail: Confirmed: the two seed-weight defaults are transposed. '+ EXERCISE' (line 965) seeds a new exercise's first set with weight = unit==="lbs" ? 60 : 27, where 60 lbs is about 27 kg, a consistent ~27 kg physical default in either unit. '+ SET' (line 1077) falls back to unit==="lbs" ? 27 : 60 when the previous set has no weight (ex.sets.at(-1)?.weight is undefined/0, e.g. after a bodyweight or hold-time set). Those fallback values are the inverse pairing and are not physically equivalent: 27 lbs is about 12 kg, while 60 kg is about 132 lbs. User impact: when the fallback fires, a lbs user gets a too-light 27 lbs seed (half the new-exercise default) and a kg user gets a too-heavy 60 kg seed (more than double), inconsistent with the '+ EXERCISE' default and with each other. It is minor because the fallback only fires when the last set had no weight (the common path copies the previous set's weight via ex.sets.at(-1)?.weight), and the seeded value is a fully editable pre-fill the user overwrites anyway. Purely a cosmetic/ergonomic annoyance, no data corruption.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to log distance, pace, heart rate, elevation, and power for a cardio workout so that I have a full record of my run/ride.
expected_behaviour: In edit mode shows a 'STATS' header with a km/mi unit toggle button (PATCH /api/v1/fuel/profile/ to save preference). Six stat boxes: DISTANCE (stored as km, displayed in chosen unit), PACE (free text, normalized to MM:SS on blur via normalizePace — accepts 8:34, 8'34", 8.34, 8,34, 8m34s with optional /km or /mi suffix), HEART RATE (avg bpm, integer), ELEVATION (stored as meters, displayed in m or ft), POWER (watts, integer). In read mode all boxes always render (empty = em-dash) to prevent the 'data disappeared' bug. Values commit on NumericInput blur.
inventory_notes: Unit toggle is disabled while the profile PATCH is in-flight. Pace field uses inputMode='text' (not numeric) so iOS keyboard includes colon/apostrophe characters. normalizePace pass-through for descriptive text like 'tempo'.
error_detail: Confirmed real. NumericInput seeds its internal text state from the value prop only once at mount (useState, workout-detail.tsx:187) with no sync effect and no key-based remount. When the user toggles km<->mi via the STATS unit button (line 1163), setUnit mutates FuelProfile.distance_unit; on mutation success the ["fuel-profile"] query is invalidated and refetched (queries.ts:1505-1513), so useDistanceUnit returns the new unit and CardioStatsEditor recomputes distanceDisplay/elevationDisplay via kmToDisplay/metersToDisplay (lines 1151,1154). The DISTANCE and ELEVATION input boxes receive the new value prop but keep showing the stale pre-toggle number because their internal text state never re-syncs. Two real impacts: (1) visible UX wrongness - the box shows e.g. "5.0" next to a "mi" unit label after toggling from km; (2) silent data corruption - if the user then blurs that field without re-typing, commit() parses the stale text and pushes it through the NEW unit converter (displayToKm/displayToMeters, lines 1183-1185, 1244-1246): a stored 5.0 km still shown as "5.0" commits as displayToKm(5.0,"mi")=8.045 km, silently writing an incorrect canonical distance/elevation into detail_json with no error surfaced. Non-editing display (StatBox displayValue) is correct; only the live edit input is affected.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to log rounds, work/rest intervals, peak HR, avg HR, and calories for a HIIT session so that I can track intensity over time.
expected_behaviour: In edit mode renders a StatsEditor with fields: ROUNDS (numeric), WORK (sec), REST (sec), HEART RATE · PEAK (bpm), HEART RATE · AVG (bpm), KCAL. All are NumericInput fields committing on blur. In read mode all boxes always render. Stored in detail_json as rounds, work_s, rest_s, peak_hr, avg_hr, calories.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to log skills with sets and reps or holds for a calisthenics session so that I can track progression in bodyweight movements.
expected_behaviour: In edit mode shows a 'SKILLS' section with '+ SKILL' button. Each skill has a name input and sets. MetricToggle offers only BODY (bodyweight_reps) and HOLD (hold_time) — WEIGHT is excluded (calisthenics does not allow weighted_reps). Sets are NumericInput fields for reps or hold_s. '+ SET' seeds from the last set's metric type. Trash buttons delete sets or whole skills. Stored in detail_json.skills.
inventory_notes: MetricToggle filters out 'weighted_reps' option. If a draft somehow has a weighted set (migration edge), setMetric falls back to bodyweight_reps for display.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to log named movement blocks for a mobility session so that I can record what I practiced.
expected_behaviour: In edit mode shows 'BLOCKS' tags + a text input with Enter-to-add behavior. Existing blocks render as dismissible chip tags with an ✕ button that removes them. New blocks are added via the input (Enter key), trimmed, appended to the array, and the input is cleared. In read mode shows static chip tags. Stored in detail_json.blocks as a string array. No per-block save — all changes staged until 'Save changes'.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to duplicate an existing workout so that I can quickly create a copy to plan or log a repeat session.
expected_behaviour: In read (non-editing) mode, a 'Duplicate' button renders in the action bar. Clicking it calls POST /api/v1/fuel/workouts/{id}/duplicate/. While in-flight the button shows 'Duplicating…' and is disabled. On success React Query invalidates fuel-workouts, fuel-calendar, and fuel-schedule caches. The drawer does not close or navigate to the new workout — the user must find it in the schedule/calendar.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to save an existing workout as a reusable template so that I can quickly recreate it later.
expected_behaviour: In read mode, a 'Save as template' button renders. Clicking it triggers window.prompt('Template name:'). If user enters a non-empty name, POST /api/v1/fuel/templates/ is called with the workout's category, activity, duration_minutes, and detail_json. On cancel/empty the mutation is not called. No visible feedback beyond the prompt closing (React Query invalidates template cache for next open of NewWorkoutDialog).
inventory_notes: Name input is via native window.prompt — no custom UI. If user presses Cancel in the prompt the mutation is not fired.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to delete a workout so that I can remove erroneous or unwanted entries.
expected_behaviour: A 'Delete' button in rose text is always visible in the action bar regardless of edit mode. Clicking shows window.confirm('Delete this workout?'). On confirm: calls DELETE /api/v1/fuel/workouts/{id}/, clears the autosave draft, and closes the drawer. No pending/loading state on the button (the mutation is fire-and-forget from the drawer's perspective since it closes immediately).
inventory_notes: Confirmation is via native window.confirm. Drawer closes before mutation resolves so there is no in-drawer error state for delete failures.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to browse all my completed workouts filtered by category so that I can review my training history.
expected_behaviour: Fetches GET /api/v1/fuel/workouts/?status=done&limit=200. During load shows a skeleton of 7 WorkoutRow skeletons. Once loaded, renders category filter chips at the top (ALL + each category that has at least 1 done session). Each chip shows a colored dot and count badge. Active filter chip has category-accent background. Filtering is client-side (no re-fetch). Filtered list renders as WorkoutRow buttons; clicking opens WorkoutDetail. Empty state: 'No workouts logged yet.' when filtered list is empty (or when total is 0).
inventory_notes: Hard limit of 200 workouts — no pagination. summaryChips function builds per-category compact stats (exercises+sets for strength, distance/pace/HR for cardio, etc.) shown as metadata on each row.
error_detail: Confirmed real. summaryChips() in the Fuel history list hardcodes kilometers for cardio rows and never reads the user's distance_unit preference. At history.tsx:113-114 it renders `${d.distance_km} km` and `${d.pace}/km` directly. distance_unit is a genuine, established preference (FuelProfile.distance_unit, type DistanceUnit = "km"|"mi") with a useDistanceUnit() hook and kmToDisplay() helper in use-distance-unit.ts. Both sibling components convert correctly: workout-detail-readonly.tsx CardioStatsReadOnly (lines 289-306) uses kmToDisplay(storedKm, unit) for distance and labels pace as /unit, and workout-detail.tsx (lines 1147-1187) does the same on the edit path. history.tsx imports none of these helpers (only useMemo/useState, queries, skeleton, category-meta). User impact for a mi-preference user, on every cardio history row: distance shows the raw stored km value labeled "km" (e.g. "5.0 km") instead of the converted "3.1 mi" -- both value and label wrong; pace label shows "/km" instead of "/mi". Severity is low: it is a summary chip, the canonical value is stored in km, and tapping into the workout shows correct units -- but it is a real, visible unit inconsistency for mi users. Note: pace is stored as a raw string and even the detail views only swap the pace label without numerically converting it, so the history pace defect is label-only, consistent with the rest of the app.

feature: Progress Tab — Category Selector & Progress View
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to view progress charts and statistics for each workout category so that I can see how I'm improving over time.
expected_behaviour: Renders a category chip strip (all 7 categories) defaulting to 'strength'. Clicking a chip calls GET /api/v1/fuel/progress/?category={cat}. A skeleton renders while isPending; a 'syncing…' label shows if isLoading with cached data. Routes to category-specific subview: strength (StrengthProgress), cardio (CardioProgress), hiit (HiitProgress), calisthenics (CalisProgress), mobility/sport/other (CountProgress). Each view is read-only.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see estimated 1RM trends for each lift I've logged so that I can track strength gains over time.
expected_behaviour: Data from GET /api/v1/fuel/progress/?category=strength. Lifts are sorted by most-recent session date then session count. First 4 lifts render as StrengthCards (large sparkline + delta badge). Additional lifts are collapsed behind '+ N more lifts' toggle. A toolbar appears if >4 lifts, with cards/compact view toggle and a search/filter input (visible when >8 lifts). Compact mode shows all lifts in a table-like row format with mini-sparkline. Filtering is client-side text search on lift name. Each card shows: latest estimated 1RM in user's unit, a delta badge (emerald for gain, rose for loss), and an interactive Sparkline SVG with hover tooltip (mouse + touch). Empty state: 'No strength sessions logged yet.'
inventory_notes: 1RM displayed in user's preferred unit (kg/lbs). Sparkline points are all historical 1RM estimates derived server-side. The view/compact toggle is local state.

feature: Progress Tab — Cardio Progress (Pace & Distance Sparklines)
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see pace trend and distance-per-session charts for my cardio sessions so that I can track my running/cycling improvement.
expected_behaviour: Data from GET /api/v1/fuel/progress/?category=cardio. Renders two cards side-by-side: PACE TREND (best pace = minimum pace seconds, formatted as M:SS; sparkline inverted so lower = higher on chart) and DISTANCE PER SESSION (total km + per-session distance sparkline). Sparklines are interactive (hover tooltip). Empty state when no pace or distance data: 'Log more sessions to see the trend.'
inventory_notes: Pace stored as seconds server-side, displayed as M:SS. Distance always shown in km in this view (not user's distance unit).

feature: Progress Tab — HIIT Progress (Peak HR & Totals)
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see peak heart rate trends and cumulative session totals for my HIIT sessions so that I can monitor intensity.
expected_behaviour: Data from GET /api/v1/fuel/progress/?category=hiit. Two cards: PEAK HR (max peak_hr value + sparkline over time) and TOTALS (session count + total minutes). Sparkline is interactive.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see rep or hold-time progression charts for each calisthenics skill so that I can track bodyweight mastery.
expected_behaviour: Data from GET /api/v1/fuel/progress/?category=calisthenics. Skills sorted by most-recent then session count. Same hero/compact/toolbar pattern as strength. Each card shows best performance (reps or sec depending on is_hold flag) and a sparkline. Empty state: 'No calisthenics sessions logged yet.' Same search + view toggle as strength when >4 skills.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see a count and list of my mobility, sport, or other sessions so that I know how consistently I've been showing up.
expected_behaviour: Data from GET /api/v1/fuel/progress/?category={mobility|sport|other}. CountProgress renders: session count in accent color, then a list of all sessions with date, activity name, and duration. Static read-only; no sparkline.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see my body weight trend over time with a sparkline and recent entry list so that I can monitor weight changes.
expected_behaviour: Fetches GET /api/v1/fuel/body-weight/ (useBodyWeightQuery). A trend card shows latest weight, the date of that reading, and a delta badge (rose=gained, emerald=lost relative to earliest entry). An interactive WeightSparkline (hover tooltip with date + value) renders when >=2 entries. Below, up to 14 recent entries list with date, weight in display unit, and per-entry edit (pencil) and delete (trash) icon buttons. Edit opens a modal dialog (EditEntryDialog) with date + weight fields and a Save/Cancel pair. Delete opens a DeleteConfirmDialog. Both dialogs call PATCH or DELETE /api/v1/fuel/body-weight/{id}/. A kg/lbs UnitToggle in the header updates the display unit locally (also saved to profile).
inventory_notes: EditEntryDialog shows a 409 conflict error message if user tries to move a date to one that already has an entry. List is capped at 14 entries (no pagination).

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see my sleep history with a trend sparkline and recent entries so that I can monitor sleep patterns.
expected_behaviour: Fetches GET /api/v1/fuel/sleep/. Trend card shows latest hours, its date, and a quality rating if set (e.g. 'quality 4/5'). Average (avg Nh) badge appears when >=2 entries. SleepSparkline (no hover, blue gradient) renders when >=2 entries. Up to 14 entries list with date, hours, quality badge (Q1-Q5), per-entry edit and delete buttons. SleepEditDialog: date, hours (decimal), and quality dot-picker (1–5, clearable). SleepDeleteDialog: confirmation with date + hours. 409 conflict on date collision handled with user-friendly message.
inventory_notes: SleepSparkline is not interactive (no hover tooltip). Quality is optional on both create and edit.

feature: Resting Heart Rate History & Sparkline (Progress Tab)
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see my resting heart rate trend and recent readings so that I can monitor cardiovascular fitness.
expected_behaviour: Fetches GET /api/v1/fuel/resting-hr/. Trend card shows latest bpm, its date, and a delta badge (emerald=decreased/improved, rose=increased). RHRSparkline (pink gradient, no hover) renders when >=2 entries. Up to 14 entries list with date, bpm, per-entry edit and delete buttons. RHREditDialog: date + bpm (integer). RHRDeleteDialog: confirmation with date + bpm. 409 conflict message for date collision.
inventory_notes: RHR delta: lower is better (emerald=decrease). Sparkline is static (no hover tooltip). List capped at 14 entries.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to switch between km and miles for cardio distance fields so that I can log in my preferred unit.
expected_behaviour: A pill toggle button (showing current unit: 'km' or 'mi') appears in the STATS header of the CardioStatsEditor when in edit mode. Clicking it calls setUnit() which PATCHes the user's profile via PATCH /api/v1/fuel/profile/ to save the preference. While the PATCH is in-flight (unitPending), the toggle is disabled (opacity-50). All distance and elevation inputs display in the selected unit; on commit, values are converted back to canonical storage units (km / meters). The unit preference is loaded from the profile via useDistanceUnit hook.
inventory_notes: Elevation converts: km unit → meters stored, mi unit → feet displayed (stored as meters). Toggle only visible during edit mode.
error_detail: Confirmed real, same root-cause class as FA-0317. NumericInput (workout-detail.tsx:172-218) seeds its internal text state once at mount via useState(value == null ? "" : String(value)) and has NO useEffect, and the distance/elevation instances (lines 1181 and 1242) have no key prop, so nothing re-syncs the text state when the value prop changes. When the user clicks the km/mi pill toggle (line 1163), setUnit PATCHes the profile; on success the fuel-profile query is invalidated and refetched, unit flips, and distanceDisplay/elevationDisplay recompute in the new unit (e.g. a stored 5 km becomes kmToDisplay(5,'mi')=3.11 mi). But CardioStatsEditor only re-renders (it does NOT remount; the workout draft detail.distance_km is untouched), so each NumericInput keeps its stale text "5". Two failures result: (1) the input visibly still shows "5" while the field is now labeled miles, contradicting the expected_behaviour that "all distance and elevation inputs display in the selected unit"; (2) if the user blurs the field without retyping, commit() parses the stale "5" and calls onCommit(5), which fires onChange({ distance_km: displayToKm(5,'mi') }). displayToKm(5,'mi') = round(5 * (1/0.621371) * 1000)/1000 = 8.047 km, so a workout the user entered as 5 km is silently re-stored as 8.047 km (elevation is corrupted the same way via displayToMeters). This is silent corruption of logged fitness data triggered by an ordinary "switch me to miles" toggle on an already-populated workout. Severity medium: it requires an existing populated distance/elevation plus a blur without retype, and the toggle is only visible in edit mode, but when those conditions hold the stored value is silently inflated with no user-visible warning.

feature: Weight Unit Toggle (Strength Stats & Body Weight)
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to switch between kg and lbs so that my weight inputs and body weight entries show in my preferred unit.
expected_behaviour: A kg/lbs UnitToggle appears in the BodyWeight component header and is also used in StrengthEditor for weight fields. Selecting a unit in BodyWeight updates the display immediately (local state via useWeightUnit hook). In StrengthEditor the unit is read from the profile. Weight values are always stored as kg; conversion happens at the display/commit boundary. The toggle in BodyWeight is a styled button group; changing unit in the strength editor requires accessing the profile preference.
inventory_notes: In BodyWeight the UnitToggle is visual-only local state; it does not persist to profile (unlike distance unit). In StrengthEditor weight unit comes from the profile.
error_detail: Real but minor UX gap. The weight-unit preference is device-local (localStorage key fuel:weight-unit) across ALL consumers: BodyWeight, StrengthEditor (workout-detail.tsx:947), progress.tsx:187, today-checkin.tsx:33, and workout-detail-readonly.tsx:125 all call the same useWeightUnit() hook. FuelProfile (types.ts:855-867) has NO weight_unit field anywhere in frontend or backend (grep returned zero hits), so the spec's expected_behaviour ('In StrengthEditor the unit is read from the profile') is NOT implemented — that part of the claim is a spec/doc inaccuracy, not a runtime defect. The substantive, genuine issue: unlike distance_unit (which IS server-persisted via use-distance-unit.ts reading profile.distance_unit and writing back through updateMutation), the weight unit has no per-account server-side preference. Runtime behavior is actually consistent and not broken: BodyWeight's toggle and StrengthEditor stay in sync via the shared localStorage value plus a 'fuel-unit-change' custom event, and weights are always stored as kg with conversion only at the display/commit boundary (kgToDisplay/displayToKg), so there is no data loss or display corruption. The only user-facing consequence is that the preference does not follow the account: a different browser/device, or clearing localStorage, silently resets to kg with no warning or sync. A user would only notice this on device switch or storage clear — a low-impact inconsistency relative to distance unit, not a functional bug.

feature: New Workout Draft Autosave & Restore (New Workout Dialog)
user_story: As a subscriber, I want my in-progress new-workout form to be saved locally so that if I navigate away mid-wizard my work isn't lost.
expected_behaviour: Once the user selects a category (step 0→1), the wizard state is autosaved to localStorage under key NEW_WORKOUT_KEY (debounced 800ms). A flush runs on pagehide / visibilitychange / dialog unmount. On next 'Log workout' open (no presetDate), if a draft exists and cat is set, a 'Unfinished workout from X ago' banner renders with 'Resume' (restores all fields to the saved step/category/date/status/activity/template state) and 'Start fresh' (clears the draft) buttons. On successful create the draft is cleared. Draft is keyed by tenantId.
inventory_notes: Draft restore only offered on generic 'Log workout' open; opens via a calendar day (presetDate) always start fresh. createdRef prevents the unmount flush from re-writing a draft after successful creation.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see a loading skeleton while Horizons data is fetched so that the page layout is stable and I'm not staring at a blank screen.
expected_behaviour: On mount, `useHorizonsQuery` fires `GET /api/v1/dashboard/horizons/` (staleTime 60s, only when `isLoggedIn()`). While `isLoading` is true the page renders `HorizonsSkeleton`: an `animate-pulse` set of 3 glass-card placeholder blocks with stub title bars and varying-width content lines. The skeleton disappears as soon as data resolves. Unauthenticated visitors are redirected to `/login` by the AppShell before the page renders.
inventory_notes: Query is disabled unless `isLoggedIn()` returns truthy. AppShell redirects unauthenticated users before reaching this page; `/horizons` is not in `publicPages`.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see an error message if Horizons data fails to load so that I know the system is unavailable rather than thinking the page is empty.
expected_behaviour: If `useHorizonsQuery` returns a non-null `error`, the page renders only the 'Horizons' heading and a `glass-card-horizons` block with `text-rose-text` reading 'Failed to load Horizons.' followed by `error.message` (if the error is an `Error` instance) or 'Please try again.' There is no retry button; the user must refresh the page.
inventory_notes: No retry affordance in the UI — relies on the user doing a full page refresh or waiting for TanStack Query's default retry logic.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see my last 30 days of engagement as a colour-coded grid so that I can quickly see where I've been active and where I've dropped off.
expected_behaviour: The `MomentumStrip` renders a responsive grid (10 cols on mobile, 15 on md, 30 on lg) of square cells — one per day — for the 30 days in `data.momentum`. Each cell is coloured bright signal (with glow) if in the current streak, dim signal (45% opacity) if active but not in streak, or low-opacity surface-elevated if inactive. A dot is overlaid on cells where `has_journal` is true and the day is active. Each cell has a `title` and `aria-label` describing date, message count, journal presence, and streak membership. The whole component has `role='img'` with a top-level aria-label summarising streak length. The streak badge above the grid shows 'N DAY STREAK' (signal styling) when streak > 0, 'No active streak' when there is some history but no current streak, or 'Start a conversation to build momentum' when there is no activity at all. A legend below keys colour to state.
inventory_notes: Streak calculation is client-side: iterates `days` right-to-left, breaking on the first inactive day. No click/interaction on individual cells — display only. Grid column count is driven by Tailwind classes `grid-cols-10 md:grid-cols-15 lg:grid-cols-30`; the 15 and 30 breakpoints require those classes to be in the Tailwind safelist.

feature: Weekly Pulse — scrollable week rows with expand
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to browse my recent weekly summaries and expand any week with a written report so that I can reflect on how past weeks went.
expected_behaviour: The `WeeklyPulse` component receives two arrays from the API response: `weekly_pulse` (assistant-synthesised pulse rows with a `WeekRating` and `top_win` string) and `weekly_documents` (journal documents keyed to a week). When `weekly_pulse.length > 0` it renders entries from that array; otherwise falls back to `weekly_documents`. If both are empty, it shows 'Your weekly reflections will appear here. The first one arrives this Monday.' Each week renders as a `WeekRow` with ISO week number badge (e.g. 'W24'), date range string, and a rating emoji (🚀 thumbs-up, 🧘 meh, 🔥 thumbs-down) or none if no rating. Older rows are progressively faded (opacity steps down by 0.12 per index, floored at 0.55). Rows with non-empty `markdown` (documents only) show a chevron and can be clicked: toggling `expanded` state in `useState`. When expanded, markdown is rendered via `MarkdownRenderer` inside a surface-elevated container. The button is `aria-expanded` tracked; rows without markdown disable the button and hide the chevron.
inventory_notes: Rating emoji mapping is fixed client-side: thumbs-up → 🚀, meh → 🧘, thumbs-down → 🔥. Expand state is local per WeekRow; no server interaction. `isoWeekNumber` is computed entirely client-side from the `week_start` ISO date string.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see all my active goals in a card grid so that I can quickly review what I'm working towards and navigate into the journal for any goal.
expected_behaviour: After data loads, `data.goals` is filtered client-side: any goal whose `preview` contains the placeholder strings '[Goal Name]' or '[Specific, measurable outcome]' is excluded as a template artifact. Remaining `realGoals` are rendered in a 1-col (md: 2-col) `GoalCard` grid inside the 'Active Goals' `HorizonsSection`. If no real goals remain, the section shows 'No goals yet. Write about your goals in your journal, and your assistant will help you track them.' Each `GoalCard` shows the goal title (extracted from `preview`'s first `###` heading if the stored title is 'goals'), a stripped-markdown preview (max 3 lines), and the `updated_at` date in short locale format. Clicking the card navigates to `/journal/goal/<slug>` (Next.js `<Link>`). On hover, an 'View in Journal →' label becomes visible (hidden on sm, opacity transition on sm+).
inventory_notes: Template-goal filtering (`[Goal Name]`, `[Specific, measurable outcome]`) happens on the frontend. `extractDisplayTitle` falls back through stored title → first `###` heading → 'Untitled Goal'. `extractGoalPreview` strips markdown structural lines and metadata bullet points before calling `stripMarkdown`.
error_detail: Confirmed real. GoalCard renders an unconditional link to /journal/goal/<slug> (frontend/components/goal-card.tsx:55). Slugs are typed:<uuid> for migrated goals (apps/dashboard/views.py:222) or a legacy Document slug. No /journal/goal/[slug] route exists: frontend/app/journal/ contains only memory/, today/, templates/, layout.tsx and page.tsx (no goal/ subdirectory), and there is NO dynamic-segment route anywhere in app/ (a find for bracketed dirs returns nothing). The app is a static export (output:'export', no trailingSlash, no not-found.tsx), so the route is never generated. GoalCard is the sole entry point for the Active Goals section on Horizons (app/horizons/page.tsx:103), rendered for every goal, with a 'View in Journal' CTA. Clicking it never opens the goal; the entire Active Goals list is a dead end. CAVEAT on the report wording: the literal 'Next.js 404' claim is imprecise. Azure Static Web Apps navigationFallback (frontend/public/staticwebapp.config.json:22-24) rewrites the unmatched path to /index.html, so the user is bounced onto the SPA-bootstrapped landing/index page in an odd shell (usePathname becomes /journal/goal/...) rather than a clean 404 page. The failure surface differs from a literal 404, but the user-facing defect is real and equivalent in impact: the click never shows the goal.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to confirm that an assistant observation is accurate so that my assistant keeps that pattern in its model of me.
expected_behaviour: Each open `HorizonsAssistantInsight` is rendered as an `InsightCard` under the 'What I remember' section (only shown when `data.assistant_insights.length > 0`). The card shows pillar (and `topic_display_name` if present), the `statement` text, a 'New observation' (sky) badge, and the `created_at` date. Clicking 'Looks right' button immediately sets local `optimisticStatus` to `'confirmed'` via `useState`, changing the card's left-border and badge to emerald ('Confirmed') and hiding the action buttons. The `useConfirmInsightMutation` fires `POST /api/v1/insights/insights/<id>/confirm/` with no body. On success the ['horizons'] query is invalidated and the card shows 'Confirmed <date>' in the footer. On API error, `optimisticStatus` is reset to null and the buttons reappear. Both buttons are disabled (`disabled={isPending}`) while any mutation is in-flight.
inventory_notes: Optimistic update is local state only — no TanStack Query cache write. On error the optimistic state is cleared. The API also accepts an optional `note` string body, but the UI does not expose a note input field — it always calls with no body.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to mark an assistant observation as incorrect so that my assistant corrects its model and stops acting on a false assumption.
expected_behaviour: Clicking 'Not quite' on an open `InsightCard` immediately sets local `optimisticStatus` to `'refuted'`, flipping the border and badge to rose ('Refuted') and hiding action buttons. `useRefuteInsightMutation` fires `POST /api/v1/insights/insights/<id>/refute/` with no body. On success the ['horizons'] query is invalidated. On API error, `optimisticStatus` is reset to null. Both buttons are disabled while any mutation is in-flight. Refuted cards display no confirm-date in the footer (the code guards `status === 'confirmed' && insight.last_confirmed_at`).
inventory_notes: Same optimistic/rollback pattern as confirm. API accepts optional `note` body but the UI never sends it. Expired insights (status='expired') also render with a grey badge and no action buttons, following the same `isResolved` guard.

feature: Topics I've learned — voice register meta-state display
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see which topics my assistant has learned about and what communication style adjustments I've requested so that I understand why my assistant talks the way it does.
expected_behaviour: `TopicSignalsSection` receives `data.topic_signals` (may be `null`/empty). If the array is empty or null the section renders nothing at all. For each `HorizonsTopicSignal` a `TopicRow` is rendered showing: the `topic_display_name` heading, the `pillar` in monospace uppercase, a data summary ('Nw of data · confirmed N, corrected N' or 'no data yet · nothing confirmed yet'). If `has_goal` is true a 'Linked goal' pill (signal colours) is shown. If `register_offset !== 0` an `OverrideBadge` pill is rendered showing either '+1 on this topic/pillar — you asked me to be more direct' or '-1 on this topic/pillar — you asked me to ease off', using `register_scope` to say 'topic' or 'pillar'. Section subtitle reads 'The meta-state behind my voice. To shift it, tell me — e.g. 'just be direct about dining' or 'ease off subscriptions'.' This section is read-only; there are no interactive controls.
inventory_notes: The voice-register adjustment itself is made by talking to the assistant in Telegram/LINE, not via this UI. This section is purely a read-only audit trail of what the assistant has learned.

feature: Pending goal extraction — accept as goal or task
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to approve AI-extracted goals or tasks from my journal so that they are formally added to my tracked goals or task list.
expected_behaviour: The 'Suggestions from your journal' section is only rendered when `data.pending_extractions.length > 0`. Each `HorizonsPendingExtraction` renders a `PendingGoal` card with either an 'AI Extraction' badge (accent colours, for kind='goal') or 'Pattern Found' badge (signal colours, for kind='task'), showing the extracted quote, confidence level, and source date. Clicking 'Accept Goal' or 'Accept Task' fires `useApproveExtractionMutation` → `POST /api/v1/journal/extractions/<id>/approve/`. While pending the button text changes to 'Saving...' and both buttons are disabled. On success, local `resolved` state is set to 'approved' and the card body is replaced with 'Added to your goals!' or 'Added to your tasks!' (signal green). The ['horizons'] query is also invalidated. Clicking 'Dismiss' fires `useDismissExtractionMutation` → `POST /api/v1/journal/extractions/<id>/dismiss/`. On success, `resolved` is set to 'dismissed' and the card unmounts entirely (returns null).
inventory_notes: Both mutations invalidate the ['horizons'] query, but the approved/dismissed state is also reflected immediately via local component state so there is no flash between cache invalidation and re-render. There is no undo. Error states from the mutations are not shown to the user — the card stays in its action state silently on failure.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see a segmented bar representing AI model capability level so that I understand the intelligence tier of the model serving me.
expected_behaviour: `IntelligenceMeter` is a pure display component (no API call of its own). It accepts a `level` (either a single number 1–10 or a `[lo, hi]` tuple), an optional `label`, and a `compact` boolean. In full mode it renders a labelled 10-bar segmented meter with bars coloured: emerald (fill ≥ 9), sky (≥ 7), amber (≥ 5), rose (< 5). Unfilled bars are `bg-border-strong`. Bars are `flex-1 h-1.5 rounded-sm`. In compact mode it renders a single-line inline row of 10 thin bars (`h-2.5 w-1`) beside the display string (e.g. '6-7/10'). The range tuple displays 'lo-hi/10'; a single number displays 'N/10'. The exported `TIER_INTELLIGENCE` map currently maps only 'starter' → [6, 7].
inventory_notes: No data-fetch dependency — purely a presentational utility. Used wherever a tier or model capability level needs to be visualised. The only current tier mapping is 'starter': [6, 7].

feature: Gravity insights strip — cross-surface open insights teaser
user_story: As a subscriber viewing the Gravity (finance) tab, I want to see a preview of the most recent open Gravity-pillar assistant observations so that I know there are things to review without having to visit Horizons first.
expected_behaviour: `GravityInsightsStrip` re-uses the same `useHorizonsQuery` cache (no extra fetch). It filters `data.assistant_insights` to those where `pillar === 'gravity'` and `status === 'open'`, taking the first 3. If none exist the component returns null and renders nothing. Otherwise it renders a `glass-card-horizons` strip with a 'Your assistant noticed' header, a count label ('N new observation(s) waiting for your confirmation'), a bulleted list of up to 3 insight statements, and a 'View in Horizons →' link (`href='/horizons'`). On desktop (sm+) the link is in the header row; on mobile a second link renders below the list. There are no confirm/dismiss actions here — the strip is read-only.
inventory_notes: Shares the TanStack Query 'horizons' cache with the Horizons page so no duplicate fetch occurs if both are active. Rendered on the Gravity/finance tab, not on /horizons itself. Mobile vs desktop link duplication is purely cosmetic with `sm:hidden`/`sm:inline-flex` classes.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want sections of the Horizons page to fade/slide in sequentially so that the page feels alive and draws my eye across the content.
expected_behaviour: Each `HorizonsSection` wrapper (and the 'Suggestions from your journal' div) has the `animate-reveal` CSS class and a `style={{ animationDelay: '<N>ms' }}` prop. Delays are staggered: Momentum at 100ms, Weekly Pulse at 200ms, Active Goals at 350ms, What I Remember at 425ms, Topics I've Learned at 450ms, Suggestions at 500ms. The animation behaviour is defined by the `animate-reveal` Tailwind/CSS class in `globals.css`. No user interaction; purely cosmetic.
inventory_notes: No `prefers-reduced-motion` guard is applied in the component code — this is a potential a11y gap. The `animate-reveal` class itself may respect reduced-motion if defined that way in globals.css.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want old /integrations bookmarks to still work so that I am not left on a dead page.
expected_behaviour: Visiting /integrations immediately calls router.replace() to /settings/integrations, preserving all query-string parameters. No content is rendered — the component returns null. Wrapped in Suspense with a null fallback so useSearchParams() does not block the static export.
inventory_notes: Pure client-side redirect; no API call made.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see a placeholder while the integrations data loads so that the page does not flash empty content.
expected_behaviour: While useIntegrationsQuery is fetching, the page renders SectionCardSkeleton with lines=4. The skeleton is also shown by the outer Suspense boundary while the async import resolves. Once data arrives the real content replaces the skeleton.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want a confirmation message after completing an OAuth flow so that I know the connection succeeded.
expected_behaviour: If the URL query string contains ?connected=<provider> when the page mounts, an emerald-tinted banner is displayed inside the SectionCard reading 'Successfully connected <provider>.' The banner is purely display — no further API call is made. The query param is read via useSearchParams() inside IntegrationsContent.
inventory_notes: The ?connected= param is written by the backend OAuth callback redirect.

feature: OAuth error banner after failed redirect-back
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see what went wrong if an OAuth connection failed so that I know whether to retry.
expected_behaviour: If the URL contains ?error=<message> when the page mounts, a rose-tinted error banner is shown reading 'OAuth error: <message>'. No retry mechanism is on this banner; the user may press the Connect button again.
inventory_notes: Error text is taken verbatim from the query param — it comes from the backend callback view.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to connect my Telegram account so that I can chat with my AI assistant through the Telegram app.
expected_behaviour: TelegramCard shows a StatusPill('pending') and a 'Connect' button when status.linked is false. Clicking calls generateTelegramLink() → POST /api/v1/tenants/telegram/generate-link/. While pending the button label changes to 'Generating...' and is disabled. On success the response (qr_code data-URL and deep_link) is stored in local state; a QR image and an 'Open in Telegram' link (target=_blank) are shown. The useTelegramStatusQuery polls at 3 s (pairingActive=true) while the QR is visible and at 15 s otherwise; it stops polling once linked=true. Errors from the mutation are silently suppressed (caught, no UI).
inventory_notes: There is no expiry indicator on the Telegram QR. Once QR is shown there is no way to regenerate it via UI — the existing linkData stays. Errors from the mutation are caught and discarded with no error UI.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see my connected Telegram username so that I can confirm the right account is linked.
expected_behaviour: When useTelegramStatusQuery returns linked=true, TelegramCard shows StatusPill('active') and the text 'Connected as @<telegram_username>' (or 'Connected' if no username). An Unlink button is available.
inventory_notes: Polling stops once linked=true (refetchInterval returns false).

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to unlink my Telegram account so that the bot can no longer message me.
expected_behaviour: Clicking 'Unlink' calls unlinkMutation.mutate() → POST /api/v1/tenants/telegram/unlink/. An optimistic update sets linked=false and clears telegram_username in the query cache immediately. The button shows 'Unlinking...' and is disabled while pending.
