On success the cache is invalidated and the ['me'] query is also invalidated. On error the cache is rolled back to the previous state. No explicit error UI is shown in TelegramCard beyond the rollback.
inventory_notes: Optimistic update means the UI flips to 'Not connected' before the server confirms.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to connect my LINE account so that I can chat with my AI assistant through the LINE app.
expected_behaviour: LineCard shows a 'Connect LINE' button (LINE-green border) when status.linked is false. Clicking calls generateLineLink() → POST /api/v1/tenants/line/generate-link/. While pending the button is disabled with label 'Generating...'. On success a QR image, a step-by-step instruction box, an 'Open in LINE' link, and a '15 minutes' expiry note are shown. The useLineStatusQuery polls at 3 s while QR is visible, 15 s otherwise, stops once linked=true. Errors are silently suppressed.
inventory_notes: The 15-minute expiry notice is a static label; the frontend does NOT enforce or count down the expiry. Link expiry discovered only when the user tries to scan after 15 min.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see my connected LINE display name so that I can confirm the right LINE account is linked.
expected_behaviour: When useLineStatusQuery returns linked=true, LineCard shows StatusPill('active') and the text 'Connected as <line_display_name>' (or 'Connected' if no display name). An Unlink button is shown.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to unlink my LINE account so that the bot can no longer message me via LINE.
expected_behaviour: Clicking 'Unlink' in LineCard calls unlinkMutation.mutate() → POST /api/v1/tenants/line/unlink/. Optimistic update sets linked=false and clears line_display_name. Button shows 'Unlinking...' while pending. On success ['line-status'] and ['me'] caches are invalidated; on error the previous state is restored. No explicit error message is rendered.
inventory_notes: Same optimistic-update pattern as Telegram unlink.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to toggle the Gravity finance module on or off so that my assistant gains or loses access to budget and debt-tracking tools.
expected_behaviour: GravityCard renders only when tenant.gravity_available !== false. It shows an accessible toggle switch (role=switch, aria-checked) reflecting tenant.finance_enabled from useTenantQuery. Clicking calls updateFinanceSettings() → PATCH /api/v1/finance/settings/ with {finance_enabled: !current}. The tenant cache is optimistically updated immediately. If response.restart_required is true, restartFinanceAssistant() → POST /api/v1/finance/restart/ is called automatically and an amber 'Configuring your assistant...' status message appears. On any error a rose-tinted error message appears inline ('Couldn't update Gravity. Please try again.' or a save-but-restart-failed message). The toggle is disabled while busy.
inventory_notes: GravityCard is hidden entirely when tenant.gravity_available === false (platform-wide privacy pause). The restart is triggered automatically — user has no separate 'Restart' button.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to toggle the Fuel fitness module on or off so that my assistant gains or loses access to workout tracking tools.
expected_behaviour: FuelCard shows a toggle switch reflecting tenant.fuel_enabled. Clicking calls updateFuelSettings() → PATCH /api/v1/fuel/settings/ with {fuel_enabled: !current}. Optimistic update applied to tenant cache. If restart_required the app calls restartFuelAssistant() → POST /api/v1/fuel/restart/ and shows amber status. Errors show rose-tinted inline messages. When enabled and not restarting, FuelProfileStatus renders a text summary of the user's profile (onboarding_status, fitness_level, goals count, days_per_week) fetched by useFuelProfileQuery → GET /api/v1/fuel/profile/ (only runs when fuel_enabled is true).
inventory_notes: useFuelProfileQuery is conditional on fuel_enabled — if toggled off, the profile fetch stops. FuelProfileStatus renders four different text variants based on onboarding_status: pending / in_progress / completed / declined.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to toggle the Core mindfulness module on or off so that my assistant can or cannot generate guided meditations.
expected_behaviour: CoreCard shows a toggle switch reflecting tenant.core_enabled. Clicking calls updateCoreSettings() → PATCH /api/v1/core/settings/ with {core_enabled: !current}. Optimistic update applied to tenant cache. If restart_required the app calls restartCoreAssistant() → POST /api/v1/core/restart/ and shows amber status. When enabled and not restarting, a hint line 'Open the Core tab and press the orb whenever you want a sit.' is displayed. Errors show inline rose-tinted messages.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to connect my Google account so that my assistant can access Gmail, Calendar, Drive, and Tasks.
expected_behaviour: The Google integration card shows StatusPill depending on data from useIntegrationsQuery (GET /api/v1/integrations/): 'active' if status=active, 'error' if status=revoked/error, 'pending' if not connected. When not active a 'Connect' button appears (or 'Reconnect' if revoked/error). Clicking calls getOAuthAuthorizeUrl('google') → GET /api/v1/integrations/authorize/google/, which returns {url}. The page then does window.location.assign(url) to begin the OAuth redirect. While the redirect is pending the button shows 'Redirecting...' and all connect buttons are disabled. On error a rose-tinted message appears below the cards. If already connected but not active, a 'Disconnect' button is also shown (see disconnect feature).
inventory_notes: Only one provider can be in the 'Redirecting...' state at a time (connectingProvider state). The provider_email field is shown as the description when active.
error_detail: Confirmed inconsistency for the 'expired' integration status. The backend sets status='expired' in four places (tasks.py:58 missing refresh token, tasks.py:79 refresh HTTP 400/401, services.py:527 missing refresh token, services.py:545 refresh HTTP 400/401). The frontend's isRevoked flag (page.tsx:587) only matches 'revoked'/'error', so an 'expired' record is isActive=false, isRevoked=false, isConnected=true. Result: the StatusPill correctly shows 'expired' in amber (status-pill.tsx:23), but the description falls through to the provider's generic blurb (for Google: 'Gmail, Calendar, Drive & Tasks', NOT the cited literal 'Not connected yet.' — that literal only renders when a provider has no description) and the action button reads 'Connect' instead of 'Reconnect' (page.tsx:629-631). So the badge says 'this lapsed' while the description and button frame it as a brand-new connection — a genuine user-visible inconsistency. Impact is real but minor: clicking 'Connect' triggers the same OAuth flow 'Reconnect' would (handleConnect), so the user can still recover; only the framing is misleading. Cosmetic/copy-level, not a logic or data defect — medium overstates it; low is accurate.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to connect my Reddit account so that my assistant can browse my feeds and subreddits.
expected_behaviour: Identical flow to Google connect but for the reddit provider. Card shows 'Browse your feeds and subreddits without the doom-scroll.' as description when not connected. Clicking calls GET /api/v1/integrations/authorize/reddit/ → window.location.assign(url). Status badge and description update based on integration record from the integrations list.
error_detail: Confirmed: the integrations settings page treats status="expired" inconsistently for any provider, including Reddit. The backend genuinely produces this status — refresh_expiring_integrations_task sets Integration.Status.EXPIRED when no refresh_token is found (tasks.py:58) and on token-refresh HTTP 400/401 (tasks.py:79), and "expired" is a declared model choice (models.py:24). On the frontend (page.tsx:587), isRevoked only matches "revoked" or "error" — it does NOT include "expired". So when a Reddit (or Google/Sautai) integration is "expired": isActive=false and isRevoked=false, so the description falls through to provider.description ("Browse your feeds and subreddits without the doom-scroll.") as if never connected (lines 591-595), and the CTA button reads "Connect" instead of "Reconnect" (lines 629-631). Meanwhile badgeStatus falls into the isConnected branch and renders the raw status "expired" (lines 602-603), which StatusPill displays as an amber "expired" pill (status-pill.tsx:23). Net user impact: the status badge says "expired" while the body copy and button imply the integration was never set up — a mild but genuine mismatch that mis-signals connection state and the required action. Impact is limited because the badge still surfaces "expired", so the user is not left fully unaware something is wrong; the inconsistency is confined to the description text and button label. Same root-cause class as FA-0362.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to disconnect an OAuth provider so that my assistant loses access to that service.
expected_behaviour: A 'Disconnect' button is shown for any provider that has an integration record (isConnected=true), regardless of active/revoked state. Clicking calls disconnectIntegration(integration.id) → POST /api/v1/integrations/<id>/disconnect/. An optimistic update immediately removes the integration from the cache (the card reverts to 'Not connected'). On error the previous state is restored. On settled, the integrations cache is invalidated. The button shows 'Disconnecting...' and is disabled while the mutation is pending.
inventory_notes: Optimistic removal means the UI shows 'Not connected' before server confirms. No confirm dialog — single click disconnects immediately.
error_detail: Confirmed but narrow. useDisconnectIntegrationMutation() is instantiated once (page.tsx:511) and reused inside providers.map(), so its disconnect.isPending is a single global boolean. When the user clicks Disconnect on one connected OAuth provider, every OTHER connected provider's Disconnect button is simultaneously disabled and relabelled 'Disconnecting...' (lines 634-643). The provider you actually clicked does NOT show this state: the mutation's onMutate optimistically removes that integration from the ['integrations'] cache (queries.ts:448-450), and because the Disconnect button is gated on isConnected = Boolean(integration) (page.tsx:588,634), the clicked card instantly re-renders to its 'Connect' state. So the only visible artifact is that the unrelated, still-connected card briefly flickers to 'Disconnecting...' and is disabled for the duration of the network round-trip. There are only two OAuth providers (google, reddit at page.tsx:30-41), so this requires both connected at once and is a brief transient flash on a fast API call. Real but cosmetic and low-impact; the spurious disable also harmlessly prevents concurrent disconnects.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see a meaningful error if integrations fail to load so that I know to retry.
expected_behaviour: If useIntegrationsQuery returns an error, a rose-tinted message 'Could not fetch integrations. Please refresh and try again.' is shown inside the SectionCard above the provider cards. The Telegram, LINE, Gravity, Fuel, and Core cards are each wrapped in their own ErrorBoundary; a card-level failure shows a per-card rose message without collapsing the others.
inventory_notes: Per-card ErrorBoundary isolation means one card crashing does not prevent others from rendering.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see when a configuration change is queued for my assistant so that I understand why changes may not be immediate.
expected_behaviour: PendingConfigChip is rendered at the top of the integrations SectionCard. It reads from the tenant query and shows a chip when tenant.pending_config is truthy. This is a shared component used across settings pages.

feature: Connected Apps page skeleton / loading state
user_story: As a subscriber, I want the Connected Apps page to show a loading skeleton so that the layout does not jump when tokens load.
expected_behaviour: While usePATsQuery is loading (GET /api/v1/auth/tokens/), ConnectedAppsContent renders SectionCardSkeleton with lines=4. The outer Suspense boundary also shows the same skeleton. An ErrorBoundary wraps the content; if an unrecoverable error occurs, a rose-tinted message is shown.

user_story: As a subscriber with no connected apps, I want to see an explanation and a prompt to connect so that I know what this section is for.
expected_behaviour: When usePATsQuery returns an empty array, an EmptyState component is rendered with a dashed-border card. It explains that apps like YardTalk can push work-session context and includes a 'Connect an app' button. Clicking that button opens the ConnectAppModal.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see all my active tokens so that I can audit which apps have access to my account.
expected_behaviour: When tokens exist, ConnectedAppsContent renders a count line ('N token(s)'), a 'Connect an app' button in the header, and a list of TokenRow cards sorted most-recently-created first. Each TokenRow shows: token name, expired StatusPill if expires_at is past, truncated token prefix (pat_<prefix>…), scope chips, last-used / expires / created relative timestamps, and a 'Revoke' button. If a token has never been used and is more than 5 minutes old, a warning banner reads 'Not used yet. If you didn't save the token, revoke this one and create a new one.'
inventory_notes: The 'likely lost' warning fires after 5 min of non-use since creation — a heuristic that may false-positive for users who copy the token but haven't yet configured the app.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see which permissions each token grants so that I can understand what the connected app can do.
expected_behaviour: Each token's scopes array is rendered as colored chip badges. 'sessions:write' is shown with an emerald chip; 'sessions:read' with a sky-blue chip. Chips are displayed inline below the token name and prefix.

feature: Connect app modal — preset selection step
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to choose whether I am connecting YardTalk or a custom app so that the token is pre-configured with the right defaults.
expected_behaviour: Opening ConnectAppModal (via 'Connect an app' button) shows step='preset' with two option cards: 'YardTalk' (macOS app, sessions:write scope pre-selected) and 'Custom' (any app/script). Clicking either card advances to step='form' and sets the label/scope defaults accordingly. A Cancel button closes the modal. Escape key also closes it. On open, focus is moved to the first interactive element in the modal.
inventory_notes: Modal closes on Escape or backdrop click. State is reset 150 ms after close (deferred so close animation can use the prior state).

feature: Connect app modal — token configuration form step
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to configure a token label, scopes, and expiry before creating it so that I have control over what the token can do.
expected_behaviour: Step='form' shows a label input (maxLength=255, pre-filled with 'YardTalk' for that preset or empty for custom), a scope checkboxes section only for the custom preset (sessions:write / sessions:read), and an expiry dropdown (Never / 30 days / 90 days / 1 year). The 'Create token' button is disabled while mint.isPending, while the label is empty, or while custom preset has zero scopes selected. Clicking 'Create token' calls mintPAT() → POST /api/v1/auth/tokens/create/ with {name, scopes, expires_in_days?}. On success, step advances to 'reveal'. On 429 a rate-limit message is shown; on 400 a validation message is shown; otherwise a generic error is shown. A 'Back' button returns to the preset step.
inventory_notes: YardTalk preset locks scopes to sessions:write only (no scope checkboxes shown). The name fallback when empty is the preset string ('yardtalk' or 'custom') — not a user-visible label.

feature: Connect app modal — token reveal step
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see and copy the raw token immediately after creation so that I can paste it into my app before it is hidden forever.
expected_behaviour: Step='reveal' shows a read-only input with the full token value, a 'Copy' button, and setup instructions. The input auto-selects on focus. Clicking 'Copy' calls navigator.clipboard.writeText(). On clipboard success, the button label changes to 'Copied ✓' with an emerald style for 2.5 s, then resets. On clipboard failure, a rose error message explains how to copy manually. For the YardTalk preset, numbered instructions are shown (Preferences → NU → paste token). For custom, a curl example is shown. An 'I've saved it' button closes the modal. The token is not stored client-side after close.
inventory_notes: The PATCreateResponse.warning field is returned from the API but not displayed in the UI. The token can only be read from this reveal step — it is never shown again.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want a confirmation step before revoking a token so that I do not accidentally disconnect an app.
expected_behaviour: Clicking 'Revoke' on a TokenRow opens RevokeConfirmModal with the token name shown ('X will stop working immediately. Any app using it will need a new token.'). A 'Revoke' button calls revokePAT(pat.id) → DELETE /api/v1/auth/tokens/<id>/revoke/ (via the revokePAT function). An optimistic update removes the token from the ['pats'] cache immediately. The button shows 'Revoking...' and is disabled while pending. On success, the modal closes and the cache is invalidated. On error, the previous state is restored and a rose error message is shown inside the modal. Cancel closes without action. Escape key also closes.
inventory_notes: The modal is keyed by revoking?.id so it fully resets if a different token is chosen without closing.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see an error message if my tokens fail to load so that I know to try refreshing.
expected_behaviour: If usePATsQuery returns an error, a rose-tinted message 'Could not fetch tokens. Please refresh and try again.' is shown above the empty-state or token list. The outer ErrorBoundary also catches React rendering errors and shows 'Could not load Connected Apps. Please refresh and try again.' as a fallback.

user_story: As a subscriber opening the iOS app for the first time, I want the web sign-in to hand back an auth code to the app so that the app can log me in without a separate password flow.
expected_behaviour: The iOS app opens /app/authorize?response_type=code&client=ios&code_challenge=<S256>&state=<nonce>&redirect_uri=nbhd://auth/callback&intent=register|signin in an ASWebAuthenticationSession. AppAuthorizePage validates params (must be response_type=code, client=ios, method=S256, redirect_uri in ALLOWED_REDIRECT_URIS); invalid params immediately redirect to nbhd://auth/callback?error=invalid_request. Valid params are stashed in sessionStorage. If already logged in, the page POSTs to /api/v1/auth/authorize/ with the PKCE challenge and redirects to nbhd://auth/callback?code=<code>&state=<state>. If not logged in, it routes to /signup (intent=register) or /login (intent=signin) and the auth UI completes the flow before bouncing back. On any server error the app receives nbhd://auth/callback?error=server_error. The page displays a status message ('Connecting your account…', 'Finishing sign-in…', or 'Redirecting you to sign in…') as aria-live text.
inventory_notes: sessionStorage is used to carry params across the login/signup round-trip; private mode or quota failure breaks the handoff silently (the authorize page will fail to read the stash). The StrictMode double-invoke guard (ran.current) prevents the effect from firing twice in dev.

feature: Journal page initial render and hash-based routing
user_story: As a subscriber, I want the journal to open directly to today's daily note so that I can start writing immediately without extra navigation.
expected_behaviour: On mount, JournalPage reads window.location.hash (format: #kind/slug). If no hash is present, activeKind defaults to 'daily' and activeSlug defaults to todayISO() (YYYY-MM-DD computed client-side). Navigating between documents updates the hash so the URL is bookmarkable and the browser back/forward button reflects the active document. No loading state is shown by the outer page shell; the DocumentView handles per-document loading. The sidebar and document area are rendered side-by-side on desktop (lg breakpoint); only the document area is visible on mobile until the sidebar is opened.
dependencies: GET /api/v1/journal/tree/ (sidebar tree), GET /api/v1/journal/documents/{kind}/{slug}/ (prefetch on sidebar hover)
inventory_notes: Hash parse is done in useState initializer with window check for SSR safety. viewKey is incremented on every navigate() call, which force-remounts DocumentView and resets editor state even when navigating to the same kind+slug.
error_detail: Confirmed. handleNavigate (page.tsx:55-61) unconditionally calls setViewKey((k) => k + 1), and the content wrapper keys DocumentView on activeKind-activeSlug-viewKey (page.tsx:123). Because viewKey is part of the key, navigating to the already-active kind+slug still force-remounts DocumentView, resetting its local editing/draft state, which closes the editor and discards any unsaved draft. Crucially, DocumentView has NO desktop autosave: the autosave effect is gated by if(!(editing && isMobile === true)) return (document-view.tsx:232-233), so desktop drafts persist only via explicit Save/Cmd+S. The markdown editor's unmount flush (markdown-editor.tsx:272-279) only pushes pending keystrokes into the parent draft state via onChange; it never triggers a save mutation, so on a viewKey remount the flushed draft is thrown away with the rest of the state. Reachable triggers: a desktop subscriber editing today's Daily who clicks the (active-highlighted) Daily primary-nav button, or the New Entry button, both of which fire onNavigate('daily', todaySlug()) to the same destination (sidebar.tsx:252 and 272). The in-progress draft is silently lost with no confirmation. Severity is low: it requires clicking the already-active destination mid-edit, the lost text is re-typable, and the specific mobile case the claim cites is actually NOT reachable (the full-screen editing portal at z-[9999] covers the sidebar, and the mobile path autosaves anyway), so real exposure is desktop-only. The mechanism and the data-loss outcome are nonetheless verified.

user_story: As a subscriber on a mobile device, I want to access the sidebar without losing my document view so that I can navigate between notes on a small screen.
expected_behaviour: On mobile (< lg / 1023 px), a hamburger-style button in DocumentHeader opens a full-screen overlay sidebar with a translucent backdrop and slide-in animation (translate-x-0 / -translate-x-full, 300ms ease-out). Clicking the backdrop or using the X button inside the drawer closes it. The overlay sets aria-hidden when closed; it renders its own Sidebar instance without the collapsed/onToggle props. Navigating to a document calls setMobileSidebarOpen(false) to close the drawer automatically.
inventory_notes: The mobile sidebar does not receive the collapsed or onToggle props; those only apply to the desktop sidebar. Two Sidebar instances are rendered simultaneously (desktop hidden with lg:hidden, mobile with fixed overlay).

user_story: As a subscriber on desktop, I want to collapse the sidebar to gain more writing space so that I can focus on the document.
expected_behaviour: On desktop, a chevron button in the sidebar header toggles sidebarCollapsed state in JournalPage. When collapsed, the sidebar renders a minimal 48px-wide column with only an expand chevron button (aria-label='Expand sidebar'). When expanded, it renders the full 240px nav including header, new-entry button, primary nav, and scrollable sections. The toggle button has 44x44px minimum touch target.
inventory_notes: Collapsed state is local component state — not persisted in localStorage, resets on page reload.
error_detail: Verified: the expanded-sidebar collapse button (sidebar.tsx:238) renders with min-h-[36px] min-w-[36px], 8px under both the 44x44px target stated in FA-0379's expected_behaviour and the project's WCAG 2.1 AA / 44x44 touch-target standard (CLAUDE.md Frontend Conventions). This is a genuine spec deviation. However, the 'hard to hit on touch devices' framing overstates impact: the collapse button only renders when onToggle is passed, and the ONLY render site that passes onToggle is the desktop sidebar (page.tsx:66-75), wrapped in 'hidden lg:block' (visible only at >=1024px). The mobile sidebar overlay (page.tsx:110-115) does NOT pass onToggle, so this undersized button never appears on the mobile/touch-first path; that overlay has its own correctly-sized 44px close button (page.tsx:101). The sibling expand button (sidebar.tsx:202) and the collapsed-column expand button are both correctly 44px. So the affordance is desktop-gated, non-destructive (only toggles writing space, fully reversible via a 44px control), and desktop pointer precision is high. Real but low-impact: a legitimate inconsistency with the surrounding 44px buttons and the stated spec, not a meaningful tap-failure risk on the primary touch path.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want the sidebar to show my document sections as soon as they load so that I can navigate to any note.
expected_behaviour: useSidebarTreeQuery calls GET /api/v1/journal/tree/ (staleTime 120s). While isLoading is true, the scrollable section of the sidebar shows 3 skeleton lines (stardust-skeleton). On success, userDocSections filters the tree to sections where items exist or the kind is addable (goal, project). On error, nothing is shown in the document section (only the primary nav and empty-state copy remain).
inventory_notes: Empty state ('Your universe is taking shape.') is shown when !isLoading && userDocSections.length === 0. The primary nav (Daily, Tasks, Ideas, Memory) is always visible regardless of the tree state.

feature: Primary nav — navigate to Daily, Tasks, Ideas, Memory
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to click a primary nav item to jump directly to a singleton document so that I can access my tasks or ideas without searching.
expected_behaviour: The four primary nav items (Daily → today's date slug, Tasks → 'tasks', Ideas → 'ideas', Memory → 'memory') are always shown. Clicking any item calls onNavigate(kind, slug), updating hash and loading the relevant document in DocumentView. The active item gets accent highlight and a left-glow CSS effect. On hover/focus, the document is prefetched via queryClient.prefetchQuery (queryKey: ['document', kind, slug], calling GET /api/v1/journal/documents/{kind}/{slug}/) to make navigation feel instant. Minimum touch target is 44px.
inventory_notes: Daily always resolves to todayISO() — clicking Daily always goes to today, not the previously active daily slug.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see my recent daily notes in the sidebar so that I can quickly jump back to a past day.
expected_behaviour: The JournalPage extracts dailyEntries from the sidebar tree (section where kind === 'daily') and passes them as recentEntries to Sidebar. If any entries exist, they are shown in a 'Recent' section above the user document sections. Each entry renders its title as a truncated button; clicking navigates to that daily slug. Hovering/focusing an entry prefetches the document. Active entry is highlighted with accent color. The Recent section has a bottom border separator.
inventory_notes: Recent entries are the items array from the 'daily' section in the tree response. The count and ordering depend on the backend response shape.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to collapse or expand document sections in the sidebar so that I can reduce visual clutter.
expected_behaviour: User-document sections (goals, projects, and any non-singleton section with items) have collapsible accordion headers. The chevron icon rotates 90° when expanded. Initially all sections are expanded (expandedSections defaults to a Set containing 'daily', 'weekly', 'tasks', 'goals', 'ideas', 'project', 'memory'). Clicking a section header toggles its kind in/out of expandedSections. The item count badge is always visible regardless of expanded state.
inventory_notes: State is local to Sidebar and lost on navigation. Sections with zero items and no addable kind are not rendered at all.

feature: Create new goal or project document
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to create a new goal or project document directly from the sidebar so that I can organise my long-form notes.
expected_behaviour: Addable kinds (goal, project) show a + button next to the section header. Clicking it reveals an inline text input (auto-focused) and an Add button. The user types a name; Enter or clicking Add calls createMutation.mutate({kind, slug: slugify(name), title: name, markdown: ''}), which POSTs to POST /api/v1/journal/documents/. While pending, the Add button shows '…' and is disabled. On success, the sidebar tree is invalidated, the form is cleared, and onNavigate is called with the new document's kind and slug. Pressing Escape cancels. Empty or whitespace-only names are rejected client-side (button disabled, Enter guard). The slug is auto-computed by lowercasing, collapsing spaces to hyphens, and stripping non-alphanumeric characters.
inventory_notes: Only 'goal' and 'project' kinds are in ADDABLE_DOC_KINDS. The 'goals' section kind maps to 'goal' document kind via SECTION_KIND_TO_DOC_KIND. The blur handler uses a 150ms setTimeout before cancelling to allow the Add button's mousedown to fire first.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

feature: Sidebar item context menu — clear and delete
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to clear or delete a document from the sidebar context menu so that I can manage my note library without opening each document.
expected_behaviour: Each document item in a section shows a kebab-style 'more' (•••) icon. On desktop it is only visible on group hover; on touch it is always visible. Clicking opens a dropdown menu (positioned absolute, top-full of the item row). The menu contains Clear (for clearable kinds: daily, tasks, ideas, memory) and Delete. Both trigger a ConfirmDialog before executing. Clear fires useClearDocumentMutation (POST /api/v1/journal/documents/{kind}/{slug}/clear/), invalidating the document and sidebar-tree queries. Delete fires useDeleteDocumentMutation (DELETE /api/v1/journal/documents/{kind}/{slug}/), removing the query from cache and invalidating documents and sidebar-tree. If the deleted document was active, navigation falls back to today's daily note. Clicking outside the menu closes it via a mousedown document listener.
inventory_notes: The ConfirmDialog shows 'This cannot be undone. The entry will be permanently removed.' for delete and 'This will erase all content but keep the entry itself.' for clear. Only one menuRef is tracked; opening a new menu does not automatically close a previously open one unless the user clicks outside. The menu is rendered inside the item's relative-positioned container with z-20.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want a confirmation prompt before I permanently delete or clear a document so that I don't lose content by mistake.
expected_behaviour: ConfirmDialog renders as a modal with a semi-transparent blurred overlay. On mobile it slides up from the bottom (bottom-sheet-enter animation); on md+ it centers as a card (max-w-sm). The dialog has role='dialog' aria-modal='true' aria-labelledby='confirm-title'. Clicking the backdrop (the outer div, not the card) calls onCancel. Body scroll is locked while open. Default variant is 'danger' (confirm button styled in rose). Cancel and Confirm buttons each have min-h-[44px]. No loading state is shown — the mutation fires immediately after confirm.
inventory_notes: The dialog does not handle the pending/error states of the mutation it triggers — the caller is responsible. Body overflow is restored in useEffect cleanup; if the component unmounts while the dialog is closed, overflow is still cleared.

feature: New Entry button — navigate to today
user_story: As a subscriber, I want a prominent 'New Entry' button to always take me to today's daily note so that I can start journalling quickly.
expected_behaviour: The 'New Entry' button at the top of the sidebar calls onNavigate('daily', todayISO()) regardless of the current document. The button has a gradient accent background and icon that rotates 12° on hover. It always goes to today — it does not create a blank document; the backend upserts on PATCH so any date slug is always valid.
inventory_notes: todayISO() is computed using the browser's local date (new Date()), so the 'today' date is the user's local calendar date, not UTC.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want a visual placeholder while my document is loading so that the interface doesn't feel broken.
expected_behaviour: useDocumentQuery calls GET /api/v1/journal/documents/{kind}/{slug}/ (staleTime 60s). While isLoading is true, DocumentView renders a StardustSkeleton: a heading-sized block followed by 6 content lines at staggered widths and animation delays (stardust-skeleton CSS class). On error (not 404), the skeleton is replaced with a rose-tinted error card reading 'Could not load document.' and 'Try refreshing or navigating to a different entry.' A 404 from the API returns null (caught in fetchDocument), which DocumentView treats as a missing but valid document (the PATCH upserts).
inventory_notes: A 404 is explicitly caught in fetchDocument and returned as null rather than thrown. DocumentView renders the content area as if the doc exists but is blank, allowing the editor to create it on first save.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see an invitation to write when a document is blank so that I know I can start typing.
expected_behaviour: When doc?.markdown is falsy (null, undefined, or empty string) and the view is not in editing mode, DocumentView renders the EmptyState component with an animated constellation SVG and the text 'This page is blank.' On mobile the subtitle reads 'Tap the pencil to start writing.' On desktop it reads 'Tap anywhere to start writing...' The desktop read surface also shows a hover hint ('Tap anywhere to edit') that fades in on group-hover.
inventory_notes: The empty state is only shown in read mode. If the user enters edit mode, the TipTap editor is shown with an empty document regardless of this state.

feature: Document header — date navigation for daily notes
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to navigate between daily notes by day so that I can review past entries without using the sidebar.
expected_behaviour: For daily documents, DocumentHeader shows prev/next arrow buttons and a date label. Clicking the prev chevron calls shiftDate(slug, -1) and navigates. The next chevron is disabled (opacity-30, cursor-not-allowed) when slug >= todayISO() to prevent navigating to future dates. Clicking the date label opens the browser's native date picker (an <input type='date'> absolutely positioned and opacity-0 over the label; max attribute is todayISO()). Changing the date calls onNavigate('daily', value). When the active slug is not today, a 'Today' button appears that jumps back to today. A 'Current Orbit' label with a star icon is shown above the date when isToday. After a desktop save, a 'Saved' checkmark pill appears for 2 seconds.
inventory_notes: shiftDate parses the YYYY-MM-DD slug as local time (new Date(y, m-1, d)) to avoid UTC midnight off-by-one issues. The date input's value is the current slug and is controlled — navigating to a different day re-renders the input. The responsive layout collapses the full date to a short format (formatDateShort) on small screens.
error_detail: Confirmed. The Previous-day chevron (line 99) and Next-day chevron (line 140) use min-h-[40px] min-w-[40px], and the Today button (line 153) uses min-h-[40px] — all 4px under the 44x44px touch-target minimum that CLAUDE.md/DESIGN.md mandate for WCAG 2.1 AA. These buttons sit in the always-rendered daily-note header (kind === "daily") and ARE touch targets on mobile. The codebase's own dominant pattern is 44px (140 min-h-[44px] occurrences vs 13 min-h-[40px] across frontend/components+app); the sibling sidebar-toggle button two elements up (line 82) correctly uses min-h-[44px] min-w-[44px], so the 40px values are an inconsistency rather than an intentional choice. User impact is real but marginal: a 40px square is slightly harder to tap precisely on a phone, especially given the small h-4 w-4 SVG chevrons. Note the feature's expected_behaviour does not itself state a 44px requirement — the standard comes from the project-wide accessibility convention — but that convention applies and is violated.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want clear Save and Cancel buttons when editing a document so that I can confidently commit or discard my changes.
expected_behaviour: On desktop (isMobile !== true), DocumentHeader renders an 'Edit' button when not editing, and 'Save' / 'Cancel' buttons when editing. Clicking Save calls handleSave (mutateAsync PATCH /api/v1/journal/documents/{kind}/{slug}/), which then exits editing mode and shows the 2s 'Saved' indicator. Clicking Cancel exits editing without saving, resetting draft state. While saving (updatePending), the Save button shows '...' and is disabled. The Edit button is hidden (null) for typed-managed documents (tasks/goal when typed_lifecycle is true). On mobile, the Edit button is hidden in the header — the floating pencil FAB handles that.
inventory_notes: handleSave reads the latest markdown from the live TipTap editor via latestDraft() to avoid losing trailing keystrokes that the 400ms debounce hasn't emitted yet.
error_detail: Both sub-claims verified; the material one is the silent save failure. (2) document-view.tsx:313-327 handleSave awaits updateMutation.mutateAsync with NO try/catch, and the hook (lib/queries.ts:904-914 useUpdateDocumentMutation) defines only onSuccess, no onError, with no toast/error UI anywhere in the component. If the desktop Save PATCH fails (network error, 409, 5xx), the promise rejects: the await throws, execution stops before setEditing(false)/setSavedIndicator, so the editor stays open (no data loss, which is good) but the user sees NO error AND no Saved confirmation, leaving them unsure whether the save landed. The contrast is telling: the sibling handleDone (mobile path, lines 295-311) deliberately wraps the identical mutateAsync call in try/catch with a comment about not silently losing keystrokes, so the failure path was considered for mobile but omitted for desktop handleSave (and even handleDone only swallows the error to idle without surfacing it). This is a real UX gap. (1) The desktop Save/Cancel/Edit buttons at document-header.tsx:193/200/209 do use min-h-[40px], 4px under the WCAG 2.1 AA 44px target named in CLAUDE.md/DESIGN.md, and are reachable on mobile when editing (the line-185 guard renders them when editing===true). But this is a trivial spec deviation, and the codebase uses min-h-[40px] widely as a de-facto size (document-header:99/140/153, document-view:400/445), so it is a low-priority nit, not the driver. Net: real ux defect, medium, driven by the silent save failure.

user_story: As a subscriber on desktop, I want a rich-text markdown editor so that I can format my journal entries with headings, lists, and checkboxes.
expected_behaviour: On desktop, when editing is true and isMobile is not true, DocumentView renders MarkdownEditor inside a rounded card surface. MarkdownEditor is dynamically imported (lazy, shows a skeleton loading placeholder). TipTap is initialized with StarterKit, TaskList, TaskItem (nested), and the tiptap-markdown extension (html:false, transformCopiedText/PastedText). The toolbar (Bold, Italic, Heading 2, Bullet list, Task list, Indent, Outdent) is shown above the editor, sticky at top. Cmd/Ctrl+S triggers handleSave. Tab indents list items; Shift+Tab outdents. A 400ms debounce serializes markdown and calls onChange. Cursor position is saved to localStorage under cursorKey on blur and restored on open. On unmount, any pending debounced change is flushed.
inventory_notes: The editor is loaded with dynamic() (SSR: false) so it only ships in the client bundle when editing begins. The loading placeholder is sized to min-h-[50vh] to prevent layout shift. External value changes (e.g., from a refetch) are synced into the editor only when value !== lastEmittedRef.current, preventing round-trip cycles.
error_detail: Confirmed. Task lists are a supported feature (TaskList + TaskItem nested:true; toolbar Task-list button; the feature record lists Task list and "Tab indents list items; Shift+Tab outdents" as expected). But every indent/outdent path is hardwired to listItem. Toolbar Indent/Outdent buttons call sinkListItem('listItem')/liftListItem('listItem') (markdown-editor.tsx:135,142); the Tab/Shift+Tab handler in editorProps.handleKeyDown does the same (markdown-editor.tsx:254,256). TipTap's TaskItem node name is 'taskItem' (extension-list task-item/index.cjs:141; its own keymap correctly uses 'taskItem' at lines 226,234). Core sinkListItem/liftListItem (core index.cjs:2783,1214) resolve the name to a node type and call prosemirror-schema-list, whose sinkListItem requires firstChild.type==itemType and nodeBefore.type==itemType (prosemirror-schema-list index.cjs:225,232). With a taskItem selected but 'listItem' passed, blockRange finds no matching range and the command returns false — a silent no-op. Worse, the custom handleKeyDown always calls preventDefault() and returns the failing result, so it intercepts Tab/Shift+Tab before TaskItem's own correct built-in keymap can fire, actively breaking nesting the extension would otherwise handle. User impact: inside a task list, the Indent/Outdent toolbar buttons appear active but do nothing, and Tab/Shift+Tab do nothing (and do not even insert a tab, since default is prevented). Bullet/ordered lists are unaffected because they use listItem.

user_story: As a subscriber on a mobile device, I want a full-screen editor that stays above the keyboard so that typing doesn't obscure my text.
expected_behaviour: When editing is true and isMobile is true (screen width < 1024px), DocumentView renders a React portal onto document.body: a fixed full-screen overlay (z-9999, bg-[#0B0F13]) containing a header bar, scrollable TipTap editor (hideToolbar=true), and a pinned dock with EditorToolbar and a Done button. The portal tracks window.visualViewport.height/offsetTop to resize when the on-screen keyboard opens (iOS Safari fix), preventing the dock from being hidden behind the keyboard. Body overflow is locked to 'hidden' while the portal is open. The editor has 1.5s debounced auto-save (mutates PATCH); explicit Done reads the live editor via latestDraft() to flush pending keystrokes. The Done button is disabled while saving; if save throws, the editor stays open so edits are not lost. The header shows the document title or formatted date, plus a SaveStatusIndicator ('Saving…' / checkmark 'Saved' with 2s auto-hide).
inventory_notes: The portal is created with createPortal(…, document.body) — this is only rendered when isMobile === true (not null) to avoid a hydration race. The isMobile value is computed via useSyncExternalStore with a matchMedia listener. The auto-save fires 1.5s after the editor's own 400ms debounce, so the total quiet period before a server write is approximately 1.9s.

user_story: As a subscriber on a mobile device, I want a floating pencil button to open the editor so that I can tap it easily without the document header being off-screen.
expected_behaviour: On mobile (isMobile === true) and not editing, the bottom action bar shows a pencil FAB on the right side (rounded-full, accent background, 44x44 min, aria-label='Edit note'). Tapping calls handleEdit() which sets draft to doc.markdown (or empty), lastSavedRef to that value, and editing to true, triggering the full-screen portal. The FAB is hidden if typedManaged is true (tasks/goal with typed_lifecycle on).
inventory_notes: The FAB is in the same bottom bar as the QuickLogInput (daily only). For non-daily documents on mobile, the flex-1 spacer pushes the FAB to the right.

user_story: As a subscriber on desktop, I want to click anywhere in the document body to enter edit mode so that the edit experience feels natural.
expected_behaviour: On desktop (isMobile !== true) and not editing, the read-mode content surface has cursor-text and an onClick handler that calls handleEdit(). Clicks on input[type='checkbox'] are intercepted and stopped before triggering edit mode (via target.closest check) so checkbox toggles work in read mode. On hover, a 'Tap anywhere to edit' hint fades in (opacity-0 → opacity-100 via group-hover). The hint is pointer-events-none. This behaviour is suppressed for typedManaged documents (onClick is undefined).
inventory_notes: The cursor-text and group-hover hint are only applied when not typedManaged. On mobile the surface has no onClick (undefined) — the FAB is the sole edit entry point.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want my journal markdown rendered as rich HTML in read mode so that I can easily read formatted content.
expected_behaviour: When not editing and doc.markdown is non-empty, DocumentView renders MarkdownRenderer with the document's markdown and an onCheckboxToggle handler. Clicking a rendered checkbox ([ ] or [x]) calls handleCheckboxToggle(lineIndex, checked) which line-patches the markdown string, replacing [ ] with [x] or [x] with [ ], then calls updateMutation.mutate(PATCH) without entering edit mode. onCheckboxToggle is not passed (undefined) when typedManaged is true, making checkboxes non-interactive.
inventory_notes: The checkbox toggle is an immediate mutation with no optimistic update — the query is invalidated on success, causing a refetch. There is no error state shown for a failed checkbox toggle.
error_detail: Confirmed real. In MarkdownRenderer's custom input component (markdown-renderer.tsx:30-48), the checkbox onChange handler does NOT know which checkbox was clicked. It only reads props.checked (the clicked box's current state), then linearly scans content.split by newline and fires onCheckboxToggle(i, !checked) on the FIRST line whose source checked-state equals props.checked, returning immediately. There is no use of ordinal position (e.g. the react-markdown node.position AST data, which is available, or a per-render checkbox counter) to map the DOM checkbox back to its source line. Consequence: in any document with two or more checkboxes sharing the same state (two unchecked, or two checked), clicking the 2nd/3rd/etc. always toggles the FIRST same-state one. DocumentView.handleCheckboxToggle (document-view.tsx:344-365) then line-patches that wrong line index and PATCHes it, so the wrong task is completed/uncompleted and the intended one is left untouched. The path is live for all non-typed-managed journal docs (document-view.tsx:523-525 passes handleCheckboxToggle unless typedManaged is true). Multi-item checklists in journal entries are common, so a normal subscriber hits this routinely. There is also no optimistic update, so after the wrong PATCH the refetch visibly flips the wrong checkbox — the corruption is visible but not self-correcting.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want a markdown syntax reference I can open while editing so that I don't have to leave the editor to look up formatting.
expected_behaviour: When helpOpen is true, MarkdownHelpSheet renders over the editor as a bottom sheet on mobile (max-h-[70dvh], slide-up animation) or a centered modal on sm+ (max-w-lg, max-h-[80vh]). Content includes Text Formatting, Headings, Lists, Other, and Keyboard Shortcuts sections. The modifier key shown (⌘ vs Ctrl) is detected from navigator.userAgent at mount. The sheet closes on: clicking the backdrop, clicking the X button, or pressing Escape. Opening/closing uses a two-frame animation sequence for enter (setAnimating in rAF) and a 200ms timeout before unmounting (setVisible(false)) for exit. The sheet is not directly triggered from the toolbar — the caller (DocumentView) passes onHelpToggle to MarkdownEditor which stores it but the toolbar does not render a help button (the prop is named _onHelpToggle with underscore).
inventory_notes: The onHelpToggle prop in MarkdownEditor is aliased as _onHelpToggle (prefixed underscore = unused), meaning the help sheet can only be opened from DocumentView's own setHelpOpen. The editor toolbar does not include a help button — the sheet is accessible only programmatically. This may be a dead code path in current UI.

feature: Quick log input — daily note append
user_story: As a subscriber, I want a quick text input to append a log entry to my daily note without opening the full editor so that I can capture thoughts rapidly.
expected_behaviour: QuickLogInput is shown only when kind === 'daily' and editing is false. On desktop it appears at the bottom of the document area (border-t, px-4 py-2.5). On mobile it fills the left side of the bottom action bar. Submitting a non-empty trimmed string calls handleQuickLog(content) which fires appendMutation.mutateAsync (POST /api/v1/journal/documents/{kind}/{slug}/append/ with body {content}), then on success the document and sidebar-tree queries are invalidated. The input is cleared on success. While isPending, the Log button shows '...' and is disabled. Empty input is rejected client-side (button disabled, form onSubmit guard). If the append fails, 'Failed to add entry.' is shown in rose below the input.
inventory_notes: The append endpoint adds content to the end of the document markdown server-side. There is no optimistic update — the document is refetched after success. The quick log is shown for daily notes only; other document kinds (tasks, ideas, memory, goal, project) have no append UI.

feature: Current status card — obligations, tasks, goals
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see my open tasks, active goals, and payment obligations directly on my daily journal view so that I have full context at a glance.
expected_behaviour: CurrentStatusCard is rendered above the document content only when kind === 'daily' and not editing. It calls useJournalStatusQuery (GET /api/v1/journal/status/, staleTime 60s). While loading or if data is undefined, it renders null. If data has no obligations, no open_tasks, and no active_goals, it renders null (completely hidden). Otherwise it shows a card with 'Current status · as of now'. Obligations render nickname, minimum_payment (formatted as $X.XX), due_date (short format), and a status pill (green for paid, amber for partial, rose for unpaid/overdue with 'Overdue' label override). Open tasks show a checkbox button; clicking it calls completeTaskMutation (POST /api/v1/journal/tasks/{id}/complete/) which invalidates journal-status on success. Tasks may show a due_date. Active goals show title and optional target_date.
inventory_notes: Due dates are parsed with local-time trick (new Date(y, m-1, d)) to avoid UTC midnight off-by-one. The completeTask checkbox button is disabled while isPending but shows no loading indicator beyond the disabled state. There is no undo/reopen action in this card — reopening is a separate mutation (useReopenTaskMutation) not used here.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

user_story: As a subscriber, I want tasks and goal documents managed by the assistant to be read-only in the markdown editor so that I don't accidentally corrupt typed records.
expected_behaviour: When kind is 'tasks' or 'goal' AND journalStatus.typed_lifecycle is true, typedManaged is set to true. This suppresses: the Edit button in DocumentHeader (null), the click-to-edit handler on the read surface, the desktop hover hint, the pencil FAB on mobile, and the onCheckboxToggle prop on MarkdownRenderer. The read surface uses a different border/padding style (rounded-2xl border bg-white/[0.015] shadow-[inset]) without cursor-text or group-hover. The document is still fetched and displayed as rendered markdown.
inventory_notes: If useJournalStatusQuery is loading or errors, journalStatus is undefined, typed_lifecycle is falsy, and typedManaged is false — documents remain editable. The backend itself 409s markdown writes to managed docs; the frontend guard prevents reaching that state.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want the previous day's note to be pre-loaded when I'm viewing a daily entry so that clicking the back arrow feels instant.
expected_behaviour: DocumentView uses a useEffect keyed on slug+kind to prefetch the previous day's document (shiftDate(slug, -1)) via queryClient.prefetchQuery whenever kind === 'daily'. The prefetch calls GET /api/v1/journal/documents/daily/{prevSlug}/ in the background. No user interaction is required.
inventory_notes: Only the previous day is prefetched, not the next day. Hovering the sidebar's primary nav also prefetches via a separate inline callback in Sidebar.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want hovering over a sidebar document to pre-load it so that clicking it opens instantly.
expected_behaviour: Both the primary nav items and the user-document list items call prefetchDocument(kind, slug) on onMouseEnter and onFocus. prefetchDocument wraps queryClient.prefetchQuery with the standard document query key and fetchDocument. This triggers GET /api/v1/journal/documents/{kind}/{slug}/ in the background.
inventory_notes: Prefetches honour staleTime=60s — a recently cached document will not be re-fetched.

user_story: As a subscriber navigating directly to /journal/today, I want to be redirected to today's journal entry so that external links and shortcuts work.
expected_behaviour: TodayRedirect mounts, computes todayISO() client-side, and immediately calls router.replace('/journal#daily/{today}'). The component renders null — no visible flash.
inventory_notes: Uses useEffect so it only fires on the client. The redirect is a replace (no back-stack entry).

user_story: As a subscriber navigating directly to /journal/memory, I want to be redirected to the memory document so that the URL is a usable shortcut.
expected_behaviour: MemoryRedirect mounts and immediately calls router.replace('/journal#memory/memory'), which lands on the memory singleton document in the journal shell. Renders null.
inventory_notes: The hash fragment is 'memory/memory' — the first 'memory' is the kind, the second is the slug (same value for the singleton).

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see all my saved note templates so that I can manage them and pick the default.
expected_behaviour: TemplatesPage (at /journal/templates) calls useNoteTemplatesQuery (GET /api/v1/journal/templates/, staleTime Infinity). While loading, a SectionCardSkeleton with 6 lines is shown. On error, a SectionCard with an error message is shown. On success, each template is rendered as an article card with name, /slug, section count, Edit and Delete buttons. If is_default is true, a green 'active' StatusPill is shown. If no templates exist, 'No templates yet.' is displayed.
inventory_notes: staleTime is Infinity — templates are never automatically re-fetched. Only mutations invalidate the cache.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to create a custom note template with named sections so that my assistant can fill them in automatically.
expected_behaviour: TemplatesPage shows an always-visible create/edit form in a SectionCard. Fields: Name (text), Slug (auto-normalized to lowercase-hyphens), is_default checkbox. Sections are editable sub-forms (slug, title, seed content textarea, Remove section button) seeded with 5 predefined sections (morning-report, weather, news, focus, evening-check-in). '+ Add section' appends an empty section. On submit, if slug, name, and at least one section with both slug+title are present, createMutation.mutateAsync (POST /api/v1/journal/templates/) is called. While pending, submit shows 'Creating...'. On success editingId is set to null. On error, the error message is shown in a rose panel below the form. Empty sections (missing slug or title) are silently filtered out on submit.
inventory_notes: Validation is only done at the top-level (slug/name/sections.length check returns early without showing an error message to the user). Section source defaults to 'shared' if blank.
error_detail: Confirmed. In handleSubmit (templates/page.tsx:110-136), when payload.slug, payload.name, or payload.sections.length is falsy, the function returns at line 126 without setting any error/validation state. The Name and Slug inputs (lines 179-201) and section slug/title inputs (lines 222-241) carry NO required attribute, so native browser validation does not fire either. The only error UI (lines 293-302) is gated on createMutation.isError / updateMutation.isError, which can only become true after a mutation actually runs — never reached on this early return. Result: a user who fills in sections but forgets the Name (or clears the Slug) clicks Create template, the button immediately re-enables (no pending state since no mutation fires), and nothing visibly happens — no message indicating what is missing. The feature's own audit inventory_notes corroborates this. User impact is mild: the form is seeded with 5 default sections and a normalized slug placeholder, so a fully-empty submit is uncommon; the realistic trigger is a forgotten/blank Name field, which leaves the user with a dead button and no guidance.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to edit an existing template to update its sections so that my daily briefs stay current.
expected_behaviour: Clicking the 'Edit' button on a template card calls handleStartEdit(template), which sets editingId to the template's id and populates formState with the template's current name, slug, is_default, and sections (deep-cloned). The same form is reused with the title 'Edit template' and submit button 'Save template' (or 'Saving...'). On submit, updateMutation.mutateAsync (PATCH /api/v1/journal/templates/{id}/) is called. On success, editingId is reset to null. 'New template' button at page top calls handleStartCreate(), resetting the form to empty with the seeded sections and setting editingId to null.
inventory_notes: There is no cancel button in the form — the user can click 'New template' to reset, but there is no explicit discard action. Navigating away while editing loses unsaved form state.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to delete a template I no longer need so that my template list stays clean.
expected_behaviour: Clicking the 'Delete' button on a template card immediately fires deleteMutation.mutate(template.id) (DELETE /api/v1/journal/templates/{id}/) without a confirmation dialog. While isPending, the button is disabled (opacity-45). On error, a rose error panel appears at the bottom of the page. The templates query is invalidated on success.
inventory_notes: Unlike document delete in the sidebar, there is NO confirmation dialog before template deletion. The error state for delete is shown at the bottom of the page, not near the button.

feature: Landing page hero with auth routing
user_story: As an anonymous user, I want to see the product landing page so that I can understand the offering and choose to sign up or sign in.
expected_behaviour: On mount, isLoggedIn() checks localStorage for nbhd_access_token. If a token is found, router.replace('/journal') fires immediately and the page renders null (no flash). If not logged in, the full dark-themed hero section renders with animated Starfield canvas, ConstellationLines SVG, and SynapseNetwork decorative background. Two CTA links are present: 'Begin your journey' → /signup and 'Sign in' → /login. Both have min-h-[44px] touch targets. A scroll indicator arrow is absolutely positioned at the bottom. No API call is made on this page.
inventory_notes: Page renders null briefly while the useEffect redirect fires for logged-in users, avoiding a flash of the landing content. ready state is initialised synchronously from isLoggedIn() so SSR always returns false and the component hydrates cleanly.

feature: Landing page 'How It Works' feature cards
user_story: As an anonymous user, I want to read about how the product works so that I can decide whether it fits my needs.
expected_behaviour: Three static glass-card tiles ('Talk naturally', 'Patterns emerge', 'See your constellation') render below the hero section in a responsive 1-column (mobile) / 3-column (desktop) grid. Each card has a coloured icon, title, and description. On hover, the card translates up 1px via CSS transition. No API calls, no interactive state.

user_story: As an anonymous user, I want to read the product vision statement so that I can connect with the brand ethos.
expected_behaviour: A full-width dark section with a second Starfield and mirrored SynapseNetwork renders a branded blockquote. The text 'Neighborhood United' is highlighted in c-pink. Purely presentational; no interactivity or API calls.

user_story: As an anonymous user, I want a clear call-to-action at the bottom of the landing page so that I can start my signup without scrolling back to the top.
expected_behaviour: A 'Get started' Link navigates to /signup. On hover a white overlay slides up via CSS transform. Button has a scale-105 hover transform and min-h-[44px] implicit height via py-5 padding. No API calls.

user_story: As an anonymous user, I want to log in with my email and password so that I can access my dashboard.
expected_behaviour: The OnboardingShell wraps a glass-card form with email (type=email, required) and password (type=password, required) fields. On submit the button label changes to 'Signing in...' and is disabled. POST /api/v1/auth/login/ is called with {email, password}. On success, access and refresh tokens are stored in localStorage via setTokens(). If hasPendingAppAuthorize() is true, router.replace('/app/authorize') is called instead of the normal post-login flow. Otherwise fetchMe() is called (GET /api/v1/auth/me/) to check tenant status: if tenant is absent, status !== 'active', or telegram_chat_id is falsy, route to /onboarding; else route to /journal. On API error, a rose-coloured error box renders the error message plus a 'Reset your password' link to /forgot-password. On 401 with a prior session, tokens are cleared and the user is redirected to /login by the api client.
inventory_notes: Error from the server's 401 body.detail is surfaced directly (e.g. 'Incorrect email or password.') when there is no prior session. A 'Forgot password?' link appears above the password field at all times. The error box also repeats the forgot-password link for discoverability.
error_detail:
Confirmed real, but milder than the QA claim of a hard bounce loop. login/page.tsx:35 computes isOnboardingNeeded = !me.tenant || me.tenant.status !== "active" || !me.tenant.user.telegram_chat_id. The third clause only checks the Telegram channel, but LINE is a first-class onboarding channel (types.ts:206-208 expose line_user_id and preferred_channel "telegram"|"line"; onboarding/page.tsx:23 treats isLineLinked as valid linkage). A LINE-only subscriber has telegram_chat_id = null, so isOnboardingNeeded resolves to true and login pushes them to /onboarding instead of /journal on every login.

Correction to the QA description: the user is NOT permanently stuck. On landing at /onboarding the page recognizes LINE (onboarding/page.tsx:22-24, messagingLinked = isTelegramLinked || isLineLinked), so an active LINE-only user resolves to the "launch" scene which shows "Your universe is ready" with a manual "Start exploring" button routing to /journal (launch-sequence.tsx:23,66-70). Real impact: a redundant STEP-3-OF-3 launch screen plus a manual click on every login, never a lockout. It is also inconsistent with the root landing page (app/page.tsx:90-92), which redirects any logged-in user straight to /journal with no channel check. Net effect is recurring extra friction for LINE-only subscribers only. Hence ux (not logic) and low severity.

user_story: As an anonymous user, I want to navigate to the forgot-password page from the login form so that I can recover access to my account.
expected_behaviour: Two separate links to /forgot-password exist on the login page: one above the password field at all times and one inside the error banner that only shows after a failed login attempt. Both are plain Next.js Link navigations with no API call.

user_story: As an anonymous user, I want to create a new account so that I can start my 7-day free trial.
expected_behaviour: Form has four fields: display name (optional text), email (required), password (required, client-validated ≥8 chars), confirm password (required). A PasswordStrengthMeter renders live feedback below the password field once any character is typed (scores: Weak/Fair/Good/Strong based on length ≥8, uppercase, digit, special-char). On submit, if passwords do not match the error 'Passwords do not match.' shows; if password < 8 chars the error 'Password must be at least 8 characters.' shows — both without API call. Otherwise POST /api/v1/auth/signup/ is called with {email, password, display_name}. On success tokens are stored and the same hasPendingAppAuthorize() branch logic as login applies. Legal links to /legal/terms and /legal/privacy appear below the submit button.
inventory_notes: Display name is optional (passed as undefined if empty). The 'Already have an account? Sign in' link navigates to /login.
error_detail:
CONFIRMED, but lower impact than the QA claim implies. Post-signup routing (signup/page.tsx:47) and login routing (login/page.tsx:35) both compute isOnboardingNeeded using only the Telegram chat id (me.tenant.user.telegram_chat_id) and ignore LINE entirely. The TenantUser type (types.ts:11-19) carries telegram_chat_id but has NO line_user_id field, so this routing path literally cannot see LINE linkage. A LINE-only subscriber (telegram_chat_id null, line_user_id set, tenant active) is therefore routed to /onboarding on every login/signup instead of straight to /journal, even though fully provisioned and connected. That part of the claim is accurate, and it is identical between signup and login.

What the claim overstates: the user is NOT dead-ended. The onboarding page (onboarding/page.tsx:22-24) detects LINE via the async useLineStatusQuery; once lineStatus.linked resolves, messagingLinked is true, the page advances to the launch scene, and an active+provisioned tenant sees Your universe is ready with a Start exploring button to /journal (launch-sequence.tsx:23, 66-70). So the impact is a forced extra Step-3 Launching your universe screen plus one extra click on every sign-in, not a lockout. There is also a brief flash where lineStatus is still undefined, during which an already-LINE-connected user momentarily sees the Connect your messenger scene before the query resolves.

Net: a LINE subscriber never gets the clean direct-to-journal experience a Telegram subscriber gets; every login/signup detours them through onboarding/launch. Real and repeatable, but a UX friction/regression rather than a logic-breaking lockout, hence low (not medium) severity and category ux.

user_story: As an anonymous user creating an account, I want real-time password strength feedback so that I can choose a strong password.
expected_behaviour: PasswordStrengthMeter renders null when password is empty. For any non-empty password it shows a 4-segment bar (rose/amber/teal colours) and a text label. Strength is calculated client-side: +1 for ≥8 chars, +1 for uppercase, +1 for digit, +1 for special character. Labels: 1=Weak (rose), 2=Fair (amber), 3=Good (teal), 4=Strong (teal). All transitions use CSS duration-300.
inventory_notes: Purely client-side; no API call.

user_story: As an anonymous user, I want to request a password reset email so that I can recover my account without remembering my password.
expected_behaviour: A single email field (required) with a 'Send reset link' submit button (disabled while loading or while email is empty). On submit, POST /api/v1/auth/password-reset/request/ is called. On any non-429 success or error the form is hidden and a confirmation message shows: 'Check your inbox. If an account exists for <email>, you'll get a reset link within a minute or two. The link expires in 3 days.' This preserves the no-email-enumeration guarantee. If the server returns 429, instead of the confirmation a rose error box shows: 'Too many reset requests. Please wait an hour before trying again.' A 'Back to sign in' link navigates to /login.
inventory_notes: Rate limit is per-email 3/hr and per-IP 5/hr according to the code comment. The 429 branch is the only error state the UI surfaces explicitly; all other errors fall through to the success confirmation to avoid revealing account existence.

user_story: As an anonymous user who received a reset-password email, I want to set a new password so that I can regain access to my account.
expected_behaviour: The page reads uid and token from the URL query string via useSearchParams. If either is absent, linkBroken=true and a rose error message renders immediately: 'This reset link is missing required information. Request a new one from the forgot-password page.' (links to /forgot-password). Otherwise the form renders with two fields: 'New password' (minLength=8, autoComplete=new-password) and 'Confirm password' (same). Client-side validation: if passwords don't match, 'Passwords don't match.' error shows without API call. On submit POST /api/v1/auth/password-reset/confirm/ is called with {uid, token, new_password}. On success, access and refresh tokens are stored and router.push('/journal') fires. On error, the server's message is shown (e.g. 'Reset link is invalid or has expired.'). The component is wrapped in Suspense with null fallback to satisfy Next.js static export constraints around useSearchParams.
inventory_notes: On success the user is immediately logged in (tokens stored) and sent to /journal — no separate login step required. The linkBroken check fires before any network call, providing instant UX on malformed links.

feature: iOS app authorize handoff — PKCE flow entry
user_story: As an iOS app user, I want the web authorize endpoint to handle my PKCE handoff so that the app can exchange a one-time code for an access token without my credentials being exposed.
expected_behaviour: The page at /app/authorize is opened inside an iOS ASWebAuthenticationSession. On mount (guarded by a ran.current ref against React StrictMode double-invoke), it reads PKCE params from the URL query (response_type, client, redirect_uri, code_challenge, code_challenge_method, state, intent). If params are missing or invalid (response_type≠'code', client≠'ios', codeChallengeMethod≠'S256', redirectUri not in ALLOWED_REDIRECT_URIS=['nbhd://auth/callback']), it redirects to nbhd://auth/callback?error=invalid_request immediately. Valid params are stashed in sessionStorage. If the user is already logged in (localStorage has access token), POST /api/v1/auth/authorize/ is called with {code_challenge, code_challenge_method, redirect_uri, state, client:'ios'} and the returned code triggers a redirect to nbhd://auth/callback?code=<code>&state=<state>. If not logged in, the user is routed to /login (intent='signin') or /signup (intent='register'). After login/signup succeeds, hasPendingAppAuthorize() returns true and those pages redirect back to /app/authorize to complete the exchange. Status messages ('Connecting your account…', 'Finishing sign-in…', 'Redirecting you to sign in…') render in an aria-live='polite' paragraph.
inventory_notes: sessionStorage is used (not localStorage) so params survive same-session client-side navigation but not a new browser session. If sessionStorage throws (private mode / quota), the stash silently fails and the handoff cannot survive a login round-trip — the authorize page handles the missing stash by redirecting error=invalid_request. The nbhd:// redirect is a no-op in a regular browser.

user_story: As a newly signed-up subscriber, I want a loading state while my account data is being fetched so that the onboarding wizard appears smoothly.
expected_behaviour: While useMeQuery is loading (GET /api/v1/auth/me/), an OnboardingShell wrapping a SectionCardSkeleton with 6 lines renders. Once the data resolves, the skeleton is replaced by the correct scene (persona, messaging, or launch).

user_story: As a new subscriber without a provisioned tenant, I want to choose an AI companion persona so that my assistant has a personality that suits me.
expected_behaviour: PersonaScene renders when me.tenant is absent. It calls usePersonasQuery (GET /api/v1/tenants/personas/ via fetchPersonas) to fetch available personas. Personas render as a 2-column grid of selectable buttons. Default selected is 'neighbor'. Clicking a persona highlights it with a purple border and checkmark; the selection updates local state only. Clicking 'Continue' calls onboardTenant({display_name: me.display_name, agent_persona: selected}) via POST /api/v1/tenants/onboard/ (useOnboardMutation). During the call the button shows 'Setting up...' and is disabled. A module-level Set (autoOnboardAttempted) guards against double-submission per userId. On success, 'me' and 'tenant' React Query caches are invalidated, causing the onboarding page to re-fetch and advance to the messaging scene.
inventory_notes: No error UI is shown if onboardTenant fails — the autoOnboardAttempted entry is deleted on failure, allowing a retry. If personas haven't loaded yet, the grid is omitted and only the Continue button renders (still functional with the default 'neighbor' key).

user_story: As a subscriber with a tenant but no messaging channel linked, I want to connect my Telegram account so that I can start chatting with my AI assistant.
expected_behaviour: MessagingScene renders when tenant exists but neither Telegram nor LINE is linked. Platform choice buttons for Telegram and LINE show initially. Clicking 'Connect Telegram' calls POST /api/v1/tenants/telegram/generate-link/ (useGenerateTelegramLinkMutation). On success, the platform picker is replaced by a pairing panel showing: a QR code image (hidden on mobile, visible sm:block), an 'Open in Telegram' deep-link button (prominent on mobile), a pulsing dot 'Waiting for you to connect...', a countdown timer showing seconds until the link expires (derived from expires_at, resetting link to null when it reaches zero), a 'Use LINE instead' switch button (clears the active link state), and a 'Generate new link' regenerate button. useTelegramStatusQuery polls every 3 seconds while telegramLink is set (pairingActive=true). When the backend confirms linked=true, the scene renders null and the parent page re-evaluates to advance to LaunchSequence.
inventory_notes: Only one platform link (Telegram or LINE) is active at a time; switching clears the other. Countdown runs client-side via setInterval; when it reaches zero setTelegramLink(null) clears the link so the pairing panel reverts to the platform picker.

user_story: As a subscriber with a tenant but no messaging channel linked, I want to connect my LINE account so that I can start chatting with my AI assistant via LINE.
expected_behaviour: Same scene as Telegram connection but for LINE. Clicking 'Connect LINE' calls POST /api/v1/tenants/line/generate-link/ (useGenerateLineLinkMutation). The pairing panel shows a green LINE-branded button 'Open in LINE', a LINE-coloured pulsing dot, and a countdown. useLineStatusQuery polls every 3 seconds while lineLink is set. When lineStatus.linked is true the scene renders null and advances to LaunchSequence.
inventory_notes: The QR code image src is a base64 data URL from the API response. Mobile users see a smaller (h-32) QR behind the deep-link button; desktop users see h-44.

feature: Onboarding wizard — step 3: Launch sequence (provisioning wait)
user_story: As a subscriber who has linked a messaging channel, I want to see progress while my private AI container is being provisioned so that I know when it is ready.
expected_behaviour: LaunchSequence renders when tenant exists and at least one channel is linked. useProvisioningStatusQuery polls GET /api/v1/tenants/provisioning-status/ every 5 seconds until ready=true. A ConstellationProgress component shows a 5-node graph ('Private space', 'AI model', 'Secure links', 'Warming up...', 'Ready') advancing through complete/active/pending states based on both elapsed wall-clock seconds (STEP_TIMINGS=[0,8,18,30,48]) and the real isReady flag. A rotating tip card cycles every 8 seconds through 3 privacy/usage tips. If isReady, the headline changes to 'Your universe is ready' and a 'Start exploring' button appears navigating to /journal. If elapsed > 90 seconds and not yet ready, a 'Retry provisioning' button appears that calls POST /api/v1/tenants/retry-provisioning/ (useRetryProvisioningMutation). On retry error, 'Could not retry right now. Try again shortly.' shows in rose-400.
inventory_notes: isReady is true when provisioningStatus.status === 'active' AND container_id is truthy. The visual step count caps at 4 (not 5) until isReady is confirmed to avoid prematurely showing all nodes as done. ConstellationProgress draws connecting lines between nodes: solid teal between two completed nodes, gradient teal→purple between a complete and active node, dashed between others.

user_story: As any user visiting an auth or onboarding page, I want a consistent branded background so that the experience feels cohesive.
expected_behaviour: OnboardingShell renders a full-height dark nebula background with a Starfield canvas at 60% opacity, centres its children vertically and horizontally, and renders SiteFooter at the bottom. Used by login, signup, forgot-password, reset-password, /app/authorize, and all three onboarding scenes.
inventory_notes: The Starfield respects prefers-reduced-motion: when true, stars are drawn once without requestAnimationFrame animation loop.

user_story: As a subscriber with an active session, I want my auth token to be transparently refreshed so that I am not interrupted with a forced re-login mid-session.
expected_behaviour: All API calls go through apiFetch. Before each request, if the access token's JWT exp claim is within 60 seconds of expiry, a deduped POST /api/v1/auth/refresh/ call is awaited proactively. If the token is valid but the server returns 401, the same refresh is attempted reactively. Concurrent calls share a single in-flight refresh via refreshPromise deduplication. On successful refresh, the new access token (and rotated refresh token if ROTATE_REFRESH_TOKENS is enabled) is stored in localStorage. If refresh fails, tokens are cleared and window.location.href is set to /login (unless already on /login).
inventory_notes: Token expiry is decoded client-side without signature verification (only the exp claim matters). The 60-second skew window ensures proactive refresh absorbs clock skew and network latency. A 401 on the retry (after refresh) results in clearTokens and redirect to /login.

user_story: As a visitor, I want an animated starfield background so that the pages have a distinctive visual identity.
