expected_behaviour: A canvas element fills its container absolutely (aria-hidden, pointer-events-none). On mount, stars are initialised at density 1 per 4000px². Each star has a random position, radius (0.3–1.8px), opacity, speed, and colour from 4 options (white, purple, teal, pink). Stars flicker via a sine wave on their opacity. When prefers-reduced-motion is detected, stars are drawn once without animation. Canvas resizes with the window. RAF is cancelled on component unmount.
inventory_notes: prefers-reduced-motion check is done once at mount; it does not reactively update if the user changes their OS setting mid-session.
error_detail: Confirmed. The spec says: when prefers-reduced-motion is true, "stars are drawn once without animation." The code does not honor this. At line 79, draw() unconditionally calls rafRef.current = requestAnimationFrame(draw) at the end of every invocation. The branch at lines 82-86 (if (prefersReduced) draw(); else rafRef.current = requestAnimationFrame(draw);) was clearly intended to make draw() run a single time for reduced-motion users, but because draw() always re-schedules itself, the initial direct call kicks off the same infinite RAF loop. So reduced-motion users still get a continuous clear+repaint of all stars every frame. User impact is limited but real: the flicker sine-wave IS correctly suppressed (lines 69-72, flicker = star.opacity when prefersReduced), so the rendered image is visually static and there is no perceptible motion, which means the WCAG 2.3 vestibular-trigger concern is largely moot in practice. The genuine harm is wasted work: an idle full-canvas repaint loop runs forever on a static image, consuming CPU/GPU and battery for exactly the reduced-motion cohort the branch meant to spare, and it violates the documented draw-once contract. Real but low-severity logic defect, not a misread.

user_story: As an anonymous user, I want to view legal documents without logging in so that I can review policies before subscribing.
expected_behaviour: All four legal pages (/legal/terms, /legal/privacy, /legal/refund, /legal/commerce-disclosure) are listed in AppShell's `publicPages` array and also caught by the `pathname.startsWith('/legal/')` fallback, so the auth-redirect guard (`router.replace('/login')`) never fires for any /legal/* route. Pages render entirely as static HTML — no API calls, no loading states, no error states. The `LegalPage` wrapper renders immediately with a panel card containing the page title, a 'Last updated' date, a prose body, a divider, and a cross-links footer. No skeleton, spinner, or suspense boundary is present.
inventory_notes: /legal/commerce-disclosure is NOT in the explicit publicPages array (only terms/privacy/refund are listed by name) but IS covered by the `pathname.startsWith('/legal/')` catch-all on line 262. Any future legal page under /legal/* is automatically public by that catch-all.

user_story: As an anonymous user on any legal page, I want to click the brand name so that I can navigate back to the homepage.
expected_behaviour: The `LegalPage` component renders a Next.js `<Link href='/'>` with the text 'Neighborhood United' styled as a small mono uppercase label in `text-ink-muted`. Hovering transitions it to `text-ink`. Clicking navigates to `/` via client-side routing. No API call is made.

user_story: As an anonymous user reading one legal document, I want footer links to all other legal pages so that I can quickly navigate between them.
expected_behaviour: Each `LegalPage` instance renders a `flex flex-wrap gap-4` row of four `<Link>` elements below a divider: Terms of Service (/legal/terms), Privacy Policy (/legal/privacy), Refund Policy (/legal/refund), and Commerce Disclosure (/legal/commerce-disclosure). All four links appear on all four pages (including the page the user is currently on — there is no active/current-page suppression). Hover transitions text from `text-ink-faint` to `text-ink-muted`. Navigation is client-side, no API call.
inventory_notes: The current page's own link is always rendered, meaning a user on /legal/terms sees a 'Terms of Service' link in the footer that does nothing useful (navigates to the same page). No `aria-current` attribute is applied.

user_story: As an anonymous user, I want to read the Terms of Service so that I understand the rules and obligations of using the platform.
expected_behaviour: GET /legal/terms renders a static prose document titled 'Terms of Service', last updated February 13, 2026. Eleven numbered sections cover: service description, account eligibility (18+), subscription/billing (monthly via Stripe, auto-renew, cancel via Stripe portal), acceptable use, intellectual property, third-party services (Telegram, LINE, Stripe, AI providers), limitation of liability (12-month cap), disclaimers (no warranty), termination/suspension, changes to terms (email notification), and contact email `mj@bywayofmj.com` rendered as a `mailto:` link. No API calls, no form, no interactive state beyond the shared navigation links.
inventory_notes: Contact email `mj@bywayofmj.com` is hardcoded as a `mailto:` anchor. Clicking opens the user's mail client.

user_story: As an anonymous user, I want to read the Privacy Policy so that I understand how my personal data is collected, used, and protected.
expected_behaviour: GET /legal/privacy renders a static prose document titled 'Privacy Policy', last updated May 4, 2026. Twelve sections cover: commitment to privacy, data collected (email, display name, Telegram/LINE IDs, usage metrics, Stripe payment info), data use, third-party sharing (Stripe, Telegram/LINE, AI model providers, Azure), PII redaction pipeline (ML model + deterministic checks on workspace context; direct messages unredacted; placeholder rehydration), data retention (30 days post-cancellation), cookies/local storage (JWT in localStorage, no tracking cookies), user rights (access/correct/delete via email), security (HTTPS/TLS), children's privacy (no under-13), policy change notification, and contact email. A single external link to the open-source PII model on HuggingFace (`target='_blank' rel='noopener noreferrer'`) is present in Section 5. No API calls.
inventory_notes: External link to https://huggingface.co/onbekend/nbhd-pii-model opens in a new tab with noopener noreferrer. `<code>` elements render inline placeholders like [PERSON_1] and [EMAIL_2] as examples inside the prose block.

user_story: As an anonymous user or subscriber, I want to read the Refund and Cancellation Policy so that I know my options if I want to stop using the service.
expected_behaviour: GET /legal/refund renders a static prose document titled 'Refund & Cancellation Policy', last updated February 13, 2026. Six sections describe: cancellation (access continues to end of billing period), two cancellation methods (Stripe Portal link on Billing page, or email to `mj@bywayofmj.com`), no-refund default policy, 48-hour full-refund exception (no messages sent, email request, 5-business-day processing), billing dispute escalation (email before bank dispute), and contact. Three `mailto:mj@bywayofmj.com` anchor links are present. No API calls.
inventory_notes: The 'Open Stripe Portal' reference in Section 2 is plain text only — no actual link to the Billing page is rendered in the legal doc itself.

feature: Commerce Disclosure page (特定商取引法)
user_story: As an anonymous user (particularly in Japan), I want to read the statutory commerce disclosure so that I can verify the seller's legally required disclosures before purchasing.
expected_behaviour: GET /legal/commerce-disclosure renders a static prose document titled 'Commerce Disclosure', last updated February 20, 2026, with a Japanese subtitle '特定商取引法に基づく表記'. Sections disclose: legal name (withheld, disclosed on request), head of operations (withheld), address (withheld), phone number (withheld, hours 10:00–18:00 JST weekdays), email `mj@bywayofmj.com`, pricing (Starter $12/mo, Premium $40/mo, BYOK $8/mo USD, taxes included), no additional fees, payment method (Stripe credit card), payment timing (immediate + recurring), service delivery (immediate on payment), cancellation and refund policy (no prorated refund; defect-based refund at discretion), and operating environment (modern browsers + Telegram/LINE). One `mailto:` link present. No API calls.
inventory_notes: This page is NOT in the explicit `publicPages` array in app-shell.tsx — it relies solely on the `pathname.startsWith('/legal/')` guard. If that guard were ever removed, /legal/commerce-disclosure would start redirecting unauthenticated users to /login while /legal/terms, /legal/privacy, and /legal/refund would remain public. The page intentionally withholds the operator's legal name, address, and phone number and states they will be disclosed on request — this may be insufficient under the Japanese 特定商取引法 (Act on Specified Commercial Transactions).

feature: Scroll-triggered header border on legal pages
user_story: As a user scrolling through a long legal document, I want a visible header separator so that I can tell I have scrolled past the top of the page.
expected_behaviour: AppShell attaches a passive `scroll` event listener to the `#main-content` element. When `scrollTop > 8px`, the `headerBorder` state is set to `true`, which adds a visible bottom border to the sticky header. On legal pages the header is rendered (AppShell wraps all pages), so this behaviour applies. Scrolling back to the top removes the border. No API call; no legal-page-specific code — inherited from AppShell.
inventory_notes: Legal pages do not suppress the global nav header — authenticated users on legal pages see the full nav bar with their user menu and nav items.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

feature: Animated entrance reveal on legal page load
user_story: As a user navigating to a legal page, I want a smooth entrance animation so that the page feels polished rather than abrupt.
expected_behaviour: The `LegalPage` wrapper applies `animate-reveal` (a CSS keyframe class defined in globals.css) to the outermost content card. This triggers once on initial mount/navigation. There is no user interaction required; no state or API dependency.
inventory_notes: The animation is applied via a Tailwind utility class. There is no explicit `prefers-reduced-motion` guard inside the legal component itself — whether it is suppressed depends on whether globals.css or tailwind.config.ts applies a motion media query to `animate-reveal`.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want a persistent sidebar of settings tabs so that I can jump between any settings section without losing context.
expected_behaviour: On md+ viewports the layout renders a sticky left sidebar (`top-[72px]`) with 8 tabs: Account, Integrations, Connected Apps, People, Scheduled Tasks, Usage, Billing, AI Provider. The active tab gets `bg-accent text-white`; inactive tabs are `text-ink-muted` with hover styles. Each link has `aria-current="page"` when active. On `mouseenter` the layout calls `queryClient.prefetchQuery` for that tab's data so navigation feels instant. No API call is made; the layout prefetches the child queries.
dependencies: All settings tab queries via React Query prefetch
inventory_notes: Prefetch fires on mouseenter only (not touch); mobile uses touchStart instead.
error_detail: Confirmed: the prefetchTabData switch in frontend/app/settings/layout.tsx (lines 125-155) has cases for all 7 other settings tabs but none for /settings/ai-provider, so hovering that sidebar tab (or touching it on mobile) falls through to default: break and prefetches nothing. The AI Provider page (frontend/app/settings/ai-provider/page.tsx:53-55) does have real data dependencies — useTenantQuery() (queryKey ["tenant"]) and useByoCredentialsQuery() (queryKey ["byo-credentials"]) — so there genuinely is data that could be warmed on hover. The expected_behaviour explicitly lists AI Provider among the 8 tabs and says navigation should feel instant via prefetch-on-mouseenter, making AI Provider the one tab omitted from the pattern. User impact is low: navigating to AI Provider still works correctly (its own useQuery hooks fetch on mount); the user just doesn't get the hover head-start the other 7 tabs get. Impact is further softened because the ["tenant"] query has a 5-minute staleTime and is typically already populated by the dashboard/Billing tab, so usually only ["byo-credentials"] would be cold — at most one short round-trip is not pre-warmed. A genuine omission in a perf/polish optimization, not a functional bug.

feature: Settings navigation layout — mobile tile list
user_story: As a subscriber on mobile, I want to see all settings sections as a card list so that I can navigate without a sidebar.
expected_behaviour: On viewports narrower than md, when on `/settings` (root), the layout renders the 7 non-Account tabs as a stacked list of full-width tiles each with icon, label, and a right chevron. Tapping a tile navigates to that sub-page. `onTouchStart` prefetches data. When on any sub-page, a `← Settings` back-link is shown instead of the tile list. The tile list is hidden when `pathname !== '/settings'`.
inventory_notes: The Account tab is filtered out of the mobile tile list because the Account page IS the `/settings` root.

feature: Account settings page — initial loading skeleton
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see a loading state while my account data is fetched so that the UI feels responsive.
expected_behaviour: While `useMeQuery` is loading, four `<SectionCardSkeleton lines={N}>` cards are rendered in place of the Account, Persona, Agent Configuration, and Danger Zone sections. Once data resolves the real content replaces the skeleton. On hard error (`me` is undefined after load) the Account section shows "Could not load account details."

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to edit my display name so that my assistant addresses me correctly.
expected_behaviour: The Display Name field shows the current value (or "Not set") with an Edit button. Clicking Edit switches to an inline text input pre-filled with the current value. Clicking Save calls `PATCH /api/v1/tenants/profile/` with `{ display_name }`. On success the field reverts to read-only, a "Saved!" toast banner appears for 3 s, and the form closes. If the new value equals the old one, the form closes with no API call. On error the error message replaces the toast. The Save button shows "Saving..." and is disabled while in flight.
inventory_notes: No minimum-length validation is enforced client-side; an empty submit is guarded by falling back to the existing value.
error_detail:
Confirmed by code. handleSaveProfileField writes both success and failure text into a single saveMessage state: on success it sets "Saved!"/etc. (page.tsx:327-331), and in the catch block it sets the error string into the SAME variable (page.tsx:334: setSaveMessage(error instanceof Error ? error.message : "Failed to save. Please try again.")). There is exactly one banner rendering saveMessage (page.tsx:789-793), hardcoded to border-signal/30 bg-signal-faint text-signal. The --signal token is teal/green (#4ECDC4, globals.css:18-20), conventionally a success/positive color. So a 400/500 from PATCH /tenants/profile/ surfaces the error message styled identically to a "Saved!" confirmation — error feedback rendered in success-green. User impact: a user may misread a failed save as successful, or be momentarily confused, assuming their change persisted when it did not. Real but minor: the message TEXT still says "Failed"/echoes the backend error, so the content disambiguates, and the banner auto-clears after 3s (page.tsx:349). No data loss or blocked workflow — purely a color/affordance mismatch. The palette has amber/status tokens available for a proper error variant; their absence here makes this low rather than medium.</confirmed_detail>
<parameter name="suggested_fix">Track success vs. error separately (e.g. add a saveError boolean or a typed status alongside saveMessage), and conditionally style the banner: keep border-signal/30 bg-signal-faint text-signal on success, switch to an error variant on failure using the amber/rose tokens already in the palette (amber-border/amber-bg/amber-text). Optionally add role="alert" for accessibility.</suggested_fix>
<parameter name="fix_complexity">trivial

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to change the language my assistant uses so that it communicates in my preferred language.
expected_behaviour: The Language field shows the human-readable label for the current language code. Clicking Edit shows a `<select>` with 16 options (English through Indonesian). Choosing Save calls `PATCH /api/v1/tenants/profile/` with `{ language }`. On success the edit form closes and a "Saved!" banner appears for 3 s. If the selected language matches the current one the form closes without an API call.
error_detail: Confirmed. The language save flow (handleLanguageSave -> handleSaveProfileField("language", ...)) runs through the shared handler whose catch block (page.tsx:334) writes the error string into saveMessage: setSaveMessage(error instanceof Error ? error.message : "Failed to save. Please try again."). That state has exactly ONE render site (page.tsx:789-793), styled as a success banner: border-signal/30 bg-signal-faint text-signal (signal/teal is the success token). There is no error-styling branch and no separate error state. So when a language-change API request fails, the failure text is shown inside a teal success banner — a real user reads a failed save as a confirmed save and may believe their language preference persisted when it did not. Same root cause as FA-0440 (display_name) since both flow through the single shared handler and single banner; it equally affects timezone and location saves.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to change my timezone so that my assistant schedules events and crons in my local time.
expected_behaviour: The Timezone field (spans full 2-column width) shows a formatted label including offset abbreviation. Clicking Edit shows a grouped `<select optgroup>` covering Popular, North America, Europe, Asia, South America, Africa regions. Save calls `PATCH /api/v1/tenants/profile/` with `{ timezone }`. On success a contextual message is shown: if the timezone changed, "Saved! Agent timezone updated. Changes take effect on next message."; otherwise "Saved!". The message auto-clears after 3 s.
inventory_notes: The static `TIMEZONE_GROUPS` list in `page.tsx` is separate from the full `TimezoneSelector` component used in cron forms; the account page uses its own inline grouped select.
error_detail: Confirmed by source. (1) offsetLabel() (page.tsx:192-217) has a static map of only 18 zones and returns the literal "UTC" for every other zone (line 216: return map[tz] ?? "UTC"). findTimezoneLabel() (219-221) renders `${tz} (${offsetLabel(tz)})`. The TIMEZONE_GROUPS dropdown lists well over 100 IANA zones, so the large majority render misleading labels like "Africa/Lagos (UTC)" (actually UTC+1), "Europe/Brussels (UTC)" (UTC+1/+2), "Asia/Bangkok (UTC)" (UTC+7). The wrong label shows both in the read-only display (line 581: findTimezoneLabel(me.timezone || "UTC")) and in every edit-mode option (line 595). (2) Duplicate entries: Europe group has Europe/Zurich (lines 107 and 111) and Europe/Madrid (lines 101 and 112) twice; Africa group has Africa/Cairo (lines 171 and 186) twice. The React option key is `${group.region}-${tz}` (line 594), so these yield duplicate visible options and duplicate React keys (e.g. "Europe-Europe/Zurich"), triggering a console key warning and redundant dropdown rows. User impact: a user picking/reviewing their timezone sees a factually wrong UTC offset next to most zones and the same city listed twice. The saved value is the correct IANA string itself, so no data corruption — purely a misleading display plus duplicate render, hence medium not high.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to set my city so that my assistant provides localised weather and recommendations.
expected_behaviour: The Location field spans full 2-column width and shows the city string or "Not set — used for weather and local info". Clicking Edit shows a text input with `maxLength={255}`, `autoFocus`, and a helper note. Save calls `PATCH /api/v1/tenants/profile/` with `{ location_city }`. On success the banner reads "Saved! Weather forecasts will use your location.". If city unchanged or empty, form closes with no API call. Error is shown inline.
inventory_notes: Coordinates are NOT sent from the frontend; the API comment says coordinates will be resolved by the agent on next briefing.

feature: Account settings — read-only email, username, tenant status
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see my email, username, and tenant status so that I know my account details.
expected_behaviour: Email and Username fields are read-only display cells — no Edit button. Tenant field shows a `<StatusPill>` reflecting `me.tenant.status` or "No tenant provisioned" if absent. None of these fields are editable from the UI.

feature: Account settings — Telegram connection status chip
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see my Telegram connection status on the account page so that I know if my messaging is set up.
expected_behaviour: The Telegram cell shows `@username` (or "Connected") if `me.telegram_chat_id` is set. If not set it shows a `<StatusPill status="pending"/>` and a link button "Connect Telegram" that navigates to `/settings/integrations`. No API call is made from this cell.

feature: Account settings — LINE connection status chip
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see my LINE connection status so that I know if LINE messaging is active.
expected_behaviour: Mirrors Telegram cell logic: shows `line_display_name` or "Connected" if `me.line_user_id` is set; otherwise shows pending pill and a "Connect LINE" link to `/settings/integrations`.

user_story: As a subscriber with both Telegram and LINE connected, I want to choose which channel receives scheduled messages so that notifications arrive where I want them.
expected_behaviour: The Preferred Channel widget is only rendered if BOTH `me.telegram_chat_id` AND `me.line_user_id` are set. Two pill buttons appear: Telegram and LINE. The active one is highlighted (`bg-accent` or `bg-[#06C755]`). Clicking either calls `PATCH /api/v1/tenants/line/preferred-channel/` via `useSetPreferredChannelMutation`. If `lineQuotaExhausted` is true (from `useLineStatusQuery`) the LINE button is disabled and has a tooltip; a helper text explains the monthly quota reached state.
inventory_notes: This widget is hidden entirely if only one channel is connected, regardless of preferred_channel value.

feature: Account settings — agent persona view and change
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to pick my assistant's personality so that it communicates in a style I prefer.
expected_behaviour: The Agent Persona card shows the current persona's emoji, label, and description with a Change button. Clicking Change reveals the `<PersonaSelector>` grid (emoji + label + description cards from `GET /api/v1/tenants/personas/`). The selected card gets an accent ring and checkmark. Clicking Save calls `PATCH /api/v1/tenants/preferences/` with `{ agent_persona }`, then shows "Saved!" for 3 s and exits edit mode. If the selected persona equals the current one, the form closes without an API call. A footer note says changes take effect on next container restart.
inventory_notes: PersonaSelector is a pure presentational grid; all state and mutation live in the parent page.

feature: Account settings — agent configuration status & apply-now
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see if my assistant configuration is up-to-date and manually trigger an update so that changes take effect immediately.
expected_behaviour: The Agent Configuration card shows `last_refreshed` as a relative timestamp ("just now", "X minutes ago") from `GET /api/v1/tenants/refresh-config/`. It also shows the running container image SHA (first 7 chars) and an amber badge if a newer image tag is available. When `hasPendingConfigUpdate || image_outdated` is true, an amber banner appears with an "Apply Now" button (or "Available in N minutes" if on cooldown). Clicking Apply Now calls `POST /api/v1/tenants/refresh-config/`. On success: "Configuration refreshed. Your assistant will restart momentarily.". On 429/cooldown: "Available in N minutes". On other error: the error message. All messages auto-clear after 4-5 s. Button is disabled while refreshing or on cooldown.
inventory_notes: The `PendingConfigChip` component is also rendered in the Account card header for a second visual cue.

feature: Account settings — delete account (no active subscription)
user_story: As a subscriber without an active subscription, I want to permanently delete my account immediately so that my data is removed.
expected_behaviour: The Danger Zone section has a "Delete my account" button. Clicking it opens an inline confirmation panel listing what gets deleted ("Account deleted immediately", AI memory, journal, no recovery). The user must type "DELETE" exactly in a mono input. The Submit button label is "Delete my account" and is disabled until the input matches. On confirm, calls `DELETE /api/v1/auth/me/` (via `useDeleteAccountMutation`). If `result.scheduled` is false, `window.location.assign('/signin?deleted=1')` redirects immediately.
inventory_notes: The component uses `window.confirm` nowhere; the type-DELETE pattern is the only confirmation gate.

feature: Account settings — schedule account deletion (active subscription)
user_story: As a subscriber with an active subscription, I want to schedule my account for deletion so that I retain access until my billing period ends.
expected_behaviour: When `me.tenant.has_active_subscription` is true, the delete panel shows a billing-period notice and changes the button label to "Schedule deletion". After typing "DELETE" and confirming, calls the deletion mutation. If `result.scheduled` is true, the modal closes and the UI re-renders to the "pending deletion" state (the `me` query is invalidated). The Danger Zone then shows the scheduled date and a "Cancel — keep my account" button.

feature: Account settings — cancel scheduled account deletion
user_story: As a subscriber who has scheduled deletion, I want to cancel the deletion so that I keep my account.
expected_behaviour: When `me.tenant.pending_deletion` is true, the Danger Zone renders an alert panel showing the scheduled deletion date and a "Cancel — keep my account" button. Clicking it calls `useCancelDeletionMutation`. On success the panel disappears (the `me` query is invalidated, `pending_deletion` becomes false). On error an inline error message appears inside the panel.

feature: Integrations — Telegram connect via QR code / deep link
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to connect my Telegram account so that my assistant can message me there.
expected_behaviour: The TelegramCard renders a pending StatusPill and a "Connect" button when `status.linked` is false. Clicking Connect calls `POST /api/v1/tenants/telegram/generate-link/`, which returns `{ qr_code, deep_link }`. A QR code image and an "Open in Telegram" button then appear. The component polls `GET /api/v1/tenants/telegram/status/` every 3 s while the link is visible (15 s otherwise). When `status.linked` becomes true, the card switches to the connected state showing `@username`. The pending link is not automatically expired client-side.

The button shows "Unlinking..." while pending and is disabled. On success the card reverts to the unlinked/pending state (query invalidated).

feature: Integrations — LINE connect via QR code / deep link

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to enable or disable the Gravity finance plugin so that my assistant can help me with budgeting.
expected_behaviour: GravityCard is conditionally hidden when `tenant.gravity_available === false`. Otherwise a toggle switch reflects `tenant.finance_enabled`. If `result.restart_required` is true, immediately calls `restartFinanceAssistant()` and shows an amber "Configuring your assistant... this takes about a minute." while in progress. On restart error shows a rose error inline. If the card throws, an `<ErrorBoundary>` catches it.
entry_points: frontend/app/settings/integrations/page.tsx:GravityCard ; PATCH /api/v1/tenants/settings/finance/ (inferred from useUpdateFinanceSettingsMutation) ; POST restart endpoint (dynamic import of restartFinanceAssistant from @/lib/api)

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to enable or disable the Fuel fitness plugin so that my assistant can track my workouts.
expected_behaviour: FuelCard toggle mirrors GravityCard pattern: `tenant.fuel_enabled`, `PATCH /api/v1/tenants/settings/fuel/`, `restartFuelAssistant()` on restart_required. Additionally renders `<FuelProfileStatus>` when enabled showing the onboarding_status text from `GET /api/v1/fuel/profile/` (pending / in_progress / completed / declined). Wrapped in ErrorBoundary.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to enable or disable the Core meditation plugin so that I can access on-demand meditations.

When enabled and not restarting shows a hint "Open the Core tab and press the orb whenever you want a sit." Wrapped in ErrorBoundary.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to connect my Google account so that my assistant can access Gmail, Calendar, Drive, and Tasks.

Button shows "Redirecting..." while the mutation is pending and is disabled. After OAuth, the user returns to `/settings/integrations?connected=google` and sees a success banner.

feature: Connected Apps — list personal access tokens

expected_behaviour: On load, calls `GET /api/v1/auth/tokens/`. While loading shows `<SectionCardSkeleton>`. If fetch errors, shows a rose banner. If no tokens, shows an empty-state card with a "Connect an app" CTA. If tokens exist, shows sorted list (newest first) of `<TokenRow>` cards with: name, `pat_<prefix>…` monospace label, scope chips, last-used/expires/created relative timestamps. If a token was created >5 min ago and never used, shows a "Not used yet" rose hint.

feature: Connected Apps — mint a PAT (YardTalk preset)
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to mint a token for YardTalk so that the macOS app can push session summaries to my assistant.
expected_behaviour: Clicking "Connect an app" opens a 3-step modal: Step 1 (preset): choose YardTalk or Custom card. Choosing YardTalk pre-fills name="YardTalk", scopes=["sessions:write"], expiry=Never and jumps to Step 2 (form). The form shows the name input and expiry select (no scope UI for YardTalk). Clicking "Create token" calls `POST /api/v1/auth/tokens/create/` with `{ name, scopes, expires_in_days? }`. On success moves to Step 3 (reveal) showing the raw token in a read-only input, a Copy button (clipboard API), and YardTalk-specific setup instructions. Closing the modal resets all state after a 150 ms delay.
inventory_notes: The raw token is shown ONLY in the reveal step. After closing, it is never displayed again. On 429 error shows a rate-limit message. On 400 shows a validation message.

feature: Connected Apps — mint a PAT (custom preset)
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to create a custom API token with specific scopes and expiry so that any app or script can integrate with my assistant.
expected_behaviour: Choosing "Custom" in the preset step advances to a form with: name input, scope checkboxes (`sessions:write` / `sessions:read` — both pre-checked with write), and expiry select (Never / 30 days / 90 days / 1 year). The "Create token" button is disabled if name is empty OR no scopes are selected. On success the reveal step shows the token plus a curl example for `POST /api/v1/sessions/create/`.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to revoke a token so that a connected app immediately loses access.
expected_behaviour: Each `<TokenRow>` has a "Revoke" button. Clicking opens the `<RevokeConfirmModal>` showing the token name and a warning that it stops working immediately. Clicking "Revoke" in the modal calls `DELETE /api/v1/auth/tokens/<id>/`. On success the modal closes and the tokens list query is invalidated. On error, an inline rose error message appears in the modal. The modal is opened with `Escape`-to-close and backdrop-click-to-close.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to set the time window when my assistant can proactively message me so that I am not disturbed at inconvenient hours.
expected_behaviour: The `<WorkingHoursSection>` renders first on the Scheduled Tasks page. Loading shows `<SectionCardSkeleton lines={2}>`. In read mode shows the enabled range (e.g. "6:00 AM – 12:00 PM") or "Off", plus the label and timezone. Clicking Edit shows On/Off pill buttons and (when On) a set of 5 time-block pills: Morning (6–12), Daytime (10–16), Afternoon (12–18), Evening (18–24), Night (0–6). Saving calls `PATCH /api/v1/tenants/heartbeat/` with `{ enabled, start_hour }`. On success: "Saved! Changes take effect within 15 minutes."; on failure: "Failed to save. Please try again.". Messages auto-clear after 4 s.
inventory_notes: The five time blocks have fixed start hours; there is no arbitrary time picker. The `PendingConfigChip` is rendered inside this section.

feature: Scheduled Tasks — create a cron job with template quick-start
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to create a scheduled task from a template so that I can quickly set up common workflows like morning briefings.
expected_behaviour: Clicking "Add scheduled task" opens a create panel (no modal). A horizontal scroll of 6 template buttons (Morning Briefing, News Digest, Weather Report, Daily Schedule, Evening Recap, Weekly Review) auto-fills the name, message, and cron expression. The active template pill is highlighted. The create form has: Name (required text input), TimezoneSelector, ScheduleBuilder, Delivery select (Announce / Silent), SessionModeSelector (Foreground/Background), and a TipTap PromptEditor for the task prompt. CapabilityChips let you insert `[tool]` tags at the cursor. A toggleable agent capabilities reference panel lists 8 built-in capabilities. On submit, calls `POST /api/v1/cron/jobs/` with `{ name, schedule, foreground, payload, delivery, enabled:true }`. On success the form resets and `✓ Created` flashes on the submit button for 2 s. On error the button turns red and shows the error message for 4 s.
dependencies: GET /api/v1/auth/me/ (for timezone default), GET /api/v1/tenants/integrations/ (for connectedProviders in CapabilityChips)
inventory_notes: The Name field has `required` HTML validation. The PromptEditor is TipTap-based. CapabilityChips show only connected-provider chips for Google etc.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want a visual schedule builder so that I can set a recurring schedule without knowing cron syntax.
expected_behaviour: The `<ScheduleBuilder>` defaults to easy mode when the cron expression can be parsed. Easy mode offers Frequency select (Every day, Weekdays, Weekends, Weekly, Monthly), conditional weekday pills for weekly frequency, a day-of-month select (1st–28th) for monthly, and Hour / Minute selects (minute in 00/15/30/45 increments). The cron expression shown as a human label ("Every day at 09:00") updates live above the builder. Switching to "Use cron expression" reveals a mono text input. Switching back parses the raw expression; if it is an unsupported pattern, shows a rose parse-error message.
inventory_notes: Monthly supports days 1–28 only (avoids end-of-month ambiguity). The easy→advanced toggle always succeeds; advanced→easy fails gracefully with an inline error if the expression can't be round-tripped.
error_detail: Confirmed off-by-one in the weekly frequency path of the schedule builder. WEEKDAY_LABELS maps array index 0=Mon through 6=Sun (lines 30-38). In the weekly day picker (lines 364-379) the array index is stored directly into state.weekdays, and buildCronFromState joins those indices verbatim into the cron day-of-week field (lines 119-123). But the cron consumer uses standard Unix/QStash DOW semantics where 0=Sunday and 1=Monday, confirmed by the project's own system crons (apps/cron/management/commands/register_system_crons.py:78, "0 5 * * 0" documented as "Weekly Sunday 05:00"). Result: selecting Mon emits cron 0 (fires Sunday); Tue emits 1 (fires Monday); every weekly selection fires one day earlier than displayed. The default weekdays:[1] (line 252) shows "Tuesday" in the UI (WEEKDAY_NAMES[1]) but emits cron 1 = Monday. The bug is masked on round-trip because parseCronExpr reads DOW values back into the same index space (lines 173-189) and cronToHuman/WEEKDAY_NAMES (lines 209-215) map index->name with the identical offset, so the UI always re-displays the same wrong label and never surfaces the mismatch. The weekdays preset (1-5) and weekends preset (0,6) are correct because they are hardcoded to proper cron values; only the custom weekly path is affected. User impact: weekly assistant crons run one day earlier than the day the user picked, and Sun (index 6) collides with Saturday in cron (where 6=Saturday).

feature: Scheduled Tasks — timezone selector (in cron forms)
user_story: As a subscriber, I want a searchable timezone dropdown in the cron create/edit form so that my tasks fire at the right local time.
expected_behaviour: The `<TimezoneSelector>` renders two controls: a text search input and a grouped `<select>`. It queries `Intl.supportedValuesOf('timeZone')` client-side for the full IANA list (falls back to `['UTC']`). Typing in the search filters by timezone name or UTC offset label. When the filter hides the currently-selected timezone, the component auto-selects the first visible option and fires `onChange`. Groups are derived from the IANA prefix (e.g. "America", "Europe", "Asia"). No API call.
inventory_notes: This is distinct from the account-page timezone selector, which uses a static curated list. This one uses the full runtime IANA list via Intl.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

feature: Scheduled Tasks — list existing cron jobs
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see all my scheduled tasks so that I can review what my assistant does automatically.
expected_behaviour: Below the create card, calls `GET /api/v1/cron/jobs/`. While loading: `<SectionCardSkeleton lines={5}>`. On error: rose error card with the error message and a `check_gateway_health` hint. If empty: "No scheduled tasks configured yet." If populated: a list of `<article>` cards each showing job name, human-readable schedule, `enabled/paused` StatusPill, delivery channel pill, and foreground/background pill. The first 200 chars of the prompt (with internal date prefix stripped) are shown below. Loading and error are distinct states.
inventory_notes: The date-context prefix injected by `_inject_date_context()` (up to `"1 day away.\n\n"`) is stripped by `stripPromptPrefix` before display.

feature: Scheduled Tasks — enable/disable a cron job
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to pause or resume a scheduled task without deleting it so that I can temporarily stop it.
expected_behaviour: Each task row has a toggle button labelled "Disable" (when enabled) or "Enable" (when disabled). Clicking calls `PATCH /api/v1/cron/jobs/<name>/` via `useToggleCronJobMutation` with `{ enabled: !current }`. Button is disabled with `disabled:opacity-45` while the mutation is pending.
error_detail: Confirmed at the code level. A single shared toggleMutation instance (page.tsx:214, from useToggleCronJobMutation) is reused by every row rendered in cronJobs.map(...) (page.tsx:820). The Enable/Disable button's disabled={toggleMutation.isPending} (page.tsx:884) reads the mutation's GLOBAL pending flag with no per-row scoping (no check against toggleMutation.variables to limit disabling to the in-flight job). So while ANY one job's toggle request is in flight, every other row's Enable/Disable button is disabled simultaneously. Notably the delete path directly beside it uses per-job state maps (singleDeletePending[jobIdentifier], page.tsx:824/905), confirming per-row guarding was a known pattern that simply was not applied to the toggle button — an inconsistency, not intended behaviour. User impact is real but minor: the optimistic update (queries.ts:1017-1026) flips the toggled row's label immediately and onSettled invalidates promptly (queries.ts:1032-1033), and a toggle is a single fast API call, so the all-buttons-disabled window is typically sub-second. Only a user rapidly toggling several jobs in succession would notice the other rows briefly frozen; in normal one-at-a-time use the list does not appear broken. Hence low severity, not a medium/high frozen/broken framing.

feature: Scheduled Tasks — edit a cron job (desktop inline form)
user_story: As a subscriber on desktop, I want to edit an existing scheduled task inline so that I can refine its schedule or prompt.
expected_behaviour: Clicking "Edit" on a job row sets `editingName` and renders an inline form with TimezoneSelector, ScheduleBuilder, Delivery select, SessionModeSelector, PromptEditor, and CapabilityChips. The form is pre-populated from `toEditForm(job)` (stripping the date prefix from the message). Submitting calls `PATCH /api/v1/cron/jobs/<name>/` via `useUpdateCronJobMutation`. On success `✓ Saved` flashes green for 2 s. On error the button turns red for 4 s.
inventory_notes: Only one job can be in edit mode at a time (single `editingName` state). Opening a new edit does NOT close the previous one unless the user explicitly cancels.
error_detail: Confirmed real but mild. In handleUpdate the success path runs setEditingName(null) (L396) immediately before setSuccess(setEditFeedback) (L397). Both run in the same async continuation after the awaited mutateAsync, and under React 19 automatic batching collapses them into one re-render where editingName is already null. The success UI (the Saved-checkmark text branch at L1101-1102 and success button styling via getSaveButtonClass at L1097) exists only inside the inline form, conditionally mounted on editingName === jobIdentifier (L1028). The mobile portal (L936) is gated on the same condition and has no success affordance at all (its button shows only ... / Save at L948). So in the render where status becomes success, the host form is already unmounted and the success flash can never appear. The create path has the identical structure (setShowCreate(false) then setSuccess, L358-359), so its success flash at L680 is also dead code, confirming a consistent pattern. Impact is real but low: on success the form collapses and the list re-renders with the updated job via query invalidation, an implicit success signal; the save works and errors DO surface (the error branch at L1020/L1117 renders because the error path leaves the form mounted). So this is a missing positive-confirmation affordance, not a true silent failure; the feels-like-silent-failure framing is overstated.

feature: Scheduled Tasks — edit a cron job (mobile full-screen overlay)
user_story: As a subscriber on mobile, I want a full-screen edit experience for a scheduled task so that the form is usable on a small screen.
expected_behaviour: When `editingName` is set and `isMobile === true` (matched via `window.matchMedia('(max-width: 767px)')`), the edit form is rendered via `createPortal` to `document.body` as a fixed full-screen overlay. The app header is hidden (`visibility: hidden`) and body scroll is locked (`overflow: hidden`) while the overlay is open. A sticky header bar shows the job name, Save, and Cancel buttons. The form content is scrollable. Save calls the same update mutation.
inventory_notes: The overlay is only rendered when `isMobile === true` (starts `null` on SSR, set after mount via matchMedia). Mismatched hydration is possible on first render.
error_detail: Confirmed. In handleUpdate, on success setEditingName(null) (line 396) makes editingName === jobIdentifier false, so the entire edit UI unmounts immediately. Line 397's setSuccess() then writes status:success into editFeedback, but on mobile nothing is left to surface it: the mobile portal Save button (lines 942-948) only renders "..." while pending or "Save" otherwise (no success state), and the portal's only feedback paragraph (lines 1020-1022) renders error text only. The 2s success window (SAVE_CLEAR_DELAY_MS, lines 180/310) elapses against an unmounted tree. Net mobile impact: after tapping Save the full-screen overlay simply disappears with no explicit success confirmation; the only implicit cue is the overlay closing plus the refetched list. Low severity because the save genuinely succeeds and the closing overlay is a weak implicit signal. Nuance the QA verdict missed: the desktop inline form DOES contain the "Saved" success markup (lines 1099-1103), but it is effectively dead code for the same reason (setEditingName(null) unmounts the form before that branch can render). So the tear-down-before-feedback bug is not strictly mobile-only; only the missing-markup observation is specific to the mobile portal. The core claim stands.

feature: Scheduled Tasks — single-job delete with inline confirm
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to delete a specific scheduled task with a quick confirm so that I don't accidentally remove it.
expected_behaviour: Each job row has a "Delete" button. Clicking it sets that job into "confirming" state: the row background shifts to rose, and inline "Delete? ✓ ✕" buttons replace the Delete button. A 3 s auto-cancel timer resets confirmation if not acted upon. Clicking ✓ calls `DELETE /api/v1/cron/jobs/<name>/` via `useDeleteCronJobMutation`. The job is also deselected from the multi-select set. Clicking ✕ or waiting 3 s cancels. On delete error, the error shows inline with a 4 s auto-clear.
inventory_notes: Delete confirmation is per-row, not a modal, so multiple rows can be in confirming state simultaneously.

feature: Scheduled Tasks — multi-select and bulk delete
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to select and delete multiple scheduled tasks at once so that I can clean up efficiently.
expected_behaviour: Each job row has a checkbox (44px touch target). A "Select all" header row appears when ≥2 jobs exist. Checking rows adds them to `selectedIds`. When any are selected a bulk action bar slides in with: count, "Set foreground", "Set background", "Delete selected", and "Clear" buttons. Clicking "Delete selected" shows a confirming sub-state with "Delete N tasks? This cannot be undone." Confirming calls `POST /api/v1/cron/jobs/bulk-delete/` via `useBulkDeleteCronJobsMutation`. On success shows a toast "N tasks deleted" and clears selection. On error shows an inline error in the bar.
inventory_notes: The bulk action bar uses `max-h` CSS transition (0 → 8rem) for animated reveal/hide.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to set multiple tasks to foreground or background mode at once so that I can control which tasks send messages to me.
expected_behaviour: In the bulk action bar, "Set foreground" and "Set background" buttons call `PATCH /api/v1/cron/jobs/bulk-foreground/` via `useBulkUpdateForegroundMutation` with `{ ids, foreground: true/false }`. On success clears selection and shows a toast "N tasks set to foreground/background". On error shows a toast with the error message.

user_story: As a subscriber who visits the old /settings/automations URL, I want to be redirected to the current page so that bookmarks still work.
expected_behaviour: The `automations/page.tsx` is a client component that on mount calls `router.replace('/settings/cron-jobs')` and renders nothing. This is a permanent redirect implemented in the frontend, not via HTTP.
inventory_notes: Because it uses `router.replace`, the `/settings/automations` URL is removed from browser history.

feature: People — entity registry list with curated filter
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see the people my assistant has detected so that I can verify and correct name bindings.
expected_behaviour: Calls `GET /api/v1/tenants/settings/entity-registry/`. Loading shows "Loading…" text with `role=status`. Error shows a rose alert. Empty state shows "No people are tracked yet." When entries exist, shows count ("Showing N of M") and a "Show only curated" checkbox (default ON). Curated = entries with a non-empty relationship or notes field. Non-curated auto-detections are hidden by default (with a count notice). A search input filters by placeholder/name/relationship/notes across visible entries. When the filter yields nothing a helpful message appears with buttons to clear filters.
inventory_notes: The page loads `fetchPIIDenylist` concurrently for the denylist section.

feature: People — edit entity name, relationship, and notes
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to edit the name, relationship, and notes for a detected person so that my assistant uses accurate information.
expected_behaviour: Each entry card shows three text inputs (Name, Relationship, Notes) pre-filled from the draft state. Edits are tracked in a local `drafts` map (keyed by placeholder). The Save button is enabled only when `dirty` (draft !== server state) and is disabled when another mutation is pending for that row. Clicking Save calls `PATCH /api/v1/tenants/settings/entity-registry/<placeholder>/` with the draft as the patch body. On success the entity-registry query is invalidated. On error, an inline rose alert appears per-row. Clicking Cancel resets the draft to the server value.
inventory_notes: Draft state persists across query refetches (only new entries without a draft are seeded from server data). Deleted entries are pruned from drafts.

feature: People — delete an entity registry entry
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to delete a name binding so that my assistant stops using that placeholder mapping.
expected_behaviour: Each entry card has a "Delete" button (rose-styled). Clicking calls `window.confirm()` with a message explaining the scope. If confirmed, calls `DELETE /api/v1/tenants/settings/entity-registry/<placeholder>/`. On success the query is invalidated and the row disappears. On error, the per-row error map is set showing the error message inline.
inventory_notes: This is the one place in Settings that uses `window.confirm()` for confirmation instead of an inline or modal pattern.

feature: People — ignore a name (add to PII denylist)
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to mark a detected name as not-personal so that my assistant stops redacting it in future messages.
expected_behaviour: Each entry row has an "Ignore" button, disabled if the entry has no name. Clicking calls `POST /api/v1/tenants/settings/pii-denylist/` with `{ name }`. On success the denylist query is invalidated and the row shows "Ignored — won't be redacted" instead of the Ignore button (checked via `denylistKeys.has(name.toLowerCase())`). Button is disabled while saving, deleting, or already ignored.

feature: People — bulk select and ignore multiple entries
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to bulk-ignore multiple detected names so that I can quickly clean up false-positive auto-detections.
expected_behaviour: Each entry row has a checkbox. A "Select all visible" checkbox with indeterminate state handles visible-filtered rows. When ≥1 is selected, a sticky bulk action bar appears with "Ignore N selected" button (rose, glow). Clicking calls `POST /api/v1/tenants/settings/pii-denylist/bulk/` with `{ names: [...] }`. On success clears selection and invalidates denylist query. If some entries were skipped (empty/invalid names) shows a partial-success message. On hard error shows a rose error.
inventory_notes: Select-all operates only over currently-visible filtered entries, not all entries in the registry.

feature: People — ignored words list and re-enable redaction
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see which words I've marked as not-personal and restore redaction for any of them so that I can correct mistakes.
expected_behaviour: A separate SectionCard below the entity registry calls `GET /api/v1/tenants/settings/pii-denylist/`. Loading shows "Loading…" with role=status. Error shows a rose alert. Empty state says "Nothing ignored yet." Each entry shows the word key and the `decided_at` date. A "Re-enable redaction" button calls `DELETE /api/v1/tenants/settings/pii-denylist/<key>/`. On error falls back to `window.alert()` (low-effort path per comment). Button shows "Re-enabling…" while pending.
inventory_notes: Uses `window.alert()` for the error case in undo-ignore, unlike the standard inline error pattern used everywhere else on the page.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to choose which AI model my assistant uses so that I can balance capability and cost.
expected_behaviour: Calls `GET /api/v1/tenants/me/` and `GET /api/v1/tenants/byo-credentials/`. Loading shows `<SectionCardSkeleton lines={5}>`. Models are rendered as a responsive grid of button cards. Each card shows: name, `<IntelligenceMeter>`, tagline, input/output rate. State badges: "Active" (accent, model is applied), "Switching…" (amber pulse, selected but not yet applied), "Coming Soon" (disabled), "Locked" (BYO required but not connected), "Free" (accent outline). Clicking a non-coming-soon available model calls `PATCH /api/v1/tenants/settings/preferred-model/` with `{ preferred_model }`. While any mutation is pending all cards get `opacity-60`. Clicking a BYO-gated model that is locked opens the Connect Anthropic modal instead.
inventory_notes: The active model is `tenant.effective_model || tenant.preferred_model || DEFAULT_MODEL`. The applied model is `tenant.applied_model || activeModel`. Divergence between these two triggers "Switching…". Limited-time offer cards are hidden when `offer.active` is false.

feature: AI Provider — limited-time free model offer banner
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to be informed about a free model offer so that I can take advantage of it without being charged tokens.
expected_behaviour: When `tenant.free_model_offer.active` is true, a bordered accent panel appears above the model grid explaining the offer model name and fallback model. The offer model card also has a "Free" badge. The offer model card only appears in `visibleModels` when `offerActive` is true (filtered out otherwise).

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to choose a lighter model for background tasks so that I stretch my monthly budget further.
expected_behaviour: The "Scheduled Task Models" section lists 5 tasks (Morning Briefing, Evening Check-in, Week Ahead Review, Background Tasks, Heartbeat Check-in). Each has a `<select>` with "Use default (current model)" plus all available active models. Changing any select immediately calls `PATCH /api/v1/tenants/settings/task-model-preferences/` with `{ task_model_preferences: { [task.slug]: selectedModelId } }` via `useTaskModelPreferencesMutation`. All selects are disabled while the mutation is pending. BYO-locked and limited-time-offer models (when offer inactive) are filtered from the per-task options.
inventory_notes: Changes are described as applying "within ~30 seconds".

feature: AI Provider — BYO Anthropic cards (when byo_models_enabled)
user_story: As a subscriber with BYO enabled on my tier, I want to see Anthropic and OpenAI provider cards so that I know I can bring my own credentials.
expected_behaviour: When `tenant.byo_models_enabled` is true, a "Use your own subscription" SectionCard appears above the model grid, containing `<BYOProviderCard>` for Anthropic and OpenAI. The Anthropic card shows: status pill (Not connected / pending / verified / error/expired), body text, eyebrow, and a CTA button. CTA label: "Connect →" (no cred), "Cancel" (pending), "Disconnect" (verified), "Reconnect →" (expired/error). OpenAI card is permanently `disabled` with a "Coming soon" badge. If the cred has `last_error` and is in error state, the error is shown in a rose box.
inventory_notes: The BYO section is completely hidden when byo_models_enabled is false.

feature: AI Provider — Connect Anthropic modal (2-step flow)
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to connect my Anthropic Claude account so that inference is billed to my own subscription.
expected_behaviour: Step 1 (info): Shows `claude setup-token` CLI command with a Copy button (clipboard API, 2.5 s "Copied" state). Shows privacy disclosure (encrypted, isolated container, revocable). Clicking "I have my token →" moves to Step 2. Step 2 (paste): A textarea (min 32 chars required, spellCheck off) for the OAuth token. "Connect" button is disabled until token ≥32 chars. On submit calls `POST /api/v1/tenants/byo-credentials/` with `{ provider:'anthropic', mode:'cli_subscription', token }`. On success modal closes and `onSettled` invalidates byo-credentials query. On error: 400 → format error, 502 → storage failure, other → generic. Modal always closeable via Escape, backdrop click, or ✕ button; closing mid-request aborts the fetch via `AbortController`. State resets 200 ms after close.
inventory_notes: The modal prevents the user from being trapped: Cancel always works even while the request is in flight (AbortController abort). Pending cred has no automatic verifier — the CTA on BYOProviderCard shows "Cancel" in pending state.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to disconnect my Anthropic subscription so that my assistant reverts to the platform's default model.
expected_behaviour: Modal opens with `open={true}` when `openModal === 'disconnect-anthropic'`. Shows: warning icon, fallback model name (computed from `DEFAULT_MODEL`), bullet list of consequences (credential deleted from Key Vault, container restarts ~30s, Claude Sonnet disappears from picker). "Disconnect" button calls `DELETE /api/v1/tenants/byo-credentials/<id>/`. On success modal closes; `onSettled` invalidates credentials. On error, rose alert appears in the modal. Cancel always available and aborts in-flight request. State resets 200 ms after close.

user_story: As an anonymous user, I want protected pages to redirect me to login so that I cannot access subscriber content without being authenticated.
expected_behaviour: On every route change, AppShell checks `isLoggedIn()` (presence of `nbhd_access_token` in localStorage). If the current pathname is not in the `publicPages` list and the token is absent, the shell immediately calls `router.replace('/login')`. No loading state is shown; the redirect fires synchronously on mount and on every pathname change via a `useEffect` dependency on `[pathname, isPublicPage, router]`.
inventory_notes: isLoggedIn() is a localStorage check only — an expired but present token still passes the guard. The reactive 401 → refresh → redirect in apiFetch is the real session-expiry enforcer.

user_story: As a user on landing, auth, or game pages, I want the page to fill the entire viewport without a header or tab bar so that immersive experiences are not obscured by navigation chrome.
expected_behaviour: AppShell checks whether the current pathname is in `fullBleedPages` (`/`, `/signup`, `/login`, `/forgot-password`, `/reset-password`, `/promo/redeem`, `/promo/redeemed`, `/onboarding`) or equals `/constellation/play`. If so, it renders only an `ErrorBoundary` wrapper, a skip-to-main-content link, and `<main>` — no header, no tab bar, no background layers. All other public pages (legal) get a minimal public layout with header and footer.
inventory_notes: The constellation play page (`/constellation/play`) is also full-bleed so the Phaser canvas owns the full viewport on mobile and touch events at the top/bottom edges are not stolen by sticky chrome.

user_story: As an anonymous user browsing legal or promo pages, I want a consistent header and footer with branding and navigation links so that I can orient myself and access important policy information.
expected_behaviour: Non-full-bleed public pages render a layout with: a top header containing `<BrandLogo size={32}>`, a centred content area `max-w-6xl`, and `<SiteFooter>` at the bottom. The background gradient and subtle grid overlay (`BackgroundLayers`) are present. No user menu or nav items are shown.

feature: Authenticated app shell — sticky header with scroll border
user_story: As a subscriber, I want a persistent header that shows the brand logo and navigation so that I can orientate myself and move between sections at any time.
expected_behaviour: The authenticated shell renders a sticky `<header>` at z-30. The header contains: brand logo (links to `/journal`), a centred pill-nav (desktop ≥lg only), a trial badge / trial dot (sm breakpoint distinction), and a user menu. The header has a transparent bottom border and no shadow at the top of the page; once the `#main-content` scroll container scrolls past 8px, a `border-b` and `shadow` animate in via a `headerBorder` state flag. The border resets when navigating to a new pathname (the scroll listener is re-attached).
inventory_notes: The scroll listener is attached to `#main-content` (the scrollable main element), not `window`. If that element is not yet in the DOM when the effect runs (e.g. on fast navigation), the listener is silently skipped.
error_detail:
The finding bundles two claims; one is real, the other is largely a misread.

REAL - nested anchor elements: In the authenticated shell the brand logo is rendered as a Link to /journal (app-shell.tsx:354-359) wrapping BrandLogo, but BrandLogo itself unconditionally renders its own Link to / (brand-logo.tsx:17). There is no prop to suppress that inner link (only showText exists). The result is an anchor (href=/journal) containing another anchor (href=/), which is invalid HTML. During parsing, browsers auto-close the outer anchor before the inner one, so the inner href=/ wins: clicking the logo navigates to / (the full-bleed landing/galaxy), NOT to /journal as the FA-0494 expected_behaviour requires. It is also an accessibility defect (ambiguous nesting, inconsistent keyboard activation). Impact is modest since the full nav bar offers other routes and / resolves sensibly for logged-in users, hence low severity. The public-page usage at app-shell.tsx:325 is NOT wrapped and is fine.

NOT A DEFECT - headerBorder reset: The claim that the border/shadow stays stuck on the next page is largely a misread. The scroll listener binds to #main-content, the overflow-y-auto scroll container that persists across client navigations (app-shell.tsx:406-408). On Next.js App Router navigation the framework resets this scroll container to top; that programmatic scroll fires a scroll event, which runs handleScroll then setHeaderBorder(main.scrollTop greater than 8) = false. So the border is recomputed to false on navigation in practice. The spec's own wording (border resets because the listener is re-attached) describes the wrong mechanism, but the user-visible outcome is correct. Adding an explicit setHeaderBorder(false) would only make it deterministic; its absence is not a real user-facing bug.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want the navigation to only show the sections I have enabled (Gravity, Fuel, Core) so that I do not see features I have not unlocked.
expected_behaviour: `useNavItems(tenant)` derives the nav list from the tenant object. Journal, Constellation, and Horizons are always shown. Gravity (`/finance`) is shown only if both `tenant.finance_enabled` AND `tenant.gravity_available` are true. Fuel (`/fuel`) shows only if `tenant.fuel_enabled`. Core (`/core`) shows only if `tenant.core_enabled`. Settings is always last. The same list is used for both the desktop pill nav and the mobile tab bar. If the tenant query is still loading, `tenant` is undefined and only the three always-visible items plus Settings appear.
inventory_notes: Gravity is double-gated: `finance_enabled` (per-tenant opt-in) AND `gravity_available` (platform-wide flag). A tenant who previously enabled finance but on a platform build where `gravity_available=false` will not see the tab.

user_story: As a subscriber on a large screen, I want a compact pill-shaped navigation bar in the header so that I can switch between sections without losing my place on the page.
expected_behaviour: Visible at `lg` breakpoint and above. Renders as a `<nav>` with `role=navigation` and `aria-label='Main navigation'`. Each nav item is a `<Link>` with `aria-current='page'` set on the active route. Active item gets accent background and text; inactive items show muted text with hover highlight. The active item is determined by `pathname.startsWith(item.href)`.
inventory_notes: `pathname.startsWith` means any sub-route of `/journal`, `/fuel`, etc. marks the parent as active. Both `/journal` and `/journal/documents/daily/today` would mark Journal as active.

user_story: As a subscriber on a mobile device, I want a bottom tab bar so that I can navigate to main sections with my thumb without reaching for a header.
expected_behaviour: Visible below `lg` breakpoint. Renders as a `<nav>` with `role=navigation` and `aria-label='Mobile navigation'`. Tabs are equally distributed (`flex-1`). The active tab shows an accent-coloured icon scaled up 110%, accent label text, and a small glowing dot below. Inactive tabs show `ink-faint` colour. Min touch target height is `44px` (explicit `min-h-[44px]`). Bottom padding uses `env(safe-area-inset-bottom)` for iPhone notch. Active state uses the same `pathname.startsWith` logic.
inventory_notes: The tab bar is positioned at the bottom of the flex column in the authenticated shell layout and sits outside the scrollable `<main>`. It is hidden on full-bleed pages.
error_detail: Confirmed. The active-tab dot at app-shell.tsx:247 is position:absolute (absolute bottom-1.5) with no left/right/inset, and no ancestor in the mobile tab bar is positioned: the parent Link (line 221: flex flex-1 flex-col items-center justify-center ... min-h-[44px]), the flex row div (line 212: flex items-center justify-around h-14), and the nav (line 207: z-[50] lg:hidden ... -- z-index without position is inert). Verified .mobile-tab-shadow (globals.css:474) only sets box-shadow and the only other nav rule (globals.css:553) is a print-media display:none. So the dot's containing block falls through to the root shell column at app-shell.tsx:336 (relative flex h-[100dvh] flex-col). Horizontally the dot keeps its static position (still under its tab), but bottom:6px resolves against the full 100dvh viewport, not the tab. Since MobileTabBar is the last flow child, the bar sits at viewport bottom with pb-[env(safe-area-inset-bottom)], so on notched iPhones (the primary target) the dot floats down into/below the safe-area inset at the physical screen bottom, detached from the active label rather than sitting just beneath it. Impact is purely cosmetic: a misplaced decorative glow dot on devices with a non-zero bottom inset. No functional/accessibility impact (aria-current conveys active state). The QA claim is correct in mechanism; the visible symptom is vertical detachment toward the screen bottom (horizontal static position is preserved).

user_story: As a subscriber, I want a user menu in the header so that I can see who I am signed in as and sign out.
expected_behaviour: A circular button in the top-right of the header shows the user's initials (first letter of each word in `display_name`, or first letter of email, max 2 characters, uppercase, monospace). Clicking toggles a dropdown panel that shows the display name or email (truncated) and a 'Sign out' button. The dropdown is dismissed on any click outside its container (`mousedown` listener on `document`). Clicking 'Sign out' calls `handleLogout()`, which POSTs to `POST /api/v1/auth/logout/` with the refresh token, then clears localStorage tokens (`clearTokens()`), clears the persisted React Query cache (`clearPersistedCache()`), and navigates to `/login`. The server-side revocation failure is caught and swallowed — logout always completes locally.
inventory_notes: Initials are computed from `display_name` splitting on spaces; a single-word name or email address yields one initial. The dropdown has `z-40` ensuring it overlays page content. `aria-expanded` is set on the button.

user_story: As a subscriber on trial, I want to see how many trial days I have remaining in the header so that I know when to subscribe.
expected_behaviour: The `TrialBadge` component renders only when `tenant.is_trial` is true. If the trial is active (`has_active_subscription` is false but trial not yet expired), it shows an accent-coloured badge with text `Trial: Xd` where X is `trial_days_remaining`. If the trial has ended (`is_trial && !has_active_subscription`), it shows a rose-coloured badge with 'Trial ended'. The badge is hidden on `sm` screens and below (uses `hidden sm:block`).
inventory_notes: The tenant data is embedded in the `me` response (`me.tenant`), not a separate tenant query.

user_story: As a subscriber on trial using a mobile device, I want a visual indicator of my trial status in the header so that I am aware without taking up tab-bar space.
expected_behaviour: `TrialDot` renders only when `tenant.is_trial` is true. On `sm` screens it replaces the text badge with a 2px coloured dot (accent = active, rose = ended). On hover, a tooltip appears above the dot showing either the days remaining or 'Trial ended'. Visible only on `sm` and below (uses `block sm:hidden`).
inventory_notes: The tooltip uses CSS `group-hover:block` — it is not accessible to keyboard or screen reader users.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want a graceful error screen if a page crashes so that I can recover without a blank white screen.
expected_behaviour: The `ErrorBoundary` class component wraps all authenticated page content inside `<main>`. When any descendent throws during render, `getDerivedStateFromError` sets `hasError=true`. If no custom `fallback` prop is provided, a centred panel is shown with: a `Runtime Error` mono label, an `h2` with 'Something went wrong', the error message, and a 'Try again' button that resets `hasError` to false (re-mounts the children). The in-shell variant uses a custom fallback: a rose-bordered box with 'Something went wrong loading this page.' and a link back to `/journal`. A root-level `ErrorBoundary` wraps the entire `Providers` tree without a fallback (falls through to the default).
inventory_notes: The 'Try again' button re-renders children; it does not refetch data or reload the page. If the underlying error is persistent (e.g. a type error on every render pass), the boundary will immediately re-enter the error state.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want brief success or error notifications to appear at the bottom of the screen so that I know whether my actions succeeded without losing my place.
expected_behaviour: A module-level pub/sub emitter (`_globalListeners`, `emitToast`) sends toast events to the single mounted `<GlobalToastHost>` in `Providers`. `Toast` animates in (`opacity-100 translate-y-0`) 10ms after mount and starts its dismiss animation 300ms before `durationMs` (default 3000ms) expires. The toast renders fixed at `bottom-6 left-1/2 -translate-x-1/2 z-50` as a rounded pill. Error toasts are rose-600; success toasts are emerald-600. The toast has `role=status aria-live=polite`. `emitToast` is also callable from outside React (QueryClient `onError` default). Mutations fire an error toast automatically for all non-401, non-skipErrorToast errors via the QueryClient `defaultOptions.mutations.onError` handler.
inventory_notes: Only one toast is visible at a time — a new `emitToast` call replaces the current toast via `setToast`. `useToast` is a secondary API for components that manage their own local toast state; both share the same `_toastCounter` but only `emitToast` reaches the global host.

feature: React Query provider with cross-session cache persistence
user_story: As a subscriber, I want the app to load with my recent data visible immediately so that I do not see a loading spinner every time I return.
expected_behaviour: `Providers` creates a `QueryClient` with `staleTime=60s`, `gcTime=24h`, `refetchOnWindowFocus=true`, `retry=1`. On construction, `seedQueryClient` synchronously reads `localStorage['nbhd_qc_v3']` and hydrates matching queries (persisted prefixes: me, tenant, preferences, personas, sidebar-tree, horizons, constellation, galaxy, fuel-*) with their saved data AND original fetch timestamp. After mount, `installPersistence` subscribes to the QueryCache and debounce-flushes (500ms) updated queries matching the prefix list back to localStorage. On logout, `clearPersistedCache` removes the storage key.
inventory_notes: The cache key is `nbhd_qc_v3`; a version bump invalidates all persisted data on the next load. Data is per-browser not per-user — if two users share a browser, the second user sees the first user's cached data until queries re-fetch.

feature: Proactive JWT refresh (pre-expiry and reactive 401 retry)
user_story: As a subscriber, I want API calls to succeed transparently even when my access token is about to expire so that I am not interrupted by session errors mid-task.
expected_behaviour: `apiFetch` decodes the JWT `exp` claim (without signature verification) and proactively calls the deduped refresh if the token expires within 60 seconds. If the proactive refresh fails, the stale token is used and the reactive path takes over. On a 401 response, `apiFetch` calls the deduped `getDedupedRefresh()` and retries the original request with the new token. Concurrent callers that hit 401 simultaneously all await the same in-flight refresh promise. If a refresh fails (e.g. refresh token expired), tokens are cleared and the user is redirected to `/login` (if not already there). On a 401 with no prior refresh token, the error detail from the server response is surfaced to the caller (used to show password-reset CTAs on the login page).
inventory_notes: Tokens are stored in localStorage (`nbhd_access_token`, `nbhd_refresh_token`). The refresh endpoint may rotate the refresh token (server config `ROTATE_REFRESH_TOKENS`); the response `refresh` field is persisted if present. A hard redirect via `window.location.href` is used (not `router.push`) because `apiFetch` has no access to the Next.js router.

feature: Tenant-polling for model switch apply confirmation
user_story: As a subscriber who has just changed my AI model, I want the UI to automatically detect when the new model is active so that I see a live confirmation without refreshing the page.
expected_behaviour: `useTenantQuery` polls `GET /api/v1/tenants/me/` every 5 seconds while `tenant.preferred_model !== tenant.applied_model`. Once `applied_model` matches `preferred_model`, polling stops. Background polling (`refetchIntervalInBackground=false`) is disabled to avoid waking hibernated containers for no reason. The UI (on the settings/AI model page) reads `applied_model` to show a 'Switching…' or 'Active' badge.
inventory_notes: The polling is conditional: `refetchInterval` returns `false` (no polling) when data is not yet loaded or when models are already in sync.

user_story: As a new subscriber whose account is being provisioned, I want the UI to automatically move past a provisioning screen once my container is ready so that I am not stuck waiting.
expected_behaviour: `useProvisioningStatusQuery` polls `GET /api/v1/tenants/provisioning-status/` every 5 seconds while `ready` is false. Once `ready` is true, polling stops. This query is used by pages/components that gate on container readiness. `useRetryProvisioningMutation` calls `POST /api/v1/tenants/retry-provisioning/` and then invalidates `provisioning-status`, `me`, and `tenant` queries so the UI re-evaluates immediately.
inventory_notes: The retry endpoint returns `retry_after_seconds` when the system is rate-limiting retries; the frontend does not enforce this delay — it is informational only.

user_story: As a subscriber who has pending config updates, I want the UI to notify me when the update has been applied so that I know my assistant is running with the latest settings.
expected_behaviour: `useRefreshConfigStatusQuery` polls `GET /api/v1/tenants/refresh-config/` every 15 seconds ONLY while `has_pending_update` is true; otherwise polling is off and window-focus refetch covers the case. `useRefreshConfigMutation` calls `POST /api/v1/tenants/refresh-config/` and invalidates the status query on success.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want status labels to use consistent colour-coded pills so that I can quickly recognise the meaning of a status at a glance.
expected_behaviour: `StatusPill` maps a `status` string to a background/text colour pair from a fixed `tones` lookup table covering: active, paused, pending, running, succeeded, failed, skipped, manual, schedule, provisioning, suspended, deprovisioning, deleted, revoked, expired, error, low, medium, high, thumbs-up, thumbs-down, meh, done, in_progress, rescheduled, rest. Unknown statuses fall back to a slate tone. The `displayLabels` map overrides the rendered label: `suspended` → 'paused', `in_progress` → 'in progress'. Accepts `size` prop (`sm` or `md`) which changes padding and font size. Colours are CSS variables from the design system.
inventory_notes: All colours come from CSS variables (`var(--status-emerald-bg)` etc.), not hardcoded hex. Label is `capitalize` via Tailwind, but `displayLabels` overrides may override capitalisation for multi-word values.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want content areas to show a pulsing placeholder while data is loading so that the layout does not shift when data arrives.
expected_behaviour: `Skeleton` renders a `div` with `animate-pulse rounded bg-border`. Accepts a `className` for sizing. Two pre-built composite skeletons: `StatCardSkeleton` renders a card-shaped skeleton with three bars (header, value, subtext); `SectionCardSkeleton` (accepts `lines` prop, default 3) renders a card with a title bar, subtitle bar, and N content lines. These are used as loading states in dashboard and settings sections while data queries are fetching.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to read structured markdown content (headings, lists, tables, code blocks, checkboxes) rendered properly so that my journal documents and AI responses are legible.
expected_behaviour:
`MarkdownRenderer` uses `react-markdown` with `remark-gfm` (GitHub Flavored Markdown: tables, strikethrough, task lists) and `remark-breaks` (newlines as `
`). Prose styling is applied via Tailwind typography classes. When an `onCheckboxToggle` callback is provided, GFM task-list checkboxes are rendered as interactive inputs: clicking scans the raw markdown for the matching checkbox by state (checked/unchecked) and calls `onCheckboxToggle(lineIndex, newChecked)`. Without the callback, checkboxes are rendered as disabled. Tables overflow horizontally (`overflow-x-auto`). The component is `React.memo`'d — it only re-runs the remark pipeline when `content` or `onCheckboxToggle` changes.
inventory_notes: The checkbox-matching algorithm finds the FIRST checkbox whose current checked state matches `props.checked`. This is fragile for documents with multiple checkboxes of the same state in sequence — the wrong line index may be reported. It also uses `===` on booleans so `props.checked` must be a real boolean, not null/undefined.
error_detail: Confirmed real. In markdown-renderer.tsx the checkbox onChange handler (handleChange) does NOT know which checkbox was clicked. It ignores the clicked input's identity/position and instead scans the raw markdown top-to-bottom for the FIRST line whose task-list state matches props.checked (regex match comparing isChecked === checked at lines 37-47), then reports that line's index via onCheckboxToggle(i, !checked).
