For any document with two or more checkboxes sharing the same state in document order (e.g. two unchecked '- [ ]' items), clicking any of them after the first always resolves to the FIRST one's line index. The receiving handler handleCheckboxToggle in document-view.tsx:344-365 rewrites that line ([ ]->[x] or [x]->[ ]) and persists it via updateMutation.mutate PATCH. Net effect: the user clicks checkbox B, but checkbox A is toggled and saved — a silent wrong-item mutation on journal task lists. It is wired into the live journal read path (document-view.tsx:523-525) for all editable (non-typedManaged) documents, so it affects real subscriber daily-note/task-list interactions, not a dead path. Same-state checkbox runs are the common case for to-do lists, so this fires routinely rather than as an edge case.

user_story: As a subscriber using the Gravity finance feature, I want each of my accounts displayed as a card with balance, type, and payoff progress so that I can see my financial position at a glance.
expected_behaviour: `AccountCard` renders a `<article>` with: an emoji icon (type-mapped), account nickname, type label, APR (if present), monthly minimum payment (if present), and current balance formatted as USD. For debt accounts with a `payoff_progress` value, a `<div role='progressbar'>` bar is shown with `aria-valuenow/min/max` and `aria-label='X% paid off'`. Below the bar, '% Paid' and 'Original balance' labels appear. If `onArchive` is provided, a circular archive icon button appears on hover (opacity-0 → opacity-100 on group hover, always visible on mobile). Debt accounts use accent colour; savings accounts use signal (teal) colour.
dependencies: FinanceAccount type from lib/types.ts; rendered in the Gravity (Finance) area
inventory_notes: The archive button has `aria-label='Archive {nickname}'` and `min-h-[44px]` but the visual affordance is hidden on desktop behind group-hover — keyboard users can still focus and activate it via `focus-visible:opacity-100`.
error_detail: Verified. On mobile (below the sm breakpoint) AccountCard stacks into a column (line 58: flex flex-col sm:flex-row); the balance/monthly-payment row (line 70) carries pl-13 sm:pl-0 to indent it ~52px so the figures align under the account nickname, which begins after the 40px icon + 12px gap. But pl-13 is not a real Tailwind class: Tailwind v3.4.19 (installed) default spacing jumps 12 (3rem) to 14 (3.5rem) with no 13 step (confirmed via tailwindcss/defaultTheme), and tailwind.config.ts extend.spacing (lines 12-19) only adds fluid-* and page keys, never 13. The class is silently dropped by the JIT compiler, so the row gets 0 left padding on mobile and the Monthly/Balance figures sit at the card content's left edge instead of indented under the nickname -- a small left-misalignment on narrow screens, on every AccountCard. One nuance vs the report wording: the figures are NOT flush against the card's outer edge (the parent glass-card has p-6 sm:p-8 padding) and text-right still right-aligns text within each value box; the real symptom is the lost ~52px indent / misalignment, not edge-clipping. Cosmetic, mobile-only -- hence low severity.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want the app to always use the dark theme so that it matches the Constellation brand aesthetic.
expected_behaviour: `ThemeProvider` on mount (client-only, `useEffect`) sets `document.documentElement.setAttribute('data-theme', 'dark')`, `colorScheme = 'dark'`, and updates the `<meta name='theme-color'>` to `#0b0f13`. The `Theme` type is a literal `'dark'` — `setTheme` and `toggleTheme` are no-ops. No toggle UI exists.
inventory_notes: ThemeProvider is NOT imported/used in providers.tsx or app-shell.tsx in the code read. Its usage must be in the root layout (not audited here). The context API is exposed (`useTheme`) but the toggle is intentionally a no-op.

feature: Site footer with legal and contact links
user_story: As a user on a public page, I want a footer with links to legal policies and contact information so that I can access important information and reach support.
expected_behaviour: `SiteFooter` renders a `<footer role='contentinfo'>` with: a logo link to `/` (mono, uppercase, tracking-wide), and a `<nav aria-label='Footer links'>` with links to `/legal/terms`, `/legal/privacy`, `/legal/refund`, `/legal/commerce-disclosure` (Japanese commerce law page, shown in Japanese characters '特定商取引法'), and a `mailto:mj@bywayofmj.com` contact link. All links use `hover:text-ink-muted` transition. Only shown on public (non-full-bleed, non-authenticated) pages.
inventory_notes: The commerce disclosure link points to `/legal/commerce-disclosure` which is not in the `publicPages` array in AppShell — if that route exists, AppShell would guard it (redirect to login). Verify that route is in publicPages or that it uses the `pathname.startsWith('/legal/')` check that is already present.

user_story: As a developer monitoring performance, I want Core Web Vitals (LCP, FID/INP, CLS, FCP, TTFB) logged to the console so that I can measure real-user performance without a third-party service.
expected_behaviour: `WebVitals` is mounted in `Providers` and calls `useReportWebVitals`. Each metric fires a `console.log` with format `[web-vitals] NAME=value rating=rating id=id`. CLS values are formatted to 3 decimal places; all others are rounded to integers. No data is sent to any external service.
inventory_notes: This is a development/monitoring aid, not a user-facing feature. It renders `null` (no DOM output). In production builds the hook is still active and logs to the console.

feature: Background visual layers (gradient + grid + synapse network)
user_story: As a subscriber, I want a subtle branded background on every authenticated page so that the app feels cohesive and premium without distracting from content.
expected_behaviour: `BackgroundLayers` renders three `pointer-events-none fixed inset-0 -z-10` layers: (1) a gradient from `var(--bg-gradient)`, (2) a subtle crosshatch grid at `var(--grid-opacity)`, (3) a static (non-animated) `SynapseNetwork` canvas at 4% opacity. Using `animated={false}` avoids continuous full-viewport compositing overhead while keeping the visual texture. These layers are rendered on all public (non-full-bleed) and authenticated layouts.
inventory_notes: BackgroundLayers is rendered in both the public page variant and the authenticated variant of AppShell. Full-bleed pages do not get it.

user_story: As a subscriber using a keyboard or screen reader, I want a skip link so that I can bypass the navigation and jump directly to the page content.
expected_behaviour: An `<a href='#main-content'>` with class `skip-link` is rendered as the first focusable element in every AppShell layout variant (full-bleed, public, and authenticated). The `#main-content` id is set on the `<main>` element. The skip link is visually hidden by default and should become visible on focus (styling defined in global CSS as `.skip-link` — not visible in the component files).
inventory_notes: The visual appearance of the skip link depends on `.skip-link` CSS in globals.css, which was not read as part of this audit.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to toggle the Gravity finance module on or off so that I can control whether the AI assistant has access to my financial data and tools.
expected_behaviour: PATCH /api/v1/finance/settings/ with {"finance_enabled": bool}. Requires JWT auth (IsAuthenticated). If enabling and the GRAVITY_ENABLED settings flag is False (platform-level kill switch), returns HTTP 403 with error=gravity_paused. On enable (off→on), clears the 'finance' key from tenant.welcomes_sent so the welcome re-fires. Persists tenant.finance_enabled, calls tenant.bump_pending_config(), and enqueues apply_single_tenant_config via QStash. Returns {finance_enabled, restart_required} — restart_required is True when finance_enabled changed and the tenant has a container_id, signalling the frontend to prompt the user to confirm a restart.
dependencies: QStash (apply_single_tenant_config task), apps/cron/publish.py, Tenant model (finance_enabled, welcomes_sent, container_id)
inventory_notes: GRAVITY_ENABLED is a platform-wide fail-safe-off kill switch in Django settings; production default is False. Disabling is always allowed regardless of platform pause. Plugin change only takes effect after container restart.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to restart my assistant after enabling Gravity so that the finance plugin is loaded into the running session.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/finance/restart/ — JWT auth required. Returns 400 if tenant has no container_id. Calls restart_container_app(tenant.container_id) via the Azure client; on failure returns HTTP 502 with error=restart_failed. If finance_enabled is True on the tenant, enqueues schedule_finance_welcome via QStash with a 90-second delay to allow the container cold-start before the welcome cron fires. Returns {restarted: true} on success.
dependencies: Azure Container Apps (restart_container_app), QStash (schedule_finance_welcome task, 90s delay)
inventory_notes: The 90s delay is intentional — the welcome cron targets the running container's Gateway API, which won't be reachable during the cold-start window.

user_story: As the system, I want to schedule a one-shot welcome cron 5 minutes after finance is enabled so that the subscriber receives a warm introduction to Gravity from their AI assistant.
expected_behaviour: Invoked via QStash task schedule_finance_welcome → apps/finance/tasks.py:schedule_finance_welcome_task. Looks up the tenant; no-ops if tenant not found or finance_active is False. Calls _schedule_finance_welcome → apps/orchestrator/welcome_scheduler.py:schedule_welcome with feature='finance', cron_name='_finance:welcome'. schedule_welcome checks tenant.welcomes_sent['finance']: if already delivered, skips. If a pending cron exists with a future fire date within the valid window, skips (idempotent). If a stale cron exists (date already passed), removes it and reschedules. Creates a one-shot cron on the Gateway API firing ~5 minutes out. The cron prompt instructs the agent to send a welcome via nbhd_send_to_user, then mark delivery by POSTing to /api/v1/tenants/runtime/{tenant_id}/welcomes/finance/.
dependencies: QStash (task dispatch), OpenClaw Gateway API (cron creation), Tenant.welcomes_sent, apps/tenants runtime welcome-mark endpoint
inventory_notes: Also reconciled by the daily reconcile_welcomes fleet watchdog (apps/orchestrator/tasks.py) which retries for any finance-active tenant that never received the welcome. Fire-and-forget: task-level exceptions are swallowed to avoid blocking the toggle flow.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to view and create finance accounts (debts and savings) so that I can set up my Gravity ledger from the web console or iOS app.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/finance/accounts/ — returns active accounts (is_active=True) by default; pass ?archived=true for archived accounts. Scoped to request.user.tenant; returns 404 if no tenant. POST /api/v1/finance/accounts/ — creates a new FinanceAccount; serializer sets tenant from context; original_balance is auto-set to current_balance on creation if not provided. Both require JWT auth. Validation via FinanceAccountSerializer (DRF ModelSerializer); invalid data returns HTTP 400 with field errors. Returns HTTP 201 on create.
inventory_notes: Unlike the runtime upsert path, the consumer POST always creates a new row (no nickname dedup). account_type must be one of the AccountType choices.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to update or archive a finance account so that I can correct its details or hide it when it is no longer relevant.
expected_behaviour: PATCH /api/v1/finance/accounts/<uuid:account_id>/ — partial update via FinanceAccountSerializer; scoped to tenant (returns 404 if account does not belong to the user's tenant). DELETE /api/v1/finance/accounts/<uuid:account_id>/ — soft-delete: sets is_active=False and saves update_fields=['is_active', 'updated_at']; returns HTTP 204. Both require JWT auth. Returns 404 if account does not exist for this tenant.
inventory_notes: Hard delete is not exposed. Archived accounts are excluded from totals and payoff calculations but remain queryable with ?archived=true.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to view and record financial transactions so that my debt balances are updated when I make payments or incur charges.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/finance/transactions/ — returns last 50 transactions for the tenant ordered by -date, -created_at, with account.nickname included. POST /api/v1/finance/transactions/ — requires amount (Decimal-coercible), optional date (ISO YYYY-MM-DD; defaults to today), optional account_id or account (UUID), optional account_nickname (fuzzy fallback), optional transaction_type (default 'payment'), optional description. Resolves the account via services.resolve_account (UUID exact → nickname iexact → nickname icontains). Delegates to services.record_transaction which acquires a SELECT FOR UPDATE lock on the account, checks for an existing (tenant, account, transaction_type, amount, date) tuple, and returns the existing row with duplicate=True + HTTP 200 on a dedup hit, or creates the row and mutates balance (payments/refunds decrease balance; charges/interest increase it) + HTTP 201. Returns 400 for invalid amount or date; 404 if account not found.
dependencies: Tenant model, FinanceAccount model, FinanceTransaction model
inventory_notes: The dedup key is (tenant, account, transaction_type, amount, date) — NOT description. A transfer type has no balance side-effect. Balance is clamped at max(0, current - payment) for payment/refund types.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to view a consolidated finance dashboard so that I can see my total debt, savings, active payoff plan, snapshots, and recent transactions in one call.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/finance/dashboard/ — JWT auth required; 404 if no tenant. Loads all active accounts, splits into debt_accounts and savings_accounts by account_type (DEBT_TYPES set). Computes total_debt, total_savings, total_minimum_payments. Fetches the first active PayoffPlan, last 12 FinanceSnapshots, and last 10 FinanceTransactions. Serializes via FinanceDashboardSerializer (composite of sub-serializers). Returns HTTP 200 with the aggregate payload. No pagination — bounded by the hardcoded :12 and :10 slices.
dependencies: Tenant model, FinanceAccount model, FinanceTransaction model, PayoffPlan model, FinanceSnapshot model
inventory_notes: Savings and checking and emergency_fund account types are excluded from the debt total. Only one plan can be 'active' at a time — the latest active plan is returned.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to view saved payoff plans so that I can review previously calculated debt payoff strategies.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/finance/payoff-plans/ — JWT auth required; 404 if no tenant. Returns all PayoffPlan rows for the tenant (active and inactive) ordered by -created_at. Serialized via PayoffPlanSerializer including schedule_json (the full month-by-month schedule). No filtering or pagination enforced at this endpoint.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to view monthly finance snapshots so that I can track my debt reduction progress over time.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/finance/snapshots/ — JWT auth required; 404 if no tenant. Returns last 24 FinanceSnapshot rows for the tenant ordered by -date. Each snapshot contains total_debt, total_savings, total_payments_this_month, and accounts_json (point-in-time account balances). Serialized via FinanceSnapshotSerializer.

feature: Runtime Finance Account List & Upsert (OpenClaw Plugin)
user_story: As the OpenClaw AI container, I want to list and create/update finance accounts via the internal API so that the assistant can set up and maintain the user's financial ledger during a session.
expected_behaviour: Authenticated via X-NBHD-Internal-Key + X-NBHD-Tenant-Id headers (validate_internal_runtime_request); returns 401 on auth failure. Sets RLS context with service_role=True. GET /api/v1/finance/runtime/<tenant_id>/accounts/ — returns active accounts by default; ?archived=true for inactive; ?archived=all for all. POST /api/v1/finance/runtime/<tenant_id>/accounts/ — requires nickname and current_balance. Upserts by (tenant, nickname__iexact, is_active=True): if a matching active account exists it is updated; otherwise created. On creation, original_balance is set from current_balance if not already set. Returns {id, nickname, account_type, current_balance, created} with HTTP 201 on create and 200 on update.
inventory_notes: Upsert is case-insensitive on nickname. Invalid account_type values silently fall back to 'other_debt'. The ?archived=all parameter returns all rows regardless of is_active.

user_story: As the OpenClaw AI container, I want to record a payment or transaction against an account so that the user's debt balance is updated when they report making a payment.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/finance/runtime/<tenant_id>/transactions/ — internal auth required (X-NBHD-Internal-Key + X-NBHD-Tenant-Id). Resolves account by account_nickname (fuzzy match). Requires amount; date defaults to date.today() if absent or unparseable. Delegates to services.record_transaction which atomically checks for duplicate (tenant, account, type, amount, date) via SELECT FOR UPDATE, returns existing row with duplicate=True + HTTP 200 on hit; on a new record creates the FinanceTransaction and mutates account.current_balance, returns HTTP 201. Returns 404 if account not found; 400 if amount invalid.
dependencies: apps/integrations/internal_auth.py, Tenant model, FinanceAccount model, FinanceTransaction model
inventory_notes: This is the idempotent path that closes the May 2026 agent-retry double-debit incident. Dedup key: (tenant, account, transaction_type, amount, date).

user_story: As the OpenClaw AI container, I want to set an account balance directly so that the user can correct a balance without recording a formal transaction.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/finance/runtime/<tenant_id>/balance/ — internal auth required. Resolves account by account_nickname or nickname field (iexact first, then icontains fallback). Requires new_balance (Decimal-coercible). Updates account.current_balance in place and saves update_fields=['current_balance', 'updated_at']. Returns {account_nickname, old_balance, new_balance} with HTTP 200. Returns 404 if no active account matches the nickname; 400 if new_balance is invalid.
inventory_notes: No dedup or transaction row is created — this is a raw balance override. No minimum-value clamp is enforced; the agent can set any non-negative (or even negative) value.

user_story: As the OpenClaw AI container, I want to archive an account so that paid-off or closed accounts are removed from active totals and payoff calculations.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/finance/runtime/<tenant_id>/accounts/archive/ — internal auth required. Requires account_nickname. Resolves the active account (iexact then icontains). Sets is_active=False, saves update_fields=['is_active', 'updated_at']. Returns {account_nickname, archived: true, previous_balance}. Returns 400 if nickname missing; 404 if no active account matches.
inventory_notes: Archived accounts remain in the database and can be restored via the unarchive endpoint. Balance is preserved.

user_story: As the OpenClaw AI container, I want to restore a previously archived account so that an account that was closed by mistake can be reactivated.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/finance/runtime/<tenant_id>/accounts/unarchive/ — internal auth required. Requires account_nickname. Searches is_active=False accounts (iexact then icontains). If an active account with the same nickname (iexact) already exists, returns HTTP 409 with error=name_collision and a detail message — the agent must rename one before proceeding. On success, sets is_active=True, saves, and returns {account_nickname, unarchived: true, current_balance}. Returns 400 if nickname missing; 404 if no archived account matches.
inventory_notes: The collision check protects the upsert uniqueness invariant relied upon by RuntimeFinanceAccountsView.post(). Only one active account per nickname (case-insensitive) is allowed.

feature: Runtime Payoff Plan Calculation & Save (OpenClaw Plugin)
user_story: As the OpenClaw AI container, I want to calculate and optionally save a debt payoff plan so that I can present the subscriber with a concrete timeline for eliminating their debts.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/finance/runtime/<tenant_id>/payoff/calculate/ — internal auth required. Requires monthly_budget (Decimal). Optional strategy ('snowball', 'avalanche', or 'hybrid'); if absent or invalid, all three strategies are computed and returned. Loads all active non-savings accounts with balance > 0 as DebtInput objects. Calls services.calculate_payoff (single strategy) or services.compare_strategies (all three). If save=true and strategy is specified, deactivates all existing plans (bulk update is_active=False) and creates a new PayoffPlan row. Returns {results: {strategy: {total_debt, total_interest, payoff_months, payoff_date, schedule}}}. Returns empty message if no active debts exist; 400 if monthly_budget invalid.
dependencies: apps/integrations/internal_auth.py, FinanceAccount model, PayoffPlan model, apps/finance/services.py
inventory_notes: The algorithm uses a 600-month (50-year) safety cap to prevent infinite loops. Savings/checking/emergency_fund account types are excluded from the debt list by account_type filter.
error_detail: Confirmed in apps/finance/runtime_views.py (RuntimeFinancePayoffView.post). When strategy is a non-empty but unrecognised string (e.g. "waterfall"), the guard at line 410 `if strategy and strategy in ("snowball","avalanche","hybrid")` is False, so execution falls to the else-branch (lines 413-415) which populates `results` only with keys snowball/avalanche/hybrid from compare_strategies. The save guard at line 419 is `if save and strategy`, which is truthy for any non-empty string, so when save=true the code reaches line 420 `result_data = results[strategy]` -> `results["waterfall"]` -> KeyError. There is no custom DRF exception handler defined in config or apps, so a raw KeyError is not caught by DRF (which only converts APIException/Http404/PermissionDenied) and surfaces as HTTP 500. There is no upstream validation of the strategy value (`strategy = body.get("strategy")`, line 383). Real impact: the OpenClaw AI agent calling this internal runtime endpoint with a mistyped strategy plus save=true gets an internal server error instead of a clean 400. Impact is bounded -- internal agent-only endpoint (not public), the trigger requires both a mistyped strategy AND save=true, and a correct strategy or save=false works fine; hence low severity rather than medium.

user_story: As the OpenClaw AI container, I want to fetch a concise financial overview so that I can ground a cron or conversational turn in the user's current financial state.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/finance/runtime/<tenant_id>/summary/ — internal auth required. Loads all active accounts, splits into debt and savings by DEBT_TYPES. Returns total_debt, total_savings, total_minimum_payments, debt_account_count, savings_account_count, a per-account list with nickname/type/balance/interest_rate/minimum_payment/due_day/is_debt/payoff_progress, and the active_plan (strategy, monthly_budget, payoff_months, payoff_date, total_interest) or null. HTTP 200.
inventory_notes: This is the lightweight read-only context endpoint; the richer query endpoint supports filtering/aggregation.

user_story: As the OpenClaw AI container, I want to run structured queries against the finance ledger so that I can answer the user's specific questions about spending, balances, and payoff progress without pulling all data.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/finance/runtime/<tenant_id>/query/ — internal auth via BaseQueryView (validate_internal_runtime_request + RLS). Accepts a JSON body conforming to FinanceQueryRequest (schema_version=1, resource, window, filter, fields, aggregate, aggregate_field, group_by, order_by, limit 1-500). Resources: 'accounts', 'transactions', 'plan'. Transactions require a window parameter (use {'kind':'all'} to opt out). Filters are validated against per-resource allowlists; unknown filter keys return 400. Supports sum/count/avg/min/max aggregates with optional group_by. Field projection via 'fields' list (strict allowlist; unknown fields return 400). Returns {data: [...], meta: {row_count, ...}} via BaseQueryView envelope.
dependencies: apps/common/query_view.py:BaseQueryView, apps/integrations/internal_auth.py, FinanceAccount model, FinanceTransaction model, PayoffPlan model
inventory_notes: extra='forbid' on FinanceQueryRequest means any unknown top-level key returns a Pydantic validation error (400). account_nickname group_by for transactions resolves through account__nickname join. Accounts default to is_active=True filter unless explicitly overridden.
error_detail: Confirmed real. For resource=accounts, the order_by allowlist (_ALLOWED_FIELDS, query_views.py:80-81) includes is_debt and payoff_progress, and the group_by allowlist (_ALLOWED_GROUP_BY, query_views.py:123) includes is_debt. On FinanceAccount these are Python @property methods (models.py:81-91), not DB columns. The validators only test membership in those allowlists and do not distinguish properties from real columns. So order_by=is_debt or order_by=payoff_progress passes _order's check (line 318) and reaches qs.order_by(...) at line 323; group_by=is_debt passes _validate_group_by (lines 355-356) and reaches qs.values(is_debt) in _aggregate at line 300. Django's ORM raises FieldError for a non-field name in either order_by() or values(). The handler BaseQueryView.post (apps/common/query_view.py:148-155) catches only QueryExecutionError, so FieldError is uncaught and DRF returns HTTP 500 (no custom EXCEPTION_HANDLER configured in config/). The is_debt FILTER path is correctly translated to account_type__in (lines 189-193) and is unaffected. Existing tests cover order_by=magic (unknown -> 400, tests.py:2302) but never exercise the property-named cases, so the gap is untested. User impact: the AI assistant (nbhd_gravity_query) can emit a syntactically valid query (e.g. order_by=payoff_progress for accounts closest to payoff, or group_by=is_debt for debt vs non-debt totals) and receive a 500 instead of the endpoint's intended strict-400 contract. Agent-only internal read surface; no data corruption.

user_story: As the OpenClaw AI container, I want to compute a month-by-month debt payoff schedule so that I can give the subscriber a concrete plan and projected payoff date.
expected_behaviour: Pure calculation service in apps/finance/services.py:calculate_payoff. Takes a list of DebtInput objects, a monthly_budget Decimal, and a strategy string. Applies monthly interest accrual (APR/1200 per month), pays minimums first from budget, then allocates remaining budget by strategy priority: snowball (lowest balance first), avalanche (highest APR first), hybrid (weighted score: 60% highest-rate + 40% lowest-balance). Iterates up to 600 months. Returns a PayoffResult datatype with total_debt, total_interest, payoff_months, payoff_date, and a month-by-month schedule list. compare_strategies runs all three in sequence.
inventory_notes: Amounts are rounded to 2 decimal places at each step using ROUND_HALF_UP. The 600-month cap means truly unaffordable plans (budget < total minimums) will max out and show incomplete schedules.

user_story: As the system, I want transaction recording to be idempotent on (account, type, amount, date) so that agent retries after silent timeouts do not double-debit the user's balance.
expected_behaviour: services.record_transaction opens an atomic transaction, acquires SELECT FOR UPDATE on the FinanceAccount row, queries for an existing FinanceTransaction matching (tenant, account, transaction_type, amount, date). On a hit: returns the existing row's data with duplicate=True and created=False (no balance change). On a miss: creates the FinanceTransaction, mutates current_balance (payment/refund subtract; charge/interest add; transfer has no effect), saves the account, returns the new row with duplicate=False and created=True. Both paths return the same (payload dict, bool) tuple shape.
dependencies: FinanceAccount model, FinanceTransaction model, Django atomic transactions
inventory_notes: SELECT FOR UPDATE serialises concurrent writes per account (e.g. simultaneous agent retries). Description field is not part of the dedup key — same transaction with different description is deduplicated.

user_story: As the system, I want to capture a point-in-time monthly snapshot of each tenant's balances so that the user and the AI assistant can track debt reduction progress over time.
expected_behaviour: apps/finance/snapshot.py:create_monthly_snapshots is designed to be called on the 1st of each month (via QStash or a management command). Queries all Tenants where finance_enabled=True and status=ACTIVE. For each tenant: skips if a FinanceSnapshot with that date already exists (idempotent). Loads active accounts, computes total_debt, total_savings, and total_payments_this_month (sum of payment transactions in the previous calendar month). Creates a FinanceSnapshot with accounts_json as a point-in-time list. Returns the count of snapshots created. Per-tenant exceptions are caught and logged without aborting the run.
dependencies: Tenant model, FinanceAccount model, FinanceTransaction model, FinanceSnapshot model
inventory_notes: Not yet registered as a named QStash task in TASK_MAP (no 'monthly_finance_snapshot' entry found in cron/views.py). Covered only in tests; caller must be confirmed before relying on scheduled delivery. unique_together=(tenant, date) prevents double-write.
error_detail: Both sub-claims confirmed in code; impact is latent (no production consumer or caller). (1) Count mismatch: create_monthly_snapshots increments created_count after every _create_snapshot_for_tenant call that does not raise. The helper returns None without raising for the duplicate-skip (snapshot.py:46-47) and the no-accounts skip (snapshot.py:49-51), so the count reflects tenants-attempted, not snapshots-created, contradicting the docstring at snapshot.py:23 ('Returns the number of snapshots created'). Tests codify the mismatch: test_idempotent_no_duplicate_snapshots asserts count==1 on a no-op re-run (tests.py:1437-1438); test_skips_tenants_without_accounts asserts count==1 with a comment 'counts tenants attempted' while FinanceSnapshot.objects.count()==0 (tests.py:1447-1450). (2) Not scheduled: the only callers are in apps/finance/tests.py; there is no TASK_MAP entry in apps/cron/views.py (verified the full map, lines 70-188 — it has schedule_finance_welcome and snapshot_gravity_weekly but nothing for a monthly finance snapshot), and apps/finance/tasks.py contains only schedule_finance_welcome_task. So the function never runs outside tests and no snapshots are produced in production. WHY LOW/LATENT: the inflated count has no production consumer (only tests read it); the snapshot-creation logic itself is correct and idempotent; the never-scheduled point makes the whole feature dormant rather than malfunctioning, and the feature record's own inventory_notes already flag the caller as unconfirmed and describe the function as 'designed to be called', so the wiring is acknowledged as not-yet-done rather than a regression. No user or operator experiences a misbehavior today — this is a docstring-vs-behavior inaccuracy plus a not-yet-wired feature.

feature: Finance USER.md Envelope Section (Gravity Context Injection)
user_story: As the system, I want to inject the subscriber's current finance state into the assistant's USER.md workspace file so that the AI has live financial context at the start of every session.
expected_behaviour: apps/finance/envelope.py:render_finance is registered as a USER.md section with key='finance', heading='## Gravity — finance state', order=50. Enabled gate: tenant.finance_active (folds in GRAVITY_ENABLED platform pause). refresh_on=(FinanceAccount, FinanceTransaction, PayoffPlan) — section is regenerated whenever any of these models change for the tenant. Renders: active account count and total debt, the active payoff plan summary (strategy/months/payoff date/budget), top-priority debt by strategy (avalanche: highest APR; else snowball: lowest balance), upcoming due dates within 7 days (modular 31-day arithmetic), and last 3 transactions from the past 14 days. Truncates at max_chars=1000 with a note directing the agent to call nbhd_finance_summary for full state.
dependencies: FinanceAccount model, FinanceTransaction model, PayoffPlan model, apps/orchestrator/envelope_registry.py
inventory_notes: The 7-day upcoming-payment calculation uses modular day arithmetic (a.due_day - today.day) % 31, which can produce false positives near month boundaries for due_day < today.day within the same month.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

feature: Finance Status Projection (Recurring Obligations / Monthly Folding)
user_story: As the system, I want to project each finance account's payment obligation for the current billing cycle so that the assistant's live status snapshot correctly shows which debts are paid and which are outstanding.
expected_behaviour: apps/journal/status_projection.py:_provide_finance is registered as a status provider for key='finance', enabled by _finance_active(tenant). Computes current_period_bounds(today) (the calendar month). Aggregates payment transaction totals per account for the current period. For each active FinanceAccount calls obligation_for_account(account, paid_this_period, today) and collects non-null obligations into {'obligations': [...]}. This data feeds into build_journal_status, which assembles the authoritative live-status dict used by proactive/cron reasoning. Finance obligations suppress FinanceAccount-linked Task objects in the tasks provider (to avoid double-reporting the same obligation via both channels).
inventory_notes: Also suppresses Journal Tasks linked to FinanceAccount via related_ref.object_type='FinanceAccount' while finance is active — prevents double-counting.

feature: Weekly Gravity Check-In Cron (OpenClaw Agent Turn)
user_story: As the system, I want to trigger a weekly Sunday-evening finance check-in for finance-enabled tenants so that the AI assistant proactively reviews the subscriber's progress and keeps them accountable.
expected_behaviour: Seeded into OpenClaw's cron registry via apps/orchestrator/config_generator.py:build_cron_seed_jobs when tenant.finance_active is True. Scheduled as a cron expression '0 19 * * 0' (Sunday 19:00) in the tenant's configured timezone. sessionTarget='isolated'. Delivery mode 'none' (no push notification). Payload prompt (_GRAVITY_WEEKLY_PROMPT) instructs the agent to call nbhd_gravity_query for current data, never assert finance numbers from memory, produce a brief check-in message (positive framing, 2-3 sentences), and send it via nbhd_send_to_user. Omitted entirely from the cron seed when tenant.finance_active is False (including when GRAVITY_ENABLED is False).
dependencies: OpenClaw Gateway API (cron registration), Tenant.finance_active, Tenant.timezone
inventory_notes: Fires at a different hour (19:00) than the weekly reflection cron (20:00) to prevent collision. Uses DEEPSEEK_FLASH_MODEL (gravity_weekly_checkin model slot).

user_story: As the system, I want to capture a weekly Gravity snapshot so that the assistant's history and comparison tools have a time-series of the user's financial state.
expected_behaviour: QStash task 'snapshot_gravity_weekly' → apps/insights/tasks.py:snapshot_gravity_weekly_task. Calls apps/insights/snapshots.py:compute_gravity_snapshot per finance-active tenant. compute_gravity_snapshot loads active accounts, active plan, and last 10 transactions; returns a schema_version=1 JSON payload with totals (debt/savings/minimums), per-account data (including payoff_progress), and recent transactions. Result is stored in a PillarSnapshot row (idempotent per ISO week). Skips hibernated tenants.
dependencies: FinanceAccount model, FinanceTransaction model, PayoffPlan model, apps/insights models (PillarSnapshot), QStash
inventory_notes: Phase 1 only. Category-level trajectory (requires a 'category' field on FinanceTransaction) is deferred to Phase 1.5.

user_story: As the system, I want to generate a weekly written reflection on the subscriber's financial progress so that the AI has a synthesized narrative to reference in future conversations.
expected_behaviour: QStash task 'weekly_gravity_reflection' → apps/insights/tasks.py:weekly_gravity_reflection_task. Hourly dispatcher; fires Django-side LLM synthesis for each tenant whose local time is Sunday 09:00 (different hour from the agent-side check-in). Calls apps/insights/synthesis.py:generate_weekly_reflection. That function short-circuits with skipped='finance_disabled' if tenant.finance_active is False. Otherwise calls an LLM (DeepSeek or configured model) with last week's Gravity data to produce a short reflection observation. Bills to platform (is_system=True), not user quota.
dependencies: QStash, LiteLLM/LLM routing, FinanceAccount model, apps/insights models, Tenant.finance_active
inventory_notes: Gated on tenant.finance_active (folds in GRAVITY_ENABLED). Platform billing (is_system=True) so it does not consume user token quota.

feature: Finance Plugin Injection into OpenClaw Config
user_story: As the system, I want to include the nbhd-finance-tools plugin in the OpenClaw configuration when Gravity is enabled so that the AI assistant has access to finance tool calls during its session.
expected_behaviour: apps/orchestrator/config_generator.py:generate_openclaw_config conditionally appends the nbhd-finance-tools plugin (ID from OPENCLAW_FINANCE_PLUGIN_ID setting, path from OPENCLAW_FINANCE_PLUGIN_PATH) to the plugins list when tenant.finance_active is True. Also conditionally includes the nbhd-insights-tools plugin for the weekly snapshot/comparison tools. Both are absent when the platform GRAVITY_ENABLED flag is False. Config is written to the tenant's Azure File Share; the container reads it on startup.
dependencies: Azure File Share (openclaw.json), Tenant.finance_active, OPENCLAW_FINANCE_PLUGIN_ID setting, OPENCLAW_FINANCE_PLUGIN_PATH setting
inventory_notes: The tool manifest is built once at container startup; a toggle takes effect only after a container restart. finance_active (not finance_enabled) is the gate, so the platform pause suppresses the plugin fleet-wide.

user_story: As an operator, I want a fail-safe-off platform-wide switch for Gravity so that I can pause the entire finance feature for all tenants while strengthening data privacy, without touching individual tenant records.
expected_behaviour: tenant.finance_active property = bool(tenant.finance_enabled) AND bool(settings.GRAVITY_ENABLED). When GRAVITY_ENABLED is False: FinanceSettingsView.patch returns HTTP 403 for any enable attempt; the finance USER.md envelope section skips rendering; the nbhd-finance-tools plugin is omitted from OpenClaw config; the Gravity Weekly Check-in cron is not seeded; TenantSerializer reports gravity_available=False; generate_weekly_reflection short-circuits. Disabling (setting finance_enabled=False) is always allowed regardless of GRAVITY_ENABLED. Production default for GRAVITY_ENABLED is False.
inventory_notes: Test and development settings set GRAVITY_ENABLED=True. The production default being False means Gravity is effectively paused in production unless explicitly enabled at the platform level.

user_story: As an operator, I want to sweep and delete duplicate FinanceTransaction rows and reverse their balance impact so that I can remediate the May 2026 agent-retry double-debit incident and correct affected account balances.
expected_behaviour: management command: python manage.py dedupe_finance_transactions [--tenant <uuid>] [--apply] [--verbose]. Dry-run by default. Finds duplicate clusters by (tenant, account, transaction_type, amount, date) where count > 1. Keeps the oldest row per cluster (by created_at, then id), collects excess row IDs. Computes per-account balance correction: payments/refunds add back amount; charges/interest subtract. Clamps proposed balance: debt accounts capped at original_balance; all accounts floored at 0. Prints per-tenant cluster/row counts and per-account balance corrections. With --apply: deletes duplicates and saves corrected balances in a single atomic transaction. Returns counts of deleted rows and adjusted accounts.
dependencies: FinanceAccount model, FinanceTransaction model, Django atomic transactions
inventory_notes: Idempotent: safe to run multiple times. --tenant restricts scope to a single tenant UUID. Dry-run output can be reviewed before committing. Balance clamping at original_balance prevents correction from overshooting the starting point for debt accounts.

user_story: As an operator, I want to view and search finance data in the Django admin so that I can inspect and troubleshoot individual tenant records.
expected_behaviour: Four ModelAdmin registrations: FinanceAccountAdmin (list: nickname, tenant, account_type, current_balance, interest_rate, is_active; filter by account_type, is_active; search by nickname and tenant__user__display_name), FinanceTransactionAdmin (list: account, transaction_type, amount, date; filter by transaction_type), PayoffPlanAdmin (list: tenant, strategy, payoff_months, total_interest, is_active; filter by strategy, is_active), FinanceSnapshotAdmin (list: tenant, date, total_debt, total_savings). Standard Django admin CRUD on all models.
dependencies: Django admin, FinanceAccount model, FinanceTransaction model, PayoffPlan model, FinanceSnapshot model

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to enable or disable the Fuel fitness plugin so that my assistant gains workout-tracking capabilities.
expected_behaviour: PATCH /api/v1/fuel/settings/ requires JWT auth and a body with `fuel_enabled` (bool). On off→on: creates a FuelProfile (get_or_create), clears the `fuel` key from `tenant.welcomes_sent`, enqueues `apply_single_tenant_config` via QStash, and returns `{fuel_enabled, fuel_profile_status, restart_required: true}` when a container exists. On on→off: sets fuel_enabled=False, bumps pending config, restart_required=true. Returns 400 if `fuel_enabled` is missing, 404 if the user has no tenant. Plugin change requires a container restart — the frontend must call POST /api/v1/fuel/restart/ after user confirms.
inventory_notes: Disabling then re-enabling Fuel clears the welcome delivery marker so the welcome cron re-fires. `restart_required` is only true when a container_id exists. Welcome cron scheduling happens later via /restart/, not here.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to restart my AI assistant after toggling the Fuel plugin so that the plugin is picked up without manual intervention.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/fuel/restart/ requires JWT auth. Calls `restart_container_app(tenant.container_id)` on Azure Container Apps. Returns 400 if no container_id. Returns 502 if Azure restart fails. On success, if fuel_enabled and onboarding_status=pending, enqueues `schedule_fuel_welcome` via QStash with a 90-second delay (so the container boots before the gateway is hit). Returns `{restarted: true}`.
inventory_notes: The 90-second delay is intentional — container cold-start takes ~90s. No restart is attempted if container_id is absent (e.g. unprovisioned tenant).

user_story: As the system, I want to schedule a one-shot welcome message for a newly Fuel-enabled tenant so that they are proactively welcomed without blocking the restart response.
expected_behaviour: The `schedule_fuel_welcome_task` QStash task (dispatched ~90s after container restart) checks that the tenant exists, fuel_enabled=True, and onboarding_status=pending. If so, calls `_schedule_fuel_welcome(tenant)` which delegates to `apps.orchestrator.welcome_scheduler.schedule_welcome` with feature='fuel', cron_name='_fuel:welcome'. The welcome cron fires ~5 minutes after registration. Self-healing: stale fire-past-date welcome crons are replaced. If the prior welcome was delivered (`welcomes_sent.fuel` set), returns early.
dependencies: QStash (task dispatch), OpenClaw container gateway (cron.add), apps/orchestrator/welcome_scheduler.py
inventory_notes: On failure the error is logged but swallowed — the daily `reconcile_welcomes` watchdog retries. The welcome prompt instructs the assistant to mark delivery via POST /api/v1/tenants/runtime/{tenant_id}/welcomes/fuel/ after nbhd_send_to_user succeeds, so failed sends do not stamp delivery.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to view and update my fitness profile so that the assistant knows my goals, equipment, and preferences.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/fuel/profile/ returns the FuelProfile for the authenticated tenant (404 if no profile). PATCH /api/v1/fuel/profile/ accepts partial updates for: onboarding_status, fitness_level, goals, limitations, equipment, days_per_week, preferred_days, preferred_time, additional_context, distance_unit. Both require JWT auth. Returns serialized FuelProfile. The consumer-facing endpoint does not update crons (unlike the runtime equivalent).
inventory_notes: distance_unit is always stored in km; the UI converts on display. preferred_time validation accepts morning/afternoon/evening/''. The runtime profile PATCH (OpenClaw) also triggers fuel cron updates when preferred_time changes; the consumer PATCH does not.

user_story: As the OpenClaw assistant, I want to read and update a tenant's fitness profile so that I can conduct onboarding and remember user preferences.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/fuel/runtime/{tenant_id}/profile/ and PATCH /api/v1/fuel/runtime/{tenant_id}/profile/ use X-NBHD-Internal-Key + X-NBHD-Tenant-Id auth (validated by validate_internal_runtime_request). PATCH accepts: onboarding_status (pending/in_progress/completed/declined), fitness_level (beginner/intermediate/advanced/''), goals/limitations/equipment (lists of strings), days_per_week (1-7 or null), preferred_days (list of 0-6 ints), preferred_time (morning/afternoon/evening/''). On preferred_time change, if an active plan exists, calls `_manage_fuel_cron(tenant, plan, action='update')` (best-effort). Returns only the profile fields (not healthkit_tombstones or use_session_scheduling). Returns 401 on auth failure, 404 if tenant or profile not found.
dependencies: Postgres (FuelProfile), OpenClaw container gateway (cron.list, cron.remove, cron.add) — best-effort for cron updates
inventory_notes: Profile is get_or_created on PATCH if missing. The PATCH endpoint uses `update_fields` to avoid overwriting unset fields. `use_session_scheduling` and `healthkit_tombstones` are excluded from the serialized response.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to log a completed or planned workout via the web/app so that my fitness history is recorded.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/fuel/workouts/ requires JWT auth. Validates via WorkoutSerializer (date or scheduled_at required; RPE 1-10; detail_json must be object). On create, runs normalize_detail + validate_detail through the set_contract: strength/calisthenics detail_json is validated against typed Pydantic models (WeightedRepsSet, BodyweightRepsSet, HoldTimeSet); returns 400 with LLMValidationError envelope on failure. Source is set by the serializer (defaults to 'user'). After save, detect_prs is called. Returns 201 with full workout. If scheduled_at provided without date, date is derived from tenant timezone.
inventory_notes: post_save signal bumps tenant.fuel_version atomically and (if use_session_scheduling=True) enqueues a 30s-debounced fuel cron regeneration via QStash.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to list my workouts with optional filters so that I can review my training history.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/fuel/workouts/ requires JWT auth. Supports query params: category (WorkoutCategory values), status (WorkoutStatus values), date_from, date_to, window (e.g. '4w' or '30d' — rolling window anchored on tenant-local today), limit (default 100, max 500). Results are ordered by -date, -id. Cached 30s per-tenant via @tenant_cache(tag='fuel'). Cache is invalidated on any Fuel write (fuel_version bump).
inventory_notes: window param is ignored if date_from or date_to are also provided. Timezone-aware window calculation uses today_in_tenant_tz to avoid date boundary bugs for non-UTC tenants.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to view, edit, or delete a specific workout so that I can correct errors or add details after the fact.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/fuel/workouts/{workout_id}/ returns the full WorkoutSerializer payload. PATCH accepts partial updates; runs set_contract normalization and validation on detail_json changes; calls detect_prs after save. If PATCH lands on a status=done workout that was already done and was created more than 24h ago, stamps `last_edited_by_user_at` to mark a post-completion edit. DELETE returns 204. All methods return 404 if workout not found for this tenant. JWT auth required.
inventory_notes: Workout rows are fully editable regardless of status (by design — no status gate). A post_delete signal records HealthKit tombstones if external_id is set. post_save bumps fuel_version.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to hold an edit lock on a workout while editing it so that the AI assistant cannot overwrite my in-progress changes.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/fuel/workouts/{workout_id}/edit-lock/ acquires a lock for FUEL_EDIT_LOCK_TTL_SECONDS (default 60s), sets edit_lock_owner='user' and edit_lock_until. Returns `{workout_id, edit_lock_until, edit_lock_owner, ttl_seconds, version}`. The browser heartbeats this at half the TTL. DELETE /api/v1/fuel/workouts/{workout_id}/edit-lock/ releases the lock (idempotent — returns 204 even if already released). Runtime PATCH/DELETE/skip/complete paths check `_edit_locked_response`: if lock is in the future they return 409 with `{error: edit_locked, lock_owner, retry_after_s, edit_lock_until, workout_id}` plus a Retry-After header. JWT auth required for both lock endpoints.
inventory_notes: Lock self-expires after TTL — a browser crash cannot block assistant writes indefinitely. The plan reconciler's apply_reconciliation also respects edit locks (skips deletion of locked workouts). The 409 includes Retry-After even though OpenClaw documents 429 as retry-able — the field is included for forward-compat.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to duplicate a past workout as a new planned session so that I can quickly reuse a workout I have done before.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/fuel/workouts/{workout_id}/duplicate/ requires JWT auth. Looks up the source workout by id+tenant (404 if not found). Creates a new Workout with status='planned', date=today (server UTC date), and copies category, activity, duration_minutes, detail_json. Clears plan, slot, notes, rpe, source. Returns 201 with full WorkoutSerializer payload.
inventory_notes: Date is set to server-side date.today() (UTC), not tenant-local today — potential off-by-one for tenants well ahead of UTC. No plan/slot linkage is carried over.
error_detail: Confirmed real, low-severity. WorkoutDuplicateView (apps/fuel/views.py:730-732) sets the new workout's date with date_cls.today() — a plain datetime.date.today() that reads the container's system clock (UTC in Azure). The codebase already activates the authenticated user's IANA timezone per-request via UserTimezoneMiddleware (apps/tenants/middleware.py:100-111, dj_timezone.activate), so the timezone-aware date is available but unused here. For a tenant ahead of UTC (e.g. the Asia/Tokyo canary, UTC+9), duplicating a workout between local midnight and 09:00 yields a UTC date one day earlier, so the new 'planned' session lands on the previous calendar day in the schedule. Impact is mild and recoverable: the duplicate is created as status='planned' and the full payload is returned immediately, so a misplaced workout is visible and can be rescheduled; the trigger window is a few early-morning hours and only affects east-of-UTC tenants. The audit's expected_behaviour says 'server UTC date', but its own inventory_notes flags this as a 'potential off-by-one', so the spec documents current behavior rather than endorsing it — this is a genuine UX off-by-one, not intended design.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to mark a planned workout as skipped with an optional reason so that my adherence record is accurate without deleting the session.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/fuel/workouts/{workout_id}/skip/ requires JWT auth. Sets status=skipped, skip_reason (capped to 128 chars). Saves only the changed fields. Returns full WorkoutSerializer. 404 if not found. No edit-lock check (consumer path).
inventory_notes: Distinct from DELETE: preserves the row for adherence calculation. Runtime skip path (OpenClaw) additionally checks edit_lock.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to mark a workout as done with optional notes, RPE, and duration so that my training log is complete.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/fuel/workouts/{workout_id}/complete/ requires JWT auth. Sets status=done; optionally updates notes, rpe (1-10, invalid values silently ignored), duration_minutes (coerced to int). Calls detect_prs after save. Returns full WorkoutSerializer. 404 if not found.
inventory_notes: Full save (not update_fields) — a contrasting design to the runtime complete path which uses scoped update_fields to avoid overwriting HealthKit-synced fields.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to swap two workouts' scheduled times atomically so that I can drag a session to a different day in the calendar.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/fuel/workouts/swap/ requires JWT auth. Body: {a: uuid, b: uuid}. Returns 400 if a or b are missing or identical. Both must belong to the tenant (404 otherwise). Within a DB transaction, swaps scheduled_at, window_start_at, window_end_at, and date between the two rows. Returns `{a: WorkoutSerializer, b: WorkoutSerializer}`.
inventory_notes: The runtime equivalent mirrors this exactly for the assistant path. No plan/slot re-linking occurs on swap — slots retain their original date context.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want a lightweight count of my workouts with filters so that the dashboard can show statistics without loading full records.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/fuel/workouts/count/ requires JWT auth. Supports category, status, date_from, date_to query params. Returns `{count: int}`. No caching. No limit applied.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see all my workouts grouped by day for a given month so that I can view a calendar heatmap of my training.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/fuel/calendar/?year=YYYY&month=MM requires JWT auth and both year (1900-2100) and month (1-12) query params; returns 400 if invalid or missing. Queries all workouts for that month, groups by date, and returns [{date, workouts: [WorkoutStubSerializer]}] sorted by date. Cached 60s per-tenant via @tenant_cache(tag='fuel').
inventory_notes: Uses WorkoutStubSerializer (lightweight: id, date, scheduled_at, category, activity, status, duration_minutes, rpe, plan_id).

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see aggregated progress for a workout category so that I can track improvement over time.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/fuel/progress/?category=<cat> requires JWT auth and a valid WorkoutCategory value (400 on invalid). Behaviour by category: strength → per-exercise est1RM trend (Epley formula) over last 365 done workouts; calisthenics → per-skill hold/reps trend; cardio → pace+distance trend + cumulative km; hiit → peak HR trend + totals; mobility/sport/other → session count + total minutes + recent 50 sessions list. All base on status=done. Cached 120s via @tenant_cache(tag='fuel'). Returns `{category, progress}`.
inventory_notes: est_1rm uses the Epley formula: W*(1+R/30), rounded to 1 decimal.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want a weekly workout volume summary so that I can see how much I trained in any given week.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/fuel/weekly-summary/?week_start=YYYY-MM-DD requires JWT auth. week_start defaults to the most recent Monday (UTC-based). Returns `{week_start, week_end, by_category: [{category, count, total_minutes}], totals: {sessions, minutes}}`. Cached 120s per-tenant. 400 on invalid week_start format.
inventory_notes: Only status=done workouts count. week_start defaults to server-UTC Monday, not tenant-local Monday.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to poll a cheap version counter so that an open workout drawer can detect when the assistant has updated the workout in the background.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/fuel/version/ requires JWT auth. Returns `{fuel_version: int}`. The counter is stored on the Tenant model and incremented atomically (F('fuel_version')+1) on every Workout or WorkoutPlan post_save or post_delete signal. The frontend polls this while a workout drawer is open and shows a 'refresh' pill when the counter advances.
inventory_notes: Bump is best-effort (logged on failure but never raises). Also bumped on WorkoutPlan saves/deletes.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see my recent personal records so that I can track strength milestones.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/fuel/prs/?limit=N requires JWT auth. limit defaults to 20, max 100. Returns list of PersonalRecordSerializer: {id, exercise_name, category, value, previous_value, metric, date, created_at}. Ordered by -date, -created_at. Cached 120s per-tenant. PRs are auto-detected (and created) by detect_prs() on every strength workout save (only for status=done and category=strength, using est_1rm).
inventory_notes: detect_prs creates a new PersonalRecord row whenever the computed est_1rm for any exercise exceeds the previous max for that exercise+metric. Only metric=est_1rm is detected automatically. detect_prs is also called by HealthKit sync (on matched_planned completion) and by runtime log/update paths.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to create, update, and delete fitness goals so that I can track targets like a strength or endurance milestone.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/fuel/goals/ returns all FuelGoal rows for the tenant (no limit). POST creates a goal with exercise_name, metric, target_value, optional target_date and achieved_at. PATCH /api/v1/fuel/goals/{goal_id}/ partially updates. DELETE /api/v1/fuel/goals/{goal_id}/ removes. All require JWT auth. 404 on PATCH/DELETE if not found. FuelGoal fields: exercise_name, metric, target_value, target_date, achieved_at.
entry_points: apps/fuel/views.py:FuelGoalListView ; apps/fuel/views.py:FuelGoalDetailView ; GET /api/v1/fuel/goals/ ; POST /api/v1/fuel/goals/ ; PATCH /api/v1/fuel/goals/{goal_id}/ ; DELETE /api/v1/fuel/goals/{goal_id}/
inventory_notes: No automatic goal-achievement detection — achieved_at must be set explicitly. Goals are not linked to PRs programmatically.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to log and manage my daily body weight so that I can track weight trends over time.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/fuel/body-weight/?limit=N returns up to N (default 90, max 365) entries ordered by -date. POST /api/v1/fuel/body-weight/ upserts by (tenant, date): if a row for that date exists it is updated (returns 200), otherwise created (returns 201). Requires date (YYYY-MM-DD) and weight_kg. PATCH /api/v1/fuel/body-weight/{entry_id}/ partially updates; returns 409 if moving to a date already occupied by another entry (date_conflict). DELETE /api/v1/fuel/body-weight/{entry_id}/ returns 204. All require JWT auth.
entry_points: apps/fuel/views.py:BodyWeightListView ; apps/fuel/views.py:BodyWeightDetailView ; GET /api/v1/fuel/body-weight/ ; POST /api/v1/fuel/body-weight/ ; PATCH /api/v1/fuel/body-weight/{entry_id}/ ; DELETE /api/v1/fuel/body-weight/{entry_id}/
inventory_notes: The model has a unique_together on (tenant, date). PATCH date-change conflict returns 409 with {error: date_conflict} instead of an opaque DB IntegrityError.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to log and manage my daily resting heart rate so that I can track cardiovascular fitness trends.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/fuel/resting-hr/?limit=N returns up to N (default 90, max 365) entries ordered by -date. POST upserts by date: requires date (YYYY-MM-DD) and bpm (integer 20-250); validates bpm inline (400 if out of range). PATCH /api/v1/fuel/resting-hr/{entry_id}/ partially updates. DELETE /api/v1/fuel/resting-hr/{entry_id}/ returns 204. All require JWT auth. unique_together=(tenant, date).
entry_points: apps/fuel/views.py:RestingHRListView ; apps/fuel/views.py:RestingHRDetailView ; GET /api/v1/fuel/resting-hr/ ; POST /api/v1/fuel/resting-hr/ ; PATCH /api/v1/fuel/resting-hr/{entry_id}/ ; DELETE /api/v1/fuel/resting-hr/{entry_id}/
inventory_notes: Resting HR is also ingested via HealthKit sync's `daily_metrics[].resting_hr` field. The envelope section surfaces the latest entry within 3 days.
error_detail: Confirmed. RestingHRDetailView.patch (views.py:1023-1034) calls serializer.is_valid() then serializer.save() with no date-conflict guard. The model declares unique_together = ["tenant","date"] (models.py:472), and the serializer fields are ["id","date","bpm","created_at"] (serializers.py:224-228) — 'tenant' is NOT a serializer field, so DRF does not auto-generate a UniqueTogetherValidator for (tenant, date). A PATCH that moves an entry onto a date already occupied by another entry for the same tenant therefore hits the DB unique constraint and raises an unhandled IntegrityError, surfacing as HTTP 500. The sibling BodyWeightDetailView.patch (views.py:613-634) handles the identical situation with an explicit pre-save check returning 409 date_conflict (lines 626-632), proving the RHR omission is an inconsistency, not intended behaviour. The URL is routed and reachable (urls.py:109). User impact: a 500 Internal Server Error instead of a clean 409 when a user edits a resting-HR entry's date onto a date that already has an entry for that tenant. Low severity: the collision is an uncommon edit case, the unique constraint still correctly blocks the duplicate write (no data corruption), and only the error presentation is wrong. The parallel Sleep detail view likely shares the same gap.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to log and manage my daily sleep data so that I can see how rest correlates with my training.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/fuel/sleep/?limit=N returns up to N (default 90, max 365) entries ordered by -date. POST upserts by date: requires date (YYYY-MM-DD) and duration_hours (Decimal, 0-24); optional quality (1-5, silently ignored if invalid) and notes. Returns 201/200 based on created/updated. PATCH /api/v1/fuel/sleep/{entry_id}/ partially updates. DELETE returns 204. All require JWT auth. unique_together=(tenant, date).
entry_points: apps/fuel/views.py:SleepListView ; apps/fuel/views.py:SleepDetailView ; GET /api/v1/fuel/sleep/ ; POST /api/v1/fuel/sleep/ ; PATCH /api/v1/fuel/sleep/{entry_id}/ ; DELETE /api/v1/fuel/sleep/{entry_id}/
inventory_notes: Sleep is also ingested via HealthKit sync's `daily_metrics[].sleep_hours`. User-entered quality/notes survive a HealthKit upsert because `update_or_create` only touches the keys in `defaults`.
error_detail: Confirmed. SleepDetailView.patch (apps/fuel/views.py:1126-1128) calls serializer.is_valid() then serializer.save() with no IntegrityError/conflict guard. SleepLog has unique_together=["tenant","date"] (models.py:499-502), but SleepLogSerializer's fields are ["id","date","duration_hours","quality","notes","created_at"] (serializers.py:235-239) and exclude 'tenant'. DRF's ModelSerializer only auto-generates a UniqueTogetherValidator when every column of the unique_together is a writable serializer field; since 'tenant' is absent, no validator is produced, and 'date' is writable on a partial PATCH. So a PATCH that moves a sleep entry onto a date already occupied by another row for the same tenant passes validation, hits the DB unique constraint, and raises IntegrityError. IntegrityError is not in DRF's handled-exception set, so the response is a 500. BodyWeightDetailView.patch (views.py:621-633) guards the identical case (same unique_together, same tenant-excluded serializer) with an explicit pre-save existence check returning 409 — SleepDetailView is simply missing the mirrored guard. User impact: editing a sleep entry's date onto an already-occupied day returns an opaque 500 and a logged unhandled exception/alert instead of a clean 409. Integrity is preserved by the DB constraint; only the error surface is wrong.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to create, browse, and manage reusable workout templates so that I can log frequent sessions quickly.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/fuel/templates/?category=<cat> returns all templates for the tenant, optionally filtered by category. POST creates a template (name, category, activity required; optional duration_minutes and detail_json). GET /api/v1/fuel/templates/{template_id}/ returns detail. PATCH partially updates. DELETE returns 204. All require JWT auth. Templates are ordered by -updated_at.
inventory_notes: Templates are separate from WorkoutPlan.schedule_json; they are user-created reusable blueprints, not plan-linked slots.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to view, create, update, and delete workout plans from the web dashboard so that I can manage multi-week training programs.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/fuel/plans/?status=<status> lists up to 20 plans (newest first) with workout_count and completed_count. POST creates a plan and expands the schedule_json into individual planned Workout rows via _expand_plan_workouts (creates PlanSlot rows and links Workout.slot FK). PATCH /api/v1/fuel/plans/{plan_id}/ partially updates; if schedule_json or weeks change, deletes future planned workouts and re-expands remaining weeks from the regen_start date. DELETE /api/v1/fuel/plans/{plan_id}/ deletes planned workouts and orphans completed workouts (sets plan=null). All require JWT auth.
entry_points: apps/fuel/views.py:WorkoutPlanListView ; apps/fuel/views.py:WorkoutPlanDetailView ; GET /api/v1/fuel/plans/ ; POST /api/v1/fuel/plans/ ; PATCH /api/v1/fuel/plans/{plan_id}/ ; DELETE /api/v1/fuel/plans/{plan_id}/
inventory_notes: Consumer PATCH regen uses old DELETE+re-expand, not the reconciler (which preserves UUIDs). The runtime PATCH uses the reconciler. No cron management in the consumer path.
error_detail: Confirmed real, low severity. WorkoutPlanDetailView.patch (apps/fuel/views.py:1229) computes the regen boundary with bare _date.today() (UTC). The same file (views.py:290) and the runtime plan path (runtime_views.py:1248) already use the project tenant-local helper today_in_tenant_tz(tenant), and runtime_views.py:1241-1243 carries an explicit warning that bare date.today() is computed in UTC and drifts a day in the evening for tenants offset from UTC, propagating into every materialized workout date. So this line is a genuine inconsistency, not intended behaviour. Impact: for a tenant in a positive-UTC-offset zone (e.g. JST UTC+9) editing a plan schedule_json/weeks during early-morning local hours (00:00-09:00 JST, when UTC is still the prior calendar day), today resolves to the tenant yesterday. Line 1230 deletes future PLANNED workouts on date__gte=today (including the already-current-from-the-user-view day) and line 1235 sets regen_start = max(today, start_date) to that UTC-yesterday date, so re-expansion can insert a planned workout dated one day in the past from the tenant perspective. Only PLANNED rows are affected; completed workouts are excluded/untouched. The runtime PATCH path shares the defect: reconcile_plan_state (services.py:96, default at 125) defaults today to _date.today(), and runtime_views.py:1397 calls it without passing today=, so it also anchors on UTC. Blast radius is small (only a ~9h daily window, only positive-offset tenants, only a 1-day boundary skew on planned rows), which is why low not medium.

feature: HealthKit Sync — Workout + Daily Metrics Ingestion
user_story: As an iOS subscriber, I want to sync my Apple Health workouts and daily health metrics so that my fitness data is automatically reflected in my assistant context.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/fuel/healthkit/sync/ requires JWT auth and rate-limited to 60/hour. Body: {workouts: [...], daily_metrics: [...], deleted_external_ids: [...], device_tz: 'IANA/zone'}. Gates: 404 if no tenant, 403 if suspended, 409 if fuel_disabled, 400 if body is not JSON/empty/exceeds caps (workouts<=50, daily_metrics<=31, deleted_external_ids<=100). Deletions are processed first (source=HEALTHKIT rows only; matched-planned rows with HK external_id survive because they have source=assistant/user). Tombstones are recorded on FuelProfile.healthkit_tombstones (FIFO-capped at 200) via the post_delete signal. Each workout is ingested atomically: idempotency on external_id; planned-auto-complete with select_for_update; existing manual DONE log adoption (merges HK metrics without duplicating). Daily metrics (resting_hr, sleep_hours, body_weight_kg) are upserted per-date; user quality/notes survive. Post-batch fires one push_user_md (debounce=0) and optionally one _enqueue_regen. Returns {results, daily_results, summary}.
dependencies: Postgres (Workout, BodyWeightLog, SleepLog, RestingHeartRateLog, FuelProfile), apps/orchestrator/workspace_envelope.py:push_user_md, QStash (regen_needed→regenerate_fuel_crons task)
inventory_notes: suppress_refresh() + suppress_cron_regen() wrap the ingest loop so 50-item batches don't generate 50 QStash publishes and 50 USER.md pushes. debounce_seconds=0 is load-bearing (shared debounce key with conversation capture — a windowed push would be silently dropped). Auto-complete uses _MIN_DURATION_RATIO=0.5 plausibility gate. Low-signal walks/hikes (raw_type in {walking, hiking}) don't auto-complete non-walk planned sessions. Device timezone is used to self-heal user.timezone if it is still the 'UTC' default.

user_story: As the system, I want to tombstone any deleted HealthKit-anchored workout's external_id so that a HealthKit anchor reset (app reinstall) cannot resurrect deleted workouts.
expected_behaviour: The `workout_deleted_record_healthkit_tombstone` post_delete signal fires on any Workout delete where external_id is non-empty (regardless of source). It appends the external_id to FuelProfile.healthkit_tombstones (FIFO-capped at 200, oldest evicted). The list is consulted by the HealthKit sync ingest loop before processing each item — tombstoned external_ids report status='duplicate' and are not re-created. Errors are swallowed and logged (never block the delete).
inventory_notes: Gated on external_id, not source. A matched planned session (source=user or assistant) that was completed by HealthKit carries the HK sample UUID — deleting it also tombstones the HK sample, preventing re-import.

user_story: As the OpenClaw assistant, I want to log a workout on behalf of a user when they report a session in chat so that training data is recorded from any channel.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/fuel/runtime/{tenant_id}/log/ authenticates with X-NBHD-Internal-Key + X-NBHD-Tenant-Id headers. Accepts: category, status (defaults to 'done'), duration_minutes, rpe (1-10), date (resolved via resolve_relative_date in tenant tz; fallback to tenant-local today), activity, detail_json. Runs normalize_detail + validate_detail on detail_json; returns 400 with LLMValidationError envelope on failure. Sets source=ASSISTANT. Calls detect_prs (best-effort). Returns 201 with {id, date, category, activity, status}.
inventory_notes: date resolution uses resolve_relative_date which handles 'today', 'yesterday', ISO strings, and relative phrases. source=ASSISTANT distinguishes chat-logged sessions from HealthKit imports and user-created rows.

user_story: As the OpenClaw assistant, I want to update or delete a specific workout on behalf of a user so that I can correct details or remove accidental entries from chat.
expected_behaviour: PATCH /api/v1/fuel/runtime/{tenant_id}/workouts/{workout_id}/ updates fields selectively via update_fields to avoid overwriting HealthKit-synced data. Checks edit lock first (409 + Retry-After if locked). Accepts: activity, category, status, date (ISO), duration_minutes, rpe, notes, detail_json (with normalize+validate). Re-runs detect_prs if detail_json changed. DELETE /api/v1/fuel/runtime/{tenant_id}/workouts/{workout_id}/ also checks edit lock. Both return 401 on auth failure, 404 if not found, 409 if edit-locked.
inventory_notes: Uses update_fields=specified+updated_at on save to avoid blind-reverting concurrent HealthKit sync writes. On DELETE, returns {deleted: true, id, activity, date} (not 204).

user_story: As the OpenClaw assistant, I want to mark a planned workout as skipped with a reason so that adherence is tracked when a user tells me they cannot train.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/fuel/runtime/{tenant_id}/workouts/{workout_id}/skip/ authenticates with internal headers. Checks edit lock (409 if locked). Sets status=skipped, skip_reason (capped 128 chars). Saves only changed fields. Returns {id, status, skip_reason, date}. 404 if not found, 401 on auth failure.

user_story: As the OpenClaw assistant, I want to mark a planned workout as completed when a user confirms they finished it, and record actual metrics so that training history is accurate.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/fuel/runtime/{tenant_id}/workouts/{workout_id}/complete/ authenticates with internal headers. Checks edit lock (409 if locked). Sets status=done; optionally updates notes, rpe, duration_minutes. Uses scoped update_fields=['status','notes','rpe','duration_minutes','updated_at'] — does NOT revert HealthKit-synced fields like external_id or merged detail_json. Calls detect_prs (best-effort). Returns {id, status, rpe, duration_minutes, date}.
inventory_notes: Scoped save is deliberate: a full-column save from an in-memory copy would overwrite external_id and merged detail_json that a concurrent HealthKit sync just wrote.

user_story: As the OpenClaw assistant, I want to swap two workouts' scheduled times so that I can reschedule a session at the user's request.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/fuel/runtime/{tenant_id}/workouts/swap/ authenticates with internal headers. Body: {a: uuid, b: uuid}. Both must belong to the tenant (404). Swaps scheduled_at, window_start_at, window_end_at, date atomically in a DB transaction. Returns {a: {id, scheduled_at, date}, b: {id, scheduled_at, date}}. 400 if a or b missing or identical.

user_story: As the OpenClaw assistant, I want a comprehensive snapshot of a tenant's recent fitness state so that I can answer questions and give coaching advice with up-to-date context.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/fuel/runtime/{tenant_id}/summary/ authenticates with internal headers. Returns: recent_workouts (last 20 done, including distance_km/avg_hr/peak_hr/calories from detail_json), planned_workouts (next 10 planned), active_plans (up to 3 active plans with workout_count/completed_count), latest_body_weight, latest_sleep, latest_resting_hr, trends (weekly_trends dict: sessions_7d/28d, minutes_7d/28d, by_category, recency_days, recent_prs, volume_trend), profile. Returns 401 on auth failure, 404 on tenant not found.
inventory_notes: weekly_trends computes using tenant-local day boundaries. Returns {} for trends if no done workouts in last 28 days. Measured HealthKit metrics (distance_km, avg_hr, peak_hr, calories) are surfaced per-session so the assistant coaches from real data.

feature: Runtime Fuel Audit View (OpenClaw Pre-flight Check)
user_story: As the OpenClaw assistant, I want to audit the current workout schedule state before creating or delivering any workout so that I avoid duplicates, contradictions, or orphaned crons.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/fuel/runtime/{tenant_id}/audit/ authenticates with internal headers. Cross-references three sources: (1) today's daily-note Fuel section (parsed from Document.kind='daily' via _parse_fuel_section), (2) next 14d Workout rows from Postgres, (3) OpenClaw cron registry (cron.list filtered to _fuel:* and workout-keyword-named crons). Detects conflicts: duplicate_fires (two crons at same schedule), orphan_crons (_fuel:{8-hex} with no matching Workout in next 14d), orphan_workouts (planned with scheduled_at in next 48h but no _fuel: cron). Returns a guidance string instructing the agent to deliver the locked plan verbatim if today_plan exists, or to propose one if not, and to STOP if duplicate fires detected.
dependencies: Postgres (Workout, Document), OpenClaw container gateway (cron.list via invoke_gateway_tool)
inventory_notes: cron.list failure is soft — returns cron_list_error string and empty fuel_crons rather than 500. The _FUEL_SESSION_PREFIX='_fuel:' distinguishes derived per-session crons from legacy plan-named crons. The guidance field is a machine-readable instruction string for the agent.

user_story: As the OpenClaw assistant, I want to log or delete a body weight entry on behalf of a user when they mention their weight in chat.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/fuel/runtime/{tenant_id}/body-weight/ authenticates with internal headers. Resolves date via resolve_relative_date in tenant tz (fallback to today_in_tenant_tz). Validates weight_kg (0 < val <= 500). Upserts by (tenant, date). Returns {date, weight_kg, created: bool} with 201/200. DELETE /api/v1/fuel/runtime/{tenant_id}/body-weight/ accepts date via query param or body; returns 404 if no entry for that date, 200 on success.
inventory_notes: Timezone-aware date resolution is critical — see the 2026-05-16 bug where bare date.today() (UTC) caused a JST evening entry to land on yesterday. DELETE accepts date in both query param and request body.

user_story: As the OpenClaw assistant, I want to log a sleep entry when a user tells me how they slept so that recovery data is captured from chat.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/fuel/runtime/{tenant_id}/sleep/ authenticates with internal headers. Resolves date via resolve_relative_date in tenant tz. Validates duration_hours (Decimal, 0-24). Optional quality (1-5, clamped) and notes. Upserts by (tenant, date). Returns {date, duration_hours, quality, created: bool} with 201/200. 400 on validation failure.
inventory_notes: No DELETE endpoint for the runtime sleep path (only POST/upsert). Consumer API has PATCH + DELETE.

user_story: As the OpenClaw assistant, I want to create a structured multi-week workout plan with a detailed schedule so that planned workout sessions are materialized in the user's calendar.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/fuel/runtime/{tenant_id}/plans/ authenticates with internal headers. Requires name, schedule_json (non-empty dict keyed by weekday 0-6), weeks (1-52), days_per_week (1-7). Optionally accepts start_date (resolved via resolve_relative_date; defaults to next Monday in tenant tz), objective (max 200 chars), notes, week_overrides (dict of per-week schedule overrides, null values = rest day). Validates and normalizes the entire schedule atomically via _validate_normalize_schedule and _validate_normalize_week_overrides before persisting anything. Idempotent on (tenant, name, start_date, status=active): returns 200 with deduped=true if a duplicate exists.
