On success, expands schedule into Workout rows + PlanSlot rows via _expand_plan_workouts; creates a background fuel cron (best-effort). Returns 201 with {plan fields, workouts_created}.
inventory_notes: Idempotency dedup keys on (name, start_date, status=active) — a retried or double-fired create returns the existing plan. week_overrides per-week null day values designate rest days (that day is dropped from the week's schedule). Validation failure returns LLMValidationError envelope so the assistant self-corrects. _expand_plan_workouts creates PlanSlot rows for stable UUID identity across plan regens.
error_detail: Real non-atomicity gap. In RuntimeWorkoutPlanCreateView.post the WorkoutPlan.objects.create() commits in autocommit, then _expand_plan_workouts() runs outside any transaction creating up to ~364 PlanSlot+Workout rows one at a time. A mid-loop failure leaves the plan plus partial workout rows committed. The 500 triggers an assistant retry, and the idempotency check at lines 1256-1262 matches the orphan by (tenant, name, start_date, status=ACTIVE) and returns 200 deduped=True BEFORE re-expanding, so the partial plan is silently locked in. The sibling swap view at line 481 already uses transaction.atomic(), so this view is inconsistent with the team's own pattern. Caveat: the verdict overstates recovery - the PATCH path (lines 1387-1410) runs reconcile_plan_state/apply_reconciliation which backfills missing rows, but only for dates on or after today (services.py:134-136 in_scope); future rows recover, past-dated rows stay gone, and the deduped=True response gives the assistant no signal to even attempt a PATCH. Net impact: a tenant can end up with a structurally incomplete active plan the create endpoint refuses to rebuild. Real, but requires a mid-loop write failure, hence medium not high.

user_story: As the OpenClaw assistant, I want to list a tenant's workout plans so that I can reference plan state and workout counts when coaching.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/fuel/runtime/{tenant_id}/plans/?status=<status> authenticates with internal headers. Optional status filter (PlanStatus values). Returns up to 10 plans sorted: active plans first, then by -created_at. Returns {plans: [serialized plan dicts with workout_count + completed_count]}.

user_story: As the OpenClaw assistant, I want to read, update, or delete a workout plan so that I can adjust or replace a program in response to user feedback.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/fuel/runtime/{tenant_id}/plans/{plan_id}/ returns full plan serialization including all workout rows (include_workouts=True). PATCH partially updates name, status, notes, weeks, schedule_json, days_per_week. If schedule_json or weeks change, triggers plan reconciliation via reconcile_plan_state + apply_reconciliation (not DELETE+INSERT regen — preserves workout UUIDs). Locked workouts are skipped in reconciliation (workouts_locked_skip count). Manages fuel cron: active plan → cron.update; paused/completed/archived → cron.remove. DELETE removes planned workouts, orphans completed ones (plan=null), removes cron (best-effort), returns 204. 404 on plan not found.
dependencies: Postgres, OpenClaw container gateway (cron.list, cron.remove, cron.add — best-effort)
inventory_notes: The reconciler (not the consumer PATCH's DELETE+regen approach) is used here — it preserves slot identity so workout UUIDs remain valid while a browser drawer is open. PATCH returns 500 with {error: regen_failed} if reconciliation throws. objective and week_overrides are not patchable via the runtime PATCH (only through the initial create).
error_detail: Confirmed. The runtime plan PATCH handler stores schedule_json without validation. At runtime_views.py:1358-1361 it only does an isinstance(dict) check then assigns plan.schedule_json = data['schedule_json'], with no call to _validate_normalize_schedule. The POST path (lines 1231-1237) deliberately runs _validate_normalize_schedule FIRST, which invokes set_contract.validate_detail and returns an LLMValidationError envelope (400) so the assistant self-corrects malformed strength/calisthenics sets before anything persists. PATCH skips this. After save, needs_regeneration triggers reconcile_plan_state + apply_reconciliation (lines 1387-1404), which read plan.schedule_json via _parse_schedule_template (services.py:76-93), build WorkoutSpec rows (services.py:208-228), and create Workout rows (services.py:362-377) with detail_json passed straight through; no validate_detail on this path. Malformed sets supplied to a PATCH thus land directly in Workout.detail_json, bypassing the normalization/validation contract POST enforces and never firing the self-correction loop. Corroborated by the codebase's own convention: the single-workout PATCH path (lines 296-304) DOES call normalize_detail+validate_detail on detail_json writes, so the plan-PATCH omission is an inconsistency, not intentional design. Impact is bounded: bad data originates from the assistant LLM over internal auth (not an external user); reconcile silently DROPS structurally-invalid weekday entries (non-int keys, non-dict values) so it cannot crash; but semantically-bad set contents persist into future planned workouts, degrade downstream rendering, and defeat the self-correcting validation guarantee POST provides.

user_story: As the system, I want plan slots to provide stable identity for planned workouts across plan regenerations so that a workout UUID a user's browser is viewing is not invalidated by an assistant plan update.
expected_behaviour: PlanSlot rows are created with (plan, week_index, weekday) natural key. When _expand_plan_workouts or apply_reconciliation creates a Workout, it sets Workout.slot FK to the slot. On plan regen (reconcile_plan_state + apply_reconciliation), slots in the desired schedule are kept (matching by week_index+weekday), removed slots are soft-archived (archived_at set, not deleted) if a workout still references them. Slotless planned workouts are adopted into new slots instead of creating duplicates. Kept slots apply template-driven field patches (category, duration_minutes, detail_json) to existing workouts but skip activity rename. The unique_active_plan_slot constraint enforces at most one active slot per (plan, week_index, weekday).
inventory_notes: The migration 0013 (backfill_plan_slots) materialized PlanSlot rows for pre-existing plans. Archived slots linger so historical workouts keep their FK; only future workouts on archived slots stop being generated.

feature: Per-Session Fuel Cron Derivation and Regeneration
user_story: As the system, I want to derive and reconcile per-session workout reminder crons from Postgres Workout.scheduled_at rows so that the OpenClaw container's cron registry stays in sync with the planned schedule.
expected_behaviour: derive_fuel_cron_jobs() is a pure function that reads planned Workout rows with scheduled_at in the next 48h and returns one cron-job dict per session named '_fuel:{short_id}' (8-hex UUID prefix), using date-specific cron expressions (M H D MON *) in the user's timezone. regenerate_fuel_crons() diffs the desired set against the container's current _fuel:* crons (via cron.list), adds missing, removes stale. Only fires for tenants with FuelProfile.use_session_scheduling=True. Gated on tenant not hibernated/suspended. Called via QStash on Workout post_save/post_delete (30s debounce, idempotency_key='regen-fuel-{tenant_id}') and by the hourly fleet reconcile (reconcile_fuel_crons_task).
dependencies: QStash (debounced task dispatch), OpenClaw container gateway (cron.list, cron.add, cron.remove), Postgres (Workout, FuelProfile)
inventory_notes: _FUEL_SESSION_PREFIX='_fuel:' with 8-hex short-id distinguishes derived per-session jobs from legacy _fuel:{plan_name} plan-level jobs. Hibernated/suspended tenants are skipped (gateway calls would fail). The hourly reconcile catches drift (e.g. container restart).
error_detail: RuntimeFuelAuditView.get() computes today = date.today() at apps/fuel/runtime_views.py:1510, which is the server-local (UTC in production Azure Container Apps) date rather than the tenant-local date. This today drives the daily-note document lookup (slug=str(today), line 1515), the 14-day planned-workout window (date__gte=today, lines 1526-1527), and orphan detection. Every other date-sensitive path in the SAME file uses today_in_tenant_tz(tenant) — lines 148-151, 742, 814, and 1248 — and the plan-create docstring at lines 1241-1243 explicitly warns "Never bare date.today(): that is computed in UTC and drifts a day in the evening for tenants offset from UTC." The helper today_in_tenant_tz is already imported at line 16. For east-of-UTC tenants (e.g. the canonical Asia/Tokyo tenant, UTC+9), between 00:00 and 09:00 local time the UTC date lags by one day, so the audit resolves 'today' as yesterday: it reads yesterday's daily Fuel section (which the guidance at lines 1657-1666 then instructs the agent to deliver "verbatim" as today's locked plan) and shifts the 14-day window one day early. Impact is a real but narrow morning-window advisory issue: the audit is a read-only tool that biases the agent's guidance, it does not mutate data, and the agent can still self-correct from other context — hence low severity. Confirmed at the code level: a single inconsistent call against an established same-file convention plus an explicit in-file warning against this exact pattern.

user_story: As the system, I want to render a Fuel fitness state section in the tenant's USER.md so that the OpenClaw assistant always has current fitness context without requiring an explicit tool call.
expected_behaviour: The `render_fuel` function is registered via @register_section (key='fuel', order=40, enabled when tenant.fuel_enabled=True, refresh_on=(Workout, BodyWeightLog, SleepLog, RestingHeartRateLog)). Renders: today's planned workout (with local time and duration), 4-week trends digest (weekly_trends_digest), last done session within 14 days (with RPE, duration, distance, avg HR, Apple Health provenance flag), last body weight with 7-day delta, last sleep entry, resting HR from past 3 days. Truncated to max_chars=1200 with a '_(truncated — call nbhd_fuel_summary)_' note. Returns '' if nothing to show.
inventory_notes: Refresh is triggered by post_save/post_delete on any of the four registered models. The 1200-char cap covers the typical day's data; the truncation message tells the assistant to call nbhd_fuel_summary for the full state.

user_story: As the system, I want a daily cron to detect fuel-enabled tenants whose welcome was never delivered and retry scheduling so that no subscriber is silently left without a Fuel onboarding message.
expected_behaviour: The `reconcile_welcomes_task` QStash task (daily) iterates all tenants where fuel_enabled=True. For each, calls _schedule_fuel_welcome which calls schedule_welcome (checks welcomes_sent.fuel; if not delivered, schedules a one-shot _fuel:welcome cron). Results are tallied by status (fresh, replaced, pending, already_delivered). Failures per tenant are caught and counted (never abort the batch). Returns per-feature counters.
dependencies: QStash (task scheduling), OpenClaw container gateway (cron.add), Postgres (Tenant, FuelProfile)
inventory_notes: Also run by management command `backfill_welcomes`. Only re-fires if welcomes_sent.fuel is not set. The welcome cron itself runs inside the OpenClaw container and marks delivery via POST /api/v1/tenants/runtime/{tenant_id}/welcomes/fuel/.

user_story: As the system, I want all workout detail_json to be normalized and validated before persistence so that strength/calisthenics sets always have a correct typed shape and cannot be stored in an ambiguous form.
expected_behaviour: normalize_detail() is a pure function that deterministically corrects set `type` fields using the exercise registry (normalize_exercise from apps.common.llm_lookups) and can flip a workout's category between strength/calisthenics when all exercises point to the same category. Returns (new_detail, new_category, overrides). validate_detail() then coerces every set to a typed shape (WeightedRepsSet, BodyweightRepsSet, HoldTimeSet via Pydantic discriminated union) and validates; on failure returns a LLMValidationError that the runtime/serializer surfaces as a 400 with a self-correcting envelope. Both are called on every write path: RuntimeLogWorkoutView, RuntimeWorkoutDetailView.patch, WorkoutSerializer.validate, RuntimeWorkoutPlanListCreateView.post, _validate_normalize_schedule.
inventory_notes: Only strength and calisthenics are validated against the typed contract; cardio/HIIT/mobility/etc. keep their flat by-category shape. The _normalized key in detail_json records corrections for debugging. Management command fuel_set_type_dryrun runs this as a read-only audit against production data.

user_story: As an operator, I want to audit what migration 0010's set-type stamp would change before running it so that I can assess blast radius without any writes.
expected_behaviour: python manage.py fuel_set_type_dryrun reads all Workout, WorkoutTemplate, and WorkoutPlan rows and reports: rows/sets affected and resulting type distribution. Writes nothing. Output per model: rows=N, rows_to_update=M, sets=K, sets_restamped=J, resulting_types={...}. Also surveys WorkoutPlan.schedule_json for set-bearing definitions (needed if any >0, since migration 0010 did not transform them).
inventory_notes: Read-only / safe to run in production. Intended to be run before migration 0010 but is idempotent and can be run at any time.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to browse my historical pillar snapshots so that I can understand how my finance data has changed over time.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/insights/history/?pillar=gravity&window=8w&granularity=weekly requires JWT authentication (IsAuthenticated). pillar must currently equal 'gravity' — any other value returns 404 with {error: pillar_not_available}. granularity must be 'daily', 'weekly', or 'monthly'; otherwise 400. window supports NNw/NNd/NNm formats up to 730 days; invalid formats or values > 730 days return 400. Returns a list of PillarSnapshot rows ordered by -ts with id, pillar, granularity, ts (ISO 8601), and payload. Returns 404 {error: no_tenant} when the authenticated user has no associated tenant.
inventory_notes: Only pillar=gravity is enabled (ALLOWED_PILLARS). Other pillars are gated until their snapshot pipelines ship. Default window is 12w.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to retrieve a single pillar snapshot by UUID so that I can drill into a specific time period's data.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/insights/snapshots/<snapshot_uuid>/ requires JWT authentication. Fetches PillarSnapshot scoped to the authenticated tenant — cross-tenant access is blocked because the query always filters by tenant. Returns 404 {error: not_found} if the snapshot UUID doesn't exist or belongs to a different tenant. Returns 404 if the pillar of the found snapshot is not in ALLOWED_PILLARS (currently gravity only).
inventory_notes: Tenant isolation enforced via .get(id=snapshot_id, tenant=tenant). Pillar gating applied post-fetch.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to compare two historical snapshots side-by-side so that I can see how my finances changed between two periods.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/insights/compare/?pillar=gravity&period_a=<uuid>&period_b=<uuid> requires JWT authentication. Both period_a and period_b UUIDs are mandatory; missing either returns 400 {error: missing_param}. Both snapshots must belong to the authenticated tenant and the requested pillar; otherwise 404. Returns both serialized snapshots plus a totals_delta dict with signed string deltas for debt, savings, and minimum_payments (period_b minus period_a). For gravity the _gravity_totals_delta function computes Decimal deltas with explicit sign prefix.
inventory_notes: diff_func for non-gravity pillars is absent so totals_delta returns {}. Pillar gate applies first.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to retrieve rolling baseline statistics for a specific topic so that the assistant can tell me if my current data is anomalous.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/insights/baseline/?pillar=gravity&topic=debt&window_weeks=12&granularity=weekly requires JWT authentication. pillar must be in ALLOWED_PILLARS; topic is required (400 if missing). window_weeks is clamped to [1, 104] (2 years). Returns stats including sample_size, mean, stdev, latest, latest_z (z-score), trend (least-squares slope per snapshot step), freshness_days, and supported (False when the topic has no extractor). For gravity, extractors exist for debt, savings, and minimum_payments only — other topics return sample_size=0 and supported=False.
inventory_notes: Category-level topics (dining, subscriptions, etc.) lack extractors because FinanceTransaction has no category field yet — they return supported=False. The LLM is expected to detect this and fall back to raw history.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to list the assistant's memory of patterns it has noticed about me so that I can review and act on them.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/insights/insights/?pillar=gravity&topic=debt&status=open&limit=20 requires JWT authentication. Filters by pillar (must be in ALLOWED_PILLARS if provided), topic slug, and status (open/confirmed/refuted/expired). Invalid status returns 400. limit is clamped to [1, 100] and defaults to 20. Returns insights ordered by -created_at. Each row includes statement, confidence (0..1 float), topic slug/id, status, author_model_version, and last_confirmed_at / last_refuted_at timestamps.
inventory_notes: Refuted insights are accessible via status=refuted — they are kept in DB so the assistant remembers being wrong, but the HorizonsView excludes them from the user-facing card list.

user_story: As a subscriber (or the assistant acting on my behalf), I want to create a new insight observation so that the assistant builds a compounding memory about me.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/insights/insights/record/ requires JWT authentication. Body must include pillar (must be in ALLOWED_PILLARS), topic (string, required), statement (string, required), and optionally evidence_refs (dict) and confidence (float in [0.0, 1.0]). model_version is captured up to 128 chars. topic is resolved via resolve_topic: exact canonical slug → alias match → creates a proposed TopicRegistry row. Returns 201 with the serialized insight (status=open). Missing or invalid fields return 400.
inventory_notes: Topic creation is atomic (transaction.atomic). Slug deduplication suffixes with _2, _3, etc. confidence defaults to 0.0 if omitted.
error_detail: Two sub-claims; only the second is real, and it is data-integrity, not a medium logic bug. CLAIM 1 (404 vs 400 for missing pillar) is NOT a defect: _validate_record_body returns 404 pillar_not_available whenever pillar is not in ALLOWED_PILLARS (only gravity), so a missing or empty pillar yields 404 by intentional, documented design. The module docstring (views.py lines 19-20) states only pillar=gravity is accepted and others 404 until snapshot pipelines ship; the same 404-pillar pattern is used across all endpoints (e.g. VoicePrefSet 572-576). The spec line about missing fields returning 400 is deliberately overridden for the pillar field by the gating design. The 404 body returns allowed=[gravity], fully actionable, so a caller learns exactly what to send whether pillar was absent or merely unsupported. The omitted-vs-unsupported status distinction is cosmetic when only one pillar value is ever valid; no real user/operator harm. CLAIM 2 (non-atomic topic+insight) is REAL but LOW: _record_insight_impl (views.py 355-365) calls resolve_topic then AssistantInsight.objects.create. resolve_topic is decorated transaction.atomic (topic_resolver.py 47) and commits a PROPOSED TopicRegistry row (78-85) when its atomic block exits. RecordInsightView.post (424-434) does not wrap the call, and there is no ATOMIC_REQUESTS setting in config, so Django autocommits the proposed topic before the insight create runs. If the create fails in the narrow inter-statement window, an orphaned proposed topic persists, and grep found no cleanup cron to reap it. Impact is benign (proposed topics are expected, curated registry churn and the orphan has no insight referencing it) and the trigger window is narrow, hence low severity.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to confirm an insight the assistant raised so that it knows this pattern is accurate and compounds its understanding.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/insights/insights/<insight_uuid>/confirm/ requires JWT authentication. Fetches the insight scoped to the authenticated tenant; returns 404 if not found. Sets status=confirmed, last_confirmed_at=now, and appends {at, kind: 'confirm', note?} to user_responses JSON array (note truncated to 512 chars). Idempotent: repeat confirms are allowed and each appends a new history entry. Saves only the changed fields via update_fields.
inventory_notes: No status transition guard — can confirm a refuted insight. The user_responses list grows unboundedly.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to refute an incorrect insight so that the assistant remembers it was wrong and doesn't raise the same mistake again.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/insights/insights/<insight_uuid>/refute/ requires JWT authentication. Fetches the insight scoped to the authenticated tenant; returns 404 if not found. Sets status=refuted, last_refuted_at=now, and appends {at, kind: 'refute', note?} to user_responses (note truncated to 512 chars). Refuted rows are kept in the database permanently — the assistant's behavioral rules instruct it to check refuted insights to avoid re-raising corrected patterns. Saves only the changed fields.
inventory_notes: Refuted insights are intentionally excluded from HorizonsView's assistant_insights card list but remain accessible via the insights list endpoint with status=refuted.

user_story: As a subscriber (via the assistant), I want structured signals for a topic so that the assistant can calibrate its voice register appropriately.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/insights/signals/?pillar=gravity&topic=debt&window_weeks=12&granularity=weekly requires JWT authentication. topic is required (400 if missing). If the topic slug is not found in TopicRegistry, returns a stub with topic_known=false and hard_floors.reason=topic_unknown. Otherwise returns five blocks: data (baseline stats), calibration (confirmed/refuted/open counts and ratio), intent (has_stated_goal, goal_scope, goal_summary from typed Goal or legacy Document(kind=GOAL)), user_voice_pref (register_offset, tone, volume from topic or pillar scope), and hard_floors (can_be_direct requires sample_size>=4; can_exceed_observation requires response_total>=3).
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (PillarSnapshot, AssistantInsight, TopicRegistry, UserVoicePref, Goal, Document tables)
inventory_notes: Dual-reads typed Goal model and legacy Document(kind=GOAL) — prefers typed Goal. Hard floors are mechanical constraints that the LLM behavioral rules make absolute; explicit UserVoicePref.register_offset is the only way to lift them.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to set my voice-register preference for a pillar or topic so that the assistant adjusts its tone and directness to match my comfort level.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/insights/voice-prefs/set/ requires JWT authentication. Body must include pillar (in ALLOWED_PILLARS) and register_offset (-1, 0, or +1). Optional fields: tone (gentle|direct), volume (silent|weekly|live), topic (slug string). If topic is provided and not found in TopicRegistry, resolve_topic auto-creates a proposed entry. Performs update_or_create keyed on (tenant, pillar, topic) so the operation is idempotent. Returns the serialized voice pref. Invalid register_offset or enum values return 400.
inventory_notes: volume=silent suppresses the weekly proactive synthesis for that pillar. register_offset is stored permission that lifts the assistant's hard floors.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to list my voice-register preferences so that I can review how I've configured the assistant's communication style.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/insights/voice-prefs/?pillar=gravity&topic=debt requires JWT authentication. Optionally filters by pillar (must be in ALLOWED_PILLARS) and topic slug. Returns prefs ordered by (pillar, topic__slug). Each pref includes scope ('topic' or 'pillar'), register_offset, tone, volume, and timestamps. Topic-level prefs are separate rows from pillar-wide prefs (topic FK is nullable).

user_story: As the OpenClaw container runtime, I want to retrieve a tenant's pillar snapshot history so that the assistant can reason about financial trajectory during a conversation.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/insights/runtime/<tenant_uuid>/history/ uses X-NBHD-Internal-Key + X-NBHD-Tenant-Id headers for authentication (validated by validate_internal_runtime_request). Returns 401 {error: internal_auth_failed} on mismatch. Sets RLS service_role context via set_rls_context. Otherwise identical behavior to the user-facing PillarHistoryView: pillar gating, granularity/window validation, snapshot list. Tenant is resolved from the URL param (not from session). Returns 404 if tenant UUID doesn't exist.
inventory_notes: permission_classes=[AllowAny] — security is enforced entirely by the header-based internal auth check, not Django's auth system. All runtime views share this pattern.

user_story: As the OpenClaw container runtime, I want to retrieve a specific snapshot by UUID so that the assistant can drill into a particular week's data.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/insights/runtime/<tenant_uuid>/snapshots/<snapshot_uuid>/ authenticates via X-NBHD-Internal-Key headers. Tenant-scoped snapshot fetch; returns 404 {error: not_found} if the snapshot doesn't exist or belongs to another tenant. Returns 404 if the snapshot's pillar is not in ALLOWED_PILLARS.

user_story: As the OpenClaw container runtime, I want to compare two snapshots so that the assistant can explain period-over-period changes to the user.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/insights/runtime/<tenant_uuid>/compare/?pillar=gravity&period_a=<uuid>&period_b=<uuid> authenticates via internal key headers. Requires both period_a and period_b query params (400 if either is missing). Both snapshots must belong to the tenant and the specified pillar; otherwise 404. Returns both serialized snapshots plus totals_delta (signed string deltas for debt/savings/minimum_payments for gravity).
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

user_story: As the OpenClaw container runtime, I want to retrieve baseline statistics for a topic so that the assistant can determine whether the user's current data is anomalous.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/insights/runtime/<tenant_uuid>/baseline/?pillar=gravity&topic=debt&window_weeks=12 authenticates via internal key headers. Returns identical compute_baseline output as the user-facing PillarBaselineView. topic is required. window_weeks clamped [1, 104]. Non-supported topics return sample_size=0 and supported=False.

user_story: As the OpenClaw container runtime, I want to retrieve the assistant's existing insight memory for a tenant so that the assistant can avoid re-raising patterns it has already confirmed or been corrected on.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/insights/runtime/<tenant_uuid>/insights/?status=open&pillar=gravity authenticates via internal key headers. Filterable by pillar (must be in ALLOWED_PILLARS), topic slug, and status. limit clamped [1, 100], default 20. Returns insights ordered by -created_at. Identical payload to user-facing InsightListView.

user_story: As the OpenClaw container runtime, I want to record a new insight I've raised in conversation so that the assistant's memory compounds across sessions.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/insights/runtime/<tenant_uuid>/insights/record/ authenticates via internal key headers. Requires pillar (in ALLOWED_PILLARS), topic, statement; optionally evidence_refs, confidence (0.0-1.0), model_version. Calls resolve_topic to canonicalize the topic string. Creates AssistantInsight with status=open. Returns 201 with serialized insight. Missing/invalid fields return 400.
inventory_notes: This is the primary path by which the assistant's in-conversation observations are persisted. The marker extraction path (extract_and_record_insights) is a secondary parallel path.
error_detail: Part 2 (the TOCTOU race) is real; part 1 (404 vs 400) is a cosmetic spec-wording mismatch only. _make_unique_slug (topic_resolver.py:38-44) does .filter(pillar,slug).exists() then resolve_topic .create()s (lines 78-85) against unique_together=(pillar,slug) (models.py:94). No IntegrityError handling or get_or_create exists in the record path. @transaction.atomic gives rollback, not isolation, so under READ COMMITTED two concurrent transactions creating the same new (pillar,slug) both pass .exists() and both .create(); the loser raises an unhandled IntegrityError. TopicRegistry is GLOBAL (keyed pillar+slug, not tenant), so different tenants' OpenClaw containers proposing the same brand-new topic simultaneously can collide. On the direct record paths (_record_insight_impl views.py:355 via runtime_views.py:331 and RecordInsightView) this surfaces as a 500. Low impact: trigger requires two near-simultaneous creates of an identical NOT-yet-seeded topic string; common topics are pre-seeded (migration 0002) so most calls early-return on the canonical/alias branches and never reach .create(); the loser loses one insight write and gets one retryable 500; and the parallel marker path (markers.py:80-94) already swallows the exception. Re part 1: _validate_record_body (runtime_views.py:88-92) returns 404 (pillar_not_available) for a bad pillar where the FA-0607 spec says 400; this is deliberate, consistent across 6+ sites, returns a structured error, never crashes, and only the internal runtime caller sees it.

user_story: As the OpenClaw container runtime, I want to confirm an insight when the user agrees with an observation so that the assistant's calibration improves over time.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/insights/runtime/<tenant_uuid>/insights/<insight_uuid>/confirm/ authenticates via internal key headers. Sets status=confirmed, last_confirmed_at=now, appends confirm event to user_responses. Idempotent — multiple confirms each add a history entry. Returns 404 if insight doesn't exist for the tenant.

user_story: As the OpenClaw container runtime, I want to refute an insight when the user corrects the assistant so that the assistant doesn't repeat the same mistake.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/insights/runtime/<tenant_uuid>/insights/<insight_uuid>/refute/ authenticates via internal key headers. Sets status=refuted, last_refuted_at=now, appends refute event to user_responses (note truncated to 512 chars). Row is kept permanently. Returns 404 if insight doesn't exist for the tenant.

user_story: As the OpenClaw container runtime, I want to retrieve structured signals for a topic so that the assistant can select its voice register (observation/hypothesis/soft-prescription/direct) for the current conversation.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/insights/runtime/<tenant_uuid>/signals/?pillar=gravity&topic=debt authenticates via internal key headers. Returns the identical five-block signal payload as the user-facing PillarSignalsView: data, calibration, intent, user_voice_pref, hard_floors. hard_floors.can_be_direct requires sample_size>=4; can_exceed_observation requires response_total>=3. Unknown topic slug returns stub with topic_known=false.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (PillarSnapshot, AssistantInsight, TopicRegistry, UserVoicePref, Goal, Document tables), apps/integrations/internal_auth.py
inventory_notes: Hard floors are described in AGENTS.md as mechanical absolute constraints the LLM cannot bypass. The user_voice_pref.register_offset is a stored explicit override that can lift them.

user_story: As the OpenClaw container runtime, I want to persist a user's voice-register override when they say 'just tell me' or 'ease up on X' so that the preference survives across sessions.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/insights/runtime/<tenant_uuid>/voice-prefs/set/ authenticates via internal key headers. Identical validation and upsert behavior as the user-facing VoicePrefSetView. pillar must be in ALLOWED_PILLARS; register_offset must be -1, 0, or +1. Unknown topic slugs are auto-created as proposed. Returns the serialized pref.
inventory_notes: The assistant's behavioral rules in envelope.py define the trigger phrases ('just tell me about X', 'be more cautious') that should cause the container to call this endpoint.

user_story: As the OpenClaw container runtime, I want to list the tenant's voice preferences so that the assistant can read the stored register overrides at the start of a conversation.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/insights/runtime/<tenant_uuid>/voice-prefs/ authenticates via internal key headers. Optional filters: pillar (must be in ALLOWED_PILLARS), topic slug. Returns prefs ordered by (pillar, topic__slug). Identical payload to the user-facing VoicePrefListView.

user_story: As the OpenClaw container runtime, I want a cross-pillar day-scoped activity snapshot so that cron prompts (Personal Question, Heartbeat) can ground their nudges in what actually happened yesterday rather than only long-term memory.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/insights/runtime/<tenant_uuid>/yesterdays-signals/ authenticates via internal key headers. Uses the tenant's local timezone (tenant.user.timezone) to define 'yesterday' — so a workout logged at 11pm local time falls on the correct date even after UTC rollover. Returns three sub-dicts: fuel (workouts_done yesterday/today, days_since_last_workout), journal (entry count, energy, days_since_last_entry, last_energy_reading), and lessons (approved yesterday, pending count). Also computes notable_gaps list with threshold flags: journal_dark_3+_days, fuel_quiet_5+_days, energy_stale_7+_days.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (Workout, WorkoutStatus, JournalEntry, Lesson tables), apps/integrations/internal_auth.py
inventory_notes: Deliberately distinct from PillarSignalsView (per-pillar, per-topic, window-scoped). Gravity/Core pillars omitted — no model for Core yet. Called by nbhd_yesterdays_signals OpenClaw tool.

user_story: As the platform, I want to write a weekly Gravity snapshot for every eligible tenant so that the assistant has historical trajectory data to reason over.
expected_behaviour: QStash calls POST /api/v1/cron/tasks/snapshot_gravity_weekly/ (via trigger_task dispatcher). The task iterates tenants where finance_enabled=True, status=ACTIVE, hibernated_at IS NULL. For each tenant it checks whether a weekly PillarSnapshot already exists for the current ISO week — if yes, it is skipped (idempotent). Otherwise, compute_gravity_snapshot is called: reads FinanceAccount (active=True), PayoffPlan (active=True), and the 10 most recent FinanceTransactions, serializes totals and account list into a JSON payload, and writes a PillarSnapshot row with granularity=weekly and ts=now. Returns counts dict {written, skipped_existing, errored}. Per-tenant errors are caught and logged without aborting the batch.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (PillarSnapshot, FinanceAccount, FinanceTransaction, PayoffPlan, Tenant tables), QStash (scheduling)
inventory_notes: Hibernated tenants are intentionally skipped to avoid triggering an image-refresh cascade. This means hibernated tenants accumulate history gaps. The payload schema is versioned (schema_version=1); keep in sync with FinanceDashboardView.

feature: Weekly Gravity Reflection Cron (Synthesis / Weekly Pulse)
user_story: As the platform, I want to automatically generate a weekly LLM-written financial reflection for each tenant on Sunday morning so that users discover a personalized Weekly Pulse without the assistant needing to be active.
expected_behaviour: QStash calls POST /api/v1/cron/tasks/weekly_gravity_reflection/ hourly. For each tenant where finance_enabled=True and status=ACTIVE (including hibernated — synthesis is Django-side), _is_sunday_morning_local checks whether the tenant's local time is Sunday at 09:00. If yes, generate_weekly_reflection is called. Guard conditions checked in order: finance not active → skipped; Document(kind=WEEKLY) with current ISO week's Monday slug already exists → skipped (idempotent); UserVoicePref.volume=SILENT at pillar scope → skipped; no PillarSnapshot or insight data in last 4 weeks → skipped. Otherwise calls OpenRouter (DeepSeek V4 Pro, falling back to DeepSeek V4 Flash) with a structured prompt including recent snapshots, open/confirmed insights, and voice_pref tone. LLM response is either 'NO_REFLECTION' (skipped) or prose containing one [[insight:topic_slug]] marker. Marker is extracted via extract_and_record_insights (writes AssistantInsight + strips tokens), then the cleaned prose is written as Document(kind=WEEKLY, slug=monday_date) via update_or_create. Token usage is recorded with is_system=True (billed to platform, not tenant).
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (PillarSnapshot, AssistantInsight, UserVoicePref, Document, Tenant tables), OpenRouter (DeepSeek V4 Pro / V4 Flash), QStash (hourly schedule), apps/billing/services.py:record_usage
inventory_notes: The Document(kind=WEEKLY) slug is the ISO Monday date (e.g. '2026-06-22'), enabling the HorizonsView's existing weekly_documents fallback path to surface it with zero frontend change. Max input context is 12,000 chars (truncated). Max output is 600 tokens. LLM errors are caught; the reflection is silently skipped for that week rather than raising.

feature: Insight Marker Extraction from Outbound Replies
user_story: As the platform, I want to automatically extract [[insight:slug]]statement[[/insight]] markers from every outbound assistant reply so that insights are recorded even when the assistant forgets to call nbhd_insights_record explicitly.
expected_behaviour: extract_and_record_insights(text, tenant, pillar) is called at every outbound message path (Telegram poller, Telegram webhook drain, LINE webhook). It scans text with a regex for [[insight:slug]]...[/insight]] markers (case-sensitive, DOTALL). For each match: slug is passed to resolve_topic; statement is truncated to 1000 chars; an AssistantInsight row is created with status=open. Marker tokens are stripped from the text (only the statement stays in the user-facing reply). Per-marker failures are logged and do not block the reply from going out. Returns cleaned text.
inventory_notes: pillar defaults to 'gravity' — call sites must pass an override once Fuel/Core extend the marker rules. The same text is never processed twice (each channel processes the reply once on the way out).
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

user_story: As an operator, I want to manage a curated taxonomy of topics per pillar so that the assistant's insight observations are consistently categorized and synonyms collapse to the same canonical topic.
expected_behaviour: TopicRegistry rows are seeded on every deploy via migration 0002 which calls seed_topics() — idempotent get_or_create. Gravity topics include income, fixed_expenses, subscriptions, discretionary, dining, debt, savings, large_purchases with seeded aliases. Fuel topics include sleep_quantity, sleep_quality, hydration, meals, energy_level, exercise, alcohol, caffeine. When the assistant uses an unknown slug, resolve_topic creates a proposed row (status=proposed, source=proposed_by_model). Operators promote proposed topics to canonical or merge them via aliases in Django Admin (TopicRegistryAdmin, TopicAliasAdmin). TopicAlias resolution is case/whitespace-insensitive via __iexact on the normalized candidate.
inventory_notes: Embedding similarity resolution is deferred (Step 3 in resolve_topic is TODO). Slug deduplication appends _2, _3 on collision. deprecated status exists for retired topics. Alias match is on the normalized (NFKC, lowercase, collapsed whitespace) form of the candidate.
error_detail: Confirmed as a real but low-severity robustness gap. resolve_topic (apps/insights/topic_resolver.py:47-85) is decorated @transaction.atomic and uses a read-then-create pattern: _make_unique_slug (lines 38-44) runs an existence-check SELECT loop, then TopicRegistry.objects.create (line 78) inserts, with no IntegrityError handling. The model enforces unique_together(pillar, slug) (apps/insights/models.py:94). Two requests for the SAME tenant both supplying the same novel slug can both observe the slug as absent and both attempt the INSERT; the second raises IntegrityError. The markers path catches this (apps/insights/markers.py:80-94 wraps resolve_topic + create in try/except Exception with logging), but _record_insight_impl (views.py:355, called unguarded from RecordInsightView.post at views.py:433) and _upsert_voice_pref_impl (views.py:638, called unguarded from VoicePrefSetView.post at views.py:669) have no outer catch, so a collision surfaces as an unhandled 500. User impact: the per-tenant OpenClaw assistant (sole caller) gets a transient 500 on the colliding record/voice-pref call; a retry succeeds because the slug then resolves. Real-world probability is very low: deployment is single-tenant-per-container and OpenClaw processes turns serially per tenant (concurrent-turn rejection), so two simultaneous identical-novel-slug requests require an edge case such as a retry overlapping the original or a cron-driven extraction turn overlapping an interactive turn. Still a genuine gap: the guarded-vs-unguarded asymmetry across the three call sites shows it is an oversight, not intended behaviour.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see the 'Topics I've learned' section in Horizons so that I can review the assistant's per-topic engagement and voice-register state.
expected_behaviour: Called from HorizonsView (GET /api/v1/dashboard/horizons/) as part of the response payload. summarize_topic_signals(tenant) aggregates topics the tenant has engaged with across four sources: AssistantInsight FK, typed Goal FK, legacy Document(kind=GOAL) FK, UserVoicePref FK, and PillarSnapshot extractor-supported topics. For extractor topics it calls compute_baseline per (pillar, slug) to check sample_size > 0. Returns a list of per-topic dicts: pillar, topic_slug, topic_display_name, sample_size (last 12w), confirmed (all-time), refuted (all-time), has_goal (typed or legacy), register_offset, register_scope. Sorted by (pillar, display_name).
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (AssistantInsight, Goal, Document, UserVoicePref, PillarSnapshot, TopicRegistry tables)
inventory_notes: Volatile per-turn fields (latest_z, trend, freshness_days) are intentionally excluded — those belong in compute_signals for the LLM's per-turn judgment, not in a UI that re-renders on each Horizons load. N+1 avoided via bulk-load of insight counts and voice prefs.

feature: Observation Mode Rules + Memory Counts (USER.md / AGENTS.md injection)
user_story: As the OpenClaw container runtime, I want the Gravity observation-mode rules and the assistant's current confirmed/refuted memory counts injected into AGENTS.md and USER.md so that the assistant always operates with the correct behavioral constraints and self-awareness.
expected_behaviour: Two injection paths: (1) render_observation_mode registered via @register_section(key='insights_observation_mode', order=15) — enabled when tenant.finance_active is True; refresh_on=(AssistantInsight, PillarSnapshot); emits a compact USER.md section with open/confirmed/refuted counts capped at 600 chars. (2) render_observation_mode_rules(tenant) appended to AGENTS.md by apps.orchestrator.personas.render_workspace_files when finance_enabled is True — includes the full _OBSERVATION_GATE and _REGISTER_SELECTION prompt blocks (~6KB static text kept out of USER.md to avoid bootstrap truncation).
inventory_notes: The split between USER.md (counts only) and AGENTS.md (full rules) was intentional to recover ~6KB of bootstrap budget that was being silently truncated. The register_offset and hard_floor mechanics are defined here in _REGISTER_SELECTION and enforced by the LLM.

user_story: As an operator, I want to run a one-shot backfill so that every eligible tenant has at least one Gravity snapshot immediately after deployment rather than waiting for the next weekly cron fire.
expected_behaviour: python manage.py backfill_gravity_snapshot delegates to snapshot_gravity_weekly_task(). Idempotent via ISO-week dedup — running multiple times on the same day produces no duplicates. Skips hibernated and non-finance tenants. Prints written/skipped_existing/errored counts. No command-line arguments needed.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (PillarSnapshot, FinanceAccount, FinanceTransaction, PayoffPlan, Tenant tables)

user_story: As an operator, I want to seed or re-seed the canonical topic registry so that new pillar topics are available for the assistant without requiring a database migration.
expected_behaviour: python manage.py seed_topics calls seed_topics() which iterates SEED_TOPICS (Gravity and Fuel) and calls get_or_create for each topic and its aliases. Fully idempotent — existing rows are not modified. Prints per-pillar created counts for topics and aliases. Also runs automatically on every deploy via migration 0002.
inventory_notes: Adding a new pillar or topic requires updating SEED_TOPICS and adding a sibling data migration that calls seed_topics() again.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see all my connected OAuth integrations so that I know which services are linked to my account.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/integrations/ — requires IsAuthenticated session. Returns all Integration rows for the user's tenant, serialised as {id, provider, status, provider_email, scopes, connected_at, updated_at}. If the user has no tenant, returns an empty list. Fields are all read-only. Providers: google, sautai, reddit.
inventory_notes: ReadOnlyModelViewSet — no write routes; only list/retrieve + a custom disconnect action.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to retrieve details of a specific connected integration so that I can inspect its status and scopes.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/integrations/<uuid:pk>/ — IsAuthenticated required. Returns a single Integration record scoped to the requesting user's tenant. Returns 404 if the integration belongs to a different tenant. Same serialiser as the list endpoint.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to begin connecting Google Workspace or Sautai so that my AI assistant can access those services on my behalf.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/integrations/authorize/<provider>/ — IsAuthenticated required. For non-Composio providers (google, sautai): validates that client_id and client_secret are configured in settings; builds a signed+HMAC Django state token (user_id, provider, nonce) stored in cache with 600s TTL and also in a process-local fallback dict; returns JSON {url: <authorization_url>} with full OAuth params including offline access_type and prompt=consent. For Composio providers: calls initiate_composio_connection, caches connection_request_id keyed by state, returns {url: <composio_redirect_url>}. Returns 400 for unknown providers or missing credentials.
dependencies: Composio API (for reddit), Google OAuth, Sautai OAuth, Django cache, Django signing
inventory_notes: State nonce is double-stored in cache and process-local dict so cache failures (Redis down) don't break the flow.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

feature: OAuth callback — complete OAuth flow (direct providers)
user_story: As a subscriber, I want the OAuth callback to automatically save my tokens so that my account is linked without manual steps.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/integrations/callback/<provider>/ — AllowAny, no authentication (browser redirect). Validates ?error from provider, validates and consumes state nonce (HMAC check + nonce replay-prevention). Loads user and tenant from state. Exchanges code for tokens via POST to provider token_url. Fetches provider email (Google only via /oauth2/v3/userinfo). Calls connect_integration which stores tokens in Azure Key Vault and upserts Integration row, then writes gws-credentials.json to the tenant's Azure File Share for the gws CLI. Triggers update_tenant_config (non-fatal on failure). Redirects to FRONTEND_URL/settings/integrations?connected=<provider> on success or ?error=<code> on failure. Possible error codes: unknown_provider, missing_params, invalid_state, user_not_found, no_tenant, oauth_not_configured, exchange_failed, callback_failed.
dependencies: Azure Key Vault, Azure File Share, Google OAuth token endpoint, Sautai OAuth token endpoint, Postgres, Django signing/cache
inventory_notes: Sets RLS context (service_role=True) before any DB writes. Token refresh_token may be absent on repeat logins without prompt=consent — connect_integration defaults it to provider config scopes if scopes not supplied by caller.

feature: Composio OAuth callback — complete Composio-managed OAuth
user_story: As a subscriber, I want Composio to handle the Reddit OAuth redirect so that my Reddit account is securely connected through the managed auth service.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/integrations/composio-callback/<provider>/ — AllowAny. Validates state nonce from query string. Retrieves connection_request_id from cache (keyed by state); returns 400 error=expired_request if missing. Calls complete_composio_connection which polls Composio (wait_for_connection with 30s timeout), checks status==ACTIVE, extracts email, and upsert Integration row with composio_connected_account_id (no Key Vault write). Redirects to /settings/integrations?connected=<provider> or ?error=callback_failed.
inventory_notes: Only reddit is currently a Composio provider. Composio manages token refresh; no Key Vault write occurs.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to disconnect a connected integration so that the service can no longer access my external accounts.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/integrations/<uuid:pk>/disconnect/ — IsAuthenticated, scoped to caller's tenant. Calls disconnect_integration: for Composio providers deletes the Composio connected_account; for Key Vault providers attempts to delete the Key Vault secret (best-effort). Deletes gws-credentials.json from Azure File Share for Google providers. Hard-deletes the Integration row. Bumps tenant pending config. Calls update_tenant_config (non-fatal if container is hibernated — returns applied='pending'). Returns {status: 'disconnected', applied: 'ok'|'pending'} HTTP 200.
dependencies: Azure Key Vault, Azure File Share, Composio API, OpenClaw container (config regeneration), Postgres
inventory_notes: Hard-delete is intentional — soft revoked/expired states confuse the UI. Config regen failure is non-fatal; container picks up new config on next wake.

user_story: As the platform, I want to proactively refresh OAuth tokens before they expire so that subscriber integrations remain active without user intervention.
expected_behaviour: QStash calls the task endpoint that invokes refresh_expiring_integrations_task(). Queries all ACTIVE integrations where token_expires_at is null or within 15 minutes. For each: loads refresh_token from Key Vault; if missing, marks EXPIRED. Calls refresh_integration_tokens which POSTs to provider token_url with grant_type=refresh_token, then calls connect_integration to persist updated tokens. On HTTP 400/401, marks EXPIRED; on other errors, marks ERROR. Returns dict {checked, refreshed, expired, errored}. Composio-managed integrations (reddit) are skipped from this task as Composio handles their own refresh.
dependencies: Azure Key Vault, Google OAuth token endpoint, Sautai OAuth token endpoint, QStash, Postgres
inventory_notes: Composio integrations have no token_expires_at so they could be selected; but they have no provider client credentials configured so they'd be immediately marked ERROR. This is a latent bug if reddit integrations have token_expires_at=null.
error_detail: Confirmed real. The QStash-wired cron refresh_expiring_integrations_task (registered at apps/cron/views.py:86 as refresh_expiring_integrations) flips healthy Composio-managed reddit integrations to ERROR on every run. Chain verified end to end: (1) Reddit is a real Provider (models.py:20) and a Composio-managed provider (services.py:28, COMPOSIO_MANAGED_PROVIDERS contains reddit). (2) complete_composio_connection persists it with status=ACTIVE and a defaults dict that omits token_expires_at (services.py:120-130); the field is null=True with no default (models.py:46), so it is stored NULL. (3) The task query selects ACTIVE rows where Q(token_expires_at__isnull=True) (tasks.py:32-35), matching every reddit integration. (4) get_provider_client_credentials(reddit) returns empty strings because reddit is absent from PROVIDER_GROUP (services.py:302-305, 338-344). (5) The guard if not client_id or not client_secret (tasks.py:43) is therefore always true for reddit, so the row is set to ERROR and errored is incremented (tasks.py:44-46). There is NO is_composio_provider() guard in the task, even though that helper exists (services.py:33) and is applied elsewhere (views.py:225,384). Composio providers do not use OAuth client-credential refresh at all; their tokens are retrieved live from Composio (_get_composio_access_token, services.py:174-219). User impact: a user who connects Reddit silently has the integration flipped to ERROR on the next refresh cron tick, breaking it until manual reconnect, and reconnecting only resets the clock until the next run. The existing test test_marks_error_when_oauth_credentials_missing demonstrates this exact ERROR mechanism; no test covers the reddit/Composio case.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to authenticate to the Django control plane using a tenant-scoped key so that only my tenant's data is accessible.
expected_behaviour: All runtime/* endpoints call _internal_auth_or_401 which delegates to validate_internal_runtime_request. Validates X-NBHD-Internal-Key and X-NBHD-Tenant-Id headers. Dual-validation: first tries per-tenant Tenant.internal_api_key (constant-time compare); if not set falls back to global settings.NBHD_INTERNAL_API_KEY. Tenant-id must match URL param. Every attempt (success/failure) is audit-logged with key_provenance and outcome fields. Raises InternalAuthError (401) on: missing key, missing tenant, tenant mismatch, bad key, no key configured. Sets RLS context (service_role=True) on success.
inventory_notes: Phase 1 dual-key migration in progress. Per-tenant keys take priority; global key is fallback to be removed once audit shows 0 legacy_global hits for 48h.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to list Gmail messages for the tenant so that the AI assistant can read and summarize the user's emails.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/gmail/messages/ — internal auth required. Optional query params: q (Gmail search query), max_results (1-10, default 5). Calls get_valid_provider_access_token(provider='google') which may refresh the token on-demand if expiring within 120s. Calls list_gmail_messages with the access token. Response is PII-redacted via redact_tool_response. Returns {provider, tenant_id, ...Gmail payload} HTTP 200. Error cases: 404 integration_not_connected, 409 integration_inactive/inactive/scope_insufficient, 503 provider_not_configured, 502 integration_refresh_failed or provider_request_failed.
dependencies: Google Gmail API, Azure Key Vault (token retrieval), Postgres, apps/pii
inventory_notes: PII redaction runs on every response before returning. Scope check ensures gmail.readonly or gmail.modify scope is present.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to fetch the full body of a specific Gmail message (and optionally its thread) so that the AI assistant can read and respond to individual emails.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/gmail/messages/<message_id>/ — internal auth required. Optional query params: include_thread (default true), thread_limit (1-10, default 5). Validates message_id is non-empty. Calls get_gmail_message_detail with access token. PII-redacted. Returns {provider, tenant_id, ...message payload} HTTP 200. Same error taxonomy as the list endpoint.
dependencies: Google Gmail API, Azure Key Vault, Postgres, apps/pii

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to list the user's calendar events for a given time window so that the AI assistant can discuss and plan around scheduled events.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/google-calendar/events/ — internal auth required. Accepts window_kind (enum: today/yesterday/tomorrow/all/this_week/last_week/month_to_date/last_month/year_to_date/last_year/last_n_days/next_n_days/last_n_weeks/last_n_months/since/between) plus optional window_value, or legacy time_min/time_max RFC3339 strings. Returns error 400 if both shapes provided. max_results 1-20 (default 10). Resolves window in tenant timezone. Calls list_calendar_events. PII-redacted. Returns {provider, tenant_id, ...events}.
dependencies: Google Calendar API, Azure Key Vault, Postgres, apps/pii
inventory_notes: window_kind='all' passes no time bounds to Google, which may return large result sets. Agent should prefer specific windows.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to fetch the user's free/busy slots so that the AI assistant can suggest available meeting times.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/google-calendar/freebusy/ — internal auth required. Accepts same window_kind/time_min/time_max params as the events endpoint. Calls get_calendar_freebusy with the resolved time range. Returns {provider, tenant_id, ...freebusy payload}. Note: no PII redaction applied (unlike Gmail/events). Same error taxonomy as other Google endpoints.
dependencies: Google Calendar API, Azure Key Vault, Postgres
inventory_notes: PII redaction is NOT applied to free/busy responses, unlike Gmail and calendar events.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to read and write journal entries for the tenant so that the AI assistant can recall and record the user's reflections.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/journal-entries/ — lists JournalEntry rows for tenant, ordered by -date,-created_at. Optional date_from/date_to (YYYY-MM-DD, must be provided together, max 31-day span). POST creates a new entry via JournalEntryRuntimeSerializer. Both require internal auth. GET returns {tenant_id, entries: [...], count}; POST returns {tenant_id, entry: {...}} HTTP 201. Returns 400 on bad date range.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to record a weekly review so that the AI assistant can persist the user's weekly reflections.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/weekly-reviews/ — internal auth required. Creates a WeeklyReview via WeeklyReviewRuntimeSerializer with tenant context. Returns {tenant_id, review: {...}} HTTP 201. Returns 400 on validation failure.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to list and create goals for the tenant so that the AI assistant can track the user's long-term objectives.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/goals/ — filters by optional ?status, ?pillar, ?parent_goal_id. Returns {tenant_id, goals: [...], count}. POST — validates via GoalSerializer; dedup-checks against find_duplicate_goal (fuzzy title match on active+recently-closed goals); if duplicate found returns {goal, deduped:True} HTTP 200 (not 201); otherwise creates and returns HTTP 201. Internal auth required for both.
inventory_notes: Dedup returns HTTP 200 with deduped=True rather than 409 — the agent receives the existing row and can proceed without retrying.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to retrieve and update a specific goal so that the AI assistant can reflect and modify the user's tracked objectives.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/goals/<goal_id>/ — returns {tenant_id, goal} or 404. PATCH — partial update via GoalSerializer with tenant context; returns updated goal. Internal auth required.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to mark a goal as achieved so that the user's accomplishments are recorded in the journal.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/goals/<goal_id>/achieve/ — internal auth required. Calls goal.mark_achieved() which transitions status. Returns {tenant_id, goal} HTTP 200, or 404 if goal not found.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to mark a goal as abandoned so that dropped objectives are recorded without cluttering active goals.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/goals/<goal_id>/abandon/ — internal auth required. Calls goal.abandon(). Returns {tenant_id, goal} HTTP 200 or 404.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to list and create tasks for the tenant so that the AI assistant can manage the user's action items.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/tasks/ — filters by ?status, ?pillar, ?parent_goal_id, ?due_before, ?due_after. Returns {tenant_id, tasks, count}. POST — dedup-checks via find_duplicate_task (title match against open + recently-closed tasks); if duplicate returns {task, deduped:True} HTTP 200; otherwise creates and returns HTTP 201. Internal auth required.
inventory_notes: Idempotency backstop prevents cron maintenance turns from resurrecting completed tasks as new open duplicates.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to retrieve and update a specific task so that the AI assistant can adjust task details during conversation.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/tasks/<task_id>/ — returns {tenant_id, task} or 404. PATCH — partial update via TaskSerializer; returns updated task. Internal auth required.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to mark a task as done so that completed work is reflected in the user's journal.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/tasks/<task_id>/complete/ — internal auth required. Calls task.complete() (sets status=done, completed_at=now). Returns {tenant_id, task} HTTP 200 or 404.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to mark a task as skipped so that the user can move past items they've consciously decided not to do.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/tasks/<task_id>/skip/ — internal auth required. Calls task.skip(). Returns {tenant_id, task} HTTP 200 or 404.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to defer a task to a later date so that the user's task list reflects their actual priorities.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/tasks/<task_id>/defer/ — internal auth required. Calls task.defer(). Returns {tenant_id, task} HTTP 200 or 404.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to fetch a live authoritative snapshot of open tasks, active goals, and recurring finance obligations so that cron and proactive messages reflect the user's actual current state.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/current-status/ — internal auth required. Calls build_journal_status(tenant, today_in_tenant_tz). Returns {tenant_id, ...status_projection} HTTP 200. Projection includes open tasks, active goals, recurring finance obligations from the ledger by calendar month (not Task.status). Designed specifically to avoid stale-nag class where cron messages re-derive status from stored narration.
inventory_notes: Uses tenant-local date for today, not UTC.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to read the user's daily markdown note so that the AI assistant has context for today's conversation.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/daily-note/ — internal auth required. Optional ?date (YYYY-MM-DD); defaults to tenant-local today. Uses Document model (kind=daily, slug=date). Creates document via get_or_create if missing with default template. Returns {tenant_id, date, markdown, sections} where sections is parsed from markdown headers.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to append content to the user's daily note so that AI-generated observations and summaries are persisted to the journal.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/daily-note/append/ — internal auth required. Body: {content (required), date (optional), section_slug (optional)}. If section_slug provided: replaces content within the matching '## Section Heading' block, or appends a new section if not found. If no section_slug: appends a timestamped bullet '- **HH:MM** (PersonaName) — content'. Creates Document if missing. Returns {tenant_id, date, markdown, sections} HTTP 201.
inventory_notes: Section replacement finds the first matching '## Heading' and replaces up to the next '## ' marker — does not handle '#' or '###' headings.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to read and overwrite the user's long-term memory document so that persistent facts about the user are available across sessions.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/long-term-memory/ — internal auth. Creates Document(kind=memory, slug=long-term) via get_or_create if missing. Returns {tenant_id, markdown}. PUT — body {markdown}; creates or fully replaces the document's markdown. Returns {tenant_id, markdown} HTTP 200. Both require internal auth.
inventory_notes: PUT is a full replacement — no merge logic.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to fetch a combined context bundle (recent daily notes, long-term memory, backbone docs, constellation) at session start so that the AI assistant begins every conversation fully informed.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/journal-context/ — internal auth. Optional ?days (1-30, default 7). Returns: recent_notes (daily Documents >= cutoff date), long_term_memory (markdown string), backbone {goals: rendered markdown, tasks: rendered markdown, ideas: doc} using dual-read renderers from apps/journal/envelope. Also returns constellation key if build_constellation_context returns data. Calculates cutoff as (now - days) in tenant timezone.
inventory_notes: Backbone goals/tasks use the dual-read renderers so typed Goal/Task rows and Document-based rows both surface. Constellation data is omitted (key absent) if quiet.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to list undistilled YardTalk work sessions so that the AI assistant can extract and persist insights from recent coding/work sessions.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/sessions/pending/ — internal auth. Optional ?limit (1-25, default 10). Returns unprocessed Sessions (processed_at=null, test_mode=false) ordered by -session_start. Returns {tenant_id, count, sessions: [...serialized]}.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to mark a session as distilled so that it is not re-processed in future cron runs.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/sessions/<session_id>/mark-processed/ — internal auth. Body: {processed_summary: object}. Idempotent: if already processed, returns existing {session_id, processed_at, processed_summary, already_processed:true} HTTP 200 without overwriting. Otherwise sets processed_at=now and processed_summary; returns HTTP 200. Returns 400 if processed_summary is not a dict.
inventory_notes: Idempotency is unconditional — even concurrent calls return the same record.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to capture lessons the user learns during conversation so that they appear in the Constellation graph immediately.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/lessons/ — internal auth. Body: {text (required), context, source_type (enum: conversation/journal/reflection/article/experience), source_ref, tags (list)}. Creates Lesson with status=approved and approved_at=now (auto-approved). Triggers process_approved_lesson (embedding + connections, best-effort). If tenant has >=5 approved lessons, triggers refresh_constellation (best-effort). Returns {tenant_id, lesson} HTTP 201.
inventory_notes: Embedding and clustering failures are swallowed (logged only) so the lesson write always succeeds.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to search approved lessons by semantic similarity so that the AI assistant can recall relevant past insights during conversation.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/lessons/search/ — internal auth. Required ?q (query text). Optional ?limit (1-50, default 10). Calls search_lessons (pgvector semantic search). Returns {tenant_id, query, count, results: [{...lesson, similarity: float}]}. Returns 500 on search failure.
inventory_notes: Similarity score is appended to each result as a float.
error_detail: Confirmed, but narrow. search_lessons() (apps/lessons/services.py:106-119) returns a lazy, unevaluated QuerySet; the pgvector CosineDistance SQL only executes when the result is iterated. In RuntimeLessonSearchView.get (apps/integrations/runtime_views.py:1716-1741) that iteration happens at line 1738, OUTSIDE the try/except at lines 1724-1735. So the try/except only guards (a) QuerySet construction and (b) the eager generate_embedding(query) HTTP call to OpenAI inside search_lessons (services.py:108), which is the most likely failure point and IS correctly mapped to the spec'd {"error":"search_failed"} 500 envelope. But a failure during the actual DB execution (Postgres OperationalError, pgvector extension unavailable, network drop to DB) is raised at line 1738 and is NOT caught. No custom DRF EXCEPTION_HANDLER is configured (verified absent in config/), and a raw django.db error is not an APIException, so DRF's default handler will not convert it; the caller gets a generic unhandled 500 instead of the search_failed envelope. Impact is low: this is an internal /runtime/ endpoint consumed by the OpenClaw runtime (machine, not an end user); DB-execution failure is rare and usually coincides with a broader outage; and the runtime treats both a raw 500 and the search_failed envelope as failure. The only real effect is a violation of the documented error-envelope contract in this one DB-failure path, slightly degrading error consistency/observability.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to list pending (unapproved) lessons so that the AI assistant can surface them for user review.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/lessons/pending/ — internal auth. Returns all Lessons with status=pending for the tenant, ordered by -suggested_at. Returns {tenant_id, count, lessons: [...]}.
inventory_notes: Runtime auto-approves lessons on creation now; pending lessons only arise from the manual web UI create path.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to fetch enriched constellation notes (pinned galaxy notes, star reflections, tutoring signals) so that the AI assistant can discuss the user's learning graph.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/constellation/notes/ — internal auth. Modes (in precedence): ?star_id=<int> returns full context for one approved star; ?q=<text> returns semantic search over stars; no params returns recently active stars. Optional ?limit (1-25, default 5), ?days (1-365, default 30). Calls constellation_notes_payload. Returns payload with tenant_id added. 400 on bad star_id. 500 on unexpected error.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to search across all the user's documents with full-text search so that the AI assistant can find relevant past notes quickly.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/journal/search/ — internal auth. Required ?q. Optional ?kind (filter by document kind), ?limit (max 50, default 20). Uses Django Postgres SearchVector (title weight A, markdown weight B) + SearchRank with websearch query type. Returns {query, count, results: [{kind, slug, title, snippet, updated_at, rank}]} where snippet is a 300-char excerpt around the first term match.
inventory_notes: Uses PostgreSQL websearch syntax. Does NOT use pgvector semantic search — that is in the lesson search endpoint.
error_detail:
Confirmed real. In RuntimeJournalSearchView.get (apps/integrations/runtime_views.py:1995) the limit param is parsed as min(int(request.query_params.get('limit','20')),50) inside a method with no surrounding try/except. A non-numeric value such as ?limit=abc raises an uncaught ValueError. No custom DRF EXCEPTION_HANDLER is configured (grep over config/ and apps/integrations/ found none), so it propagates to Django's default handler and yields a 500 instead of a 400 validation error. This contradicts the pattern used everywhere else in the file: every other limit-bearing endpoint calls _parse_positive_int(...) wrapped in try/except ValueError returning HTTP 400 (canonical example lines 1716-1722). The identical bare-int bug also exists at line 2687. Impact is bounded: the route is internal-auth gated (_internal_auth_or_401) and called by the OpenClaw container runtime (nbhd_journal_search tool), not by end users or the public, and the caller normally passes a valid integer or omits the param. It is a genuine robustness/contract gap (wrong status code, inconsistent with sibling endpoints) with a narrow trigger surface, hence low severity rather than the claimed medium.</confirmed_detail>
<parameter name="suggested_fix">Replace the bare cast with the existing helper, mirroring lines 1716-1722: wrap limit = _parse_positive_int(request.query_params.get('limit'), default=20, max_value=50) in a try/except ValueError that returns a Response with status 400 and detail str(exc). Apply the identical change at line 2687.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to read and overwrite any document by kind+slug so that the AI assistant can manage all structured document types.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/document/?kind=<kind>&slug=<slug> — internal auth. Validates kind+slug via validate_kind_slug (path-component safety, NTFS-hostile names rejected). Daily notes must have YYYY-MM-DD slug. Creates Document via get_or_create if missing. Returns {tenant_id, id, kind, slug, title, markdown, updated_at}. PUT body: {kind, slug, markdown, title?}; same validation; creates or fully replaces. Returns 200 if existed, 201 if created.
inventory_notes: PUT is a full replacement with no merge. validate_kind_slug closes the auto-create-on-get NTFS-hostile slug bug.
error_detail: Real validation asymmetry. The GET branch of RuntimeDocumentView enforces a YYYY-MM-DD regex for kind=daily (runtime_views.py:1873-1881) before the general validate_kind_slug() check. The PUT branch (runtime_views.py:1940) applies only validate_kind_slug() and omits the daily-date check. validate_kind_slug (apps/journal/path_validation.py:42-64) accepts any slug matching RUNTIME_SLUG_RE (leading alphanumeric, then alphanumeric/dot/underscore/slash/dash) for a valid kind, so a PUT with kind=daily and slug=my-notes succeeds and creates a Document(kind=daily, slug=my-notes) row. RuntimeJournalContextView (runtime_views.py:1394) selects recent daily notes with slug__gte=str(cutoff), a plain Postgres text comparison: 'm' (0x6d) sorts above '2' (0x32) in ASCII, so 'my-notes' >= '2026-06-15' is true and the garbage row passes the date-cutoff filter and is included in the journal context bundle that seeds every agent session; ordered by slug it sorts after all real ISO-date slugs and surfaces as the apparent most-recent daily note. The feature's expected_behaviour explicitly states daily notes must have a YYYY-MM-DD slug and that PUT uses the same validation, so the PUT branch not enforcing it is a genuine gap, not intended behaviour. Impact is bounded: the caller is the trusted-internal OpenClaw container/LLM agent (internal auth, not an external attack surface), so it requires the agent to PUT a non-date daily slug (a typo or generatively-chosen label); when it does, the daily-note context bundle is silently corrupted and can mislead subsequent agent sessions. The same gap also exists in the daily-note set-section endpoint near line 2286, which only defaults an empty slug to today and otherwise relies solely on validate_kind_slug.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to append time-stamped content to any document so that AI observations are added non-destructively to existing notes.
expected_behaviour:
POST /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/document/append/ — internal auth. Body: {kind (default daily), slug (optional; defaults to today for daily or kind for singletons), content (required)}. Validates kind+slug. Creates Document if missing. Appends '

### HH:MM — PersonaName
content
' to existing markdown. Returns {tenant_id, id, kind, slug, title, markdown, updated_at} HTTP 201.
inventory_notes: Different from daily-note append: always appends as a H3 timestamped block with persona name; no section-slug targeting.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to fetch all syncable documents as workspace-relative file paths so that the AI assistant can mirror the journal to the local filesystem.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/memory-sync/ — internal auth. Calls render_memory_files(tenant) from apps/orchestrator/memory_sync. Returns {tenant_id, files: {path: markdown_content}, count}. Files dict is workspace-relative paths. NOTE: memory_search is disabled fleet-wide; actual search routes through nbhd_journal_search → Postgres. This endpoint is a journal-of-record mirror only.
inventory_notes: memory_search (SQLite on Azure File Share) was disabled fleet-wide due to SMB lock/fsync corruption risk.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to report token usage for polling-mode turns so that billing and usage tracking are accurate.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/usage/report/ — internal auth. Body: {event_type (required, str), model_used (required, str), input_tokens (non-negative int), output_tokens (non-negative int)}. Calls record_usage(tenant, ...). Returns {status: 'ok'} HTTP 200. Returns 400 on missing required fields or invalid token counts (floats-not-integer, booleans, negative).
inventory_notes: Strict int parsing rejects booleans (bool is int subclass in Python) and float-with-decimal.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to report BYO provider errors so that the subscriber's AI Provider settings page shows an actionable error banner.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/byo/error/ — internal auth. Body: {provider (str, currently only 'anthropic'), reason (enum: billing/auth/auth_permanent/or other), message, model_used}. If reason not in {billing, auth, auth_permanent}, returns {status: 'ignored'} HTTP 200 (transient errors not surfaced). For actionable reasons, calls mark_credential_error which sets BYOCredential.status=error and last_error to a user-facing hint. If no matching credential found, returns {status: 'no_credential'} HTTP 200. Returns {status: 'ok', credential_id} on success. Always idempotent.
inventory_notes: Only 'anthropic' is a known provider. Unknown providers return 400. Non-actionable reasons (rate limits, transient overloads) are explicitly ignored.
