user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to update the user's profile fields (timezone, display_name, language, location) after user confirms in conversation so that the platform stays in sync with user preferences.
expected_behaviour: PATCH /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/profile/ — internal auth. Allowed fields: timezone (validated via ZoneInfo), display_name (<=100 chars), language (<=10 chars), location_city (<=255 chars), location_lat+location_lon (float pair, validated ranges -90..90 and -180..180). At least one valid field must be provided (400 no_changes otherwise). Saves user fields. If timezone changed: updates all managed CronJob rows schedule.tz, bumps config, calls update_tenant_config. If location changed: bumps config, calls update_tenant_config.
dependencies: Postgres (User, CronJob models), OpenClaw container (config regeneration via apps/orchestrator)
inventory_notes: Timezone change propagates to all managed cron rows and triggers container config refresh so agent picks up new userTimezone.
error_detail: Confirmed in code. RuntimeProfileUpdateView.patch validates fields inconsistently. timezone (invalid name) and location_lat/location_lon (out-of-range or non-numeric) return explicit 400 errors (invalid_timezone, invalid_coordinates). But display_name (>100 chars), language (>10 chars), and location_city (>255 chars) — and any of these arriving empty after .strip() — are silently skipped: the guards (if name and len(name) <= 100, etc.) simply do not append to updated_fields, with no error path (lines 2376-2392). If every field the caller supplied falls into a silent-skip branch, updated_fields is empty and the endpoint returns 400 no_changes / "No valid fields to update." (lines 2413-2417), which misrepresents the failure: a value rejected for being too long is reported identically to sending no fields. The caller is the OpenClaw container runtime (an LLM agent under internal auth), not a human, so there is no UI to surface the dropped value. Impact: the agent cannot distinguish "no field sent" from "field rejected for length," so it may believe the profile is in sync when the update was actually dropped, or cannot explain to the user why a stated preference (e.g. a long display name) did not take. Real but genuinely low — these are edge-case lengths on user-confirmed conversational values, and the partial-success case (one valid field plus one too-long field) still saves the valid field, dropping only the bad one.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to scan active goals, tasks, project docs, finance accounts, and fuel records for rows affected by a user's report so that the AI can suggest targeted updates rather than re-reading all data.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/reconcile/scan/?claim=<sentence> — internal auth. Tokenizes the claim (lowercase, strip stopwords, len>=3). Checks finance/fuel keyword triggers. Scores Goals (active, up to 50), Tasks (open/in-progress, up to 100), project Documents (kind=project, up to 50) by token substring match. Conditionally scores FinanceAccounts (if finance_triggered and tenant.finance_active) and Workouts/BodyWeightLog (if fuel_triggered and tenant.fuel_enabled). Each candidate includes kind, id, title, score, matched_tokens, current_state, and update_tools suggestion. Returns sorted by score, capped at ?limit (default 15, max 25).
dependencies: Postgres (Goal, Task, Document, FinanceAccount, Workout, BodyWeightLog models)
inventory_notes: Finance scan only runs if tenant.finance_active=True; fuel scan only if tenant.fuel_enabled=True. Claim is truncated to 500 chars. This is keyword-based (not semantic) matching.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to post a summary of a cron run so that the user's main chat session is informed about what the background cron just communicated.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/cron-phase2-summary/ — internal auth. Body: {summary (required, non-empty str), job_name (required)}. Computes a cron expression firing 2 minutes from now in tenant timezone (date-specific: m h d M * — fires once). Creates a _sync:<job_name> one-shot cron via cron.add gateway tool with sessionTarget=main, payload text = '[Sync — job_name] summary After noting this, run: cron remove _sync:<job_name>'. Returns {ok:true, sync_cron_name, fires_at} or 502 if gateway call fails.
inventory_notes: The cron expression encodes exact minute/hour/day/month, firing exactly once. The payload instructs the agent to self-remove via 'cron remove' after processing.

feature: Runtime typed cron create — pure reminder
user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to create a typed 'pure reminder' cron so that the user receives a scheduled fixed-text reminder without LLM generation.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/crons/pure_reminder/ — internal auth. Body: {name, schedule, text}. Creates typed CronJob via create_typed_cron with pattern=pure_reminder. Returns {tenant_id, cron: {id, name, pattern, schedule, managed, gateway_job_id}} HTTP 201. Returns 409 on name conflict (CronNameConflictError), 400 on validation errors or Pydantic schema mismatches.
dependencies: Postgres (CronJob model via apps/cron), OpenClaw container (gateway cron.add)

feature: Runtime typed cron create — quote user intent
user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to create a 'quote user intent' cron that re-states the user's own goal at schedule time so that the assistant grounds its message in what the user originally said.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/crons/quote_user_intent/ — internal auth. Body: {name, schedule, text, refresh_facts_via? (optional)}. Creates typed CronJob with pattern=quote_user_intent. Same 201/409/400 response contract as pure_reminder.

feature: Runtime typed cron create — domain summary
user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to create a 'domain summary' cron that queries a data source and renders a templated message so that regular reports are delivered to the user without ad-hoc LLM generation.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/crons/domain_summary/ — internal auth. Body: {name, schedule, query_tool, query_args (object), render_block}. Creates typed CronJob with pattern=domain_summary. Same 201/409/400 response contract.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container cron-enforcement plugin, I want to fetch the pattern context for a firing cron so that the correct validator and prompt injection are applied.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/crons/<cron_name>/pattern_context/ — internal auth. Calls fetch_cron_pattern_context(tenant_id, cron_name). Returns {pattern, typed_payload, name, prompt_injection} HTTP 200, or 404 {error: not_typed} if the cron has no typed pattern.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container cron-enforcement plugin, I want to know whether to inject the live-status grounding rule into a firing cron so that custom crons ground on live data while system crons avoid double-injection.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/crons/<cron_name>/grounding/ — internal auth. Returns {inject: false, rule: ''} for: crons starting with _SYSTEM_GENERATED_PREFIXES (_sync:/_fuel:), or crons whose payload.message contains CRON_PREAMBLE_MARKER (full preamble already baked). Returns {inject: true, rule: CRON_GROUNDING_RULE} for all other crons (typed patterns, user/freeform, legacy, agent-created, unknown).
inventory_notes: Cron rows not found in DB return inject=True (unknown/agent-created crons default to getting grounding).

user_story: As an OpenClaw container cron-enforcement plugin, I want to validate an outbound message against the firing cron's pattern contract so that typed crons deliver content that matches their declared template.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/crons/<cron_name>/validate_outbound/ — internal auth. Body: {content: str}. Returns {ok: bool, reason?: str, fallback_content?: str} HTTP 200 always (no 4xx for contract failures — returns ok=false with explanation instead). Returns 400 only if content is not a string.
inventory_notes: Validation failures return HTTP 200 with ok=false rather than 4xx — the enforcement plugin decides what to do (use fallback_content or block).

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to list and create workspaces for the tenant so that the AI assistant can organize work across multiple named contexts.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/workspaces/ — internal auth. Returns all Workspace rows ordered by -is_default, -last_used_at, name. Includes active_workspace_id and WORKSPACE_LIMIT. POST body: {name (required, max 60 chars), description}. Name must not start with reserved prefixes. If first workspace ever: auto-creates default workspace first. Enforces WORKSPACE_LIMIT (409 on breach). Generates unique slug. Creates workspace, embeds description via _embed_workspace_description, sets as active. Returns HTTP 201.
inventory_notes: Description is embedded via pgvector for future semantic workspace routing (not currently used for retrieval in this endpoint).

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to rename, update, or delete a workspace so that the user's workspace organization stays accurate.
expected_behaviour: PATCH /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/workspaces/<slug>/ — internal auth. Updates name (max 60, not reserved) and/or description (re-embeds description). PATCH with no valid fields still returns 200 with empty updated:[]. DELETE — prevents deletion of default workspace (409 cannot_delete_default). If deleting active workspace, falls back active_workspace to default. Hard-deletes the row. Returns {tenant_id, deleted_id, fell_back_to_default}.
inventory_notes: Default workspace cannot be deleted — prevents tenants from ending up with no workspace.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to switch the tenant's active workspace so that the AI assistant operates in the correct context for the current conversation.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/workspaces/switch/ — internal auth. Body: {slug (required)}. Finds workspace by slug (404 if not found). Sets tenant.active_workspace and saves. Updates workspace.last_used_at to now. Returns {tenant_id, workspace, previous_workspace_id}.
inventory_notes: switch/ URL must be registered before <slug>/ to avoid it being captured as a workspace slug.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to initiate Reddit OAuth via Composio on behalf of the user so that the AI assistant can guide the user through connecting their Reddit account.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/reddit/connect/ — internal auth. Body: {callback_url (required)}. Calls initiate_composio_connection to get a Composio redirect_url + connection_request_id. Returns {redirect_url, connection_request_id} HTTP 200. 400 on missing callback_url, 503 on IntegrationProviderConfigError, 500 on unexpected error.
inventory_notes: Connection_request_id is NOT cached here (unlike the subscriber flow via OAuthAuthorizeView); the agent must pass it to the complete endpoint directly.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to complete the Reddit OAuth flow by polling Composio until active so that the connection is confirmed and persisted.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/reddit/complete/ — internal auth. Body: {connection_request_id (required)}. Calls complete_composio_connection which polls Composio for up to 30 seconds for status=ACTIVE, then upserts Integration row. Returns {connected: true, provider_email} HTTP 200. 400 on missing connection_request_id. 409/503 on specific integration errors. 500 on unexpected error.
inventory_notes: 30-second synchronous poll on the Django request thread. If user doesn't complete OAuth within 30s, connection fails.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to check if Reddit is connected for the tenant so that the AI assistant knows whether Reddit tools are available.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/reddit/status/ — internal auth. Checks for active Integration row with provider=reddit. Returns {connected: bool, provider_email: str} HTTP 200.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to disconnect the Reddit integration for the tenant so that the user's Reddit access is revoked when they ask.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/reddit/disconnect/ — internal auth. Calls disconnect_integration(tenant, 'reddit') which deletes the Composio connected account (best-effort) and hard-deletes the Integration row. Returns {disconnected: true} HTTP 200. 500 on unexpected error.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to execute Reddit actions (digest, search, post, comment, etc.) via the Composio tool bridge so that the AI assistant can interact with Reddit on the user's behalf.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/reddit/tool/ — internal auth. Body: {action (required), ...params}. Maps action to Composio tool slug via _REDDIT_ACTION_MAP: digest→REDDIT_GET_R_TOP, search→REDDIT_SEARCH_ACROSS_SUBREDDITS, my_activity→REDDIT_GET_REDDIT_USER_ABOUT, comments→REDDIT_RETRIEVE_POST_COMMENTS, post→REDDIT_CREATE_REDDIT_POST, reply→REDDIT_POST_REDDIT_COMMENT, edit→REDDIT_EDIT_REDDIT_COMMENT_OR_POST, delete_post→REDDIT_DELETE_REDDIT_POST, delete_comment→REDDIT_DELETE_REDDIT_COMMENT, new→REDDIT_GET_NEW, controversial→REDDIT_GET_CONTROVERSIAL. Calls Composio tools.execute. PII-redacts result. Returns result HTTP 200. 400 on unknown action, missing required params, or Composio unsuccessful result. 503 on provider config error. 500 on unexpected error.
inventory_notes: dangerously_skip_version_check=True is passed to Composio tools.execute. 400/404 from Composio triggers a 'reconnect required' ValueError surfaced to agent as 400.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

feature: GWS credentials file share write/delete (side effect of OAuth connect/disconnect)
user_story: As the platform, I want to write Google OAuth credentials to the tenant's Azure File Share in gws CLI format so that the OpenClaw container's gws CLI can authenticate to Google Workspace.
expected_behaviour: Called automatically by connect_integration when provider_group='google': writes {type: authorized_user, client_id, client_secret, refresh_token} as JSON to 'ws-<tenant_prefix>/gws-credentials.json' on Azure File Share using storage account key retrieved from Azure Management API. On disconnect_integration: deletes the file (best-effort, swallows errors). Uses AZURE_MOCK env var to skip real Azure calls in dev/test.
dependencies: Azure File Share, Azure Storage Management API, azure-storage-file-share SDK
inventory_notes: avoids overwrite=True kwarg due to azure-storage-file-share 12.24/azure-core 1.39 bug where it leaks through to requests HTTP transport. Uses explicit length= param instead.

user_story: As an operator, I want to view and filter integration records in Django admin so that I can diagnose OAuth issues and monitor connection states.
expected_behaviour: Django admin at /admin/integrations/integration/ — standard ModelAdmin with list_display (provider, tenant, status, provider_email, connected_at) and list_filter (provider, status). No custom actions. Read/write access to all Integration fields including key_vault_secret_name and composio_connected_account_id.
inventory_notes: Sensitive fields (key_vault_secret_name) are visible to admin users; no masking.

feature: Legacy journal entry list and create

expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/journal/ returns a paginated list of JournalEntry rows scoped to the authenticated user's tenant, ordered by -date/-created_at, with optional ISO date filters via ?date_from= and ?date_to= (silently ignores bad formats). POST /api/v1/journal/ creates a new entry; requires IsAuthenticated; 400 on validation failure; 201 on success. Response carries Deprecation+Warning headers. Cached via @tenant_cache(ttl=60, tag='journal') on GET.
inventory_notes: Deprecated in favour of the v2 Document API. Deprecation headers are present on POST too but the view issues them only on WeeklyReview endpoints in practice.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to retrieve, update, or delete a specific journal entry so that I can correct or remove historical records.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/journal/<uuid>/ returns the entry scoped to the tenant or 404. PATCH accepts partial updates via JournalEntrySerializer. DELETE removes the row and returns 204. All methods require IsAuthenticated; 404 if the entry belongs to a different tenant.
inventory_notes: Deprecated. No Deprecation headers are emitted on this view.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to fetch my daily note for a given date in sectionized form so that I can display it with template structure in the dashboard.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/journal/daily/<YYYY-MM-DD>/ returns the DailyNote for that date, creating it with the tenant's default template markdown if absent. Response includes date, markdown, template_id/slug/name, and parsed sections array. Returns 400 if the date string is not valid ISO. Requires IsAuthenticated.
inventory_notes: Calls upsert_default_daily_note which auto-seeds the default NoteTemplate for the tenant if none exists. Legacy path — Document API is preferred.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to read and write my daily note sections through a template-aware view so that section content is persisted without corrupting the markdown structure.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/journal/daily/<date>/template/ returns the note with sections. PUT accepts {template_id, sections[]} — validates section objects (slug, title, content, source), re-renders the markdown via materialize_sections_markdown, and saves. 400 if sections is not an array or date is invalid; 404 if template_id is provided but unknown; 200 on success. Requires IsAuthenticated.
inventory_notes: Legacy path. The view also has a DELETE method that removes entries by index, but it is not routed in urls.py for this path — it exists only as dead code.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to update a single named section of my daily note so that I can save partial edits without re-sending the entire document.
expected_behaviour: PATCH /api/v1/journal/daily/<date>/sections/<slug>/ requires {content: string}. Finds or creates the DailyNote, loads its sections, updates the matching slug's content in-place (or appends a new section if the slug is not found), re-renders markdown, and saves. 400 on bad date, missing content, or section validation failure. Returns full sectionized response on 200. Requires IsAuthenticated.
inventory_notes: Will auto-create sections not present in the template, making the section list open-ended.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to append a timestamped entry to my daily note so that I can log events inline in a chronological feed.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/journal/daily/<date>/entries/ accepts {content, time?, mood?, energy?}. Appends a markdown entry block to the note's markdown with author='human' and saves. Returns {date, entries} with 201. Deprecated headers emitted. 400 on bad date or serializer failure. Requires IsAuthenticated.
inventory_notes: Deprecated. Use DailyNoteSectionView or DocumentAppendView instead.

feature: Legacy daily note entry patch/delete by index
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to edit or delete a specific journal entry by its index so that I can correct or remove inline log items.
expected_behaviour: PATCH /api/v1/journal/daily/<date>/entries/<index>/ updates content/mood/energy on the entry at that index and re-serializes. DELETE removes it. Both return 404 if the note or index is out of range; Deprecated headers emitted. Requires IsAuthenticated.
inventory_notes: Deprecated. Index-based addressing is fragile and will silently shift if other entries are concurrently inserted.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to read and replace my long-term memory document so that I can manage persistent context the assistant always carries.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/journal/memory/ returns {markdown, updated_at} from Document(kind='memory', slug='long-term') for the tenant; returns empty string if not found (no 404). PUT /api/v1/journal/memory/ upserts the document with the provided markdown (validated by MemoryPatchSerializer). Returns 200 with {markdown, updated_at}. Requires IsAuthenticated.
inventory_notes: Writes to the v2 Document model, not the legacy UserMemory model. Post-save signal triggers a debounced QStash workspace memory sync.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to create and list weekly reviews so that I can track weekly mood, wins, challenges, lessons, and intentions.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/journal/reviews/ returns all WeeklyReview rows for the tenant ordered by -week_start. POST creates a new review via WeeklyReviewSerializer. Both emit Deprecated headers. Requires IsAuthenticated.
inventory_notes: Deprecated. No migration to Document model is planned; the weekly review is still only on the WeeklyReview legacy model.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to read, update, or delete a specific weekly review so that I can correct or remove historical weekly summaries.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/journal/reviews/<uuid>/ returns the review scoped to the tenant or 404. PATCH accepts partial updates. DELETE removes and returns 204. Deprecated headers on all responses. Requires IsAuthenticated.
inventory_notes: Deprecated. Paired with WeeklyReviewListCreateView.
error_detail: Confirmed: the DELETE handler in WeeklyReviewDetailView (apps/journal/views.py:455-459) returns Response(status=status.HTTP_204_NO_CONTENT) without the headers=DEPRECATION_HEADERS argument, whereas every sibling handler passes it consistently — GET (line 445), PATCH (line 453), and the paired list/create view's GET (429) and POST (436). The feature record's expected_behaviour explicitly states "Deprecated headers on all responses." So DELETE alone omits the Deprecation: true + Warning: 299 headers (defined at lines 62-65). Real-world impact is minimal: the delete itself works correctly (returns 204, tenant-scoped), and these headers are purely informational deprecation telemetry that an end-user/subscriber never perceives. The only affected consumer is a developer/operator inspecting response headers to track deprecated-endpoint usage; for the DELETE method they get an incomplete signal. This is a genuine but cosmetic spec-vs-code consistency gap on a deprecated endpoint, not a functional or security defect — matching the low-severity ux concern.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to list and create note templates so that I can structure my daily notes with custom sections.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/journal/templates/ returns all NoteTemplate rows for the tenant, ordered by -is_default, name. POST creates a new template via NoteTemplateSerializer; validates sections (slug uniqueness, required fields, valid source). Returns 201 on success. Requires IsAuthenticated.
inventory_notes: Unique constraint on (tenant, slug). Creating a duplicate slug returns a 400.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to view, edit, and delete note templates so that I can keep my daily note structure up to date.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/journal/templates/<id>/ returns the template or 404. PATCH updates fields; if is_default is set to true after the PATCH, triggers an async update_tenant_config_task (best-effort, non-blocking). DELETE removes the template; if it was the default, promotes another template as default; returns 400 if it's the only remaining template. Requires IsAuthenticated.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres, OpenClaw container (async config update via orchestrator)
inventory_notes: Deleting the default template auto-promotes another. Config update to OpenClaw is fire-and-forget.
error_detail: Confirmed asymmetry. TemplateDetailView.patch() fires update_tenant_config_task.delay(tenant.id) whenever the edited template is the default (apps/journal/views.py:504-511), regenerating the agent's templates.md skill reference. TemplateDetailView.delete() promotes an alternate to is_default=True when the current default is deleted (lines 520-525) but never fires update_tenant_config_task, so the change to the effective default is not pushed. The default template materially affects container config: apps/orchestrator/personas.py render_templates_md() (lines 320-345) calls apps.journal.services.get_default_template() (services.py:207, filters is_default=True) and emits templates.md describing that template's name/slug/sections to the agent. After a delete-and-promote, the container's templates.md keeps describing the now-deleted template until an independent config bump (image bump, wake, or another template PATCH) refreshes it. Impact: a transient staleness window where the assistant's daily-note template reference points at a template the user just deleted. No data loss, no crash, and the DB stays correct (a default always exists via the explicit promotion plus get_default_template's fallback). Low severity / low frequency (only when deleting the default template while alternates exist), but a genuine missing config-push relative to the PATCH path's own established pattern.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to approve a pending extraction from the web UI so that extracted goals, tasks, or lessons are persisted to my journal.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/journal/extractions/<uuid>/approve/ looks up a PendingExtraction in status=PENDING scoped to the tenant. Routes to the appropriate approval callback (_approve_goal, _approve_task, _approve_lesson). Sets status=APPROVED and resolved_at=now. Returns {id, status: 'approved'} on 200; 404 if not found or already processed; 500 if the approval callback raises. Requires IsAuthenticated.
inventory_notes: Lessons are auto-approved during nightly extraction; this endpoint mainly handles PENDING goals and tasks surfaced on the web dashboard.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to dismiss a pending extraction from the web UI so that unwanted suggestions are not added to my journal.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/journal/extractions/<uuid>/dismiss/ looks up a PendingExtraction in status=PENDING scoped to the tenant, sets status=DISMISSED and resolved_at=now, returns {id, status: 'dismissed'}. 404 if not found or already processed. Requires IsAuthenticated. No side-effect callbacks.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to list and create documents across kinds (daily, weekly, tasks, goals, ideas, memory, project) so that I can manage my personal knowledge base.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/journal/documents/?kind=<kind> lists documents filtered by kind; for kind=daily supports ?date_from and ?date_to via slug comparison (slug is always YYYY-MM-DD for daily). Orders daily by -slug (date desc), others by -updated_at. POST /api/v1/journal/documents/ creates or returns an existing document (get_or_create by tenant+kind+slug); 201 if new, 200 if exists. Default markdown is seeded from the tenant's NoteTemplate for daily kinds, from static templates for others. Requires IsAuthenticated.
inventory_notes: Slug for daily must match YYYY-MM-DD or validation raises. Post-save signal queues a debounced workspace memory sync via QStash.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to read, update, or delete any document by kind and slug so that I can manage my journal content freely.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/journal/documents/<kind>/<slug>/ returns the document; auto-creates singletons (tasks, ideas, memory). When tenant.experimental_typed_journal_lifecycle is true, kind=tasks is synthesized from typed Task rows and kind=goal from typed Goal rows — the markdown in the response is rendered live, not read from the DB. PATCH accepts {markdown, title}; rejects markdown writes to tasks/goal kinds when typed lifecycle is on (409 with typed_lifecycle_readonly error). DELETE is forbidden for kind=daily (403); other kinds require the document to exist (404 on miss). Requires IsAuthenticated; GET cached by @tenant_cache(ttl=60, tag='journal').
inventory_notes: The 409 typed_lifecycle_readonly guard is critical: it prevents silent data loss when tasks/goals are typed rows but the UI tries to write markdown.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to wipe a document's content without deleting it so that I can start a fresh daily note or singleton without losing the document's identity.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/journal/documents/<kind>/<slug>/clear/ sets markdown to '' and saves. Returns the serialized document on 200. 404 if document does not exist (does not auto-create). Slug must pass _validate_slug (400 otherwise). Requires IsAuthenticated.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to append a timestamped log entry to any document so that I can add inline notes without overwriting existing content.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/journal/documents/<kind>/<slug>/append/ accepts {content: string, time?: HH:MM}. Get-or-creates the document, appends a '### HH:MM — MJ\n<content>' block to the markdown tail, and saves. Returns the updated document with 201. Requires IsAuthenticated.
inventory_notes: Author label is hardcoded to 'MJ'. For agent-authored appends via the runtime path, the agent uses nbhd_daily_note_set_section which calls append_log_to_note and uses the persona name.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to fetch today's daily note without specifying the date so that my dashboard can always show the current day.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/journal/today/ computes today's date in UTC (timezone.now().date()), get-or-creates a Document(kind='daily', slug=<today>) with default markdown, and returns it serialized. Requires IsAuthenticated.
inventory_notes: Uses server UTC date, not tenant local date. For tenants in non-UTC timezones the 'today' result may differ from what the user expects around midnight.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to retrieve the structured sidebar tree of all my documents so that the dashboard can render navigation without per-kind queries.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/journal/tree/ returns a list of {kind, label, items[]} where each item has slug, title, updated_at. Future daily notes (slug > today) are hidden. Daily notes are capped at 30, weekly reviews at 12. Cached via @tenant_cache(ttl=120, tag='sidebar'). Requires IsAuthenticated.
inventory_notes: Only returns Document rows, not typed Goal/Task rows. The 'goals' slot in the tree is keyed to kind='goal' Documents.
error_detail: Confirmed code-level asymmetry. In SidebarTreeView.get (document_views.py:445-468) the queryset has no explicit order_by, so it inherits Document.Meta.ordering=['-updated_at'] (models.py:198-200). Only daily notes get an explicit chronological re-sort (lines 461-463: tree['daily'].sort by slug desc). Weekly reviews are passed straight to tree.get('weekly', [])[:12] with no slug sort, so the cap keeps the 12 most-recently-EDITED reviews, not the 12 most-recent weeks, and the 12 shown are ordered by edit time rather than week order. Weekly slugs are date strings (YYYY-MM-DD, the week's Monday — confirmed by dashboard/views.py:60-72 _WEEKLY_SLUG_RE), so they are lexicographically date-sortable exactly like daily slugs; the fix is identical to the daily path. User impact: a user with more than 12 weekly reviews (sustained ~3+ months of weekly use) who edits an old review bumps it into the visible top-12 and silently displaces a more-recent week from the sidebar; within the list the order is edit-recency, not chronological. The data is never lost (the document still exists and is reachable directly), so this is a visibility/ordering glitch, not data loss — low severity, and only manifests beyond the 12-item cap.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to view my current journal status (open tasks, active goals, debt obligations) so that the journal page always reflects live state rather than a stale snapshot.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/journal/status/ requires IsAuthenticated. Calls build_journal_status(tenant, today) where today is in the tenant's local timezone. Returns a dict with as_of, typed_lifecycle, finance_enabled, open_tasks, active_goals, obligations, plus any additional provider keys. Providers that raise are isolated: their key is added to 'unavailable' but the rest of the response still returns. Finance obligations fold FinanceTransaction payments within the current calendar month — a partial payment shows period_status='partial'.
inventory_notes: Finance projection uses calendar-month window, not due-day window. Task with related_ref pointing to a FinanceAccount is suppressed from open_tasks when finance is active to avoid double-signaling.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to list and create typed tasks so that I can track actionable items with proper status, due dates, and goal linkage.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/journal/tasks/ filters on ?status, ?pillar, ?parent_goal_id, ?due_before (ISO date), ?due_after (ISO date); returns 400 if date/uuid params are malformed; returns all matching Task rows ordered by -updated_at. POST creates a Task via TaskSerializer; returns 201. Both require IsAuthenticated.
inventory_notes: Task creation via this endpoint skips the dedup guard in apps/journal/dedup.py — that guard is only applied on the agent/runtime path via apps/integrations/runtime_views. User-initiated UI creates are never blocked.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to read or update a specific task's fields so that I can correct titles, due dates, or descriptions without using status-transition endpoints.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/journal/tasks/<uuid>/ returns the Task or {error: not_found}/404. PATCH /api/v1/journal/tasks/<uuid>/ accepts partial updates via TaskSerializer. Both require IsAuthenticated and scope to the user's tenant.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to mark a task complete so that its status and completed_at timestamp are recorded correctly.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/journal/tasks/<uuid>/complete/ calls Task.complete() which sets status=done and completed_at=now() via update_fields=['status','completed_at','updated_at']. Returns the serialized task on 200; 404 if not found. Requires IsAuthenticated.
inventory_notes: Using this endpoint (rather than a raw PATCH to status) ensures completed_at is set correctly and the dedup closed-window logic sees it.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to reopen a completed task so that I can work on it again after it was prematurely marked done.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/journal/tasks/<uuid>/reopen/ sets status=open and completed_at=None via update_fields. Returns serialized task on 200; 404 if not found. Requires IsAuthenticated.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to list and create typed goals so that I can track multi-day intentions with pillar tagging and target dates.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/journal/goals/ filters on ?status, ?pillar, ?parent_goal_id (UUID, 400 if malformed); returns all matching Goal rows ordered by -updated_at. POST creates a Goal via GoalSerializer; returns 201. Both require IsAuthenticated.
inventory_notes: Goals support hierarchical parent_goal (self-referential FK). Duplicate detection is not applied on the user-facing path.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to read or update a specific goal's fields so that I can refine description, pillar, or target date.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/journal/goals/<uuid>/ returns the Goal or 404. PATCH accepts partial updates via GoalSerializer. Both require IsAuthenticated and scope to the user's tenant.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to mark a goal as achieved so that its status and achieved_at timestamp are recorded.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/journal/goals/<uuid>/achieve/ calls Goal.mark_achieved() which sets status=achieved and achieved_at=now() via update_fields. Returns serialized goal on 200; 404 if not found. Requires IsAuthenticated.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to mark a goal as abandoned so that it no longer appears in my active goals.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/journal/goals/<uuid>/abandon/ calls Goal.abandon() which sets status=abandoned via update_fields. Returns serialized goal on 200; 404 if not found. Requires IsAuthenticated.

user_story: As the system, I want to run nightly extraction for a tenant so that lessons, goals, and tasks are automatically surfaced from the day's journal and delivered to the user.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/journal/extract/ accepts {tenant_id} JSON body. Auth: QStash signature OR X-Deploy-Secret header. Returns 200 always (so QStash does not retry on soft failures). Resolves today's daily note (v2 Document first, legacy DailyNote fallback). Calls LLM (claude-sonnet-4-6 with DeepSeek fallback) to extract lessons/goals/tasks; with typed lifecycle on, also receives task/goal reconciliation deltas. Lessons are auto-approved and written to Lesson table. Goals and tasks are auto-approved and written to Goal/Task tables with dedup. Delivers a single Telegram/LINE summary message with Remove/Undo buttons. Triggers embed_daily_note (pgvector chunking) and run_agenda_hint_pass as best-effort post-extraction steps. Sets tenant.last_nightly_extraction_at to prevent double-firing.
dependencies: QStash (auth + scheduling), Supabase/Postgres, OpenRouter (LLM via claude-sonnet-4-6/DeepSeek), Telegram Bot API, LINE Messaging API, apps/lessons, apps/pii, apps/billing (usage tracking)
inventory_notes: Returns 200 on app-level failures to avoid QStash infinite retry. If LINE delivery fails, falls back to Telegram. Extraction is skipped (skipped: no_content) if the note is under 100 chars. LLM context is capped at 6000 chars for the note and 4000 for open items.

user_story: As the system, I want to apply LLM-proposed task/goal state changes from journal evidence so that typed rows are kept in sync with what the user actually did.
expected_behaviour: Called internally by run_extraction_for_tenant when tenant.experimental_typed_journal_lifecycle is true. Validates each LLM delta: task_updates apply complete/in_progress/skip/defer; subtasks_added creates child Tasks with parent FK; goal_updates apply achieve/abandon. Each applied delta creates a PendingTaskAction row with before_state snapshot. All mutations are tenant-scoped. Skips malformed entries, unknown task/goal IDs, and already-in-target-state items (idempotent). Returns list of applied PendingTaskAction rows for inclusion in the delivery summary.
inventory_notes: Subtask creation includes a resurrection guard via titles_match to prevent the extraction loop. The LLM output is treated as untrusted: UUIDs must reference rows that actually exist and belong to the tenant.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to undo a reconciliation action from the morning summary so that I can revert auto-applied task/goal state changes.
expected_behaviour: Called via Telegram/LINE callback router when the user taps an 'Undo' button (callback_data='task_action:undo:<id>'). undo_task_action(pending) restores the task/goal's status and timestamps from before_state. For SUBTASK_CREATE, deletes the created subtask and clears the FK before deleting to avoid CASCADE corruption. Sets PendingTaskAction.status=UNDONE and resolved_at=now. Returns True on success, False if the referenced object no longer exists.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres, Telegram Bot API / LINE (for callback routing)
inventory_notes: The SUBTASK_CREATE undo must clear the task FK on the PendingTaskAction before deleting the subtask row to prevent CASCADE from removing the audit record.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

user_story: As the system, I want to chunk and embed the daily note nightly so that the agent can perform contextual recall via pgvector search.
expected_behaviour: Called at end of run_extraction_for_tenant as best-effort. embed_daily_note(tenant, today) finds Document(kind='daily', slug=today), splits markdown by ## headings (then by paragraphs if > 2000 chars), skips chunks < 80 chars. Deletes all existing DocumentChunk rows for the document before re-creating (idempotent). Each chunk is date-prefixed and embedded via generate_embedding (OpenAI text-embedding). Creates DocumentChunk rows with pgvector 1536-dim embeddings. Returns count of chunks created.
inventory_notes: Failure is non-fatal — an exception is caught and logged; extraction still completes. Chunks from the current day overwrite previous chunks for the same document.

user_story: As the system, I want to classify which open agenda threads were mentioned in today's journal so that the proactive layer can weight topics the user is warm or cold towards.
expected_behaviour: Called at end of run_extraction_for_tenant as best-effort. run_agenda_hint_pass(tenant, journal_content) fetches open_threads from the orchestrator agenda service, calls gpt-4o-mini (with Gemma/DeepSeek fallback) to classify which threads appear in the journal and how (warm/redirect/ignore/organic). For each valid match, calls record_signal and (for 'organic' + ASSISTANT_COMMITMENT kind) mark_organic. Hallucinated thread kinds/item_ids are silently dropped. Returns summary dict {matches, warm, redirect, ignore, organic}. Never raises — errors are caught and logged.
inventory_notes: Only fires after main extraction succeeds. A hint pass failure has zero effect on extraction output.

user_story: As an OpenClaw agent, I want to query journal entries, tasks, and goals with flexible filters, windows, and aggregations so that I can retrieve precise historical data without manual date arithmetic.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/journal/runtime/<tenant_id>/query/ accepts JournalQueryRequest JSON (schema_version=1, resource: entries|tasks|goals, window, window_field, filter, fields, aggregate, group_by, order_by, limit 1-500). Auth via BaseQueryView (internal per-tenant key). Validates filter keys, fields, group_by, aggregate, window_field against per-resource whitelists; returns 400 with query_error envelope for invalid inputs. Tasks support overdue filter (due_date < today excluding done/skipped). Window resolution uses tenant timezone. Aggregate supports count only for journal resources (no numeric columns). Returns {data, meta: {row_count, query_hash}}.
inventory_notes: extra='forbid' on the request schema means any unknown field returns 400. Only aggregate='count' is valid for all three resources (no numeric columns exist). window_field defaults per resource: entries->date, tasks->due_date, goals->target_date.

feature: Document post-save: workspace memory sync signal
user_story: As the system, I want to mirror journal documents to the tenant's Azure File Share whenever a document is saved so that the OpenClaw agent has up-to-date files.
expected_behaviour: post_save signal on Document calls queue_memory_sync_on_document_save. In production (QStash configured), publishes a sync_documents_to_workspace QStash task with idempotency_key='sync-documents-to-workspace-<tenant_id>-<YYYYMMDDHHmm>' after the transaction commits, in a daemon thread (non-blocking). Bucket-deduplication via QStash Upstash-Deduplication-Id means bursts of saves within the same minute coalesce to one delivery. In dev/test (no QStash), runs synchronously after commit.
dependencies: QStash (dedup publish), Azure File Share (via apps/orchestrator/memory_sync)
inventory_notes: Previous implementation used ':' in the idempotency key which QStash silently rejected, meaning no Document save propagated for an extended period. Now uses '-' as separator.

user_story: As the system, I want to auto-approve pending extractions that appear in a saved tasks or goals document so that the approval flow is seamless when the user edits the document directly.
expected_behaviour: post_save signal on Document fires auto_resolve_pending_extractions. Only handles kind=tasks (TASK extractions) or kind=goal (GOAL extractions). For each PENDING PendingExtraction of matching kind for the tenant, checks substring match or 70%+ word overlap with the document markdown. Matching ones are bulk-updated to status=APPROVED with resolved_at=now.
inventory_notes: The companion signal auto_resolve_pending_extractions_on_goal/task fires on Goal/Task creation (not update), checking the new row's title+description against PENDING extractions.

feature: Typed-row post-save: auto-resolve pending extractions on Goal/Task creation
user_story: As the system, I want to auto-approve pending extractions when a new Goal or Task is created so that user-initiated writes via the typed API don't leave extractions stuck in PENDING.
expected_behaviour: post_save signals on Goal and Task (created=True only) call _auto_resolve_extractions_for_typed_row. Checks the new row's title+description against PENDING extractions of matching kind for the tenant. Substring or 70%+ word-overlap matches are bulk-approved. Mirrors the Document-keyed handler for the typed lifecycle path.
inventory_notes: Only fires on creation (created=True). Updates to existing Goal/Task rows do not trigger this.

user_story: As the system, I want to detect duplicate task or goal titles on the agent/runtime create path so that completed or existing items are never resurrected.
expected_behaviour: find_duplicate_task(tenant, title, now) and find_duplicate_goal(tenant, title, now) are called before creating from nightly extraction or agent tool. Candidates: all open/in-progress tasks (any age) plus closed tasks updated within 14 days. Match logic: exact normalized equality (case/punctuation insensitive), or for titles with >= 3 content tokens: full containment or Jaccard >= 0.6. Returns the matching row or None. Generic 2-token titles ('Call mom') only match verbatim to prevent swallowing longer specific tasks.
inventory_notes: Not applied on the user-facing lifecycle_views.py path — only on the agent runtime and nightly extraction paths. The 14-day closed window is the key guard against the task-resurrection loop.

feature: USER.md envelope: goals, tasks, conversation digest, recent journal sections
user_story: As the system, I want to maintain four USER.md sections (active goals, open tasks, conversation digest, recent journal) so that the agent always has a compact current-state summary in its always-loaded context.
expected_behaviour: Four sections are registered via @register_section in envelope.py. render_goals_summary emits a one-line count + retrieval pointer (not inline content) to stay within the 12KB USER.md budget; falls back to legacy Document pointer for stale tenants. render_open_tasks_summary similarly emits count + due-this-week count + retrieval pointer. render_conversation_digest delegates to build_conversation_digest (router app). render_recent_journal renders up to 3 recent non-today daily note previews (250 chars each). Document saves trigger goals+tasks section refresh via refresh_on=(Document,); conversation digest is NOT refresh_on=() to avoid per-message storm.
inventory_notes: render_goals/render_open_tasks (not the _summary variants) are used for session-start injection and nbhd_journal_context tool — these return full content. The USER.md variants are summaries + pointers only.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

user_story: As the system, I want a plug-in registry for live-status providers so that new features can contribute to the journal status snapshot without modifying build_journal_status.
expected_behaviour: register_status_provider(key, enabled, provide) adds a StatusProvider to the module-level _PROVIDERS dict. status_providers() returns all registered providers. Replacement (same key) is allowed for idempotency. Built-in providers (tasks, goals, finance) register at module import time in status_projection.py; feature providers from other apps register in AppConfig.ready(). unregister_status_provider(key) is available for tests.
inventory_notes: The test suite asserts that all built-in domain keys are present and that a freshly registered provider appears in the snapshot with no change to build_journal_status.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to list and create named workspaces so that I can maintain separate conversation contexts for different domains of my life.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/workspaces/ returns all workspaces ordered by -is_default, -last_used_at, name, plus active_workspace_id and limit (4). POST /api/v1/workspaces/ validates name (required, <= 60 chars, not a reserved prefix), auto-creates a default workspace if this is the first, enforces 4-workspace limit (409 on overflow), generates a unique slug, creates the workspace with description_embedding (pgvector via OpenAI), sets it as the tenant's active workspace, returns 201. Requires IsAuthenticated.
inventory_notes: The 4-workspace limit is enforced at the application layer, not by a DB constraint. The default workspace is auto-created on the first explicit create call.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to rename or delete a workspace so that I can keep my workspace list clean.
expected_behaviour: PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/<slug>/ accepts {name?, description?}; re-embeds description on change; validates name length and reserved prefix. DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/<slug>/ rejects deleting the default workspace (409 cannot_delete_default); if the deleted workspace was active, falls back to the default workspace. Returns {deleted_id, fell_back_to_default}. Requires IsAuthenticated.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to switch my active workspace so that subsequent messages are routed to the correct OpenClaw conversation context.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/workspaces/switch/ requires {slug}. Finds the workspace scoped to the tenant (404 if not found), sets tenant.active_workspace, updates workspace.last_used_at. Returns {workspace, previous_workspace_id}. Requires IsAuthenticated.
inventory_notes: switch/ route must appear before <slug>/ in the URL configuration to avoid the slug pattern capturing 'switch' as a workspace slug.

user_story: As a subscriber using an external tool like YardTalk, I want to push a structured work session record so that my assistant has cross-tool work context.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/sessions/create/ requires HasSessionsWriteScope (PAT with sessions:write scope). Throttled: PATSessionIngestMinuteThrottle + PATSessionIngestDayThrottle. Validates session_end > session_start. Idempotency via Idempotency-Key header: if a Session with matching idempotency_key already exists for the tenant, returns 200 with existing record. On success creates a Session row and auto-creates a Document(kind='project') for the project name (slugified). Returns 201 with session data.
inventory_notes: The IntegrityError path on race conditions re-fetches and returns the existing session. test_mode=True sessions are excluded from list queries by default.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to list my pushed work sessions with optional filters so that I can review what my assistant knows about my work history.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/sessions/ requires HasSessionsReadScope. Filters: ?project_identity (exact), ?project (exact, only if no project_identity), ?since (session_start__gte), ?include_test=true (include test_mode rows, excluded by default). Limit capped at 100 (default 50); 400 on non-integer limit.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to retrieve or delete a specific work session so that I can inspect or remove individual records.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/sessions/<uuid>/ returns the Session scoped to the tenant or 404. DELETE removes it and returns 204. Both require HasSessionsReadScope.

feature: Seed missing journal templates management command
user_story: As an operator, I want to seed default note templates for all tenants and backfill DailyNote template FK fields so that all existing tenants have a consistent template setup.
expected_behaviour: python manage.py seed_missing_journal_templates [--tenant-id <uuid>] [--dry-run]. Without --dry-run, backfills DailyNote rows with null template_id to point to the default template. Dry-run prints planned actions. Outputs counts: tenants processed, templates created, notes linked.
inventory_notes: seed_default_templates_for_tenant will overwrite sections only if they still match the old 3-section default set (morning-report, log, evening-check-in). Custom templates are left intact.

feature: Migrate documents to typed models management command
user_story: As an operator, I want to migrate legacy Document(kind=goal|tasks) rows into the typed Goal and Task tables so that tenants can be upgraded to the typed lifecycle.
expected_behaviour: python manage.py migrate_documents_to_typed_models [--tenant <uuid>] [--dry-run]. For goal documents: deduplicates by title-key+pillar (keeps most-recently-updated, folds older markdown into description), maps intent_status to Goal.Status, extracts target_date from target JSON, skips already-migrated docs (Goal.migrated_from_document FK check). For tasks documents: parses markdown checkbox lines, extracts due dates from 'by/due YYYY-MM-DD', skips exact-title-from-same-document duplicates. Source Document rows are NOT deleted (only marked via FK). Idempotent.
