user_story: As a subscriber, I want to list all my workspaces so that I can see what conversation contexts exist and which one is active.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/workspaces/ requires JWT authentication (IsAuthenticated). Returns 200 with {tenant_id, workspaces: [...], active_workspace_id, limit:4}. Workspaces are ordered: default first, then by last_used_at desc, then by name. Each workspace object includes id, name, slug, description, is_default, is_active, created_at, last_used_at. Returns 404 if the user has no tenant.
inventory_notes: is_active is computed at serialization time by comparing workspace.id to tenant.active_workspace_id — not a stored field.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to create a new workspace so that I can route conversations into a separate context.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/workspaces/ requires JWT auth. Body: {name (required, max 60 chars), description (optional)}. Validates: name non-empty, ≤60 chars, not starting with reserved prefixes '_sync:' or '_fuel:' (400 reserved_prefix). Enforces WORKSPACE_LIMIT=4 total per tenant (409 workspace_limit_reached). On first workspace creation, auto-creates a 'General' default workspace first. Generates a unique slug via slugify+collision suffix. Sets tenant.active_workspace to the new workspace. Also calls embed_workspace_description() to generate a pgvector embedding of the description (failure is swallowed, workspace is still created). Returns 201 with {tenant_id, workspace, default_workspace_created}.
inventory_notes: The WORKSPACE_LIMIT=4 check counts after the default-workspace auto-creation, so a tenant creating their first workspace triggers two DB inserts. Embedding failures are silently swallowed — workspaces without embeddings are skipped during routing classification but still usable.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to rename or redescribe a workspace so that I can adjust how it is labelled and how the assistant routes messages to it.
expected_behaviour: PATCH /api/v1/workspaces/<slug>/ requires JWT auth. Body may include {name, description} (both optional). Name validations: non-empty, ≤60 chars, no reserved prefixes. Description update re-generates the pgvector embedding. Only provided fields are updated (partial update). Returns 200 with {tenant_id, workspace, updated: [<field_names>]}. Returns 404 if slug does not belong to the tenant.
inventory_notes: slug itself is never changed on PATCH — only name and description. Embedding regeneration on description change is synchronous and inline.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to delete a non-default workspace so that I can clean up contexts I no longer need.
expected_behaviour: DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/<slug>/ requires JWT auth. Returns 404 if not found. Returns 409 cannot_delete_default if is_default=True. If the deleted workspace was the active workspace, sets tenant.active_workspace to the default workspace before deleting. Returns 200 with {tenant_id, deleted_id, fell_back_to_default}.
inventory_notes: The fallback-to-default on active-workspace deletion queries for is_default=True — if the default workspace somehow doesn't exist, active_workspace is set to None.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to switch to a different workspace so that subsequent messages are routed to that conversation context.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/workspaces/switch/ requires JWT auth. Body: {slug (required)}. Returns 400 if slug is missing, 404 if slug not found for tenant. Sets tenant.active_workspace and updates workspace.last_used_at to now(). Returns 200 with {tenant_id, workspace, previous_workspace_id}.
inventory_notes: NOTE in workspace_urls.py: switch/ URL must be declared before <slug>/ to prevent the slug pattern catching the literal string 'switch'.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to list the tenant's workspaces so that I can display or manage them on behalf of the user.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/workspaces/ uses internal-key auth (_internal_auth_or_401). Returns same shape as the subscriber console list endpoint. Returns 401 on bad key, 404 on unknown tenant. Identical business logic via shared workspace_services.py helpers.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to create a workspace on behalf of the user so that I can set up new conversation contexts mid-session.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/workspaces/ uses internal-key auth. Same validations as the subscriber console: name required, ≤60 chars, no reserved prefixes (_sync:, _fuel:), WORKSPACE_LIMIT=4. Auto-creates General default on first workspace. Sets tenant.active_workspace. Returns 201 or 409 on limit. The reserved-prefix guard (_sync:, _fuel:) exists specifically to prevent the agent from creating workspace-routing traps during cron runs (documented 2026-05-14 incident).
inventory_notes: Reserved prefixes RESERVED_WORKSPACE_NAME_PREFIXES = ('_sync:', '_fuel:') are the critical guard against the 2026-05-14 incident where an agent created '_sync:Heartbeat Check-in' workspace which trapped routing for 9 days.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to rename or redescribe a workspace on behalf of the user so that context labels stay accurate.
expected_behaviour: PATCH /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/workspaces/<slug>/ uses internal-key auth. Identical validation and behaviour to the subscriber PATCH endpoint. Returns 404 on unknown slug, 400 on validation failure, 200 on success.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to delete a workspace on behalf of the user so that stale contexts can be removed.
expected_behaviour: DELETE /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/workspaces/<slug>/ uses internal-key auth. Same logic as subscriber DELETE: 404 on unknown, 409 on default, active-workspace fallback to default before deletion. Returns 200 with {deleted_id, fell_back_to_default}.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to switch the active workspace so that subsequent user messages get routed to the correct conversation context.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/workspaces/switch/ uses internal-key auth. Body: {slug}. Returns 400 on missing slug, 404 on unknown slug. Sets tenant.active_workspace and updates workspace.last_used_at. Returns 200 with {workspace, previous_workspace_id}.
inventory_notes: Same URL ordering gotcha: switch/ must be declared before <slug>/ in the URL conf (enforced in apps/integrations/urls.py).

user_story: As the system, I want to auto-create a 'General' default workspace when a tenant creates their first workspace so that there is always a fallback routing context.
expected_behaviour: Called internally by both workspace create paths (subscriber and runtime) when Workspace.objects.filter(tenant=tenant).exists() is False before the POST. Creates a single workspace with name='General', slug='general', is_default=True, and a predefined catch-all description. Also generates and stores a pgvector embedding of the description. Returns the existing default if one already exists (idempotent). Never called directly via HTTP.
inventory_notes: DEFAULT_WORKSPACE_NAME='General', DEFAULT_WORKSPACE_SLUG='general'. The default workspace cannot be deleted via any endpoint (409 returned).

user_story: As a subscriber using an external app (e.g. YardTalk), I want to push a structured work session to NU so that my AI assistant can stay aware of my cross-tool work context.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/sessions/create/ requires PAT authentication with 'sessions:write' scope (HasSessionsWriteScope). Throttled at 60/minute and 5000/day per PAT (PATSessionIngestMinuteThrottle, PATSessionIngestDayThrottle). Body: {source (max 128), project (max 256), project_identity (max 512, optional), project_type (max 128, optional), session_start (datetime), session_end (datetime, must be after session_start), summary (text), accomplishments (list, optional), blockers (list, optional), next_steps (list, optional), references (dict, optional), test_mode (bool, optional), schema_version (int, optional)}. Idempotency: if HTTP header Idempotency-Key is provided and matches an existing session for the tenant, returns 200 with the existing session (race condition also handled via IntegrityError catch). On success, creates Session row and auto-creates a Document(kind='project') for the project name if none exists. Returns 201 with full session detail.
inventory_notes: JWT-authenticated requests (subscriber console) bypass the PAT scope check via HasPATScope.has_permission — pat=None branch returns True. The session model has a UniqueConstraint on (tenant, idempotency_key) where idempotency_key != '' to enforce database-level dedup.

user_story: As a subscriber or external app, I want to list my work sessions with optional filters so that I can review past context or feed them to the assistant.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/sessions/ requires PAT 'sessions:read' scope or JWT auth. Query params: project_identity (takes priority over project for filtering), project (name filter), since (ISO datetime, filters session_start__gte), include_test (bool, default false — excludes test_mode=True sessions), limit (int, default 50, max 100). Returns 400 on invalid limit. Returns list of session detail objects ordered by -session_start (model Meta default). Returns 404 if no tenant found.
inventory_notes: project_identity filter takes priority: if provided, project param is ignored. No pagination cursor — limit cap is enforced but no total count is returned.
error_detail: Confirmed. In SessionListView.get (apps/journal/session_views.py:162-164) the `since` query param is read with no parsing and passed straight to qs.filter(session_start__gte=since). session_start is a Django DateTimeField (session_models.py:51), so a malformed value like ?since=not-a-date makes Django's DateTimeField.to_python/get_prep_value raise django.core.exceptions.ValidationError at query-evaluation time (when SessionDetailSerializer(qs, many=True).data forces the queryset). No custom EXCEPTION_HANDLER is configured (config/settings/base.py REST_FRAMEWORK block), so DRF's default handler applies, and it only converts Http404/PermissionDenied/APIException subclasses, NOT django.core.exceptions.ValidationError. The exception therefore propagates as an HTTP 500. The sibling TaskListView already validates dates via _parse_iso_date and returns 400 (lifecycle_views.py:27-36, 208-213), and the SAME view validates `limit` with a 400 (session_views.py:170-176), so `since` is an inconsistent omission. User impact: an authenticated API client passing a typo'd or wrong-format `since` gets an opaque 500 instead of an actionable 400, plus a noisy server error. Low severity: requires malformed input from an authenticated caller, no data-integrity or security impact, only a wrong status code and degraded error UX.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to retrieve a specific work session by ID so that I can inspect its details.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/sessions/<uuid:session_id>/ requires PAT 'sessions:read' scope or JWT auth. Returns 200 with full session detail including all content fields and created_at. Returns 404 if session_id does not exist for the authenticated tenant.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to delete a work session so that I can purge test data or incorrect entries.
expected_behaviour: DELETE /api/v1/sessions/<uuid:session_id>/ requires PAT 'sessions:read' scope or JWT auth (note: only read scope is required — the DetailView uses HasSessionsReadScope). Returns 404 if not found. Logs 'session_deleted' on success. Returns 204 No Content.
inventory_notes: Gotcha: DELETE uses HasSessionsReadScope, not HasSessionsWriteScope — a read-scoped PAT can delete sessions. This is likely a bug: the detail view sets permission_classes = [HasSessionsReadScope] and handles both GET and DELETE.
error_detail: Confirmed real. SessionDetailView (apps/journal/session_views.py:185) sets permission_classes = [HasSessionsReadScope] at the class level, so it applies to BOTH its get and delete handlers. The delete handler (lines 195-204) permanently destroys a session via session.delete(). Because the guard only requires the sessions:read scope, a PAT issued with scopes ["sessions:read"] only can permanently delete sessions despite being a token the user provisioned as read-only. The PAT create endpoint explicitly allows an arbitrary subset of scopes (apps/tenants/pat_views.py:18-25 validates only against ALLOWED_PAT_SCOPES = {sessions:write, sessions:read}), so a read-only-only PAT is genuinely user-provisionable. The codebase itself treats the two scopes as distinct write/read tiers: the write path SessionCreateView correctly uses HasSessionsWriteScope (line 81), confirming the inconsistency is a bug, not intended behaviour. Impact is bounded: the delete is filtered by tenant=tenant (line 200), so a token can only delete its own tenant's sessions (no cross-tenant exposure), and JWT console requests bypass scope checks entirely -- read-only PATs are the only affected principal. Hence medium, not high. A user who shares or stores a deliberately read-only PAT unknowingly grants it destructive delete capability over their own session data.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to fetch sessions that have not yet been distilled into journal/tasks/goals so that I can process them during heartbeat.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/sessions/pending/ uses internal-key auth. Query param: limit (int, default 10, max 25). Returns sessions where processed_at IS NULL and test_mode=False, ordered by -session_start. Returns 400 on invalid limit. Returns {tenant_id, count, sessions: [...]} with 200.
inventory_notes: Excludes test_mode sessions unconditionally. The agent is expected to call mark-processed after distillation to prevent re-processing.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to mark a session as distilled so that it doesn't appear in the pending queue again.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/sessions/<session_id>/mark-processed/ uses internal-key auth. Body: {processed_summary: dict (optional)}. Idempotent: if session.processed_at is already set, returns 200 with {already_processed: true} and the existing processed_at/processed_summary without overwriting. Validates processed_summary must be a dict (400 if not). On first call, sets processed_at=now() and processed_summary=body dict. Returns 200 with {session_id, processed_at, processed_summary, already_processed}. Returns 404 if session not found.
inventory_notes: The processed_summary is a free-form dict — the agent can record what task ids, journal doc ids, or skip reasons it generated.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to search across all journal documents by keywords so that I can retrieve relevant past context for the user.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/journal/search/ uses internal-key auth. Query params: q (required, websearch-mode), kind (optional, filters Document.kind), limit (int, default 20, max 50). Returns 400 if q is empty. Uses Postgres full-text search with SearchVector('title', weight='A') + SearchVector('markdown', weight='B') and SearchQuery(search_type='websearch'). Returns documents ranked by SearchRank, filtered to rank > 0.0. Each result includes kind, slug, title, snippet (300-char extract around first match term), updated_at, rank. Returns {query, count, results} with 200.
inventory_notes: Search is websearch-mode (supports quoted phrases, - negation). The snippet extraction is a simple Python substring — not a Postgres ts_headline — which may miss multi-term matches. OpenClaw's built-in memory_search was disabled fleet-wide; this is the canonical search path.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to run structured queries against journal entries, tasks, and goals with filters, windows, aggregates and field projection so that I can answer complex questions about the user's data.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/journal/runtime/<tenant_id>/query/ uses internal-key auth via BaseQueryView. Request body (schema_version=1): {resource: 'entries'|'tasks'|'goals', window (optional), window_field (optional, resource-specific defaults), filter (dict), fields (list, optional), aggregate ('sum'|'count'|'avg'|'min'|'max', optional), aggregate_field (optional), group_by (optional), order_by (optional), limit (1-500, default 50)}. Strict extra='forbid' Pydantic schema — unknown keys return 400. Filter keys are whitelisted per resource (entries: mood/energy; tasks: status/pillar/parent_goal_id/has_due_date/overdue; goals: status/pillar/parent_goal_id/has_target_date). The 'overdue' filter resolves against tenant timezone. group_by requires aggregate. aggregate_field required for non-count aggregations. Returns {data, meta} envelope. Window resolution and hash-based caching handled by BaseQueryView base class.
inventory_notes: Only count aggregation is currently meaningful — all NUMERIC_AGGREGATE_FIELDS sets are empty, so sum/avg/min/max on any journal resource will raise 400 invalid_aggregate_field. The overdue filter uses tenant_tz() for accurate local date comparison.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to create a personal access token so that I can authorize an external app (e.g. YardTalk) to push session data to my NU account.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/auth/tokens/create/ requires JWT authentication. Throttled at 10/hour per user (UserPATMintHourThrottle). Body: {name (max 255, required), scopes (list, optional, defaults to ['sessions:write','sessions:read']), expires_in_days (int, optional, 1-365)}. Validates scopes against ALLOWED_PAT_SCOPES = {'sessions:write', 'sessions:read'} — unknown scopes return 400. Generates a raw token (pat_<43-char base64url>), stores SHA-256 hash only (raw token never stored). Returns 201 with {id, name, token (raw, shown once), token_prefix, scopes, expires_at, created_at, warning}.
inventory_notes: Raw token is returned exactly once at creation — not stored. Token hash is SHA-256. Default scopes if none provided: ['sessions:write', 'sessions:read']. Token prefix (first 8 chars) is stored for display.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to list my active PATs so that I can see which apps have access and identify tokens to revoke.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/auth/tokens/ requires JWT authentication. Returns all non-revoked PATs for the user (revoked_at__isnull=True). Fields: id, name, token_prefix, scopes, last_used_at, expires_at, revoked_at, created_at. Ordered by -created_at (model Meta). Raw token is never returned here.
inventory_notes: Only non-revoked tokens are listed. Expired (expires_at <= now) tokens ARE included in the list — the is_valid property is not applied as a filter here.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to revoke a PAT so that an external app can no longer push data to my account.
expected_behaviour: DELETE /api/v1/auth/tokens/<token_id>/ requires JWT authentication. Looks up PAT by id AND user (ownership check). Returns 404 if not found. Returns 400 if already revoked. Sets revoked_at=now() and saves. Returns 204 No Content.
inventory_notes: Revocation is soft-delete (revoked_at timestamp). Authentication backend (apps/tenants/authentication.py) checks pat.is_valid which checks revoked_at is None before allowing subsequent requests with this PAT.

user_story: As the system, I want to generate a pgvector embedding of workspace descriptions so that incoming messages can be routed to the most semantically relevant workspace.
expected_behaviour: Called synchronously on workspace create and PATCH (description field). Calls apps/lessons/services.py:generate_embedding(description) to produce a 1536-dimension vector, stored in Workspace.description_embedding (VectorField). Empty/blank descriptions store None (NULL). Exceptions are caught and logged — workspace creation never fails due to embedding errors. Workspaces with NULL embeddings are skipped in routing classification but remain usable as fallback.
inventory_notes: The embedding is used downstream by the router's workspace classification logic. It is not exposed directly as an API endpoint.

feature: Auto-create Project Document on Session Ingest
user_story: As the system, I want to auto-create a project Document when a new session is pushed so that the project is reflected in the journal document tree.
expected_behaviour: Called as a side-effect within SessionCreateView.post after a successful Session.objects.create(). Uses Document.objects.get_or_create(tenant, kind='project', slug=slugify(project_name)[:128], defaults={'title': project_name}). If project name slugifies to an empty string, the call is skipped silently. Idempotent — second push for the same project is a no-op.
inventory_notes: Only creates the document stub (title + slug); does not populate markdown content. slug is limited to 128 chars after slugify.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container, I want to track which sessions have been distilled into journal primitives so that the heartbeat cron can find sessions still needing processing.
expected_behaviour: The Session model carries processed_at (DateTimeField, nullable, db_indexed) and processed_summary (JSONField). NULL processed_at means pending distillation. The runtime pending endpoint queries processed_at__isnull=True. The mark-processed endpoint sets both fields atomically. processed_summary is a free-form dict recording what the assistant created (task ids, journal doc ids, skip reason, etc.).
inventory_notes: No automatic expiry or TTL on unprocessed sessions. test_mode=True sessions are excluded from the pending queue.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to manually create a lesson so that I can add insights I've captured outside the assistant flow
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/lessons/ — IsAuthenticated required. Accepts text (required), context, source_type (one of conversation/journal/reflection/article/experience), source_ref, tags. Creates Lesson with status='pending', tenant scoped to request.user.tenant. Returns 201 with LessonSerializer data (excludes embedding vector). Does NOT auto-approve or trigger embedding on direct creation via this path — embedding is deferred to the approve action.
inventory_notes: Default status is 'pending'; the assistant runtime path (RuntimeLessonCreateView) uses status='approved' directly. No dedup check on manual create.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to list my approved lessons so that I can browse my learning constellation
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/lessons/ — IsAuthenticated required. Returns all tenant lessons with status='approved' by default; accepts ?status= query param to filter (e.g. ?status=pending). Ordered by -created_at. Pagination is disabled (pagination_class=None). Returns 200 with LessonSerializer array excluding the embedding vector field.
inventory_notes: The ?status filter is only applied on the list action; the retrieve/update/delete actions use unfiltered tenant queryset.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to approve a pending lesson so that it joins my constellation and gets embedded
expected_behaviour: POST or PATCH /api/v1/lessons/{pk}/approve/ — IsAuthenticated required. Sets status='approved' and approved_at=now(). Triggers process_approved_lesson (OpenAI text-embedding-3-small, then create bidirectional LessonConnection edges at threshold=0.75). If >=5 approved lessons exist, triggers refresh_constellation (cluster+label+position). Errors in embedding/clustering are silently swallowed. Returns 200 with full LessonSerializer. Rejects if status field in body is not 'approved' (400 with redirect hint).
inventory_notes: Embedding + clustering failures are swallowed with `except Exception: pass` — lesson remains approved even if embedding fails, but won't appear in semantic search.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to dismiss a pending lesson so that I can remove irrelevant suggestions from my queue
expected_behaviour: POST or PATCH /api/v1/lessons/{pk}/dismiss/ — IsAuthenticated required. Sets status='dismissed', clears approved_at. Returns 200 with LessonSerializer. Rejects if status field in body is not 'dismissed' (400 with redirect hint). No embedding or clustering triggered.
inventory_notes: Dismissed lessons are not deleted; they remain queryable via ?status=dismissed.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see my pending lessons so that I can review and approve or dismiss them
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/lessons/pending/ — IsAuthenticated required. Returns all tenant lessons with status='pending'. No pagination. Returns 200 with LessonSerializer array. Same result as GET /api/v1/lessons/?status=pending but via explicit action endpoint.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to manually refresh my constellation so that clusters and positions update after bulk changes
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/lessons/refresh/ — IsAuthenticated, tenant required. Runs refresh_constellation: (1) agglomerative clustering (average-linkage, threshold=0.84, max_size=8, coherence_min=0.72) on embedding-bearing approved lessons, (2) TF-IDF cluster label generation with domain-weight multiplier, (3) PCA-based 2D position computation. Requires >=5 approved lessons with embeddings for clustering to fire; fewer returns early with zero-cluster stats. Returns 200 with {total, clustered, clusters, noise, clusters_labeled, positions_computed}.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres, numpy (scipy-style SVD via numpy.linalg.svd), pgvector
inventory_notes: Clustering is a full O(n^2) pass in Python — expensive for large lesson sets. The DEFAULT_CLUSTER_MIN_LESSONS=5 guard prevents running on sparse tenants.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to view my constellation graph so that I can see how my lessons are connected and clustered
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/lessons/constellation/ — IsAuthenticated required. Returns all approved lessons as nodes (ConstellationNodeSerializer: id, text, context, tags, cluster_id, cluster_label, source_type, source_ref, position_x/y, created_at), stored LessonConnection edges (ConstellationEdgeSerializer), on-the-fly affinity_edges computed from pairwise cosine similarity (min_sim=0.1 for <=5 lessons, 0.3 otherwise, max 150 embedding-bearing lessons), and cluster summaries with top 3 tags. Returns 200.
inventory_notes: Affinity edge computation skips pairs already in stored edges. The O(n^2) embedding pass is capped at 150 lessons.

user_story: As a subscriber using the iOS constellation game, I want to load my full galaxy state so that the game can render all stars, connections, and clusters
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/lessons/galaxy/ — IsAuthenticated required. Returns {stars: GalaxyStarSerializer[], edges: [...stored GalaxyEdgeSerializer + affinity_edges dict...], clusters: [...]}. GalaxyStarSerializer includes game state fields: star_stage, last_tutored_at, last_visited_at, galaxy_note, journal_count, connection_count, position_x/y, context, source_ref. Affinity edges computed same way as /constellation/. Returns 200.
inventory_notes: Edges list mixes serialized dicts (stored) and raw dicts (affinity) — same structure but different provenance. connection_type='affinity' for computed edges.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want a quick galaxy summary so that my game HUD shows current star counts and recent activity
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/lessons/galaxy/summary/ — IsAuthenticated required. Returns {total_stars, proto_count, ignited_count, radiant_count, supernova_count, cluster_count, recent_activity (last 5 stars by last_visited_at with id, text[:80], visited ISO)}. All counts aggregate approved lessons only. Returns 200.
error_detail: Confirmed. galaxy_summary (apps/lessons/views.py:282) reads `tenant = self.request.user.tenant` with no guard, whereas its three sibling detail=False actions all guard with `if not hasattr(request.user, "tenant"): return 400 {"error": "tenant_required"}` — refresh (122-123), galaxy_insights (325-326), galaxy_reflect (357-358) — and the ViewSet's own get_queryset guards the same way (line 50). Tenant.user is a OneToOneField with related_name="tenant" (apps/tenants/models.py:142), so for an IsAuthenticated user with no associated Tenant, accessing .tenant raises RelatedObjectDoesNotExist (subclass of Django ObjectDoesNotExist). There is no custom DRF EXCEPTION_HANDLER, and DRF's default handler does not catch ObjectDoesNotExist, so it propagates to a 500 with traceback instead of the intended controlled 400. User impact: an authenticated-but-tenantless account (staff/superuser via createsuperuser, or any user created outside the atomic tenant-provisioning path) calling GET /lessons/galaxy/summary/ gets an unhandled 500 instead of the clean 400 every sibling endpoint returns. Severity is low because the galaxy/game surface targets subscriber tenants, who are always created atomically with a Tenant (apps/tenants/services.py:35-48); only staff/superusers or rare tenantless accounts can trigger it, and there is no data exposure or corruption — purely an inconsistent error contract (500 vs 400).

feature: Galaxy insights — tutoring signals read surface
user_story: As a subscriber (or the OpenClaw assistant via a future tool), I want to read recent tutoring session signals so that the assistant can reference how I engaged with each star
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/lessons/galaxy/insights/?limit=10 — IsAuthenticated, tenant required. Returns recent TutoringSession records (capped by ?limit, default 10, must be >0). Each entry includes star_id, star_text[:120], phases_completed, player_restated_accurately, player_found_edge_cases, connections_made count, topic_shifted, mastery_achieved, new_star_stage, created_at. 400 on invalid limit. Returns 200.
inventory_notes: Designed as the backing surface for a future nbhd_tutoring_insights OpenClaw tool — currently callable by the subscriber dashboard.

user_story: As a subscriber playing the galaxy game, I want a contextual co-pilot line when I land on or linger near a star so that I get a warm, grounded reflection about where I am in my knowledge
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/lessons/galaxy/reflect/ — IsAuthenticated, tenant required. Body: {star_id, recent_star_ids[], nearby_star_ids[], ship: {x,y}, mode: 'land'|'ambient'}. Validates star exists as approved for tenant (404 otherwise). Checks cache (key: copilot:reflect:{tenant_id}:{mode}:{star_id}:{ship_cell_x}:{ship_cell_y}, TTL=180s). On miss: loads all approved stars, computes spatial evidence (5 idea-space neighbours by PCA distance, cluster state, similar stored edges, recent flight path, next-star suggestion), redacts all free text through PII RedactionSession (batched sentinel join), then calls OpenRouter (claude-haiku-4.5, max_tokens=120) unless rate-limited (30 calls/tenant/minute) or disabled. Always falls back to deterministic warm line. LLM responses cached with redacted line + _mints; rehydrated per-response via live entity map. Returns {line, point: {star_id, label, reason} | null, source: 'llm'|'fallback'}. Usage attributed as is_system=True (does not eat tenant quota). Returns 200.
dependencies: OpenRouter API (claude-haiku-4.5), Django cache (Redis in prod), apps/pii/redactor, Supabase/Postgres, numpy
inventory_notes: PII redact/rehydrate seam: lesson text, cluster labels, galaxy notes all go through RedactionSession. Residual [TYPE_N] placeholders scrubbed to 'someone' at egress. Cache hit still rehydrates against LIVE entity map to avoid serving stale names.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to land on a star so that it is marked as visited and I see its full detail
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/lessons/{pk}/land/ — IsAuthenticated required. Validates star is approved (400 otherwise). Sets last_visited_at=now(). Returns StarDetailSerializer: id, text, context, tags, cluster_id/label, source_type/ref, star_stage, position_x/y, galaxy_note, journal_entries (last 5, text[:200], entry_type, created_at), connection_count, tutoring_sessions_count, last_tutored_at, last_visited_at, created_at, approved_at. Returns 200. Does NOT trigger star growth — visit alone does not promote a star.
inventory_notes: Growth only fires on tutoring, notes, or connections — bare visits are free.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to start a tutoring session on a star so that I can deepen my understanding through a guided 5-phase dialogue
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/lessons/{pk}/tutor/start/ — IsAuthenticated required. Star must be approved (400 otherwise). Generates UUID session_id, builds new state dict (tenant-bound, JSON-serializable), loads star's journal entries and connection candidates, calls OpenRouter (claude-sonnet-4.6, temp=0.7, max_tokens=600, response_format=json_object) to generate the first restate-phase message. On LLM failure, falls back to a hardcoded restate prompt. State stored in Django cache (key=tutoring:{session_id}, TTL=2h). Returns {session_id, message, current_phase='restate', phase_index=0, total_phases=5}. Usage attributed is_system=True. Returns 200 or 500 on hard error.
dependencies: OpenRouter API (claude-sonnet-4.6), Django cache (Redis in prod), Supabase/Postgres, apps/billing/services.record_usage
inventory_notes: Session state is in cache, not DB — expires after 2h of inactivity. Connection candidates capped at 12 neighbors to prevent prompt bloat.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to send a message during tutoring so that the assistant guides me through the next phase
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/lessons/{pk}/tutor/message/ — IsAuthenticated required. Body: {message (1-5000 chars, required), action: 'continue'|'skip'|'end'}. If action='end', delegates to end_tutoring. If action='skip' or message matches skip phrases (skip/let's move on/next/move on), advances phase locally. Calls LLM with full message history. LLM response (JSON) captures honest signals: restated_accurately, found_edge_cases, connections_found (validated against real neighbor ids only — hallucinated PKs dropped), connection_text, topic_shifted. Advances phase on phase_complete=true. If mastery_achieved or last phase complete, fires end_tutoring and returns merged result under session_close. Returns {session_id, message, current_phase, phase_index, total_phases, phase_complete, mastery_achieved}. 404 on session_not_found.
dependencies: OpenRouter API (claude-sonnet-4.6), Django cache (Redis in prod), Supabase/Postgres
inventory_notes: connection_id validation is strict — only PKs from _build_connection_candidates (real DB neighbors) are accepted; others are silently dropped. Skipping past the last phase sets mastery_achieved=True and ends the session.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to explicitly end a tutoring session so that my progress is persisted and my star grows
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/lessons/{pk}/tutor/end/ — IsAuthenticated required. Body: {session_id (required)}. Loads state from cache (404 if missing). Increments star.tutoring_sessions_count, sets last_tutored_at and last_visited_at to now. Calls apply_star_growth (monotonic stage promotion: score = sessions*3 + notes*1.5 + connections; thresholds: ignited>=1 any, radiant>=3 sessions or >=3 notes or >=5 connections or score>=5, supernova>=8 sessions or >=8 notes or score>=14). Creates TutoringSession DB record with full messages transcript, phases_completed, mastery_achieved, honest signals, new_star_stage. Deletes cache key. Returns {session_id, tutoring_session_id, phases_completed, mastery_achieved, new_star_stage}. 404 on session or star not found.
inventory_notes: Star stage is monotonic — apply_star_growth only promotes, never demotes. Optional tenant_id param enables cross-replica session isolation check.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to retrieve the current tutoring session state so that I can recover a session after a page reload
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/lessons/{pk}/tutor/state/?session_id={uuid} — IsAuthenticated required. session_id query param required (400 if missing). Loads state from cache; returns 404 if expired or not found. Returns {session_id, star_id, star_text[:120], current_phase, phase_index, total_phases, phases_completed}. Does not modify state. Returns 200.
inventory_notes: Session state TTL is 2h from last save. No cache refresh on read.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to list reflections attached to a star so that I can review what I've written about that lesson
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/lessons/{pk}/journal/ — IsAuthenticated required. Returns all StarJournalEntry records for this star, ordered by -created_at (model default). Returns 200 with StarJournalEntrySerializer array: id, star, text, entry_type, tags, created_at.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to write a reflection on a star so that it deepens the star and grows its stage
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/lessons/{pk}/journal/create/ — IsAuthenticated required. Body: {text (required), entry_type ('tutoring'|'revisit'|'free', default='free'), tags (list)}. Creates StarJournalEntry for the star. Calls apply_star_growth on the star (monotonic promotion). Returns 201 with StarJournalEntrySerializer data plus {star_stage: <new_stage>}.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to pin a note to a star so that it appears in the galaxy panel and persists as context
expected_behaviour: POST or PATCH /api/v1/lessons/{pk}/pin-note/ — IsAuthenticated required. Body: {note (required, max 1000 chars)}. Sets star.galaxy_note and saves. Returns 200 with StarDetailSerializer. No embedding or growth triggered.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to manually connect two stars so that I can record a link I noticed between lessons
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/lessons/{pk}/connect/ — IsAuthenticated required. Body: {target_star_id (required), connection_type ('similar'|'builds_on'|'contradicts'|'user_linked', default='user_linked')}. Validates target exists as approved for same tenant (404 otherwise). Rejects self-connection (400). Creates bidirectional LessonConnection pair via get_or_create (similarity=1.0) — idempotent for existing pairs. Calls apply_star_growth on both source and target stars. Returns 200 with {source, target, connection_type, source_star_stage, target_star_stage}.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to list my lesson clusters so that I can see which topic groups have formed
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/lessons/clusters/ — IsAuthenticated required. Returns approved, clustered (cluster_id not null) lessons grouped by (cluster_id, cluster_label). Each entry: {cluster_id, cluster_label, count, top_tags (up to 3 most common)}. Returns 200.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to search my lessons by meaning so that I can find relevant stars for a topic I'm thinking about

Runs pgvector CosineDistance query over approved lessons with non-null embeddings, annotates with similarity=(1-distance), ordered by -similarity, capped at limit (default 10, must be >0). Returns 200 with [{...LessonSerializer, similarity: float}]. 400 on invalid limit.

Returns 'Added to your learning constellation!'.

user_story: As the OpenClaw assistant, I want to create a lesson captured during conversation so that it is immediately added to the subscriber's constellation
expected_behaviour: POST /integrations/runtime/{tenant_id}/lessons/ — internal-auth only (AllowAny + per-tenant key validation). Calls process_approved_lesson (embedding + connections). Re-clusters if >=5 approved. Returns 201 with {tenant_id, lesson: LessonSerializer}. 400 on missing text or invalid source_type/tags. 401 on auth failure. 404 on unknown tenant.

user_story: As the OpenClaw assistant, I want to search the subscriber's lessons by semantic similarity so that I can surface relevant past learnings during conversation
expected_behaviour: GET /integrations/runtime/{tenant_id}/lessons/search/?q={query}&limit=10 — internal-auth only. Requires non-empty q (400). Limit clamped 1-50. Generates query embedding, runs pgvector CosineDistance ranked search over approved, embedding-bearing lessons. Returns 200 with {tenant_id, query, count, results: [{...LessonSerializer, similarity: float}]}. 500 on embedding or DB failure.

feature: Runtime constellation notes — assistant pull tool (nbhd_constellation_notes)
user_story: As the OpenClaw assistant, I want to pull enriched constellation context so that I can reference the subscriber's recent star work, pinned notes, journal reflections, and tutoring signals
expected_behaviour: GET /integrations/runtime/{tenant_id}/constellation/notes/?q=&star_id=&limit=5&days=30 — internal-auth only. Three modes in precedence: ?star_id gives full context for one approved star; ?q runs semantic search (generate_embedding + pgvector); default returns recently active stars (tutored, visited, or journaled on within days window, OR has galaxy_note at any time). Each star context includes: id, text, stage label, galaxy_note, tags, cluster_label, tutoring counts, last_tutored_at/last_visited_at, last 2 (compact) or 5 (full) journal entries, last 2 (compact) or 5 (full) tutoring session honest-signal summaries. Returns 200 with {mode, count, stars, summary: {total_stars, by_stage}, tenant_id}. 400 on invalid params, 500 on failure.
dependencies: OpenAI Embeddings API (for ?q mode), Supabase/Postgres, pgvector, apps/integrations/internal_auth
inventory_notes: Compact mode (default, for session init context) limits to 2 journal entries/tutoring sessions and 180-char text cap. Full mode (star_id or q) uses 5 entries and 400-char cap.
error_detail: Confirmed N+1 query inefficiency in the q (semantic search) mode of constellation_notes_payload. search_lessons() (apps/lessons/services.py:106-119) returns a Lesson queryset with NO prefetch_related, whereas the recent_active_stars() path (agent_context.py:88-99) explicitly prefetches journal_entries and tutoring_sessions. The search path (agent_context.py:207) passes its unprefetched stars into build_star_context(star, full=True), which lazily evaluates star.journal_entries.all() and star.tutoring_sessions.all() (agent_context.py:134-135) -- 2 extra queries per star. With limit capped at 25 (runtime_views.py:1809) that is up to ~50 additional simple FK queries per nbhd_constellation_notes pull-tool call; at the default limit of 5 it is ~10 extra. Impact is real but bounded and non-functional: this is an internal assistant pull-tool endpoint (RuntimeConstellationNotesView), not a hot user-facing path; the queries are indexed FK lookups, and overall latency in search mode is already dominated by generate_embedding(query). No correctness or UX impact -- only marginally slower tool responses, and only in search mode. The QA verdict (concern / logic / low) is accurate.

user_story: As the OpenClaw assistant, I want constellation activity injected into session initialization so that I start each session aware of what the subscriber has been working on in their galaxy
expected_behaviour: GET /integrations/runtime/{tenant_id}/journal/context/ includes constellation_data when build_constellation_context returns non-empty. Returns {active_stars: [...build_star_context compact...], summary: {total_stars, by_stage}} for stars active within 30 days (tutored, visited, journaled) or having galaxy_note. Stars ordered by most-recent engagement (last_tutored_at, last_visited_at, newest journal entry, approved_at). Omits key entirely when no active stars. The constellation section is part of the larger journal context response.
inventory_notes: This injects constellation context proactively on every session start, capped at 5 stars. Uses compact star context (2 journal entries, 2 tutoring sessions, 180-char cap) to keep the session-init payload lean.

user_story: As the system, I want to include recent lessons in the assistant's USER.md so that the assistant always has the subscriber's latest learning context
expected_behaviour: render_recent_lessons(tenant, limit=3) renders the 3 most recent approved lessons as bullet points. Each bullet: first line of lesson.text (truncated at 140 chars with '...'). Returns empty string (section omitted from envelope) if no approved lessons. Registered as order=60 section, refresh_on=Lesson model writes. Used by the orchestrator envelope system to build USER.md.
inventory_notes: Section heading is '## Recent lessons'. Refresh triggered by any Lesson write (approval, creation, dismissal).

user_story: As the system, I want to include active constellation context in USER.md so that the assistant can reference the subscriber's pinned notes, reflections, and tutoring signals on every turn
expected_behaviour: render_constellation_activity(tenant) calls render_constellation_envelope(tenant, limit=3): finds up to 3 recently active approved stars (last 30 days tutored/visited/journaled or any galaxy_note), renders each as: '- **{text[:120]}** _({stage})_' with optional indented galaxy_note, latest journal reflection, and tutoring insight one-liner. Returns empty string if no active stars. Registered as order=62, refresh_on=(Lesson, StarJournalEntry).
inventory_notes: TutoringSession model has no tenant FK so it can't drive refresh directly; Lesson writes (from end_tutoring saving the star) cover the tutoring signal refresh path.

user_story: As an operator, I want to backfill embeddings for approved lessons that are missing them so that all lessons are searchable and clusterable
expected_behaviour: python manage.py backfill_lesson_embeddings — No args. Queries all approved lessons with embedding__isnull=True, calls process_approved_lesson on each (generates OpenAI embedding + LessonConnections). On success, re-clusters all tenants that have backfilled lessons if they have >=5 approved lessons. Prints per-lesson success/failure and final count. Errors per-lesson are caught and logged without stopping the run.
dependencies: OpenAI Embeddings API, Supabase/Postgres, pgvector, numpy
inventory_notes: Re-clusters ALL tenants with at least one backfilled lesson, not just those that were affected — conservatively safe but potentially redundant.

user_story: As an operator, I want to remove near-duplicate lessons across all active tenants so that the constellation stays clean and non-redundant
expected_behaviour: python manage.py dedup_lessons [--tenant UUID] [--dry-run] [--threshold FLOAT] — Processes ACTIVE tenants (or specific one). For each tenant: (1) deletes lessons tagged 'goal' or with context starting 'Goal' (these don't belong in constellation), (2) computes O(n^2) pairwise cosine similarity matrix from embeddings, (3) union-finds groups above threshold (default=0.65), (4) keeps longest lesson text in each group, deletes rest. On non-dry-run, triggers refresh_constellation after deletion. Dry-run prints groups without deleting.
inventory_notes: Goal-node pruning happens regardless of dry-run flag for the deletion, but is still gated by the dry_run check in code. Threshold default is 0.65 (help text says 0.75 — discrepancy in the code). dedup_lessons_task() is the QStash-callable wrapper.
error_detail: Confirmed: the --threshold argument's help text is wrong. DEDUP_THRESHOLD = 0.65 (line 20) is the actual default and is wired into the argument via default=DEDUP_THRESHOLD (line 30), but that same line's help string reads "Similarity threshold (default 0.75)". An operator who runs --help and trusts the text expects deduplication at 0.75 cosine similarity, but the command actually runs at 0.65 — a more aggressive setting that groups and deletes lessons at lower semantic similarity (deletion at line 121, keeping only the longest text per group). Impact is real but bounded: it only affects operators who run the command WITHOUT an explicit --threshold (an explicit value overrides the default cleanly), and the command ships a --dry-run flag (lines 28, 111-115) that previews every lesson it would remove with its exact similarity score, so a careful operator catches the discrepancy before data loss. The code is internally consistent at 0.65; this is a help-text/documentation bug, not a logic error, hence low severity.

user_story: As an operator, I want to re-extract all lessons from daily notes history so that the constellation reflects improved extraction quality
expected_behaviour: python manage.py reseed_lessons [--tenant UUID] [--dry-run] [--since YYYY-MM-DD] — Deletes ALL existing Lesson records and PendingExtraction(kind=LESSON) for each tenant (non-dry-run). Sweeps Document(kind=DAILY) then DailyNote records (avoiding duplicates by date), batches into 5500-char chunks, calls journal extraction LLM, extracts lessons, applies embedding-based dedup (_embedding_duplicate), creates approved lessons with process_approved_lesson. Rate-limits with 1s sleep between batches. Re-clusters on any additions. Dry-run shows extractions without saving.
dependencies: OpenAI Embeddings API, LiteLLM/OpenRouter (extraction LLM), Supabase/Postgres (Document, DailyNote, Lesson, PendingExtraction), numpy
inventory_notes: Deletes ALL lessons before re-extracting (not scoped to journal-sourced only). The reseed_lessons_task() QStash variant preserves lessons with source_ref='reseed' and fans out to per-tenant tasks via QStash publish.

feature: Lesson reseed QStash task — per-tenant fan-out
user_story: As the system, I want to fan out lesson reseed across all active tenants via QStash so that re-extraction runs in parallel without blocking
expected_behaviour: reseed_lessons_task() enqueues one QStash message per ACTIVE tenant via publish_task('reseed_lessons_single_tenant', tenant_id=...). reseed_lessons_single_tenant_task(tenant_id) then runs the per-tenant reseed: deletes non-reseed lessons and LESSON PendingExtractions, gathers notes, batches, calls extraction LLM, deduplicates via embedding, creates approved lessons (source_ref='reseed'), calls process_approved_lesson, and refresh_constellation if any added. Returns {tenant, notes, extracted, deduped, added}.
dependencies: QStash, OpenAI Embeddings API, LiteLLM/OpenRouter (extraction LLM), Supabase/Postgres
inventory_notes: Unlike the management command, the per-tenant task only deletes lessons where source_ref != 'reseed' — preserving a prior reseed's output during retry.

user_story: As the system, I want to run lesson deduplication on a schedule so that the constellation stays clean without manual operator intervention
expected_behaviour: dedup_lessons_task() calls the dedup_lessons management command via call_command() and returns {ok: True, output_bytes, output_lines}. Lesson text content is not echoed to logs or the return payload (security: avoids surfacing user content in QStash dashboards). Callable as a QStash endpoint.

feature: Lesson rewrite to actionable — management command
user_story: As an operator, I want to rewrite observation-style lessons into actionable advice so that the constellation has forward-looking guidance rather than backward-looking observations
expected_behaviour: python manage.py rewrite_lessons_actionable [--dry-run] [--tenant UUID] — Queries all approved lessons (or per-tenant). For each: calls OpenRouter (claude-sonnet-4.6, temp=0.2) with the rewrite prompt. If rewritten text differs: saves updated lesson.text, calls process_approved_lesson (regenerates embedding, creates new connections). Re-clusters all affected tenants. Dry-run prints before/after without saving. Skips lessons where LLM returns unchanged text.
dependencies: OpenRouter API (claude-sonnet-4.6), OpenAI Embeddings API, Supabase/Postgres
inventory_notes: One-time data migration tool. Regenerating embeddings after rewrite rebuilds connections from scratch.
error_detail: Confirmed real. After a lesson text is rewritten and saved, process_approved_lesson (services.py:99-103) regenerates the embedding and calls create_connections (services.py:79-96), which ONLY adds edges via LessonConnection.objects.get_or_create. No code path deletes the lesson pre-existing LessonConnection rows on a text/embedding change; deletions happen only via the model CASCADE on lesson deletion. The audit record inventory_notes even claim rewriting rebuilds connections from scratch, but the code strictly accretes, so edges that were only similar under the OLD embedding survive forever. Impact: the constellation/galaxy views (views.py:143-206, 210-277) return every stored LessonConnection as a graph edge AND suppress the recomputed affinity edge for any pair that already has a stored edge (strong_pairs/stored set at views.py:159-162, 237-240). A stale stored edge therefore draws a spurious link and hides the correct affinity edge for that pair. Secondary: get_or_create with similarity only in defaults never updates similarity on still-valid edges, so weights also drift stale. Severity low: only reachable via the operator-run one-time rewrite_lessons_actionable command (plus reseed/backfill tools); no user-facing lesson-text-edit endpoint re-runs process_approved_lesson, so it is not hit in normal product use; impact is cosmetic graph pollution, not data loss; star-stage promotion (growth.py:33 counts connections_out) is promote-only so stale counts cannot cause a visible demotion.

user_story: As an operator, I want to seed sample lessons with pre-built clusters and connections so that I can test the constellation UI without real user data
expected_behaviour: python manage.py seed_sample_lessons --tenant UUID [--clear] — Creates 25 lessons across 6 clusters (Creative Discipline, Deep Relationships, Body & Energy, Leadership & Teams, Money & Time, Self-Knowledge) with hardcoded positions and tags. Creates 28 LessonConnection edges (within-cluster and cross-cluster bridges). All created as status='approved'. No embeddings generated (no OpenAI call). --clear deletes existing lessons first.
inventory_notes: No embedding vectors — lessons won't appear in semantic search or pgvector clustering. Positions are random within [-0.8, 0.8] with seed 42.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

user_story: As an operator, I want to browse and manage lessons, connections, tutoring sessions, and star journal entries in the Django admin so that I can diagnose and fix data issues
expected_behaviour: Django admin registers LessonAdmin (list_display: id, tenant, status, source_type, cluster_id, cluster_label, shared, created_at; filters: tenant, status, source_type, shared; search: text, context, source_ref, tags), LessonConnectionAdmin (list_display: from_lesson, to_lesson, connection_type, similarity; filter: connection_type), TutoringSessionAdmin (list_display: star, mastery_achieved, new_star_stage, skipped; filter: mastery_achieved/stage/skipped; id+created_at readonly), StarJournalEntryAdmin (list_display: tenant, star, entry_type; filter: entry_type, tenant; id+created_at readonly).

user_story: As the system, I want to monotonically promote a star's stage as the subscriber engages with it so that the constellation visually reflects depth of engagement
expected_behaviour: apply_star_growth(star) computes stage from persisted counts: score = tutoring_sessions_count*3 + journal_entries.count()*1.5 + connections_out.count(). Stage ladder: proto (score=0, no engagement), ignited (>=1 of any), radiant (sessions>=3 or notes>=3 or connections>=5 or score>=5), supernova (sessions>=8 or notes>=8 or score>=14). Only promotes (STAGE_RANK check). Saves star_stage field if promoted. Called by: end_tutoring, star_journal_create, star connect action.
inventory_notes: DB queries on journal_entries and connections_out are issued inside compute_star_stage — may be N+1 if called in a loop. Monotonic: a star can never regress.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

feature: Agglomerative clustering + label generation + position computation
user_story: As the system, I want to cluster, label, and position lessons in 2D so that the constellation game can render meaningful spatial groupings
expected_behaviour: refresh_constellation(tenant) orchestrates three steps: (1) cluster_lessons: requires >=5 embedding-bearing approved lessons; computes O(n^2) cosine similarity, runs average-linkage agglomerative clustering (threshold=0.84, max_cluster=8), ejects outliers (coherence_min=0.72), bulk-updates cluster_id. (2) generate_cluster_labels: TF-IDF over tags + text tokens, domain tags weighted 2x over behavioral tags, ambiguity fallback. (3) compute_positions: PCA SVD projection to 2D, cluster centroids on orbit circle (radius=0.62), per-cluster territory scaling, noise lessons near center; clips to [-1.0, 1.0]. All updates in atomic transactions. Returns {total, clustered, clusters, noise, clusters_labeled, positions_computed}.
inventory_notes: Called after every approval, Telegram callback approval, runtime lesson creation, dedup run, and backfill. O(n^2) in Python — performance concern at large lesson counts.

feature: Embedding generation and similarity-based connection creation
user_story: As the system, I want to generate an embedding for a newly approved lesson and link it to similar lessons so that semantic search and the constellation graph work correctly
expected_behaviour: process_approved_lesson(lesson): calls generate_embedding(lesson.text) via POST https://api.openai.com/v1/embeddings with model text-embedding-3-small (1536 dimensions, timeout=10s). Saves embedding. Then create_connections: find_similar_lessons uses pgvector CosineDistance against same-tenant approved lessons (threshold=0.75, limit=5, stops on first result below threshold). Creates bidirectional LessonConnection via get_or_create (connection_type='similar'). Raises ValueError on missing OPENAI_API_KEY or empty API response.
inventory_notes: Threshold is 0.75 cosine similarity for auto-connections (vs 0.65 for dedup). API call uses requests with 10s timeout — synchronous, called inline in the approval flow.

user_story: As a system, I want to provision a full OpenClaw container environment for a new subscriber so that they can start using the AI assistant immediately after billing confirmation.
expected_behaviour: Triggered by `provision_tenant(tenant_id)` (called from `provision_tenant_task` via QStash). Requires tenant in PENDING or PROVISIONING status, otherwise logs and returns. Executes an ordered pipeline: (1) generates openclaw.json via `generate_openclaw_config`, runs `audit_config_security` — warnings log to PlatformIssueLog, errors raise and reset tenant to PENDING; (2) creates Azure Managed Identity (`mi-nbhd-<uuid>`); (3) generates a 48-byte `secrets.token_urlsafe` internal API key, writes it to Key Vault as `tenant-<uuid>-internal-key`, stores hash on Tenant; (4) optionally creates a per-tenant OpenRouter sub-key via `create_sub_key` if `OPENROUTER_PER_TENANT_KEYS_ENABLED` — failure here is non-fatal (falls back to shared key); (5) assigns KV + ACR pull roles to the managed identity; (6) creates Azure File Share `ws-<uuid[:20]>` and registers environment storage; (7) uploads openclaw.json to file share; (8) renders persona workspace files via `render_workspace_files`; (9) creates Container App `oc-<uuid[:20]>` via Azure ARM; (10) updates Tenant row (container_id, container_fqdn, managed_identity_id, container_image_tag, status=ACTIVE, provisioned_at). On any exception in steps 1-10 tenant is reset to PENDING and the exception re-raises. Post-provision (non-critical, do not reset status): sends Telegram welcome if chat_id present; sends Day-0 welcome email; seeds USER.md via `push_user_md(force=True)`; enqueues `seed_cron_jobs` via QStash with 60s delay.
dependencies: Azure Container Apps ARM API, Azure Key Vault, Azure Storage (File Share), Azure Container Registry (nbhdunited.azurecr.io), OpenRouter Admin API (optional), Supabase/Postgres, QStash
inventory_notes: Secret backend controlled by `OPENCLAW_CONTAINER_SECRET_BACKEND` env var (default 'keyvault'). Container name is `oc-<uuid[:20]>` — identity name is `mi-nbhd-<uuid[:20]>` (different prefix). Mock mode when `AZURE_MOCK=true` — skips all Azure calls. OpenRouter sub-key failure must not block provisioning.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

user_story: As a system, I want to fully deprovision a cancelled subscriber's container and cloud resources so that Azure billing stops and no orphaned resources remain.
expected_behaviour: Triggered by `deprovision_tenant(tenant_id)` (called from `deprovision_tenant_task` via QStash or management command `deprovision_tenant`). Sets tenant status to DEPROVISIONING immediately. Pipeline: (1) deletes OpenRouter sub-key if `openrouter_key_hash` set — failure logs and continues; (2) deletes OpenRouter KV secret if `openrouter_key_secret_name` set — failure logs and continues; (3) deletes Container App via `delete_container_app(container_id)` if container_id non-empty; (4) deletes File Share via `delete_tenant_file_share`; (5) deletes Managed Identity via `delete_managed_identity`. On success sets status=DELETED and clears container_id, container_fqdn, managed_identity_id, openrouter_key_secret_name, openrouter_key_hash. On any exception in the delete pipeline, sets status=SUSPENDED and re-raises.
dependencies: Azure Container Apps ARM API, Azure Key Vault, Azure Storage (File Share), OpenRouter Admin API (optional), Supabase/Postgres
inventory_notes: On partial Azure failures the tenant lands in SUSPENDED (not DELETED). Orphaned OpenRouter sub-keys that fail deletion here are expected to be swept by a separate `sweep_orphan_openrouter_keys` command.

user_story: As a system, I want to regenerate a tenant's openclaw.json and workspace files and push them to the Azure File Share so that the running container picks up the latest model/persona/tool configuration on its next restart.
expected_behaviour: Called by `update_tenant_config(tenant_id)`. Guards: tenant must be status=ACTIVE with a non-empty container_id, otherwise logs warning and returns. Steps: (1) `generate_openclaw_config(tenant)` → `config_to_json` → `_audit_and_log` (security check); (2) `upload_config_to_file_share` (file share is source of truth); (3) renders and uploads workspace files — AGENTS.md, tools-reference.md, channel-formatting.md, cron-management.md, error-handling.md, privacy-redaction.md, and platform-guide.md (full or silent based on `feature_tips_enabled`) are always overwritten; SOUL.md and IDENTITY.md are seeded once (`skip_if_exists=True`); workspace/rules/*.md rule files are always overwritten; (4) calls `push_user_md(tenant, force=True)` to refresh USER.md; (5) calls `refresh_system_cron_rows_from_seed` to sync Postgres CronJob rows from the current seed (signal handler enqueues `regenerate_tenant_crons` on row changes). Failures in steps 3-5 are non-fatal — logged and swallowed. The file write at step 2 IS the effective apply; containers pick up the config on next cold start.
dependencies: Azure Storage (File Share), Supabase/Postgres, OpenClaw container (gateway — only for post-write signal, not required for file write)
inventory_notes: No live hot-reload — `gateway.reload` was removed in OpenClaw 2026.5.7 (issue #541). Config-only changes take effect at next container warmup or image bump. The `force_apply_configs` command additionally advances config_version to pending_config_version after writing.

user_story: As a system, I want to apply any pending config version bump for a specific tenant so that model or setting changes reach the container on its next restart.
expected_behaviour: Invoked via QStash task `apply_single_tenant_config`. Guards: if tenant not found, returns. If `config_version >= pending_config_version`, returns (already applied — idempotent). Calls `update_tenant_config(tenant_id)` — on exception logs and returns without advancing version. On success, advances `config_version = pending_config_version`, stamps `config_refreshed_at` and `applied_model / applied_model_at`. If tenant is hibernated, logs that the wake will pick up the new config. The `_is_followup_retry` argument is accepted for backward compatibility but no longer drives retry behavior.
inventory_notes: Gated on `pending_config_version > config_version` so QStash duplicate deliveries are harmless. The file share write happens inside `update_tenant_config`; this task only gates the version advancement.

user_story: As a system, I want to update a tenant's container to a new OpenClaw image while preserving their cron state so that new features ship without losing scheduled jobs.
expected_behaviour: Three-phase: (1) Snapshot current cron jobs via `cron.list` gateway call → saves to `Tenant.cron_jobs_snapshot` (Postgres); failure here is non-fatal and falls back to seed at restore time. (2) Calls `update_container_image(tenant.container_id, image)` via Azure ARM — wakes hibernated containers in single-revision mode. Also calls `openclaw_version_for_image_tag(desired_tag)` and syncs `openclaw_version` + regenerates config if version changed (to prevent schema mismatch crash-loop). Skips hibernated tenants (`hibernated_at` non-null). (3) Schedules `restore_crons_after_image_update` QStash task with 90s delay and idempotency key `post-image-restore-<tenant_id>-<tag>`. Updates `container_image_tag` in DB.
dependencies: Azure Container Apps ARM API, Azure Storage (File Share), OpenClaw container gateway (cron.list), QStash, Supabase/Postgres
inventory_notes: Skips hibernated tenants by default. If `openclaw_version` changes due to image tag, `update_tenant_config` is called synchronously after the image bump to write the new schema to the file share before the container boots.

user_story: As an operator, I want to atomically bump a single tenant's OpenClaw version, config, and container image so that a new OpenClaw release is applied without leaving config-schema mismatches.
expected_behaviour: Called by `bump_openclaw_version` management command (sequential) and `bump_openclaw_atomic_per_tenant_task` QStash task (fan-out). Steps: (1) Snapshots current openclaw.json from file share as restore safety net. (2) Sets `tenant.openclaw_version = target_version` and saves. (3) Calls `update_tenant_config(tenant_id)` — file-share failures are not restored here (file is unchanged or file is the new schema and not restoring is correct). (4) Calls `update_container_image(container_id, f'{registry}/nbhd-openclaw:{image_tag}')`. If image push fails AND config_snapshot exists, restores old openclaw.json to file share (best-effort). On any outer exception, rolls back `tenant.openclaw_version` to `old_version`. On success, saves `container_image_tag = image_tag` and clears `hibernated_at` if was hibernated.
dependencies: Azure Container Apps ARM API, Azure Storage (File Share), Supabase/Postgres
inventory_notes: The config-snapshot restore is deliberately NOT triggered on `update_tenant_config` failures — only on image-push failures. Reason: if only the gateway.reload step fails, the file share already has the correct new config and restoring would re-trap the tenant. Documented as a key gotcha in the code.

feature: Fleet OpenClaw Version Fan-out via QStash
user_story: As a system, I want to fan out an atomic version bump across the entire active fleet via QStash so that a fleet rollout finishes within the request budget without timing out.
expected_behaviour: `bump_openclaw_atomic_per_tenant_task(tenant_id, target_version, image_tag)` is the per-tenant QStash target. Guards: if tenant not found → no-op. If already at `(target_version, image_tag)` → no-op (idempotent, covers QStash duplicate delivery). If not ACTIVE or no container_id → no-op. Otherwise calls `bump_openclaw_version_for_tenant`. Registry defaults to `settings.AZURE_ACR_SERVER`.
dependencies: Azure Container Apps ARM API, Azure Storage (File Share), QStash, Supabase/Postgres
inventory_notes: Idempotency key is version+tag pair already on tenant row — safe to re-enqueue on transient QStash failure.

user_story: As an operator, I want to push a new OpenClaw image to every active tenant with concurrency control so that a Dockerfile change reaches the fleet immediately without hitting Azure rate limits.
expected_behaviour: Management command `bump_all_tenant_images`. Requires `--tag` to be a non-'latest' concrete tag (or `settings.OPENCLAW_IMAGE_TAG`); refuses 'latest' because idempotence cannot be computed without a digest lookup. Queries active tenants with `container_id__gt=''`; optionally includes hibernated (`--include-hibernated`). Skips tenants already on target tag. Runs `update_container_image` in a `ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=5, default)`. DB write (`container_image_tag`) happens on the main thread after each future resolves. Returns non-zero exit if any tenant fails. `--dry-run` prints plan without Azure calls.
dependencies: Azure Container Apps ARM API, Supabase/Postgres
inventory_notes: Default max_workers=5 to stay within ~30 RPM Azure Container Apps ARM rate limits. DB writes are intentionally on the main thread so test transactions are visible.

user_story: As an operator, I want to deploy a custom canary image tag to a single tenant without updating the DB so that the next normal apply-pending-configs run automatically rolls the container back to the fleet tag.
expected_behaviour: Management command `canary_tenant_image --container <name> --tag <tag>`. Calls `update_container_image(container, f'{registry}/{repository}:{tag}')` via Azure ARM. Explicitly does NOT write `Tenant.container_image_tag` to Postgres. Prints a warning reminding the operator that the DB tag is unchanged. Raises `CommandError` on Azure failure.
inventory_notes: Self-reverting by design: the next `apply_pending_configs` cycle or image bump command will restore the canonical fleet tag since the DB still holds the previous value.

user_story: As a system, I want every generated OpenClaw config to be audited for security issues before being pushed to the file share so that secrets are not accidentally embedded in container configs.
expected_behaviour: `_audit_and_log(tenant, config, stage)` is called inside both `provision_tenant` and `update_tenant_config`. It calls `audit_config_security(config)` from `apps/orchestrator/config_security.py`. For each finding, creates a `PlatformIssueLog` record with severity mapped from finding.severity (error→HIGH, warning→MEDIUM). Warnings do not block. Errors accumulate, then raise `ValueError` with a summary — propagated to caller (provision resets to PENDING, update_tenant_config propagates to caller). Findings with neither error nor warning severity → LOW.
inventory_notes: Runs synchronously before every file-share write. Zero findings → early return, no DB writes.

user_story: As an operator, I want to automatically detect and re-provision tenants stuck in PENDING/PROVISIONING/ACTIVE states with missing container metadata so that half-provisioned tenants are recovered without manual intervention.
expected_behaviour: `repair_stale_tenant_provisioning(tenant_id, limit, dry_run)` — also exposed as `repair_stale_tenant_provisioning_task` (QStash, limit=50). Finds tenants in [PENDING, PROVISIONING, ACTIVE] status with empty `container_id` or `container_fqdn`. In dry_run mode, logs and returns. In live mode, calls `provision_tenant(tenant_id)` and checks the resulting fields. Returns a summary dict with evaluated/repaired/failed/skipped counts and per-tenant results. Failures per tenant are caught and counted; remaining tenants continue. Management command `repair_tenant_provisioning` exposes this with `--tenant-id`, `--limit`, `--dry-run` flags.
dependencies: Azure Container Apps ARM API, Azure Key Vault, Azure Storage, Supabase/Postgres
inventory_notes: Re-uses the full `provision_tenant` pipeline, which is idempotent for tenants already partially provisioned (it overwrites what's missing). ACTIVE tenants with partial metadata are treated the same as PENDING/PROVISIONING.

user_story: As a system, I want to automatically hibernate active tenant containers that have been idle for 2+ hours so that Azure compute costs are minimized.
expected_behaviour: QStash task `hibernate_idle_tenants_task` (hourly). Selects ACTIVE tenants with `container_id` where `last_message_at < now-2h` (or never messaged and `provisioned_at < now-2h`), and `cron_wake_at` is null or older than cutoff. Uses `SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` to avoid race with concurrent sweeps (lock released before any Azure calls). For each candidate: `refresh_from_db` on `last_message_at`, `hibernated_at`, `cron_wake_at` for TOCTOU check. Then calls `_cron_active_or_imminent(tenant, window_seconds=300)` — if a cron is in-flight (`state.runningAtMs` set) or due within 5 minutes, skips with reason logged. Otherwise calls `hibernate_idle_tenant(tenant)`. Returns {hibernated, failed, skipped_cron_wake, skipped_imminent_cron}.
dependencies: Azure Container Apps ARM API, OpenClaw gateway (cron.list for in-flight check), QStash, Supabase/Postgres
inventory_notes: Does NOT check Tenant.status here — only hibernated_at. Suspended tenants with containers are handled by a separate `hibernate_suspended_task`. The `select_for_update(skip_locked=True)` lock covers the queryset evaluation only — not the Azure calls, which would exceed `idle_in_transaction_session_timeout`.

user_story: As a system, I want to hibernate one idle tenant by capturing their cron schedule, suspending crons, scaling the container to zero, and scheduling a pre-cron wake so no cron fires are missed.
expected_behaviour: `hibernate_idle_tenant(tenant)` — called from `hibernate_idle_tenants_task` and `check_cron_wake_idle_task`. Step 1: `_capture_tenant_cron_schedules(tenant)` — calls `cron.list` against gateway, persists result to `Tenant.cron_jobs_snapshot`. On gateway failure, falls back to existing snapshot, then to `build_cron_seed_jobs`. Step 2: calls `suspend_tenant_crons(tenant)` from `apps.cron.suspension` — disables all cron jobs in the container. Failure here is logged and swallowed. Step 3: calls `hibernate_container_app(container_id)` via Azure ARM (deactivates revisions → 0 replicas). On failure, returns False without setting `hibernated_at`. Step 4: updates `Tenant.hibernated_at = now()`, clears `cron_wake_at`. Step 5: `_schedule_next_cron_wake(tenant, cron_jobs)` — computes `_find_earliest_next_run` from jobs' `state.nextRunAtMs` (falling back to croniter from `schedule.expr`), publishes QStash `wake_for_cron` task with `delay = (earliest_ms - now_ms)/1000 - 240s` and idempotency key `wake-cron-{tenant.id}-{earliest_ms}`.
dependencies: Azure Container Apps ARM API, OpenClaw gateway (cron.list, cron.disable), QStash, Supabase/Postgres
inventory_notes: _CRON_WAKE_LEAD_SECONDS = 240 (4 min lead time). If gateway unreachable at hibernation time, falls back to snapshot then seed jobs for wake scheduling. `_find_earliest_next_run` reads `state.nextRunAtMs` (nested under `state` in OC response) — NOT top-level `nextRunAtMs`.

user_story: As a system, I want to wake a hibernated tenant's container before their scheduled cron fires so that the cron is not silently dropped.
expected_behaviour: QStash task `wake_for_cron` — published by `_schedule_next_cron_wake` ~240s before the next cron. If tenant not found → returns `tenant_not_found`. If not hibernated → already awake; re-arms the next cron wake via `_capture_tenant_cron_schedules + _schedule_next_cron_wake` (chain continuity) and returns `already_awake`. If not ACTIVE → returns `not_active`. Otherwise calls `wake_hibernated_tenant(tenant)` — on failure returns `wake_failed`. On success, stamps `Tenant.cron_wake_at = now()` and publishes `check_cron_wake_idle` with a 1800s (30 min) delay.
dependencies: Azure Container Apps ARM API, QStash, Supabase/Postgres
inventory_notes: cron_wake_at stamp is used by `hibernate_idle_tenants_task` to skip tenants recently woken for a cron. The re-arm on already-awake path prevents the wake chain from breaking if the next idle-hibernation cycle fails to re-arm.

user_story: As a system, I want to wake a hibernated tenant's container when they send a message and apply any pending image/config changes so the container comes up on the latest code.
expected_behaviour: `wake_hibernated_tenant(tenant)` — called by `wake_for_cron_task` and by the Telegram/LINE webhook on inbound message for hibernated tenants. Step 1: checks if desired image tag (`OPENCLAW_IMAGE_TAG`) differs from `tenant.container_image_tag`. If stale, calls `update_container_image` with new image; syncs `openclaw_version` via `openclaw_version_for_image_tag` if version changes (`version_synced=True`); otherwise calls `ensure_plugin_runtime_deps_mount` or `wake_container_app`. Step 2: clears `hibernated_at`, stamps `last_wake_at`. Step 3: if `version_synced` or `config_version == 0` and pending <= config, calls `tenant.bump_pending_config()` to force a config regen. If `pending_config_version > config_version`, publishes QStash `apply_single_tenant_config` task. Step 4: publishes `deliver_buffered_messages` with 45s delay, retries=1. Step 5: publishes `resume_hibernated_crons` with 60s delay.
dependencies: Azure Container Apps ARM API, Azure Storage (File Share), QStash, Supabase/Postgres
inventory_notes: config_version == 0 forces a regen even when pending == config — covers tenants whose provision-time config write silently failed. version_synced forces regen even when pending == config — covers hibernated tenants who woke onto a newer image that has a different schema.

user_story: As a system, I want to re-hibernate a container that was woken for a cron but has had no user activity so that idle costs are minimized, while deferring if another cron is imminent.
expected_behaviour: QStash task `check_cron_wake_idle` — published 1800s (30 min) after `wake_for_cron`. Guards: if tenant not found → `tenant_not_found`. If no `cron_wake_at` → `not_cron_wake`. If already hibernated → `already_hibernated`. Checks if user messaged since wake (`last_message_at > cron_wake_at`) — if yes, clears `cron_wake_at` and returns `user_active`. Calls `_next_cron_within_window(tenant, window_seconds=5400)` — reads live `cron.list` then falls back to snapshot. If a cron fires within 90 minutes, publishes a deferred `check_cron_wake_idle` at `(upcoming_cron_ms - now_ms)/1000 + 1800s` and returns `deferred_for_upcoming_cron`. Otherwise clears `cron_wake_at`, calls `hibernate_idle_tenant(tenant)`, returns `re_hibernated`.
dependencies: OpenClaw gateway (cron.list), QStash, Azure Container Apps ARM, Supabase/Postgres
inventory_notes: _CRON_WAKE_IDLE_SECONDS = 1800, _CRON_LOOKAHEAD_WINDOW_SECONDS = 5400. The deferred path keeps back-to-back crons on a warm container. Failure to publish deferred check falls through to immediate re-hibernate.

user_story: As a system, I want to deliver messages that were buffered while a tenant's container was hibernated so that users receive responses after the container wakes.
expected_behaviour: QStash task `deliver_buffered_messages` — published with 45s delay after wake. Per-iteration: checks next undelivered BufferedMessage. LINE messages: `_claim_buffered_batch_for_tenant` picks up contiguous non-voice rows under the attempts cap (SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED, lease = timeout * 1.5). Batch > 1 rows are coalesced into one `/v1/chat/completions` POST with delineated content via `format_coalesced_user_content`. Response is relayed via `relay_ai_response_to_line`. Telegram messages: singleton claims via `_claim_next_buffered_message`, forwarded to `/telegram-webhook` via `forward_to_openclaw`.
