inventory_notes: Only deletes rows where delivered=True — undelivered or in-flight rows (delivered=False) are never deleted. The 7-day window provides an audit trail for recent delivery failures.

user_story: As a system, I want to generate a versioned OpenClaw config JSON from tenant parameters so that each container has a correct and secure runtime configuration.
expected_behaviour: `generate_openclaw_config(tenant)` in `config_generator.py`: generates a complete `openclaw.json` dict keyed to the tenant's `openclaw_version`. Includes: gateway config (bind=loopback, auth token as `${NBHD_GATEWAY_TOKEN}` env ref), tool policy from `generate_tool_config(tenant, version)` (allow-list, deny-list including 'gateway', plugin list, elevated=False), agent config (preferred model, persona, system prompt with cron context preamble), and `build_cron_seed_jobs(tenant)` for the initial cron seed. `config_to_json(config)` serializes to JSON string. Version-specific tool config handles schema differences between OpenClaw versions.
inventory_notes: Gateway bind must be 'loopback' (enforced by config_security audit). Auth token must use ${ENV_VAR} reference, not literal. Elevated execution must be disabled. 'gateway' must be in deny list. These are hard errors from audit_config_security and block provisioning/config update.

user_story: As a system, I want to validate generated OpenClaw configs for security invariants so that misconfigured containers cannot expose the gateway or use elevated privileges.
expected_behaviour: `audit_config_security(config)` in `config_security.py`: runs a set of security checks on a generated config dict and returns a list of `SecurityFinding` objects with severity 'error' or 'warning'. Checks: (1) `gateway.bind` must not be '0.0.0.0', 'all', or empty — error; (2) `gateway.auth.token` must start with '${' (env var ref, not literal) — error; (3) `tools.elevated.enabled` must be False — error; (4) 'gateway' must be in `tools.deny` list — error; (5) plugin allow-list validation. Findings are logged to `PlatformIssueLog` with severity mapping. Errors raise `ValueError` blocking the operation. Warnings are logged but non-blocking.
inventory_notes: Called at both provisioning time and on every config update. Findings go to PlatformIssueLog with category CONFIG_ISSUE. Regex `_SECRET_RE` scans for literal API key prefixes (sk-ant-, sk-or-v1-, sk-proj-, etc.) as a defense against key leakage into config.

user_story: As a system, I want to render persona-specific workspace files (AGENTS.md, SOUL.md, IDENTITY.md) for each tenant so that the AI assistant reflects the correct personality and behavioral guidelines.
expected_behaviour: `render_workspace_files(persona_key, tenant)` in `personas.py`: renders the set of workspace file templates for the given persona and tenant. Returns a dict with keys like `NBHD_AGENTS_MD`, `NBHD_SOUL_MD`, `NBHD_IDENTITY_MD`. Template is the global AGENTS.md from Key Vault (`AZURE_KV_SECRET_AGENTS_MD`) or templates directory, with persona personality spliced in and `prompt_extras` from `user.preferences` appended. `render_workspace_rules()`: auto-discovers all .md rule files from `templates/openclaw/rules/` and returns a filename→content dict for upload to `workspace/rules/`. Management command `refresh_persona_agents_md`: re-renders and uploads AGENTS.md for all active tenants via `ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=5)`; supports `--tenant`, `--include-hibernated`, `--dry-run`, `--max-workers`.
dependencies: Azure Key Vault (AGENTS.md template secret), Azure File Share (upload_workspace_file), apps/tenants models (user.preferences)
inventory_notes: AGENTS.md is always overwritten on config refresh. SOUL.md and IDENTITY.md are seed-once (skip_if_exists=True) — overwriting them would wipe agent-evolved persona. Prompt extras are per-tenant canary-scoped additions stored in `user.preferences['prompt_extras']['agents_md']`. refresh_persona_agents_md is idempotent — identical bytes are a no-op on SMB.

user_story: As an operator, I want to check the health of all active tenant containers so that I can identify unhealthy instances and take corrective action.
expected_behaviour: `check_tenant_health(tenant_id)`: pings `https://{container_fqdn}/health` with 10s timeout. Returns structured dict with: `tenant_id`, `display_name`, `status`, `container`, `healthy` (bool), `checks` (dict of check_name → `{ok, detail, response_time_ms}`), `config_drift` (bool if `pending_config_version > config_version`), `config_drift_detail`. Status != ACTIVE or no `container_fqdn` returns immediately with unhealthy check. `check_all_tenants_health()`: runs check_tenant_health for all ACTIVE tenants. Management command `check_health` supports `--json` output and pretty-prints per-tenant checks with icons.
dependencies: httpx (health ping), apps/tenants models, apps/common/cache (cache ping)
inventory_notes: Config drift is informational only and does not affect `healthy` status (drift is expected after deploys and resolves on the next idle cycle). The `check_health` command also pings the Django cache as a separate check.

user_story: As an operator, I want to verify every link in the gateway chain for a specific tenant so that I can diagnose connectivity or configuration issues step by step.
expected_behaviour: Management command `check_gateway_health` runs 7 sequential checks, stopping at first failure: (1) tenant exists and is ACTIVE with non-empty FQDN; (2) DNS resolution of FQDN via `socket.getaddrinfo`; (3) Key Vault secret `tenant-<uuid>-internal-key` readable and non-empty; (4) GET `https://{fqdn}/health` returns 200 within 5s; (5) `cron.list` via `invoke_gateway_tool` succeeds; (6) file share `cron/jobs.json` readable and contains at least 1 job; (7) POST to `https://{fqdn}/api/v1/integrations/runtime/{tenant_id}/daily-note/append/` with internal key returns 200/201. Exits non-zero at first failure. Defaults to first active tenant if `--tenant-id` not specified.
dependencies: Azure Key Vault, Azure File Share, apps/cron/gateway_client
inventory_notes: Check 7 tests the full Django↔container round-trip via the runtime endpoint. Writes a health-check sentinel entry to `date=1970-01-01` to avoid polluting real journal data.
error_detail: Confirmed spec deviation. handle() does a bare `return` at line 41 when a check fails instead of raising CommandError. In Django, run_from_argv only emits a non-zero exit (sys.exit(1)) when a CommandError propagates; a normal return from handle() lets the process exit 0. So check_gateway_health exits 0 even when a link in the chain fails, directly contradicting the spec's stated behavior "Exits non-zero at first failure." This is genuine, not a misread: _resolve_tenant already uses CommandError (lines 52, 55), so the import on line 7 is in active use; only the per-check failure path skips it. Impact is low: this is a hand-run operator diagnostic with NO programmatic consumers in the repo (grep across *.py/*.sh/*.yml/Makefile/*.md found zero callers besides the file itself — no CI workflow, shell script, Makefile target, or runbook invokes it and checks $?). An operator running it interactively reads the explicit color-coded "FAIL" / "Stopped at first failure." stderr output and discovers the failure regardless of exit code. The defect only bites if someone later chains it via && or wraps it in automation that trusts the exit code — a latent footgun rather than an active operator-facing failure.

user_story: As an operator, I want to list all tenants with their provisioning state so that I can audit the fleet and identify tenants needing attention.
expected_behaviour: `list_tenants` management command: queries all tenants (optionally filtered by `--status`), prints one line per tenant: `id display_name status model_tier container_id messages_this_month`. `tenant_audit` management command: prints all tenants ordered by creation date with `id | status | container_id | created_at | email`, followed by total count and per-status breakdown. Both are read-only diagnostic commands with no side effects.
inventory_notes: list_tenants includes messages_this_month for at-a-glance usage monitoring. tenant_audit is older and simpler; list_tenants is the preferred operational command with status filtering.

feature: Plugin Runtime Deps EmptyDir Mount Retrofit
user_story: As an operator, I want to retrofit the plugin-runtime-deps EmptyDir mount onto existing tenant containers so that OpenClaw's bundled-channel installer avoids EPERM errors on the SMB file share.
expected_behaviour: `ensure_plugin_runtime_deps_mount(container_id)` from azure_client: checks if the EmptyDir volume mount already exists on the Container App revision; if not, patches the app to add the mount (creates a new revision in single-revision mode, which wakes the container). Returns True if mount was added, False if already present. Management command `ensure_runtime_deps_mount`: supports `--tenant UUID` or `--all` (all active non-hibernated tenants); `--dry-run` lists targets. Also called from `wake_hibernated_tenant` when not doing an image refresh.
inventory_notes: New tenants get the mount at provisioning time. Hibernated tenants get it on natural wake. This command targets active awake tenants that predate the fix. In single-revision mode, adding a mount creates a new revision and auto-activates, which wakes the container as a side effect.

user_story: As an operator, I want to deploy a custom image tag to a single tenant for canary testing without updating the fleet's tracking record so that the canary automatically reverts on the next normal rollout.
expected_behaviour: Management command `canary_tenant_image --container oc-* --tag canary-<sha>`: calls `update_container_image(container, image)` to deploy `registry/nbhd-openclaw:canary-<sha>` to the specified container. Does NOT update `Tenant.container_image_tag` in the DB, so the canary is transparent to the fleet reconciler. On the next normal `apply_pending_configs` run, the tenant's DB tag (pointing to the prior canonical tag) will trigger a reconcile that reverts the canary to the fleet image.
inventory_notes: By design, `container_image_tag` is not updated. This means the canary auto-reverts — there is no explicit undo step. Requires AZURE_ACR_SERVER to be configured.

user_story: As an operator, I want to remove TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and OPENCLAW_WEBHOOK_SECRET from tenant containers so that per-tenant polling is disabled in favor of a central poller.
expected_behaviour: Management command `disable_tenant_telegram_polling`: accepts `--tenant-id` (repeatable) or `--all-active`. For each target tenant with `status='active'` and non-empty `container_id`: calls `update_container_env_var(container_id, 'TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN', '')` and `update_container_env_var(container_id, 'OPENCLAW_WEBHOOK_SECRET', '')`. Each env var patch creates a new revision (single-revision mode). Reports patched/failed counts.
inventory_notes: Uses raw SQL to fetch tenant IDs for the --all-active path. Each env var update triggers a new revision in single-revision mode. This is a one-shot migration command for the Telegram single-revision architecture change.

user_story: As an operator, I want to set or clear per-tenant prompt extras on a canary tenant's AGENTS.md so that experimental instructions reach a single tenant without fleet rollout.
expected_behaviour: Management command `set_prompt_extras --tenant-id UUID --section agents_md [--file PATH | --stdin | --clear]`: loads `User.preferences['prompt_extras']`, sets or removes the given section's value. Refuses to write empty extras. Saves `user.preferences` on `User` model. Prints next step: run `force_apply_configs` to push the updated workspace file to the file share. Only known sections accepted: `agents_md`. Does NOT push the file automatically.
inventory_notes: Requires a follow-up `force_apply_configs` (or `update_all_configs`) run to push the rendered AGENTS.md with the new extras to the Azure File Share. The running container reads the updated file on its next session boot.

user_story: As an operator, I want to probe whether a specific topic is grounded in a tenant's cron-visible data so that I can diagnose hallucination or missing context issues before they reach the user.
expected_behaviour: Management command `grounding_probe --tenant UUID-PREFIX --topic TEXT [--expect SUBSTRING ...]` and `probe_grounding(tenant, topic, expect_terms)` in `grounding_probe.py`: renders the user state envelope (USER.md managed region) and queries `nbhd_journal_search` for reachable documents. Checks: is topic in USER.md envelope, which docs reference the topic (ranked by relevance), which `--expect` terms appear in: a PROJECT-kind doc (canonical), elsewhere (misfiled?), or nowhere (absent). Returns a report with `grounded`, `grounded_in_project`, per-term verdicts, newest source timestamp. Management command prints a VERDICT: GREEN (in project doc), AMBER (reachable but not in project doc), or RED (not grounded). Exits non-zero if not GREEN (or not grounded if no expect terms). Read-only — no writes.
dependencies: apps/journal (search), apps/orchestrator/workspace_envelope (render managed region), apps/tenants models
inventory_notes: Exit code 0 = GREEN or grounded; 1 = AMBER/RED. Doubles as a pass/fail check in operator scripts. No data is written during the probe.

user_story: As an operator, I want to audit the state of USER.md across all active tenants so that I can decide when to deploy a fleet-wide managed region rollout.
expected_behaviour: Management command `audit_user_md [--verbose]`: for each active tenant with a container, downloads `workspace/USER.md` from the file share and classifies as: `missing` (file not found), `empty` (blank), `boilerplate` (matches OC default), `managed` (has NBHD sentinels), `agent` (has content but no sentinels). Prints classification per tenant and a summary count table. With `--verbose`, also prints first 200 chars of agent-only content for manual inspection. Errors (file share inaccessible) are counted separately. Read-only.
inventory_notes: Agent classification means the tenant's assistant has written custom content to USER.md outside platform sentinels — the first managed push will prepend the managed region and preserve the agent content below. Knowing this in advance helps operators understand what will change.

user_story: As a system, I want to push updated USER.md to all active tenants every hour so that the current local time line and latest state is always fresh when crons fire.
expected_behaviour: `refresh_user_md_fleet_task()`: queries all active tenants (including hibernated) with `container_id`. For each, calls `push_user_md(tenant, force=True, debounce_seconds=0)`. Per-tenant failures are logged and skipped. Returns `{pushed, failed}`. Run hourly by QStash to bound staleness of the `_Current local time: ...` line to ≤1 hour. Hibernated tenants are included because the Azure File Share is always mounted and the next wake-fired turn reads the freshly-pushed USER.md.
dependencies: Azure File Share, apps/orchestrator/workspace_envelope, apps/tenants models
inventory_notes: Cost per tenant: one file share read + one write (~150-400ms typical). Signal-driven refreshes cover state changes (goal/task/fuel writes); this hourly sweep bounds the time-line staleness for tenants idle between signals. The management command provides the same push on-demand with optional `--tenant UUID-PREFIX` filter.

user_story: As a system process, I want to render a structured, up-to-date snapshot of a tenant's state into USER.md so that the OpenClaw assistant always has current context on every turn.
expected_behaviour: render_managed_region(tenant) walks the envelope registry (sorted by order), calls each section's enabled() predicate, skips disabled/empty sections, catches per-section exceptions without aborting the full render. Produces a markdown string wrapped in HTML-comment sentinels (BEGIN_MARKER / END_MARKER) containing: current local time line, last refreshed timestamp, synthesis hint, then each enabled section. Returns the full string including trailing newline. Never raises — section errors are logged and skipped.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (all pillar models), Azure File Share (read existing USER.md), envelope registry (all pillar envelope.py modules)
inventory_notes: Section exceptions are swallowed; a broken pillar only drops its own section. The current-time line is re-rendered on every push, bounding staleness to the debounce/fleet-refresh window.

user_story: As a system process, I want to safely merge platform-managed state into a USER.md that may contain agent-written content so that the agent's own observations are never silently overwritten.
expected_behaviour: push_user_md(tenant, force=False, debounce_seconds=60) applies a leading-edge debounce keyed on tenant_id in Django cache (default 60s). If force=True or debounce not active, it renders the managed region, reads the current USER.md from Azure File Share (failure falls through to None), applies the three-case merge (empty/boilerplate → write managed alone; markers present → replace only the managed region; agent content without markers → prepend managed, keep existing below), then uploads the merged file back. Returns True on write, False on debounce. On exception, clears the debounce key so the next caller can retry.
dependencies: Azure File Share, Django cache (Redis / Upstash)
inventory_notes: OpenClaw's default boilerplate (# USER.md - User Profile / - Name: / - Preferred address: / - Notes:) is treated as empty and replaced cleanly, not preserved. push_user_md_in_background spawns a daemon thread; in test/dev with NBHD_DISABLE_BACKGROUND_THREADS=True it runs synchronously.

user_story: As a system, I want USER.md to refresh automatically when any pillar model changes so that the assistant always sees current state without a deploy or manual action.
expected_behaviour: register_section() wires post_save and post_delete on every model in refresh_on (weak=False to avoid GC). The universal receiver _universal_refresh_receiver resolves tenant_id from the instance (via tenant_id FK or tenant relation), then schedules push_user_md(tenant_id, debounce_seconds=0) via transaction.on_commit (in a daemon thread in production, synchronously in test). The suppress_refresh() context manager (thread-local flag) silences signals inside bulk-write blocks to avoid file-share storms. Duplicate section key registration raises ValueError at boot.
dependencies: All pillar envelope.py modules (journal, fuel, finance, insights, lessons, tenants, core, orchestrator/agenda_envelope), Django post_save/post_delete signals, Azure File Share
inventory_notes: suppress_refresh is a per-thread flag checked at signal time (not in the callback) — calling it inside a transaction's autocommit on_commit would be too late. _resolve_tenant_id falls back to None for unexpected model shapes, making the receiver a safe no-op.

user_story: As a system, I want USER.md to be re-pushed for all active tenants every hour so that the current-local-time line never goes stale between signal-driven updates.
expected_behaviour: refresh_user_md_fleet_task() iterates all ACTIVE tenants with a container_id, calls push_user_md(tenant, force=True, debounce_seconds=0) for each. Per-tenant failures are logged and skipped — one bad file share does not block the fleet. Returns {pushed, failed}. Registered as QStash cron refresh-user-md-fleet at '25 * * * *' (every hour at :25 UTC). Hibernated tenants are included because the Azure File Share is always mounted regardless of container state.
dependencies: QStash, Azure File Share, Supabase/Postgres (Tenant table)
inventory_notes: force=True bypasses the 60s debounce; debounce_seconds=0 means even a concurrent signal-driven push cannot be blocked. Cost per tenant ~150–400ms (one read + one write).

feature: refresh_user_md Management Command — Fleet / Single Tenant
user_story: As an operator, I want to force-push USER.md for all active tenants (or a specific one) after a workspace_envelope code change so that every tenant picks up the new template without waiting for organic signals.
expected_behaviour: python manage.py refresh_user_md [--tenant <uuid-or-prefix>] filters ACTIVE tenants with container_id, calls push_user_md(tenant, force=True) sequentially for each. Prints per-tenant success/error and a summary. --tenant flag accepts a UUID prefix (startswith match). Failures are counted but do not abort the run for other tenants.
inventory_notes: No parallelism — runs sequentially. For fleet-scale use the QStash fleet task instead.

user_story: As an operator, I want a read-only classification of every active tenant's USER.md state so that I can assess migration status before a fleet rollout.
expected_behaviour: python manage.py audit_user_md [--verbose] downloads workspace/USER.md from every ACTIVE tenant's file share and classifies it as: missing (download returns None/error), empty (blank), boilerplate (equals OpenClaw's default seed content), managed (contains both BEGIN/END markers), or agent (has content but no markers). Prints a per-tenant line and summary counts. --verbose adds a 200-char preview for 'agent' entries. Read-only — does not write anything.
inventory_notes: Azure read errors are counted as 'errors', not 'missing'. Classification is in-process — no LLM calls.

feature: Context Digest API for iOS On-Device Mode
user_story: As a subscriber using private/on-device mode on iOS, I want the server to provide a compact snapshot of my state so that the on-device model has context without my data ever leaving my device to a cloud model.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/chat/context/ (IsAuthenticated, ChatContextHourThrottle). Optional query param max_chars (int, clamped to 1000–16000, default 6000). Returns {context_md, max_chars, generated_at}. Calls render_context_digest(tenant, max_chars=max_chars) which: walks the same envelope registry as USER.md but skips privacy_placeholders section and applies per-section truncation (body capped at max_chars//6 chars each, with a reservation for conversation_digest). PII entity_map rehydration applied on the way out (replace [PERSON_N] tokens with real names). Cache-Control: no-store. 404 if no tenant. Hourly throttle per user.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (all pillar models via registry), PII rehydration (apps/pii/redactor.py:rehydrate_text)
inventory_notes: privacy_placeholders section is deliberately excluded — the device has no entity map so [PERSON_N] would be shown verbatim to the user. conversation_digest is reserved first in the budget even though it renders last — without reservation, bulky earlier sections starve it.

user_story: As a system process, I want AGENTS.md written to each tenant's file share to reflect their chosen persona and any tenant-specific prompt extras so that the OpenClaw container boots with the right personality and tooling instructions.
expected_behaviour: render_workspace_files(persona_key, tenant=None) resolves the AGENTS.md template via priority order: (1) Azure Key Vault secret AZURE_KV_SECRET_AGENTS_MD, (2) NBHD_AGENTS_MD_TEMPLATE env var, (3) templates/openclaw/AGENTS.md file, (4) hardcoded fallback. Substitutes {{PERSONA_PERSONALITY}} with the persona's agents_personality string. Appends tenant.user.preferences['prompt_extras']['agents_md'] if present. Conditionally appends Gravity Observation Mode rules if tenant.finance_active. Renders 4 personas (neighbor, coach, sage, spark). SOUL.md and IDENTITY.md are analogous but simpler. Also renders per-channel formatting docs and rule templates from templates/openclaw/rules/.
inventory_notes: Key Vault loads are cached in-process after first successful load (_cached attribute on the function object). Per-tenant extras are read fresh on every render — no extra cache needed. SOUL.md and IDENTITY.md are seeded once at provision (skip_if_exists=True); AGENTS.md is always overwritten.

user_story: As an operator, I want to push an updated AGENTS.md to all active tenants in parallel after deploying a new template so that the fleet picks up new instructions immediately without waiting for config bumps.
expected_behaviour: python manage.py refresh_persona_agents_md [--tenant <uuid>] [--include-hibernated] [--dry-run] [--max-workers N]. Queries ACTIVE tenants with container_id; excludes hibernated unless --include-hibernated. Parallelizes with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=5 default). For each tenant: reads persona key from user.preferences, calls render_workspace_files, extracts NBHD_AGENTS_MD key, uploads to workspace/AGENTS.md via upload_workspace_file. Raises CommandError if no matching tenants. Raises CommandError at end if any tenants failed. --dry-run prints tenant list with persona keys and returns without writing. Empty NBHD_AGENTS_MD from render raises RuntimeError (surfaced as failure).
dependencies: Azure File Share, Azure Key Vault
inventory_notes: Write is idempotent (last-write-wins identical bytes). Hibernated tenants pick up AGENTS.md at wake unless --include-hibernated is passed.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to browse available agent personas so that I can choose the assistant personality that suits me.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/tenants/personas/ (IsAuthenticated, tenant_cache TTL 300s tag 'tenant'). Returns a JSON list of {key, label, description, emoji} for the four built-in personas (neighbor, coach, sage, spark). 200 always for authenticated users (no tenant required). Response is cached 5 minutes per user.
inventory_notes: Persona data is entirely static in code; no DB read. Cache tag 'tenant' means it is busted when the tenant row changes.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to change my agent's persona so that my assistant reflects the personality I prefer.
expected_behaviour: PATCH /api/v1/tenants/preferences/ (IsAuthenticated). Body: {agent_persona: string}. Validates persona key is in PERSONAS dict; 400 with {detail: 'Unknown persona: <key>'} on unknown value. On valid key: updates user.preferences['agent_persona'] and saves. If the user has an ACTIVE tenant, calls tenant.bump_pending_config() to schedule a config refresh (which will re-render AGENTS.md). Returns {agent_persona: <current_value>}. GET returns current persona key (default 'neighbor'). Cache TTL 120s on GET.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (User.preferences JSONB), pending_config_version bump → QStash apply-pending-configs
inventory_notes: bump_pending_config() only fires if tenant exists and is ACTIVE. The AGENTS.md on the file share is not immediately updated — it lands on the next apply_pending_configs run. The persona is also used for SOUL.md and IDENTITY.md rendering.

user_story: As an operator, I want to add canary-scoped prompt rules to a specific tenant's AGENTS.md without deploying a schema migration so that I can test new instructions on one tenant first.
expected_behaviour: python manage.py set_prompt_extras --tenant-id <uuid> --section agents_md (--file <path> | --stdin | --clear). Fetches Tenant by exact UUID (not prefix match; raises CommandError on not found). Reads extras value from file or stdin; rejects empty values. Sets or clears user.preferences['prompt_extras'][section]. Saves user with update_fields=['preferences']. The only known section is 'agents_md'. After setting, instructs operator to run force_apply_configs to push to file share. Does NOT push immediately.
inventory_notes: Extras are appended to AGENTS.md content at render time by render_workspace_files. They live at user.preferences['prompt_extras']['agents_md']. Section validation uses a hardcoded _KNOWN_SECTIONS frozenset; unknown sections raise 'invalid choice' at argparse level.

feature: Daily Note Backfill from PendingMessages (One-Off Recovery)
user_story: As an operator, I want to replay historical LINE/Telegram messages into a tenant's daily note files on the file share so that OpenClaw memory-core and dreaming can index previously invisible conversations.
expected_behaviour: python manage.py backfill_daily_notes_from_messages --tenant <uuid-or-prefix> --start YYYY-MM-DD --end YYYY-MM-DD [--dry-run]. Resolves tenant by prefix (CommandError on no match or ambiguous). Queries PendingMessage for the tenant and date range (interpreted in tenant's local timezone). Groups by local date. For each day, renders a markdown block (## Replayed conversation — backfilled YYYY-MM-DD, bullet lines at HH:MM local). Downloads existing workspace/memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md (fall through to empty on error), appends the block, uploads back. --dry-run prints preview without writing. Raises CommandError on upload failure after partial writes.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (PendingMessage model, apps/router/models.py), Azure File Share (workspace/memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md)
inventory_notes: Extracts payload.message_text (full text) rather than user_text (truncated to 200 chars at the queue boundary). Multi-line messages are flattened to single lines. After run, operator must manually trigger 'openclaw memory rem-backfill' on the container. Upload failure leaves earlier days written — command instructs to check file-share state before re-running.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

user_story: As a system, I want to schedule a one-shot welcome cron in the tenant's OpenClaw container when Fuel or Gravity is enabled so that the user receives a warm introduction to the new feature.
expected_behaviour: schedule_welcome(tenant, feature, cron_name, prompt_template, fire_in_minutes=5) checks Tenant.welcomes_sent[feature]: if set, returns SKIPPED_ALREADY_DELIVERED. Calls cron_get to check for an existing cron by name. If it exists and its next fire is within 1 day, returns SKIPPED_PENDING. If next fire is >1 day (stale annual recurrence), calls cron_remove, then schedules fresh. Computes fire_at = now(user_tz) + fire_in_minutes. Builds a date-pattern cron expr (M H D M *), calls invoke_gateway_tool with cron.add (sessionTarget=isolated, kind=agentTurn, delivery.mode=none). Returns SCHEDULED or REPLACED_STALE. Raises on transport failures.
dependencies: OpenClaw container gateway (cron.add, cron_get, cron_remove via apps/cron/gateway_client.py)
inventory_notes: One-shot date pattern (M H D M *) is annual-recurring — a stale missed fire shows next_fire ~1 year ahead. The >1-day threshold catches that case and replaces it. The welcome message instructs the agent to self-remove the cron after delivery.

user_story: As an operator, I want to schedule welcome crons for tenants who had Fuel or Gravity enabled before the welcome feature shipped so that they receive the intro they missed.
expected_behaviour: python manage.py backfill_welcomes [--tenant <uuid-prefix>] [--feature fuel|finance|both]. Queries ACTIVE tenants with container_id. For each tenant: if fuel_enabled and feature in (fuel, both), calls _schedule_fuel_welcome; if finance_enabled and feature in (finance, both), calls _schedule_finance_welcome. Uses the same self-healing WelcomeStatus enum. Tallies per-feature outcome counts (scheduled, replaced_stale, skipped_pending, skipped_already_delivered, failed). Prints summary. Idempotent: re-running is safe because schedule_welcome skips already-delivered and still-pending welcomes.
dependencies: OpenClaw container gateway, Supabase/Postgres (Tenant.welcomes_sent, Tenant.fuel_enabled, Tenant.finance_active)
inventory_notes: Errors on individual tenants are caught and tallied as 'failed'; they do not abort the run.
error_detail: Confirmed inconsistency. The backfill_welcomes management command gates the finance welcome on the raw tenant.finance_enabled DB flag (backfill_welcomes.py:68), while every other welcome path gates on tenant.finance_active, which ANDs the flag with the GRAVITY_ENABLED platform kill switch (tenants/models.py:707-718). I verified the underlying scheduler schedule_welcome (welcome_scheduler.py:48-141) has NO internal GRAVITY guard — it only short-circuits on welcomes_sent[feature] and pending-cron idempotency — so the looser gate is NOT compensated downstream. With GRAVITY_ENABLED=False (the production default, settings/base.py:465) and a tenant whose finance_enabled is still True from before Gravity was paused, the command would dispatch a finance welcome cron via the OpenClaw gateway for a feature that is platform-disabled and whose tools are absent from the agent manifest — a confusing, off-brand intro. Scope-limiting facts that lower severity: (1) the CI/CD deploy step does NOT run this command — it curls the HTTP endpoint (cron/views.py:830), which correctly uses finance_active (line 867); (2) the daily watchdog reconcile_welcomes_task also correctly uses finance_active (orchestrator/tasks.py:1146). The management command is documented (and referenced in ci-cd.yml:420) only as the MANUAL operator fallback when the deploy curl fails. So the defect only manifests when an operator hand-runs python manage.py backfill_welcomes against a DB containing finance_enabled=True tenants while GRAVITY is off. Real but narrow (manual-path plus specific DB-state preconditions), not a security or data-integrity issue.

feature: Welcome Cron Daily Fleet Reconcile (QStash cron)
user_story: As a system, I want a daily watchdog that reschedules any stale or missing welcome crons so that tenants whose welcome crashed mid-delivery receive a retry within 24 hours.
expected_behaviour: reconcile_welcomes_task() iterates all ACTIVE tenants with container_id. For each: if fuel_enabled, calls _schedule_fuel_welcome; if finance_active, calls _schedule_finance_welcome. Transport failures are swallowed and tallied as 'failed'. Returns {tenants, fuel: {status_counts}, finance: {status_counts}, statuses: [...]}. Registered as QStash cron reconcile-welcomes at '30 1 * * *' (01:30 UTC daily).
inventory_notes: Closed the gap from the 2026-05-07 canary incident where an agent crashed mid-welcome turn and the stale cron prevented reschedule for ~1 year.

feature: Journal Memory Sync to File Share
user_story: As a system, I want journal documents mirrored as markdown files on the tenant's Azure File Share so that OpenClaw's on-disk memory system has a human-readable record of the user's journal.
expected_behaviour: render_memory_files(tenant) queries all non-daily Documents + dailies from the last 30 days (skips kind in [goal, tasks] for experimental_typed_journal_lifecycle tenants). Validates kind/slug path components via validate_kind_slug; skips NTFS-hostile paths. Renders each as 'memory/journal/<kind>/<slug>.md' with content '# title\n\ncontent'. Runs PII redaction (RedactionSession) and merges entity_map into tenant.pii_entity_map. upload_memory_files_to_share(tenant_id, files) creates directories, compares by size+content before writing (skips unchanged files). Returns count written. Soft-fails if the file share does not exist.
dependencies: Azure File Share (Azure Storage SDK), Supabase/Postgres (Document model), PII redactor (apps/pii/redactor.py)
inventory_notes: SQLite-over-SMB is disabled fleet-wide (corrupts on container kill); search routes through Postgres nbhd_journal_search instead. Memory files are a human-readable mirror for dreaming/grounded backfill only.

user_story: As a system, I want structured data extracted from daily journal notes each evening (local time) so that goals, tasks, and insights stay up to date without requiring the user to take action.
expected_behaviour: nightly_extraction_task() runs every hour at :00 UTC (QStash cron nightly-extraction '0 * * * *'). For each ACTIVE tenant, checks _is_nightly_extraction_window_local(tenant, now, hour=21) — true when tenant's local clock is in the 21:xx hour. Checks _already_ran_today_local(tenant, now) via Tenant.last_nightly_extraction_at. If both pass, calls apps/journal/extraction.py:run_extraction_for_tenant(tenant). Catches per-tenant exceptions and tallies errored. Returns {considered, fired, skipped_window, skipped_already_ran, errored}.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (Tenant.last_nightly_extraction_at, journal Documents), LLM via apps/journal/extraction.py
inventory_notes: Half-hour-offset timezones (IST, NPT) get a clean hit once per local day because the acceptance window is the full 60-minute hourly slot. Idempotency is per-local-date via Tenant.last_nightly_extraction_at.

user_story: As a system, I want USER.md seeded with the platform-managed envelope immediately after a tenant is provisioned so that the OpenClaw container has current state on its very first boot.
expected_behaviour: In provision_tenant(), after saving container_id/container_fqdn and status=ACTIVE, calls push_user_md(tenant, force=True) as a post-provision non-critical step. force=True bypasses debounce. Exceptions are caught and logged with a warning but do NOT roll back the tenant to PENDING. This runs synchronously (not in a background thread) because the container is not yet serving requests.
inventory_notes: AGENTS.md/SOUL.md/IDENTITY.md are also written during provisioning via render_workspace_files + create_container_app. SOUL.md and IDENTITY.md use skip_if_exists=True so future config refreshes do not overwrite agent-evolved content.

user_story: As a system, I want a welcome email sent to new subscribers immediately after provisioning so that web-signup users (who have no Telegram chat_id) receive their first contact.
expected_behaviour: send_welcome_email(tenant) is called in provision_tenant() as a non-critical post-provision step (exceptions swallowed). Idempotent via tenant.welcome_email_sent_at: if set, returns False immediately. Requires user.email; returns False if missing. Checks LineQuotaState.get().is_exhausted to determine whether to mention LINE in the CTA (failing closed — skips LINE mention if quota check fails). Renders email/welcome_subject.txt + email/welcome_body.txt templates. Sends via Django send_mail. On success, stamps tenant.welcome_email_sent_at = now().
inventory_notes: Telegram-connected users also receive an in-chat welcome via send_telegram_message(chat_id, WELCOME_MESSAGE) in step 4b. Email is idempotent on welcome_email_sent_at; Telegram is best-effort (exception swallowed).

user_story: As an operator, I want to remove Heartbeat Check-in cron jobs from tenants where heartbeat is disabled so that the agent is not woken hourly for a feature that is turned off.
expected_behaviour: python manage.py remove_zombie_heartbeats [--dry-run]. Queries ACTIVE tenants with container_id and heartbeat_enabled=False. For each: calls cron.list on the gateway; if 'Heartbeat Check-in' job is found, calls cron.remove with its id/jobId. Skips tenants with no container_fqdn. --dry-run reports what would be removed without writing. Returns removed/skipped/errors counts. Sleeps 1s between tenants to avoid Azure gateway rate limits.
dependencies: OpenClaw container gateway (cron.list, cron.remove per tenant)
inventory_notes: Also available as remove_zombie_heartbeats_task() in tasks.py (triggerable via QStash TASK_REGISTRY). Not currently registered as a recurring QStash cron.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

user_story: As a system, I want AGENTS.md and all workspace docs re-pushed to the file share every time a tenant's config is refreshed so that template, persona, and prompt-extras changes land atomically with the config version bump.
expected_behaviour: update_tenant_config(tenant_id) re-renders workspace files and uploads them after writing openclaw.json. Overwrites: AGENTS.md, all workspace/docs/ files (tools-reference.md, channel-formatting.md, cron-management.md, error-handling.md, error-handling.md, privacy-redaction.md, platform-guide.md). Seeds once (skip_if_exists=True): SOUL.md, IDENTITY.md. Uploads all .md files from templates/openclaw/rules/ to workspace/rules/. Then calls push_user_md(tenant, force=True). Channel-formatting doc chosen based on user.preferred_channel (telegram vs line). Privacy-redaction doc only added for tiers with PERSON entity redaction enabled. Workspace file failures are logged but do not abort the config write.
dependencies: Azure File Share, Azure Key Vault (AGENTS.md/SOUL.md templates), Supabase/Postgres
inventory_notes: SOUL.md and IDENTITY.md use skip_if_exists=True — they belong to the agent after first provision. Overwriting would silently erase agent-evolved persona files.

feature: Templates.md (Daily Note Template Reference) in Workspace
user_story: As a system, I want a TEMPLATES.md workspace file that describes the tenant's configured daily note sections so that the agent knows exactly which slugs to use for nbhd_daily_note_set_section calls.
expected_behaviour: render_templates_md(tenant) calls journal.services.get_default_template(tenant). If no template, returns '# Daily Note Template\n\nNo template configured yet.' Otherwise renders section list with title, slug, source, and optional seed content. Included in render_workspace_files() result as NBHD_SKILL_TEMPLATES_MD (only when tenant is provided). Uploaded to the container during provision and config refreshes.
inventory_notes: NBHD_SKILL_TEMPLATES_MD is only populated when tenant is not None — the function also serves persona-only contexts (tests, admin tools) where no tenant is available.
error_detail: Confirmed real. render_workspace_files() populates NBHD_SKILL_TEMPLATES_MD (personas.py:519-520, rendered from the tenant's live NoteTemplate via render_templates_md at personas.py:322-356). Both the config-refresh path (services.py:599) and provision path (services.py:337) call it, but in update_tenant_config() the upload step only writes keys enumerated in file_map_overwrite (services.py:602-614) and file_map_seed_once (services.py:621-624); NBHD_SKILL_TEMPLATES_MD is in NEITHER dict, so workspace/skills/nbhd-managed/daily-journal/references/templates.md is never re-uploaded on a config refresh. That file is only written from the env var at container boot by entrypoint.sh:29-35. Crucially, when a user edits their default template, TemplateDetailView.patch (views.py:495-513) explicitly fires update_tenant_config_task with the comment 'Push updated skill config to the agent's container when the default template changes' — but that refresh writes to the share without redeploying the container and silently skips templates.md, so the stated intent no-ops. The running container keeps its provision-time env var and the share keeps the stale slug list. After a section rename/add/remove, the agent's templates.md reference (the canonical slug source the prompt points it at, personas.py:311 and 354) is stale, so nbhd_daily_note_set_section calls using those slugs can write to the wrong section or error. Impact is real but bounded: it requires a post-provision default-template edit (uncommon) and the failure is a wrong-section write or tool error, not data loss — hence medium, not high.

user_story: As a system, I want a cross-pillar agenda section in USER.md that shows open-thread counts and untouched feature introductions so that any proactive cron can naturally weave in a feature intro when relevant.
expected_behaviour: Registered via @register_section(key='agenda', order=5) in apps/orchestrator/agenda_envelope.py. Enabled for all tenants (lambda t: True). Renders: open task count, open goal count, planned workout count, active payoff plans, and 'untouched introductions' (features where fuel_enabled or finance_active but welcomes_sent[feature] is null). Includes agent guidance: 'read first, weave selectively, never enumerate'. Contributes to both USER.md and context_digest. Triggered by post_save/post_delete on Task, Goal, Workout, PayoffPlan, AgendaEngagement models.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (Task, Goal, Workout, PayoffPlan, AgendaEngagement, Tenant.welcomes_sent, Tenant.fuel_enabled, Tenant.finance_active)
inventory_notes: Phase A only — engagement-aware priority (last_surfaced_at) is deferred to Phase B. 'Untouched intro' detection uses welcomes_sent[feature] is null, not a live cron check.
error_detail: Confirmed. The agenda refresh_on tuple omits the typed Goal model (agenda_envelope.py:92) but _count_active_goals reads Goal.objects.filter(status=ACTIVE).count() (line 283). No post_save on Goal triggers a USER.md refresh: the Goal receiver (journal/signals.py:181) only auto-resolves PendingExtractions, and goal write paths (lifecycle_views.py serializer.save / goal.mark_achieved) never call push_user_md. So a Goal create/achieve/abandon via API does not refresh the agenda count. The same gap exists in the journal's own goals summary (journal/envelope.py:147, refresh_on=(Document,)) which also counts typed Goals, so this is a pre-existing class issue, not unique to the agenda section. There is also an internal inconsistency: the fleet-refresh docstring (tasks.py:831) claims signal-driven refreshes cover goal saves, but they do not for typed Goal rows. Impact is minor and bounded: staleness closes on the next Document/Workout/FuelGoal/PayoffPlan write or the hourly refresh_user_md_fleet cron (tasks.py:826, ~1h for active tenants). The count is only a synthesis hint; the rendered goals line directs the agent to call nbhd_goal_list for live state and the AGENTS.md reconcile gate forces nbhd_reconcile_scan before any goal claim, so the live source of truth is the tool path, not the bootstrap count. The AgendaEngagement sub-claim is weaker (engagement rows are written by the same proactive crons that push USER.md). Net: a real but minor freshness gap in the bootstrap envelope, not a user-felt correctness bug.

feature: DeBERTa + Presidio PII Detection Engine (lazy singleton)
user_story: As a system, I want a shared lazy-loaded NER pipeline so that PII is detected without reloading the model on every request.
expected_behaviour: On first call to get_pii_pipeline(), loads the DeBERTa-v3-base model lakshyakh93/deberta_finetuned_pii (554 MB) from PII_MODEL_PATH env var (default: pii-model/ in project root; Docker: /app/pii-model). Falls back to HuggingFace download if the local path is absent. Uses vanilla PyTorch CPU inference with aggregation_strategy=simple. On load failure, caches the exception and re-raises on all subsequent calls (no retry storm). Separately, get_pattern_recognizers() initialises Presidio CreditCardRecognizer (Luhn), IbanRecognizer (checksum), and EmailRecognizer (regex) as a second singleton. Both singletons are process-level globals; a container restart is required to retry a failed model load. If DeBERTa fails, Presidio pattern recognizers still run independently.
dependencies: HuggingFace Hub (auto-download fallback), transformers library, presidio-analyzer library, PyTorch CPU
inventory_notes: USERNAME label intentionally omitted from DEBERTA_LABEL_MAP due to high false-positive rate. DATE and SSN labels also deliberately excluded. Prior ONNX/optimum path abandoned due to determinism and ABI issues (issue #695). Load error is cached: once it fails the process will never retry the model until container restarts.

user_story: As a system, I want tier-based redaction policies so that PII detection entities and score thresholds can be configured per subscriber tier.
expected_behaviour: Defined in apps/pii/config.py. Currently only a 'starter' policy is defined with enabled=true and score_threshold=0.5, covering PERSON, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PHONE_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD, IBAN_CODE, LOCATION, DATE_OF_BIRTH, PASSWORD, IP_ADDRESS, ID_DOCUMENT, ACCOUNT, CRYPTO_ADDRESS. All unknown tier strings fall back to 'starter' via TIER_POLICIES.get(tier, starter). The DEBERTA_LABEL_MAP maps ~60 model labels down to these canonical entity types, collapsing FIRSTNAME/MIDDLENAME/LASTNAME/FULLNAME/NAME/PREFIX/SUFFIX/DISPLAYNAME/ACCOUNTNAME all to PERSON. Score threshold calibrated to 0.5 for this model (prior model used 0.7).
inventory_notes: Historical premium-tier (financial-only) and BYOK-off split not implemented. All tiers use starter policy. The 0.5 threshold is intentionally lower than the prior 0.7 because the DeBERTa model's softmax distribution sits lower across the board.

user_story: As a system, I want to redact PII from an arbitrary text string so that PII is removed before the text is used downstream.
expected_behaviour: redact_text(text, tenant=None, tier=None, allow_names=None) resolves the tier from the tenant's model_tier or defaults to 'starter'. Checks the tier policy; if disabled or no entities configured, returns text unchanged. Calls _detect_pii which runs DeBERTa + Presidio, merges adjacent same-type spans, deduplicates overlaps (higher score wins; shorter span on tie), filters out allow_names (tenant's own display name, first name, last name), filters denylisted spans, assigns numbered placeholders (e.g., [PERSON_1]), and applies replacements end-to-start to preserve offsets. Returns redacted text only; does not persist entity map. On any exception, logs and returns original text unchanged.
inventory_notes: Does not persist the entity map to DB. Use RedactionSession for cross-document work or redact_user_message for inbound message path. Empty/whitespace-only text is returned unchanged.

user_story: As a system, I want consistent entity numbering across multiple documents for the same tenant so that the same person always maps to the same placeholder.
expected_behaviour: RedactionSession(tenant, tier, allow_names) seeds _type_counters from existing pii_entity_map so new mints never clobber existing numbered placeholders. Seeds _inverted_ci (case-insensitive canonical-key to (display_name, placeholder) mapping) from the existing map so known entities reuse their existing placeholder. Seeds _denylist from tenant.pii_denylist. Each call to session.redact(text) mutates _type_counters, entity_map (new mints only), and _inverted_ci in place, enabling deduplication across calls. The session's entity_map contains only new mints from this session; callers union it onto the tenant map. On any exception per text, logs and returns original text unchanged.
inventory_notes: Used by workspace memory sync (apps/orchestrator/memory_sync.py) to redact journal documents before upload to Azure File Share. Also used by lessons/copilot.py for lesson content redaction. Counter seeding fix prevents the latent clobber bug where [PERSON_1] could be minted fresh and override an existing mapping.

user_story: As a system, I want to redact PII in a user's inbound message before forwarding to OpenClaw so that third-party LLM providers never see real personal data.
expected_behaviour: redact_user_message(text, tenant, allow_user_name=True) runs a two-pass redaction. Pass 1: replaces known entities from the existing pii_entity_map using case-insensitive regex (longest names first, skipping empty originals, skipping denylisted entries). Pass 2: runs NER + Presidio on the partially-redacted output to detect new entities, drops hits that fall inside existing placeholder ranges to prevent nested placeholder corruption, resolves new entities against the CI-inverted map, and mints new numbered placeholders for genuinely new entities. New entities are persisted to the DB via Tenant.objects.filter(pk=tenant.pk).update(pii_entity_map=merged) and also updated in-memory. allow_user_name=True (default) excludes the tenant's own display name, first name, and last name from redaction; set False for tool responses so user's surname is not mixed with contact placeholders. On any exception logs and returns original text.
inventory_notes: Telemetry: logs pii_mint events with tenant_id, entity type, placeholder, score, and truncated span text for future threshold tuning. The case-insensitive collision fix (inverted_names_ci) prevents the canary bug where 'sautai'/'Sautai'/'SAUTAI' each minted a fresh placeholder.
error_detail: Confirmed. redact_user_message (and its dict wrapper redact_telegram_update) is the designated seam for stripping PII from a user's inbound message before forwarding to OpenClaw / third-party LLM providers (redactor.py docstring: PII redaction for outgoing LLM provider traffic), but it has ZERO production call sites. Grep across all non-test Python shows redact_user_message is invoked only inside redactor.py itself, by redact_telegram_update and redact_tool_response; redact_telegram_update itself has no caller at all. All three inbound channels forward raw user text unredacted: (1) Telegram poller._forward_to_container (poller.py:1281-1380) builds message_text = proactive+datetime+chat-marker + raw user text and enqueues it; (2) LINE line_webhook.py:1249 enqueues raw user text; (3) iOS chat_views.py:217 uses raw text directly. The drain path (pending_queue.py:1186,1266) POSTs payload.message_text verbatim to /v1/chat/completions. The only PII calls on these paths are OUTBOUND rehydration (poller.py:329, line_webhook.py:657, pending_queue.py:1514/1561), the reverse operation. The OpenClaw redactPatterns config (config_generator.py:1758+) only masks values in tool-error stderr/log lines, not the LLM message body. Note the irony: tool responses ARE redacted at 4 sites (FA-0915), and pending_queue.py:361's own docstring claims webhooks do "PII redaction... BEFORE enqueuing" — but user-typed message content is not redacted. User impact: every name, email, phone, or other PII a subscriber types in Telegram/LINE/iOS is sent unredacted to third-party model providers, defeating the stated privacy guarantee that providers never see real personal data. The placeholder/rehydrate machinery is only ever fed by tool-response redaction, never by inbound user text.

user_story: As a system, I want to redact PII from incoming Telegram webhook update dicts so that the complete Telegram payload forwarded to OpenClaw has PII removed.
expected_behaviour: redact_telegram_update(update_dict, tenant) modifies the update dict in place and returns it. Redacts text from update['message']['text'], update['edited_message']['text'], and update['callback_query']['message']['text'] if those keys are present. Each string is processed via redact_user_message(text, tenant). Non-text fields (IDs, from, chat objects, etc.) are not touched. Returns the mutated dict.
inventory_notes: Currently not called from the Telegram poller or webhook paths based on grep - the function exists but no external call site was found in router/poller.py or router/pending_queue.py. Tool responses use redact_tool_response instead.
error_detail: Confirmed real. redact_telegram_update (apps/pii/redactor.py:421-438) has zero production call sites: grep finds it only in its own definition and apps/pii/tests.py. The Telegram poller inbound path (apps/router/poller.py:_forward_to_container, lines 1281-1370) accumulates context markers onto the raw message_text and enqueues it via enqueue_message_for_tenant with NO redaction; the drain task in pending_queue just POSTs the prepared payload to /v1/chat/completions. The defect is actually broader than the claim: redact_user_message AND redact_text also have ZERO production callers (verified by grep over apps/ excluding apps/pii/ and tests). The entire INBOUND user-message PII redaction layer is unwired across ALL chat channels (Telegram, LINE, iOS), not just Telegram. The system is asymmetric: outbound rehydration (rehydrate_text/rehydrate_for_tenant) IS wired everywhere (poller.py:329, pending_queue.py:1517/1564, line_webhook.py:657), tool responses ARE redacted (redact_tool_response at integrations/runtime_views.py:929/1009/1091/3494), and memory_sync/lessons redact via RedactionSession — but nothing mints [PERSON_N] placeholders from the user's own typed chat messages. User impact: raw personal data (names, emails, phone numbers, addresses) a subscriber types into chat is forwarded verbatim to the third-party LLM provider, defeating the stated purpose of the PII subsystem ('PII redaction for outgoing LLM provider traffic', commit #117). The pending_queue.py:361 docstring claiming callers do 'PII redaction ... BEFORE enqueuing' is stale/aspirational — no caller does. The inventory note ('Currently not called') corroborates the gap.

user_story: As a system, I want to redact PII from integration tool responses (Gmail, Calendar, etc.) before they are returned to OpenClaw so that third-party LLM providers never see raw contact data.
expected_behaviour: redact_tool_response(data, tenant) recursively walks a JSON dict/list structure. String values are passed through redact_user_message with allow_user_name=False so the user's own name is also redacted (prevents surname mixing with contact placeholders). Keys in _TOOL_SKIP_KEYS (id, thread_id, html_link, internal_date, date, status, next_page_token, result_size_estimate, provider, tenant_id, label_ids, start, end, message_id, update_id) have their values left untouched. If the tier policy has PII disabled, returns data unchanged. On any exception logs and returns original data. Called at four integration runtime_views endpoints for Gmail list, Gmail detail, Calendar, and people/contacts lookups.
entry_points: apps/pii/redactor.py:redact_tool_response ; apps/pii/redactor.py:_redact_tool_value ; apps/integrations/runtime_views.py:927 ; apps/integrations/runtime_views.py:1007 ; apps/integrations/runtime_views.py:1089 ; apps/integrations/runtime_views.py:3492
inventory_notes: allow_user_name=False means even the subscriber's own name is redacted in tool responses. This is intentional to prevent the model from reconstructing [PERSON_1]'s surname by cross-referencing with the user's display name in the same response.

feature: PII placeholder rehydration for outbound text (rehydrate_text / rehydrate_for_tenant)
user_story: As a system, I want to restore real names from PII placeholders in AI-generated outbound text before delivery so that subscribers see real names rather than [PERSON_1] tokens.
expected_behaviour: rehydrate_text(text, entity_map) replaces all [TYPE_N] placeholders found in text by looking up the placeholder in entity_map, extracting the name via entity_registry.get_name() (handles both legacy string shape and new dict shape). Unknown placeholders are left as-is. Uses a fast path: if '[' is not in text, returns immediately. rehydrate_for_tenant(tenant, text) is the single-seam convenience wrapper that fetches tenant.pii_entity_map and delegates to rehydrate_text; safe on None tenant, absent map, or empty text. Called in: pending_queue drain for iOS and Telegram/LINE channels, LINE webhook outbound path, Telegram poller outbound path, conversation_capture, journal extraction, core services, actions/messaging (action display_summary), lessons/notifications and copilot (lesson text, chart points).
entry_points: apps/pii/redactor.py:rehydrate_text ; apps/pii/redactor.py:rehydrate_for_tenant ; apps/router/pending_queue.py:1514-1568 ; apps/router/line_webhook.py:657 ; apps/router/poller.py:329 ; apps/journal/extraction.py:302-411 ; apps/core/services.py:413 ; apps/actions/messaging.py:42-273 ; apps/lessons/notifications.py:41-110 ; apps/lessons/copilot.py:543-548
inventory_notes: Rehydration does NOT consult the denylist; if an entry is in entity_map (even if denylisted), its placeholder still rehydrates. This is intentional: denylisting stops future minting but preserves historical references in workspace files and chat history.

feature: Workspace memory sync redaction (RedactionSession in memory_sync)
user_story: As a system, I want journal documents redacted before being written to the Azure File Share so that OpenClaw never reads raw PII from the workspace.
expected_behaviour: In apps/orchestrator/memory_sync.py:render_memory_files(), a RedactionSession is created seeded from tenant.pii_entity_map. Each journal document's rendered markdown (title + markdown body) is run through session.redact() before being added to the files dict. After all documents are processed, if session.entity_map has new mints, a DB read-then-update merges them into Tenant.pii_entity_map (workspace entities override; prior message-originated entities are preserved). The upload itself happens separately via upload_memory_files_to_share(). Documents with invalid kind/slug path components are skipped.
inventory_notes: SQLite on the Azure File Share is disabled fleet-wide due to SMB fsync issues; search routes through Postgres. The session seeds counter and inverted_ci from the existing map to avoid clobbering [PERSON_1] with a new entity during sync.

user_story: As a system, I want the privacy placeholder rules injected into the agent's USER.md context so that OpenClaw always knows to preserve [TYPE_N] placeholders verbatim in replies.
expected_behaviour: apps/tenants/envelope.py registers a 'privacy_placeholders' section (order=12, high priority) that is included in USER.md only when tenant.pii_entity_map is non-empty. The section body instructs OpenClaw to preserve placeholders exactly, never guess real values, treat placeholders as the real values when reasoning, and not mention them to the user. If any entity_map entries have non-empty 'relationship' or 'notes' metadata (new dict shape), an '### Identity context' sub-section is appended listing those entries as '- `[PERSON_1]` -- daughter -- age 4.5' lines so the agent can disambiguate pronouns without seeing real names. Legacy string-only entries contribute no identity context. The section refreshes via the normal USER.md push triggered by any other registered contributor.
inventory_notes: order=12 promotes this section near the top of USER.md so it survives any future budget pressure that might truncate the file. Prior placement at order=70 caused silent truncation on tenants with large USER.md, resulting in the agent emitting raw [PERSON_X] tokens to users.

feature: Entity registry CRUD API - List entries
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to view all detected PII placeholders and their metadata so that I can review and correct my People settings.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/tenants/settings/entity-registry/ requires IsAuthenticated. Resolves the caller's own tenant via request.user.tenant; returns 404 if no tenant found. Iterates pii_entity_map via entity_registry.iter_normalized() which coerces legacy string entries to dict shape on read. Returns 200 with {entries: [{placeholder, name, relationship, notes, updated_at}]} sorted by placeholder key. Cross-tenant access is impossible by construction since the view always uses request.user.tenant.
inventory_notes: Legacy string entries (e.g. '[PERSON_1]': 'Alice') are coerced to {name: 'Alice', relationship: '', notes: '', updated_at: null} on read. The underlying storage is NOT rewritten; coercion happens only on the wire format.

feature: Entity registry CRUD API - Patch entry
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to correct the name, relationship, or notes on a PII placeholder so that the agent uses accurate identity context and outbound rehydration is correct.
expected_behaviour: PATCH /api/v1/tenants/settings/entity-registry/<placeholder>/ requires IsAuthenticated. Resolves tenant via request.user.tenant. Returns 404 if placeholder not in pii_entity_map or no tenant. Accepts body fields name (max 200 chars), relationship (max 80 chars), notes (max 500 chars) individually or in any combination; omitted fields are unchanged. Null value clears the field. Non-string values return 400. Oversized values return 400. On success, builds canonical storage value via to_storage_value() (drops empty optional fields to keep JSON compact), stamps updated_at with current ISO timestamp, writes back via Tenant.objects.filter(pk=pk).update(pii_entity_map=...) and updates in-memory. Returns 200 with {placeholder, name, relationship, notes, updated_at}.
inventory_notes: Placeholder must already exist in entity_map (the redactor mints it; this endpoint only edits). Patching name corrects future rehydration for all outbound text. If arbiter_judged_at is on the existing entry, it is preserved through the coerce/to_storage_value round-trip since arbiter_judged_at is in _KNOWN_FIELDS.
error_detail: Confirmed. PATCH coerces the entry (views.py:1086), which preserves arbiter_judged_at via _KNOWN_FIELDS (entity_registry.py:45), then rebuilds the stored value via to_storage_value() (views.py:1122-1127). to_storage_value (entity_registry.py:98-116) only accepts name/relationship/notes/updated_at, so the stamp is dropped from the written entry. On the next hourly arbiter sweep, _entries_to_judge no longer sees the stamp (arbiter.py:118-119) and re-sends the entry to the LLM. The inventory note claiming the stamp survives the coerce/to_storage_value round-trip is incorrect. Impact is minor: it only fires when a user manually edits (via People settings) an entry the arbiter had confirmed is_pii=true; denied entries are skipped by the denylist check (arbiter.py:123) regardless of the stamp. Cost is one extra batched Haiku judgment for that entry on the next sweep (fractions of a cent, batched 50/call), and it self-corrects because the re-judgment re-stamps it (arbiter.py:274). No data corruption, no PII leak, no user-visible breakage. The redactor's to_storage_value use (redactor.py:388) only mints brand-new never-judged placeholders, so it is unaffected.

feature: Entity registry CRUD API - Delete entry
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to delete a PII placeholder entry so that a false positive or stale mapping is removed from my entity registry.
expected_behaviour: DELETE /api/v1/tenants/settings/entity-registry/<placeholder>/ requires IsAuthenticated. Returns 404 if placeholder not in entity_map or no tenant. On success, removes the key from entity_map, persists via Tenant.objects.filter(pk=pk).update(), and returns 204 No Content. Other entries in the map are preserved. Note: after deletion, historical placeholder references in workspace files or chat history will no longer rehydrate (the real name will not be restored) since the mapping is gone.
inventory_notes: Deletion permanently breaks rehydration for the deleted placeholder. Unlike denylisting, which preserves the mapping while stopping future redaction, deletion removes the mapping entirely. There is no soft-delete or undo.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to view my PII false-positive denylist so that I can see which words have been excluded from redaction.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/tenants/settings/pii-denylist/ requires IsAuthenticated. Resolves tenant via request.user.tenant; 404 if absent. Reads pii_denylist JSONField (shape: {canonical_key: {reason, decided_at}}). Returns 200 with {entries: [{key, reason, decided_at}]} sorted by key. Entries without decided_at surface it as null. Entries with non-dict metadata (legacy) treat meta as {}.

feature: PII denylist API - Add single entry
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to add a word to my PII denylist so that the redactor stops treating it as PII in future messages.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/tenants/settings/pii-denylist/ requires IsAuthenticated. Body must have {name: string}. Validates: must be a string, non-empty after strip, max 200 chars, must canonicalize to non-empty via normalize_denylist_key() (casefold + strip). Returns 400 on any validation failure. Canonicalizes the name and writes {canonical_key: {reason: 'manual', decided_at: <now ISO>}} to pii_denylist. Overwrites existing entry with fresh decided_at (idempotent re-add). Returns 201 with {key, reason, decided_at}. Side effect: on future messages, the redactor's Step 1 regex pass skips the entity_map entry whose name matches this key, and the post-NER filter drops detections whose text canonicalizes to this key.
inventory_notes: Denylisting does NOT remove the entity_map entry. Rehydration of historical placeholder references in workspace files still works. The denylist only stops new redaction from firing.

feature: PII denylist API - Delete single entry
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to remove a word from my PII denylist so that redaction is re-enabled for it in future messages.
expected_behaviour: DELETE /api/v1/tenants/settings/pii-denylist/<key>/ requires IsAuthenticated. The <key> path parameter must be the canonical key (casefolded). Returns 404 if key not in denylist or no tenant. On success, removes key from denylist, persists via .update(), returns 204 No Content. Side effect: the removed key's corresponding entity_map entries resume driving Step 1 regex redaction on the next inbound message.
inventory_notes: The path parameter must be the exact canonical key (e.g., 'goal' not 'Goal'). There is no endpoint to look up the canonical form from a display name; callers must use the key from the GET list endpoint.

feature: PII denylist API - Bulk add entries
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to add many words to my denylist in a single request so that I can quickly clear a large batch of false positives without sequential round-trips.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/tenants/settings/pii-denylist/bulk/ requires IsAuthenticated. Body must have {names: [string]}. Returns 400 if names is not a list. Returns 400 if len(names) > 1000. Processes each name: non-strings are skipped with reason 'not a string', empty-after-strip are skipped with reason 'empty', names over 200 chars are skipped with reason 'exceeds max length N', names that canonicalize to empty are skipped with reason 'canonicalizes to empty'. Valid names are written to pii_denylist in a single JSONField update (atomic). Returns 200 with {added: [canonical_keys], skipped: [{name, reason}]}. Existing entries are merged (not replaced); existing unrelated entries are preserved. Re-adding an existing key refreshes its decided_at.
inventory_notes: The 'bulk/' path MUST be registered before '<str:key>/' in urls.py (declaration order matters in Django) so the string path doesn't capture 'bulk' as a key and return 405. URL ordering is documented in tenants/urls.py.

feature: LLM arbiter cron - hourly PII false-positive sweep
user_story: As a system, I want an hourly LLM judgment sweep so that NER false positives (common words tagged as PERSON/LOCATION) are automatically moved to the denylist without user intervention.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/cron/trigger/pii_arbiter/ is called by QStash on schedule '40 * * * *' (40 minutes past every hour). Requires valid QStash signature (401 if invalid). Calls pii_arbiter_task() which: (1) queries up to 200 Tenants with non-empty pii_entity_map; (2) for each tenant, collects entries needing judgment (PERSON_* and LOCATION_* placeholder types only, not EMAIL/PHONE/CREDIT_CARD/IBAN which come from deterministic recognizers; skips already-stamped arbiter_judged_at entries, denylist entries, empty names, duplicate canonical keys); (3) batches up to 50 entries per LLM call to Claude Haiku 4.5 (via OpenRouter), falls back to DeepSeek on failure; (4) Haiku returns {decisions: [{id: int, is_pii: bool}]}; (5) for is_pii=false entries, writes canonical_key to pii_denylist with {reason: 'arbiter', decided_at: now}; for is_pii=true entries, stamps arbiter_judged_at on entity_map entries; (6) all duplicate placeholders sharing a canonical key get stamped in one pass; (7) records LLM token usage via billing.services.record_usage with is_system=True; (8) returns summary dict. Idempotent: already-stamped/denylisted entries are skipped on re-runs.
dependencies: OpenRouter API (Claude Haiku 4.5 primary, DeepSeek fallback), apps/billing/services.py:record_usage, apps/tenants/models.py:Tenant.pii_entity_map, apps/tenants/models.py:Tenant.pii_denylist, QStash (scheduling)
inventory_notes: Arbiter never default-denies: if the LLM call fails or returns malformed JSON, the function defers (no denylist writes) and skips that batch until the next tick. Integer IDs are used in the payload (not name strings) to avoid Unicode normalization causing the model to echo back different apostrophes/dashes than the input. The entity_map row is never deleted by this task. Max 200 tenants and 200 entries per tenant per run cap prevents runaway billing.

feature: Entity registry data model and storage (Tenant.pii_entity_map)
user_story: As a system, I want a per-tenant JSONField to store PII placeholder mappings so that redaction and rehydration are consistent across sessions.
expected_behaviour: Tenant.pii_entity_map is a JSONField (default=dict, blank=True) on the Tenant model. Shape: {[TYPE_N]: entry} where entry is either the legacy string shape (e.g. 'Alice') or the new dict shape ({name, relationship?, notes?, updated_at?, arbiter_judged_at?}). Reads are coerced on-the-fly by entity_registry.coerce(). Writes always use the new dict shape going forward (to_storage_value). The entity_registry module provides: coerce(), get_name(), get_metadata(), to_storage_value(), iter_normalized(), inverted_names(), inverted_names_ci() (case-insensitive lookup, lowest-numbered placeholder wins on duplicates), canonical_key() (casefold+strip), is_denied() (checks pii_denylist). Migration: 0034_pii_entity_map (tenants app).
inventory_notes: Legacy string entries coerce opportunistically as redaction fires; no explicit bulk migration needed. The inverted_names_ci() function resolves the 2026-05-21 canary bug where a 826-entry map had 'sautai' stored 59 times under different placeholders due to case-sensitive merge. Lowest-numbered placeholder wins ties in CI lookup.

user_story: As a system, I want a per-tenant JSONField for canonical-keyed false-positive suppressions so that the redactor can skip user-flagged non-PII words.
expected_behaviour: Tenant.pii_denylist is a JSONField (default=dict, blank=True) on the Tenant model. Shape: {canonical_key: {reason?: 'manual'|'arbiter', decided_at?: ISO_string}}. Lookup is via entity_registry.is_denied(denylist, name) which calls canonical_key(name) and checks membership; case/whitespace variants all match the same key. Used by: redact_user_message Step 1 (skips regex replacement for denylisted entity_map entries), _filter_results (drops NER detections whose text maps to a denylisted key), RedactionSession (seeds _denylist from tenant). The denylist does NOT affect rehydrate_text; historical placeholders still restore. Migration: 0068_pii_denylist (tenants app).
inventory_notes: The pii_denylist is consulted by both RedactionSession (workspace sync) and redact_user_message (inbound messages) so the same suppression fires on all code paths. Arbiter writes use reason='arbiter'; manual UI writes use reason='manual'.

feature: Span deduplication and adjacent merge in detection pipeline
user_story: As a system, I want adjacent same-type NER spans merged and overlapping spans deduplicated so that 'Sarah Chen' becomes one [PERSON_1] instead of two placeholders.
expected_behaviour: _merge_adjacent_spans() sorts results by start position and merges consecutive spans of the same entity_type if they are separated by 0 or 1 characters (a space). The merged span uses the minimum score of its components. This handles GIVENNAME + LASTNAME both mapping to PERSON. _deduplicate_overlapping() then removes overlapping spans: keeps higher-score match; on score tie, keeps shorter (more specific) span. Applied before filtering in _detect_pii. The DeBERTa model's span boundaries may include trailing/leading whitespace from aggregation_strategy='simple'; these are trimmed before recording the span. Spans where start >= end after trimming are discarded.
inventory_notes: The overlap deduplication prevents PERSON 'Email bob@test.com' (longer, lower score) from competing with EMAIL_ADDRESS 'bob@test.com' (shorter, higher score from Presidio). Shorter/higher-score wins.
error_detail: Confirmed but minor. DATE_OF_BIRTH is listed in TIER_POLICIES['starter']['entities'] (config.py:94) as a protected type, yet no detection pathway can ever emit it: DEBERTA_LABEL_MAP (config.py:16-82) maps no model label to DATE_OF_BIRTH (the model's DATE label is deliberately dropped, commented out at config.py:73-76), and get_pattern_recognizers (engine.py:104-114) only ever produces CREDIT_CARD, IBAN_CODE, and EMAIL_ADDRESS. In _detect_pii, model spans are only emitted when DEBERTA_LABEL_MAP.get(...) returns a value that is also in the entities list (redactor.py:545-546), and Presidio spans are gated the same way (redactor.py:570-572). So DATE_OF_BIRTH is unreachable. User impact is small: the entities list is a whitelist/filter, so a dangling entry is a no-op (no crash, no mis-redaction, no wrong behaviour). The only real effect is that birth dates are silently never redacted while the starter policy advertises DATE_OF_BIRTH coverage. Crucially, config.py:73-76 documents this as an intentional tradeoff (the model fires DATE on every yyyy-mm-dd journal heading, producing a false-positive flood), so the missing detection is by design; what remains is a stale/misleading config entry implying coverage that was deliberately removed. The merge/dedup logic (_merge_adjacent_spans, _deduplicate_overlapping) is correct, as the report itself notes.

feature: Placeholder corruption guard (NER hits inside existing placeholders)
user_story: As a system, I want NER hits that overlap existing placeholder tokens to be dropped so that [EMAIL_ADDRESS_1] is never corrupted into [[PERSON_1]] by a second pass.
expected_behaviour: _hit_inside_placeholder(hit, ranges) returns True when a detected span overlaps any existing placeholder's character range. In redact_user_message (the two-pass path), after Step 1 places placeholders in the text, Step 2 runs NER on the partially-redacted string. Before applying new replacements, placeholder ranges are extracted via regex _PLACEHOLDER_RE.finditer() and any hit whose [start,end) overlaps a placeholder range is discarded. This prevents the DeBERTa model from classifying tokens inside '[EMAIL_ADDRESS_1]' as PERSON and nesting them into '[[PERSON_2]]'.
inventory_notes: Only needed in the two-pass redact_user_message path (Step 1 partially redacts, Step 2 runs NER on partially-redacted output). The one-pass RedactionSession path does not have this issue since it does not inject placeholders before running NER.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container runtime, I want to report platform issues (tool errors, missing capabilities, config problems, rate limits, auth errors) to the Django control plane so that operators can see per-tenant operational failures and resolve them.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/integrations/runtime/<tenant_id>/platform-issue/report/ — auth via `X-NBHD-Internal-Key` + `X-NBHD-Tenant-Id` headers, validated by `validate_internal_runtime_request` (dual per-tenant key / legacy global key with constant-time compare). Returns 401 on auth failure or tenant_id mismatch. Returns 404 if the tenant UUID is not found in the DB. Validates request body via `ReportIssueSerializer`: `summary` (required, max 500 chars), `category` (choice from: missing_capability, tool_error, config_issue, rate_limit, auth_error, other; default=other), `severity` (choice from: low, medium, high, critical; default=low), `tool_name` (optional, max 100 chars), `detail` (optional, max 2000 chars, must not contain PII). Returns 400 on serializer validation failure. Enforces a per-tenant rate limit of 10 reports per rolling hour window (counts existing `PlatformIssueLog` rows for that tenant in the past 60 minutes); returns 429 with message 'Rate limit exceeded. Max 10 reports per hour.' if exceeded. If `tool_name` is present, performs dedup: if a `PlatformIssueLog` for the same tenant + category + tool_name exists within the past 60 minutes, returns 200 with `{deduplicated: true}` and does NOT create a new row. If dedup does not apply (no tool_name, or no matching recent row), creates a `PlatformIssueLog` row and returns 201 with `{id: <uuid>, status: 'logged'}`. No dedup occurs when `tool_name` is absent — multiple identical reports without tool_name each create a separate row.
