Sets RLS context (`set_rls_context(tenant_id, service_role=True)`) after successful auth. Side effect: logs via Django logger at INFO with tenant_id, category, tool_name, and first 100 chars of summary.
inventory_notes: The platform_logs/urls.py defines a second URL pattern (<tenant_id>/report/) that is NOT mounted in config/urls.py — only the re-registration in apps/integrations/urls.py is live. Dedup is only active when tool_name is non-empty; omitting tool_name bypasses dedup entirely (both the first and subsequent identical reports create rows). The resolved and resolved_at fields on PlatformIssueLog have no write endpoint — they are only settable via Django admin. The rate limit counts ALL categories/severities together per tenant, not per category. `detail` field has a model-level max of TextField (no char limit) but serializer enforces max_length=2000.

user_story: As an operator, I want to view, filter, search, and resolve platform issue logs in the Django admin so that I can track and triage operational problems across all tenants.
expected_behaviour: Registered at /admin/platform_logs/platformissuelog/ via `PlatformIssueLogAdmin`. List view displays columns: tenant, category, severity, tool_name, summary_short (first 80 chars), resolved, created_at. List is filterable by category, severity, resolved, and created_at (date hierarchy). Search runs across summary, detail, and tool_name fields. The `resolved` boolean column is editable inline in the list view (`list_editable`). Fields `id` and `created_at` are read-only. The `resolved_at` field is writable in the detail form but not auto-populated on resolve — operator must set it manually. Ordering is always by `-created_at` (newest first, from model Meta). No custom actions or bulk-resolution logic beyond Django admin defaults. Access requires Django admin session auth with staff/superuser privileges.
inventory_notes: resolved_at is nullable and not automatically set when resolved is flipped to True — operators must manually fill it in the detail form. There is no signal or hook that triggers on resolution. The summary_short method truncates to 80 chars for the list column but the full 500-char summary is visible in the detail view.

user_story: As the platform, I want every internal runtime authentication attempt to be audit-logged with key_provenance and outcome so that operators can observe the fleet migration from legacy global keys to per-tenant keys and detect auth anomalies.
expected_behaviour: Every call to `validate_internal_runtime_request` (the chokepoint used by PlatformIssueReportView and all other runtime endpoints) emits an INFO-level log via the `nbhd.internal_auth` logger with structured fields: event, provided_tenant_id, expected_tenant_id, key_provenance (per_tenant | legacy_global | none), and outcome (success | bad_key | tenant_mismatch | missing_key | missing_tenant | no_key_configured). The fields are baked into the log MESSAGE string (not only in `extra`) so Azure Log Analytics plain-text formatter captures them. Both successes and failures are logged. The per-tenant key is resolved via a DB query (`Tenant.objects.only('internal_api_key').get(id=tenant_id)`) and compared via `secrets.compare_digest` (constant-time). If per-tenant key is absent or empty, falls back to `settings.NBHD_INTERNAL_API_KEY` global key. If neither matches, raises `InternalAuthError` which the calling view converts to HTTP 401.
dependencies: apps/tenants/models.py:Tenant (internal_api_key field), Django settings (NBHD_INTERNAL_API_KEY), Azure Log Analytics (log destination)
inventory_notes: This is not a platform_logs model write — it writes to the Django logger, not PlatformIssueLog. The Phase 1d migration gate requires observing zero key_provenance=legacy_global hits for 48h before removing the global key fallback. Non-UUID tenant_ids (e.g. test strings) return None from _lookup_tenant_key (ValidationError caught), causing fallback to global key without crashing.

user_story: As the platform, I want platform issue events persisted in Postgres with tenant FK, category, severity, tool_name, summary, detail, and resolution state so that the full history of operational issues is queryable and retainable.
expected_behaviour: Table `platform_issue_logs` in Postgres. Primary key is UUID (auto-generated). FK to Tenant with CASCADE delete — deleting a tenant purges all its issue logs. Fields: category (TextChoices: missing_capability, tool_error, config_issue, rate_limit, auth_error, other; default=other), severity (TextChoices: low, medium, high, critical; default=low), tool_name (varchar 100, blank allowed), summary (varchar 500, required), detail (TextField, blank allowed, intended to be PII-free), resolved (boolean, default=False), resolved_at (nullable datetime, not auto-set), created_at (auto_now_add). Default ordering is -created_at. Two DB indexes: composite (tenant, -created_at) for per-tenant time-ordered queries; composite (category, resolved) for admin filtering by category+resolution state.
inventory_notes: resolved_at has no auto-population logic — it remains NULL even after resolved=True is set. The detail field has no DB length constraint (TextField) but the API serializer caps it at 2000 chars. No signals are connected to PlatformIssueLog. No scheduled cleanup/purge job exists for old records.

feature: Send iOS/web chat turn (enqueue to OpenClaw)
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to send a chat message from the iOS/web app so that OpenClaw processes it and I can poll for the reply.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/chat/messages/ requires JWT auth. Body: {text, client_msg_id (optional), thread_id (optional)}. text is validated: empty→400, >8000 chars→400. client_msg_id max 64 chars→400 if exceeded; auto-generated UUID hex if absent. Idempotency: if client_msg_id already exists for the tenant, returns the existing AppChatMessage with HTTP 200 (no re-enqueue). Budget gate via check_budget: over-budget tenant creates an AppChatMessage with status=error, error='budget_exhausted', returns 200 (not created=True). Thread resolved from thread_id or defaults to the shared main thread (get_or_create race-safe); invalid thread_id→404. A fresh PendingMessage (channel=ios) is created, user text is decorated with datetime context + chat-context marker, then enqueued to QStash drain task. Returns 201 on new turn, 200 on replay. tenant.first_message_at stamped on first-ever message (conditional UPDATE, race-safe). Cache-Control: no-store on all responses.
dependencies: QStash (drain task scheduling), Postgres (AppChatMessage, PendingMessage, ChatThread), apps/billing/services:check_budget
inventory_notes: Idempotency check runs BEFORE thread validation: a retry with same client_msg_id but stale thread_id still replays 200. Budget-exhausted turns are recorded as ERROR immediately; no container is woken.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to poll a pending chat turn so that I can display the assistant's reply when it lands.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/chat/messages/<client_msg_id>/ requires JWT auth. Returns the AppChatMessage serialized as {client_msg_id, thread_id, status, source, user_text, reply_text, error, created_at, replied_at, waking_at, phase, phase_detail}. status is one of pending/ready/error. 404 if no row exists for this tenant+client_msg_id. Cache-Control: no-store to prevent HTTP caches from serving a stale 'pending' body after the reply lands (ETagMiddleware default of max-age=10 would otherwise add up to 10s of perceived latency).
inventory_notes: waking_at is non-null while a hibernated container is booting — clients show 'assistant is waking up' copy. phase/phase_detail provide live agent-activity narration while status=pending.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to fetch a unified chronological stream of all chat turns so that I can display messages from every channel (iOS, Telegram, LINE, cron) in one view.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/chat/messages/?since=<cursor>&limit=<n> requires JWT auth. Unions AppChatMessage (channel=app), ConversationTurn (telegram/line), and ProactiveOutbound (cron) for the tenant. Each stored turn is split into up to two message rows (user + assistant); ProactiveOutbound emits one assistant row. Cursor is base64(json([created_at_iso, id])); absent/malformed cursor restarts from epoch (fail-open, iOS dedups by id). limit clamped to MAX_PAGE_SIZE=100. Returns {messages: [...], cursor: <next>}. Each message row: {id, role, text, created_at, source, thread_id, client_msg_id?}. client_msg_id is echoed on both halves of device-originated AppChatMessage turns for client-side dedup by (client_msg_id, role). ConversationTurn/ProactiveOutbound rows are mapped to the tenant's shared main thread. Empty page echoes the incoming cursor unchanged. Cache-Control: no-store.
inventory_notes: Strictly-monotonic keyset cursor (created_at, id) is replica-safe. Backdated on-device turns (from ChatLocalTurnView) may fall behind an already-served watermark and be skipped — benign since those rows are device-local. Boundary-cluster logic ensures a same-microsecond burst is never truncated mid-cluster.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to list and create named chat threads so that I can have separate topic-focused conversations.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/chat/threads/ — JWT auth; ensures the shared main thread exists (get_or_create, race-safe via UniqueConstraint on is_main per tenant), then returns all threads for the tenant as [{id, title, is_main, created_at, last_active_at}]. POST /api/v1/chat/threads/ — creates a named thread (is_main=False, title truncated to 120 chars), returns 201 with the serialized thread. 404 if user has no tenant. Cache-Control: no-store on GET.
inventory_notes: The main thread (is_main=True) is guaranteed one-per-tenant by a partial UniqueConstraint. Every channel (Telegram, LINE, iOS) resumes the main thread. OpenClaw user param for a thread is 'thread:<uuid>' so each thread has its own transcript while USER.md/memory stays shared.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to load a conversation thread's recent history so that I can see past turns when opening a thread.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/chat/threads/<thread_id>/messages/ — JWT auth. thread_id must be a UUID belonging to the tenant; invalid/unknown→404. Returns newest 50 AppChatMessage rows, reversed to oldest-first for app append-in-order display. Response: {thread: {...}, messages: [...]} where each message is the full AppChatMessage serialization including waking_at, phase, phase_detail. Cache-Control: no-store.
inventory_notes: Limit is hardcoded at _HISTORY_LIMIT=50 with no cursor pagination on this endpoint. Pending turns (no reply yet) are included with status=pending.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to fetch a compact markdown snapshot of my personal data so that my on-device model can answer questions without sending data to a cloud provider.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/chat/context/?max_chars=<n> — JWT auth with per-hour rate throttle (ChatContextHourThrottle). max_chars clamped to [CONTEXT_DIGEST_MIN_CHARS, CONTEXT_DIGEST_MAX_CHARS], defaults to CONTEXT_DIGEST_DEFAULT_CHARS. Renders a USER.md-like digest via render_context_digest (goals, tasks, fuel, finance, recent journal, conversation digest). PII placeholders are rehydrated to real names using the tenant's entity map before the response leaves (fail-open: on rehydration error, placeholder-space text is served). Returns {context_md, max_chars, generated_at}. Cache-Control: no-store.
dependencies: Postgres/Supabase (journal, tasks, fuel, finance models), apps/pii/redactor:rehydrate_text
inventory_notes: This is the privacy boundary: inference stays on-device; only structured data flows down. PII is rehydrated here (unlike USER.md, which is consumed inside the tenant container's placeholder pipeline).

user_story: As a subscriber using private/on-device mode, I want to record a completed on-device turn so that my assistant (crons, other channels) stays aware of our conversation.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/chat/turns/ — JWT auth with per-hour rate throttle (ChatLocalTurnHourThrottle). Body: {text, reply_text (optional), client_msg_id (optional), occurred_at (optional ISO8601), thread_id (optional)}. text truncated to 8000 chars (not rejected) then empty→400. reply_text truncated to 16000. client_msg_id max 64→400. Idempotent on client_msg_id: returns existing 200. Resolves thread or defaults to main. Creates AppChatMessage with status=READY, source=ON_DEVICE, replied_at=occurred_at or now. occurred_at backdates created_at via update() for digest accuracy (fail-open: naive/too-old/too-future dates ignored). On success triggers debounced USER.md refresh (schedule_user_md_refresh) so crons see the on-device turn. Returns 201 on create, 200 on idempotent replay.
inventory_notes: No OpenClaw container turn is enqueued (reply was produced on-device). occurred_at window: up to 35 days old and 5 minutes into the future. Backdated rows behind an already-served ?since= cursor are skipped by the feed (benign; device holds them locally).

user_story: As the OpenClaw runtime, I want to POST progress events so that polling clients can display live agent-activity narration instead of a static spinner.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/internal/runtime/<tenant_id>/chat/progress/ — no JWT; authenticated via X-NBHD-Internal-Key + X-NBHD-Tenant-Id headers (validate_internal_runtime_request). Body: {client_msg_id (optional), phase (required, max 24 chars), detail (optional, max 200 chars)}. Missing phase→400. Updates AppChatMessage.phase + phase_detail only for rows with status=PENDING for this tenant. If client_msg_id supplied, filters to that specific row; otherwise updates the most-recent PENDING row (per-tenant serialization means one in-flight app turn at a time). Missing/finished turns are a 200 no-op (late events never resurrect completed turns). Returns {updated: true|false}.
inventory_notes: Best-effort narration — only PENDING turns are updated. A Telegram/LINE turn creates no AppChatMessage so this is a no-op for those channels, which is correct. phase values: '', 'waking', 'thinking', 'tool', 'composing'.

feature: Pending message queue — enqueue and drain (per-tenant serialization)
user_story: As the system, I want to serialize inbound messages per (tenant, channel, channel_user_id) so that the OpenClaw container never receives overlapping turns on the same session.
expected_behaviour: enqueue_message_for_tenant() creates a PendingMessage row and publishes a QStash drain task (retries=3). drain_pending_messages_for_tenant_task() claims the next deliverable batch via SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED with a soft delivery_in_flight_until lease. Batch composition: head row must be under attempts cap (3) and not stale (>600s); subsequent rows folded in if also fresh, under cap, and non-voice; voice rows are always singletons. Batch POSTs to tenant.container_fqdn /v1/chat/completions with model='openclaw'. On success all batch rows are marked DELIVERED, usage recorded, reply relayed (channel-specific). On failure per-row delivery_attempts incremented; past-cap rows dropped with user apology. Stale rows dropped with user apology (for LINE/TG: channel push; for iOS: AppChatMessage→ERROR + APNs error push). OpenRouter 402/credit-limit triggers budget circuit breaker (hibernate + email + user notification). Re-schedules self if more pending rows remain.
dependencies: QStash (drain task, retries), Postgres (PendingMessage), OpenClaw container HTTP, apps/billing/services:record_usage
inventory_notes: Lease factor 1.5x timeout prevents concurrent drain tasks from overlapping. Boot grace window (240s) prevents burning delivery_attempts during slow cold starts. Stale-message guard (10 min) prevents 'responding to questions from hours ago' UX bug.

user_story: As the system, I want to detect and republish drain tasks for stuck PendingMessage rows so that messages are never silently dropped when QStash delivery fails.
expected_behaviour: reap_stuck_inbound_messages_task() runs every ~60s via QStash cron. Scans for PENDING rows older than 90s with no live in-flight lease. Groups by (tenant, channel, channel_user_id) key, up to 200 keys per tick. For each stuck key publishes a fresh drain task (retries=3). The drain task handles concurrency cleanly via SKIP LOCKED. Covers three failure modes: (1) publish_task raised at enqueue time, (2) QStash DLQ after all retries exhausted, (3) gunicorn worker died mid-claim. Logs a warning only when stuck keys exist (silent on clean ticks).
dependencies: QStash (cron schedule + drain task republish), Postgres (PendingMessage)
inventory_notes: Reaper does NOT process messages itself — only republishes drain tasks. The drain's stale-age guard prevents delivery of 7-hour-old messages after a reaper-triggered drain. Batch cap (200 keys/tick) prevents budget overruns at fleet scale.

feature: iOS drain — reply persistence and APNs push
user_story: As the system, I want to persist the OpenClaw reply to AppChatMessage and push an APNs notification so that the iOS client can read the reply immediately without polling forever.
expected_behaviour: _drain_ios_batch() POSTs the batch to the container (model='openclaw', X-Channel: ios). On healthy reply: PII is rehydrated, insight markers extracted + recorded, [[chart:]] / MEDIA: markers stripped, AppChatMessage rows updated to status=READY with the clean reply_text. APNs push ('reply ready') is dispatched in a daemon thread (non-blocking) via notify_app_reply_ready. Idempotent: atomic notified_at claim prevents double-push on QStash retry/re-lease. On empty/gateway-error reply: status→ERROR, error='empty_response', APNs 'couldn't finish' push fired. On OpenRouter 402: budget circuit breaker triggered, AppChatMessage→ERROR with error='budget_exhausted'. APNs tokens pruned on 410 (unregistered) responses.
dependencies: OpenClaw container HTTP, Postgres (AppChatMessage, DeviceToken), APNs (apps/common/apns), apps/pii/redactor, apps/insights/markers
inventory_notes: Push is dispatched off the drain path (daemon thread) so a slow APNs send never holds the per-key lease. The apns-collapse-id (client_msg_id) and notified_at claim provide belt-and-suspenders idempotency.

user_story: As the system, I want to detect a hibernated container when drain POST returns 404 or connection error and wake it without burning the delivery_attempts budget.
expected_behaviour: In drain_pending_messages_for_tenant_task: if the POST raises an httpx 404 / ConnectError / ConnectTimeout AND tenant.hibernated_at is not None AND budget check passes, the drain: (a) releases the lease without incrementing delivery_attempts, (b) wakes the container via wake_hibernated_tenant(), (c) sends a 'waking up, hold on' notification (Telegram only; LINE already acked; iOS gets waking_at stamped on AppChatMessage), (d) reschedules the drain with _WAKE_DEFER_SECONDS=20 delay. Boot grace window: if the container was woken within 240s but still returns 404, the lease is released again (no attempt burn) and drain retried — prevents burning the 3-attempt cap during a slow cold start. Over-budget hibernated tenants fall through to the normal attempt-cap path (not re-woken).
dependencies: Postgres (Tenant.hibernated_at, Tenant.last_wake_at), Azure Container Apps, apps/billing/services:check_budget
inventory_notes: The Telegram poller path enqueues directly to PendingMessage (no hibernation check unlike the webhook path), so this drain-level wake is the only safety net for poller-sourced messages to hibernated containers. A healthy drain response also clears a stale hibernated_at flag set by the poller.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to register my iOS device token so that the server can push 'reply ready' notifications to my device.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/push/register/ — JWT auth. Body: {device_token (hex string 32–200 chars), environment ('sandbox'|'production', defaults to 'production'), bundle_id (optional, max 128 chars)}. Invalid token format (non-hex or wrong length)→400. Upsert via update_or_create on token (globally unique): re-points device to the registering user atomically on account switch or reinstall. Returns {registered: true} 200. DELETE /api/v1/push/register/?device_token=<token> (or in body) — deletes the token for request.user only. Returns 204. Missing token→400.
inventory_notes: Token global uniqueness means registration is a safe upsert: a device switched to a new account overwrites the old row rather than creating a duplicate. Tokens APNs reports as unregistered (410) are self-pruned by the push paths.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to send a test push notification to my own devices so that I can verify notifications are working end-to-end.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/push/test/ — JWT auth, rate-limited (PushTestMinuteThrottle). If APNs is not configured returns {sent:0, failed:0, skipped:'not_configured'} 200. If user has no registered tokens returns {sent:0, failed:0, skipped:'no_tokens'} 200. Sends a FIXED static body ('Test push — notifications are working.') to all of request.user's registered tokens, routing each to the correct APNs host (sandbox vs production). Returns {sent, failed, unregistered}. Stale tokens (APNs 410) pruned. Push body is always static — never contains any user/tenant data.
inventory_notes: Security: can only push to request.user's own tokens; token is never accepted from the request body. APNs 200 means Apple accepted the push, not that the device displayed it — this endpoint lets users verify the last hop.

user_story: As the system, I want to push an APNs notification when a cron or proactive message is delivered so that iOS-only users are notified without Telegram/LINE.
expected_behaviour: notify_proactive_ready() is called from record_proactive_outbound() (off the request path via daemon thread). Atomic notified_at claim on ProactiveOutbound ensures a single push even if the function is called multiple times for the same row. Resolves tenant.user's registered devices, routes each to sandbox/production APNs host. Push body is a 140-char preview of the proactive message text, with markdown stripped and table detection (tables get generic body to avoid lock-screen leakage). Collapse-id is 'cron:<proactive_id>'. Stale tokens (APNs 410) self-pruned. No-op if APNs unconfigured. Fail-open.
inventory_notes: The push is a wake-and-sync trigger only; authoritative text reaches the app via the ?since= feed. Dispatched in a daemon thread off the CronDeliveryView request path to avoid blocking the cron tool-call response.

user_story: As the system, I want to prepend prior proactive/cron outbounds to each inbound message so that the assistant can anchor multi-paragraph user replies to the original questions.
expected_behaviour: surface_proactive_context() called by LINE webhook, Telegram webhook, and Telegram poller for each inbound. Queries ProactiveOutbound rows for (tenant, channel, channel_user_id) within 24h, newest-first, limit 3. Keeps unconsumed rows AND consumed-but-recent rows (within 5-min follow-up window). First-time-surfaced rows are marked consumed_at atomically. Renders an '[earlier-from-you <datetime> <job>:\n<text>\n]' block (oldest-first). If any row has parsed_items (bullet/numbered list), prepends a [thread-rule:] guidance for the agent to map reply paragraphs by index. Returns empty string if nothing to surface.
inventory_notes: consumed_at is deliberately re-surfaced within the 5-min follow-up window so 'thanks, then real answer' sequences still see the context. parsed_items extraction (parse_markdown_items) requires ≥2 list items to constitute a structure.

user_story: As the OpenClaw runtime, I want to send proactive or cron messages to the user via Django so that the correct channel (Telegram, LINE, or iOS app) is used and thread continuity is maintained.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/internal/runtime/<tenant_id>/send/ (mounted via integrations/urls.py). Auth: X-NBHD-Internal-Key + X-NBHD-Tenant-Id. tenant.status != ACTIVE→200 {status:'blocked'} (no retry). Channel resolution: prefers user.preferred_channel if linked; fallbacks: any linked messaging channel; iOS app (DeviceToken exists) as last resort. No delivery surface→422. Rate limit: 20 messages/hour per tenant (in-process memory, resets on restart). Body: {message, parse_mode (Markdown|HTML|plain, default Markdown)}. PII rehydration before send. Telegram: markdown→Telegram HTML (render_telegram_html), HTML fallback to plain text on 400; LINE: push API. channel='app': only APNs + ?since= feed (no chat message sent). On success records ProactiveOutbound row, dispatches iOS APNs push. X-NBHD-Job-Name header is captured in ProactiveOutbound.job_name.
dependencies: Telegram Bot API, LINE Messaging API, Postgres (ProactiveOutbound, DeviceToken), APNs, apps/pii/redactor
inventory_notes: Rate limit is in-process dict — resets on deploy/restart. iOS-only users ('app' channel) receive the message ONLY via the ?since= feed and APNs; the text is stored in ProactiveOutbound, never in AppChatMessage.
error_detail: Confirmed. In CronDeliveryView.post the 20-messages/hour cap is CHECKED for every channel at line 156 (if not _check_rate_limit(tid)), but the counter is only INCREMENTED via _record_send(tenant_id) inside the Telegram branch (line 301, in _send_via_telegram) and the LINE branch (line 405, in _send_via_line). The app-channel branch (lines 190-196) builds its success Response inline (status=sent, channel=app) and never calls _record_send. The counter is keyed by tenant_id and shared across channels; record_proactive_outbound (apps/router/proactive_context.py:73) does NO rate limiting, it only persists the ProactiveOutbound row and dispatches the APNs push. Net effect: for iOS-only tenants (the app channel, the common App Store case per resolve_user_channel), _check_rate_limit always returns True regardless of how many proactive/cron messages were sent in the last hour, so the runaway-cron-loop safety throttle (purpose stated at line 23) is silently ineffective for that channel. The feature spec lists 'Rate limit: 20 messages/hour per tenant' as an endpoint-wide invariant, not a Telegram/LINE-only one, so this is unintended. Impact is bounded: it only bites under a runaway/looping cron, and the iOS push leg has its own notified_at idempotency, but a malfunctioning or looping cron on an iOS-only tenant could emit unlimited ?since= feed rows + APNs pushes with no backstop, exactly what this cap exists to prevent. Hence medium, not high. The existing test (apps/router/test_cron_delivery.py:77 test_rate_limit) pre-fills _rate_counts manually and uses the default Telegram channel, so it never exercises the app-channel increment path and did not catch this gap.

user_story: As the system, I want to persist every completed Telegram/LINE turn to ConversationTurn so that cron sessions can see today's conversation without the user explicitly journaling.
expected_behaviour: record_conversation_turn() called fail-open from _drain_telegram_batch(), _drain_line_batch(), and the Telegram webhook after each successful reply relay. Creates a ConversationTurn row with channel, channel_user_id, local_date (tenant-tz date), user_text (≤2000 chars), reply_text (PII-rehydrated + marker-stripped, ≤800 chars). Probabilistically (1% chance per call) prunes expired rows (>35 days) in batches of 500. Triggers debounced USER.md push (schedule_user_md_refresh) with 120s debounce via on_commit + daemon thread. AppChatMessage (iOS) is NOT captured here — the digest reads that table directly to avoid double-storage.
inventory_notes: Fail-open: a lost ConversationTurn row never 500s the user reply. Debounce prevents storming the Azure File Share on high-frequency chat. The digest renders today's turns (last 6, 130-char one-liners) + 3-day rollup.

user_story: As the system, I want to deduplicate inbound provider events so that Telegram/LINE at-least-once redelivery never produces duplicate assistant replies.
expected_behaviour: claim_inbound_event(event_key) called before any side effect by the Telegram webhook (key='tg:<update_id>') and the LINE webhook (key='line:<webhookEventId>'). Uses get_or_create on ProcessedInboundEvent.event_key (UNIQUE constraint). Returns True (process) on first claim, False (skip) on duplicate. Concurrent races resolved by the unique constraint — loser gets IntegrityError → returns False. Any unexpected error (DB outage) returns True (fail-open: risk duplicate > risk lost message). Probabilistically prunes expired rows (>3 days, 1% chance, batch 500).
inventory_notes: Retention is 3 days — comfortably longer than any provider redelivery window. Telegram poller replays only the unacked tail on restart; LINE retries for minutes. Blank/missing event key always returns True (process, conservative).

user_story: As a subscriber, I want messages sent to the shared Telegram bot to be routed to my personal OpenClaw instance so that I get personalised AI responses.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/telegram/webhook/ — CSRF-exempt, require_POST. Verified via X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token HMAC against TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET; mismatch→403. Duplicate update_id deduped via claim_inbound_event (200 ack on duplicate). /start TOKEN command handled for account linking. chat_id extracted; unknown→onboarding link; rate limited→429. Tenant looked up by chat_id. Suspended/inactive tenant→error message. Budget check before wake: over-budget→hibernate + budget-exhausted message. Hibernated tenant→handle_hibernated_message: buffers in PendingMessage, wakes container, ACK_FRESH/ACK_RECONNECT/SILENT responses. Active tenant: msg text decorated with proactive context + datetime context + chat marker, forwarded to container via forward_to_openclaw (async, 30s/120s reasoning timeout). On response: usage recorded, turn captured to ConversationTurn. Timeout→silent 200 ack (agent replies async via bot token). Inline button callbacks (lesson:, extract:, task_action:) handled before routing.
dependencies: Postgres (Tenant, ConversationTurn, PendingMessage), Telegram Bot API, OpenClaw container HTTP, apps/router/proactive_context, apps/router/services:forward_to_openclaw
inventory_notes: In production the single-revision Telegram poller is the live path; this webhook is a latency-sensitive fallback. set_rls_context(service_role=True) required because Telegram webhooks are unauthenticated and need RLS bypass.

user_story: As a subscriber using Siri, I want a deterministic no-LLM personal data snapshot so that Siri can read out my current status instantly.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/siri/status/ — JWT auth, per-minute rate throttle (SiriStatusMinuteThrottle). Returns a compact markdown snapshot of the user's personal data (same render_context_digest as ChatContextView) with PII placeholders rehydrated. Response: {snapshot_md, generated_at}. Cache-Control: no-store. No model call — purely deterministic data retrieval.
dependencies: Postgres/Supabase (journal, tasks, fuel, finance models), apps/pii/redactor
inventory_notes: Distinct from ChatContextView only in throttle class and typical max_chars default; both call the same render_context_digest.

feature: Siri respond — fast model with escalation to full agent
user_story: As a subscriber using Siri, I want spoken intents answered by a fast model or escalated to the full agent so that simple queries are instant and complex ones get the full assistant.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/siri/respond/ — JWT auth, per-minute throttle (SiriRespondMinuteThrottle). Body: {intent (required, max 2048 chars), client_msg_id (optional)}. Empty intent→400. Renders context snapshot (rehydrated). Calls a fast model (chat_completion, 8s timeout, max 300 tokens) with the intent + snapshot. If model returns [[ESCALATE]] sentinel → escalates to full agent via enqueue_tenant_turn (same path as ChatMessageView POST). Returns {answered:true, text:...} or {answered:false, escalated:true, client_msg_id:..., turn:{...}}. Model failure → silently escalates. Escalated turn client polls GET /api/v1/chat/messages/<client_msg_id>/ for the reply.
dependencies: apps/common/openrouter:chat_completion (fast model), Postgres (AppChatMessage, ChatThread, PendingMessage), QStash
inventory_notes: Any model failure falls through to escalation (never errors the user). Escalation uses the shared main thread. The fast-model path does NOT create any AppChatMessage or ConversationTurn — it is a stateless read.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

user_story: As the system, I want to detect OpenRouter spending-limit rejections during message drain and hibernate the tenant so that overspend is halted and the user is informed.
expected_behaviour: _looks_like_openrouter_credit_limit() checks for HTTP 402, or 4xx/5xx body containing 'insufficient credit', 'credit_limit', 'quota exceeded' etc. (case-insensitive). _handle_openrouter_credit_limit(): if tenant is budget-exempt OR has purchased_credit > 0 → raises the OR key ceiling via sync_or_key_limit() and returns (no hibernation). Otherwise: bumps estimated_cost_this_month to effective_cost_budget cap so check_budget fires on next inbound, hibernates container via _hibernate_for_quota, sends branded cap-exhausted email (idempotent sent-at marker), sends channel-native budget-exhausted message to user (localized). For iOS turns: AppChatMessage flipped to ERROR. Returns False (not a liveness signal) from the drain helper.
dependencies: Postgres (Tenant), apps/billing/credits:sync_or_key_limit, apps/router/billing_quota_handlers:send_cost_exhausted_email, Telegram Bot API / LINE API (error message send)
inventory_notes: Budget-exempt and credit-holding tenants are NOT hibernated on OR 402 — their key ceiling is raised instead. The reconcile path (cleared hibernated_at) does NOT fire when gateway_responded=False (budget hibernation must not be undone).
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

feature: Daily inbound media cleanup (Azure File Share)
user_story: As the system, I want to delete inbound media files older than 24 hours from all tenant file shares so that storage costs are bounded.
expected_behaviour: cleanup_inbound_media_task() called via QStash daily cron. Iterates all ACTIVE tenants with a container_id. For each: opens ShareDirectoryClient at workspace/media/inbound on the tenant's file share (ws-<first20 of tenant.id>). Lists files; for each non-directory item, checks last_modified against 24h cutoff; deletes if stale. Skips tenants with no media directory. No-op in mock mode. Logs total deleted count.
inventory_notes: Requires AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT_NAME and AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP settings. ShareDirectoryClient list failure for a tenant (directory not yet created) is silently skipped.

user_story: As the system, I want to buffer webhook payloads for hibernated tenant containers so that messages are not lost while the container wakes.
expected_behaviour: BufferedMessage rows are created by the Telegram/LINE wake-on-message path (handle_hibernated_message) when a message arrives for a hibernated container. Stores the raw webhook payload (channel, tenant FK, user_text excerpt, delivery_status=pending). After the container wakes (~45s), a QStash task (deliver_buffered_messages in apps/orchestrator/hibernation.py) replays buffered messages in order via /v1/chat/completions, marking each delivered or failed. Soft lease (delivery_in_flight_until) prevents concurrent replay. Per-row attempt cap prevents permanent wedge. Distinct from PendingMessage (which serializes warm-tenant rapid-fire messages).
dependencies: QStash (deliver_buffered_messages task), Postgres (BufferedMessage), Azure Container Apps (wake)
inventory_notes: BufferedMessage is for cold/hibernated containers; PendingMessage is for warm-but-busy containers. The PendingMessage drain also handles the hibernated-container case (404 detection + wake) for the Telegram poller path which bypasses wake_on_message.

feature: Telegram reply relay with HTML formatting and chart/photo delivery
user_story: As the system, I want to relay the OpenClaw reply to Telegram with proper formatting so that users see rich output (headings, tables, images) rather than raw markdown.
expected_behaviour: relay_ai_response_to_telegram() called from _drain_telegram_batch(). Steps: (1) PII rehydration, (2) ASCII chart leak logging, (3) [[insight:slug]] markers extracted → AssistantInsight records written + stripped, (4) [[chart:type|params]] markers rendered to PNG via render_chart, uploaded to Azure File Share, replaced with MEDIA: paths, (5) MEDIA:/workspace/ image paths sent via sendPhoto (downloads from file share, sends as multipart), (6) remaining text rendered to Telegram HTML via render_telegram_html (bold headings, aligned monospace tables, anchors), (7) long messages split on paragraph/line/word boundaries (max 4096 chars per chunk), (8) each chunk sent with parse_mode=HTML; 400 fallback to plain text (strip_telegram_html). Brief 0.3s delay between chunks.
dependencies: Telegram Bot API, Azure File Share (photo download), apps/router/charts:render_chart, apps/insights/markers, apps/pii/redactor
inventory_notes: The Telegram poller also has its own _send_rich_response for the synchronous live path; this relay covers the queue drain path. Voice rows are always singletons (never coalesced).

feature: LINE webhook signature verification and event dispatch
user_story: As the LINE platform, I want to deliver webhook events to NBHD United so that the system can react to user activity in real-time.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/line/webhook/ — CSRF exempt. First validates the X-Line-Signature HMAC-SHA256 against LINE_CHANNEL_SECRET; missing or invalid signature returns HTTP 403. Parses JSON body; invalid JSON returns HTTP 400. For each event in the events array, claims the webhookEventId in ProcessedInboundEvent (line:<webhookEventId>) before any side effect — duplicate events return immediately (idempotent). Each event is dispatched in a daemon thread (except in test mode where NBHD_DISABLE_BACKGROUND_THREADS=True runs inline). Sets RLS service-role context on each thread. Routes to _handle_message, _handle_follow, _handle_unfollow, or _handle_postback; unknown types are logged and ignored. Always returns HTTP 200 to LINE within 1 second.
dependencies: LINE Messaging API (signature source), Supabase/Postgres (ProcessedInboundEvent dedup ledger)
inventory_notes: LINE re-delivers events (deliveryContext.isRedelivery=true) when 200 is slow. The dedup gate is the only protection against double-processing. Threads are daemon so they don't prevent process shutdown but also can't outlive the process.

user_story: As a LINE user who adds the bot, I want to receive a welcome message so that I know how to connect my account.
expected_behaviour: Triggered by LINE follow event. Resolves the LINE user ID against User.line_user_id. If already linked: sends a branded short bubble Flex message welcoming back the subscriber by display name. If unlinked: sends a full branded Flex bubble instructing the user to sign up at FRONTEND_URL and connect LINE from Settings. Both paths use _send_line_flex (Push API, no reply token). No HTTP response — runs asynchronously in a daemon thread.
dependencies: LINE Messaging API Push (LINE_CHANNEL_ACCESS_TOKEN), Supabase/Postgres (User model lookup)

user_story: As a LINE user who blocks or unfollows the bot, I want my LINE account automatically unlinked so that stale IDs don't cause delivery errors.
expected_behaviour: Triggered by LINE unfollow event. Looks up User by line_user_id. Clears line_user_id and line_display_name. If preferred_channel was 'line', resets it to 'telegram'. Saves with update_fields. If user is not found (unlinked user), silently returns. No message is sent back (the user has blocked the bot). Side effect: proactive cron delivery will fall back to Telegram (or fail with no_channel_linked) on subsequent sends.
inventory_notes: preferred_channel falls back to 'telegram' even if Telegram is not linked — the user may need to reconnect.

feature: LINE text message ingest and forwarding pipeline
user_story: As a LINE subscriber, I want to send text messages to the bot so that my AI assistant can respond to my questions.
expected_behaviour: Handles LINE message events with type=text. Strips and validates text. Detects link_ prefix and routes to account-linking. Resolves tenant via line_user_id; sends a warning Flex if unrecognized. Checks tenant status: PENDING/PROVISIONING sends a 'waking up' bubble; SUSPENDED without active subscription sends billing URL. Calls check_budget — on budget exhaustion, hibernates tenant and sends error message. Calls handle_hibernated_message for hibernated containers: buffers the message, triggers wake, returns ACK_FRESH (sends 'waking' bubble), ACK_RECONNECT ('reconnecting' bubble), or SILENT (no ack). For onboarding: gates non-text during onboarding, calls get_onboarding_response. For live tenants: shows loading animation, updates last_message_at, extracts quote-reply context (LineOutboundMessage lookup), prepends proactive context + datetime marker + chat marker, then calls enqueue_message_for_tenant to place the message in PendingMessage. Drain is handled asynchronously by QStash.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (User/Tenant/PendingMessage/LineOutboundMessage/ProactiveOutbound), LINE Messaging API Push, OpenClaw container (via drain task), QStash (drain task dispatch), apps/billing (check_budget), apps/orchestrator (wake_hibernated_tenant)
inventory_notes: reply_token is captured in PendingMessage payload but NOT used at drain time — by then the 1-minute Reply API window has typically expired. Drain always uses Push API. Session key is flat (line_user_id) — no workspace routing since PR removing it.

user_story: As a LINE subscriber, I want to send voice messages and have them transcribed so that my AI assistant can understand spoken input.
expected_behaviour: Handles LINE message events with type=audio. Downloads audio binary from LINE Content API (https://api-data.line.me/v2/bot/message/<id>/content) using LINE_CHANNEL_ACCESS_TOKEN. Detects extension from content-type header (m4a/ogg/wav, default m4a). POSTs to OpenAI Whisper API (whisper-1 model) with OPENAI_API_KEY. On success: prepends '🎤 Voice message: "<transcript>"' and processes as a text message. On failure: sends a warning status bubble. Shows loading animation before transcription and again after (Whisper may consume the first 60s window). VOICE_CHAT_TIMEOUT_EXTRA = 60 extra seconds is defined but the drain timeout is resolved separately in _resolve_chat_timeout.
dependencies: LINE Content API (audio download), OpenAI Whisper API (transcription, requires OPENAI_API_KEY), LINE Messaging API Push (response delivery)
inventory_notes: Falls back gracefully — on transcription failure, sends a warning Flex bubble rather than silently dropping the message. OPENAI_API_KEY is required; if absent, immediately fails with a warning log.

user_story: As a LINE subscriber, I want to send stickers and have my assistant respond naturally so that I can communicate emotionally without typing.
expected_behaviour: Handles LINE message events with type=sticker. Extracts keywords and stickerResourceType from the event. If keywords are present (up to 5), builds a framed directive '[User sent a LINE sticker expressing: <keywords>. Respond naturally…]'. If no keywords, uses a generic '[User sent a LINE sticker (no keywords available)…]'. This synthetic text is then processed exactly like a text message through the standard pipeline (budget check, queue, container forward). Not allowed during onboarding steps — sends a 'stickers not ready yet' short bubble.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (PendingMessage), OpenClaw container (via drain), LINE Messaging API Push
inventory_notes: LINE sticker keywords are LINE-platform-generated emotional tags, not OCR; they vary by sticker pack. Emoji bullets and tone are agent-driven.

user_story: As a LINE subscriber who sends an image, video, or location, I want to receive a clear error message so that I know those types are not supported.
expected_behaviour: Handles LINE message events with types other than text, audio, or sticker (e.g. image, video, location, file). Sends a warning status Flex bubble 'I can process text, voice, and stickers. Please send one of those!' via Push API. Returns without enqueueing anything.

feature: LINE account linking via deep link token
user_story: As a subscriber who scanned the QR code or tapped the LINE deep link, I want my LINE account to be linked to my NBHD United account so that I can chat with my assistant on LINE.
expected_behaviour: Triggered when an inbound LINE text message starts with 'link_'. Strips the 'link_' prefix to get the token. Fetches the LINE display name from the LINE Profile API (GET /v2/bot/profile/<userId>). Calls process_line_link_token: looks up LineLinkToken by token value, returns error if not found or is_valid is False (expired or used). Checks for collision (same LINE user linked to a different NBHD user) — returns error if so. Sets User.line_user_id and line_display_name (updates display_name from 'Friend' if not yet personalized). Marks the token used=True. If the tenant's container is ACTIVE, publishes 'update_tenant_config' via QStash to push the updated config to the container. Sends a plain text success message '✅ Welcome, <name>! Your LINE account is now connected.' Tokens expire after 15 minutes.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (LineLinkToken, User model), LINE Profile API (display name), QStash (update_tenant_config task), OpenClaw container (config update)
inventory_notes: One-time token, 15-minute TTL. If the LINE Profile API fails, linking still succeeds with an empty display name. If another NBHD user already has the same LINE user ID linked, linking is rejected.

user_story: As a new LINE subscriber going through onboarding, I want to tap timezone buttons so that I can configure my assistant's timezone without typing.
expected_behaviour: Handles LINE postback events where data starts with 'tz_'. Resolves tenant by line_user_id. Calls handle_onboarding_callback from apps.router.onboarding. Returns an OnboardingReply with text and optional keyboard. Renders via _send_onboarding_reply: builds a short_bubble Flex, attaches Quick Reply buttons (converted from Telegram keyboard format via telegram_keyboard_to_quick_reply), pushes via LINE Push API. Postback data formats: 'tz_country:<code>' and 'tz_zone:<zone>'.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (Tenant model update), LINE Messaging API Push
inventory_notes: LINE has no language_code in webhook events, so the onboarding always asks the language question (unlike Telegram which can infer from update.message.from.language_code).

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to approve or deny pending AI-proposed actions directly from LINE so that I control what the assistant executes.
expected_behaviour: Handles LINE postback events with data starting 'gate_approve:' or 'gate_deny:'. Parses action_id from data. Fetches PendingAction scoped to the tenant. If action not in PENDING status, returns without effect. If is_expired, sets status=EXPIRED, logs ActionAuditLog, calls update_gate_message. Otherwise sets status=APPROVED or DENIED, records responded_at, creates ActionAuditLog, calls update_gate_message. Errors (action not found, parse failure) are logged. No response message is sent to the user — update_gate_message handles the LINE-side update.
inventory_notes: Only PENDING actions can be acted upon. Expired actions are auto-closed on first postback. No idempotency guard beyond the status check.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to approve or dismiss AI-extracted lessons, goals, and tasks via LINE buttons so that I control what gets saved to my constellation.
expected_behaviour: Handles postback data format 'extract:<action>:<pending_id>'. Actions: approve_lesson, approve_goal, approve_task, undo_lesson, undo_goal, undo_task, dismiss. Fetches PendingExtraction scoped to tenant. For undo actions: checks status is APPROVED (not UNDONE), calls _undo_lesson/_undo_goal/_undo_task, sets status=UNDONE, sends a warning status bubble with 'Removed: <text>'. For approve actions: calls _approve_lesson/_approve_goal/_approve_task, sets status=APPROVED, sends a success status bubble. For dismiss: sets status=DISMISSED, sends warning bubble. Already-resolved items send a friendly 'already added/skipped' text follow-up. If pending_id not found, returns silently.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (PendingExtraction, Lesson, Document models), apps/journal, apps/lessons
inventory_notes: Unlike Telegram path (which uses _answer_callback for immediate ack), LINE postbacks send status bubbles as new Push messages.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to undo a task action applied by the nightly extraction via LINE so that I can correct unwanted changes.
expected_behaviour: Handles postback data format 'task_action:undo:<action_id>'. Delegates to handle_task_action_postback_line from apps.router.task_action_callbacks. Returns (ok, message) tuple. Sends a status bubble with tone='success' or 'warning' based on the result.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (PendingTaskAction or similar), LINE Messaging API Push

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to approve or dismiss AI-suggested lessons via LINE buttons so that I control what is saved to my constellation.
expected_behaviour: Handles postback data format 'lesson:<action>:<lesson_id>'. Fetches Lesson scoped to tenant where status='pending'. If not found, returns silently. Rehydrates PII for the display text. On 'approve': sets status='approved', sets approved_at, saves, calls process_approved_lesson, sends '✅ Approved: <text[:100]>' via _send_line_follow_up. On 'dismiss': sets status='dismissed', saves, sends '❌ Dismissed: <text[:100]>'. process_approved_lesson errors are logged but don't block the approve response.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (Lesson model), apps/lessons (process_approved_lesson), apps/pii (rehydrate_for_tenant), LINE Messaging API Push
inventory_notes: Only pending lessons can be acted on. Non-pending lessons are silently ignored (no 'already handled' message).
error_detail: Real but minor UX inconsistency. In _handle_lesson_postback (apps/router/line_webhook.py:1501-1503), when a lesson:approve/lesson:dismiss button is tapped for a lesson no longer status=pending (already approved, dismissed, or gone), the handler returns silently with zero feedback. LINE lesson suggestion cards (apps/lessons/notifications.py:144-153) stay in chat scrollback and are never edited away, so a user can tap a stale button and get no response, which reads as a dead/broken button. The codebase itself shows silence is NOT the intended standard here: (1) the Telegram equivalent at extraction_callbacks.py:278-281 sends Already processed, and (2) the sibling LINE handler _handle_extraction_postback in the SAME file (lines 1427-1433) explicitly handles this with the comment 'Already resolved — send friendly acknowledgment instead of silence' and replies a thumbs-up 'That one is already added/skipped!'. So the lesson path is an oversight, not a deliberate design; the feature record's 'returns silently' merely describes current code. Impact is cosmetic only: no data corruption, no wrong action, and the status=pending guard correctly prevents double-processing. Only affects users tapping an old card after the lesson was already resolved.

feature: LINE postback — generic button forward to agent
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to tap Quick Reply buttons attached to assistant messages so that I can respond to the assistant with a single tap.
expected_behaviour: Handles any postback event not matched by tz_/gate_/extract_/task_action_/lesson_ prefixes. Resolves tenant. Calls _forward_to_container with the synthetic text '[User tapped button: "<data>"]'. This goes through the full pipeline: enqueued in PendingMessage, drained to OpenClaw, reply delivered via LINE Push. The postback data (callback_data) is used as the button identifier.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (PendingMessage, Tenant), OpenClaw container (via drain), QStash (drain task)
inventory_notes: This is the fallback for all [[button:label|data]] markers the agent emits. LINE label is capped at 20 chars; data is uncapped in the payload.
error_detail: Real but narrow. The generic postback fallback in _handle_postback (line_webhook.py:1311) forwards arbitrary tapped-button data straight to _forward_to_container, which only blocks PROVISIONING status (line 1214). It skips the SUSPENDED check, the synchronous check_budget call, and the hibernation/wake routing that the typed-message path (_handle_message, lines 1020-1067) enforces. The agent can emit arbitrary generic buttons via [[button:label|data]] markers (line_flex.py:815-839); any data not matching the tz_/gate_/extract:/task_action:/lesson: prefixes hits this fallback. The same ungated pattern exists on the Telegram poller (poller.py:831-836, with its gates only after, at 855-874). However, real impact is heavily mitigated, so the 'trigger AI turns without paying' framing is overstated: (1) Billing-suspended tenants have their container HIBERNATED (billing/services.py:556) with hibernated_at cleared to None (line 546), so the forwarded postback 404s, drops, and does NOT re-wake -- no turn, no spend. (2) Over-budget tenants already hibernated have hibernated_at set (views.py:135), so the drain hits the 404 branch (pending_queue.py:739) where check_budget re-fires and drops without waking. The ONLY genuinely exploitable window is an over-budget tenant whose container is still LIVE because _hibernate_for_quota has not fired yet: a typed message would run check_budget and hibernate/block, but a tapped generic button skips it and lands one billable turn. Even then the cost is debited via record_usage overage and the 402 breaker trips on the next call -- one leaked turn in a narrow race, not unlimited free usage.

feature: LINE pending queue drain — deliver LINE batch to container
user_story: As the system, I want to serialize and deliver LINE messages to the OpenClaw container so that messages arrive in order without overlapping concurrent turns.
expected_behaviour: QStash task drain_pending_messages_for_tenant_task with channel='line'. Claims the oldest PENDING PendingMessage for (tenant, 'line', line_user_id) key using SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED. Takes a soft lease (delivery_in_flight_until = now + timeout). Builds chat content: singleton batch uses payload.message_text directly; coalesced batch (cold-start) reformats with indexed user texts. POSTs to https://<container_fqdn>/v1/chat/completions with model='openclaw', headers X-Line-User-Id and X-Channel: line. If OpenRouter credit limit detected: hibernates tenant, sends budget-exhausted LINE text, returns False. On success: calls relay_ai_response_to_line to deliver formatted reply, calls _capture_conversation_turn to persist ConversationTurn, records usage. On failure after max attempts: marks status=FAILED, calls _send_apology_for_stale_pending_message via LINE Push. If more pending messages remain, reschedules itself via QStash.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (PendingMessage, ConversationTurn), OpenClaw container (POST /v1/chat/completions), LINE Messaging API Push (relay_ai_response_to_line), QStash (reschedule), apps/billing (record_usage)
inventory_notes: reply_token is NOT used even if present in the payload — the Reply API window is typically closed by drain time. Coalescing is only triggered on cold-start when multiple messages accumulate during container wake. SKIP LOCKED prevents two concurrent drain tasks from processing the same row.

feature: LINE AI response relay — Flex message rendering and delivery
user_story: As the system, I want to format and deliver AI assistant responses to LINE using branded Flex messages so that the user receives a polished, readable reply.
expected_behaviour: relay_ai_response_to_line is the single delivery chokepoint for all LINE AI replies (both live webhook drain and hibernation buffered-delivery path). Rehydrates PII placeholders via pii.redactor. Logs ASCII chart leaks. Extracts [[insight:slug]] markers via insights.markers. Renders [[chart:type]] markers to PNG images uploaded to Azure File Share, building image-type LINE messages with chart URLs. Strips MEDIA: markers (not supported on LINE). Extracts [[button:label|data]] quick reply patterns. Converts markdown tables to key:value plain text. Strips code blocks. Builds Flex bubble via build_flex_bubble (short_bubble, structured bubble, or plain_text fallback based on content classification). Prepends chart image messages. Attaches quick reply to last message. Truncates to max 5 messages (LINE Push limit). Falls back to plain text with _strip_markdown on Flex build failure. Emergency plain text fallback on any exception. Sends via _send_line_messages (Reply API preferred, Push fallback); records sentMessages IDs in LineOutboundMessage for future quote-reply resolution.
dependencies: LINE Messaging API Reply + Push APIs, Azure File Share (chart images), apps/pii (rehydrate_text), apps/insights (extract_and_record_insights), apps/router/charts.py (render_chart), Supabase/Postgres (LineOutboundMessage)
inventory_notes: Three-layer fallback: Flex → plain markdown-stripped text → emergency stripped text on exception. Reply API is tried first (free quota); Push is the fallback. LineOutboundMessage is written best-effort (errors logged, never block delivery).

user_story: As the system, I want to render structured AI responses as branded LINE Flex messages so that subscribers receive a polished, mobile-optimized reading experience.
expected_behaviour: build_flex_bubble classifies content via classify_content: 'short' (<200 chars, no newlines, or <=2000 chars) → build_short_bubble (compact accent-bar bubble); 'structured' (has markdown headers, 3+ bullet items, emoji headers, or 4+ double-newline paragraphs >500 chars) → full branded bubble with sections, header block (first section title becomes colored header), bullet-list components with depth-aware indentation, tappable link components; 'plain_text' (>2000 chars, unstructured) → plain text path. build_status_bubble renders success/error/warning tones with icon+colored background. All bubbles have a 'Tap to copy' clipboard footer with up to 5000 chars. Inline markdown is stripped from all Flex text components. Max 30 body components per bubble (LINE ~50KB limit guard). Carousel builder (build_flex_carousel) supports up to 12 bubbles with optional postback action buttons.
inventory_notes: Clipboard action text is capped at 5000 chars. Flex bubble altText (screen reader / notification preview) is capped at 400 chars. The 30-component guard prevents LINE API 400s for large responses.

feature: LINE quick reply extraction and attachment
user_story: As the system, I want to extract [[button:label|data]] markers from AI responses and attach them as LINE Quick Reply buttons so that subscribers can respond with a single tap.
expected_behaviour: extract_quick_reply_buttons strips [[button:label|data]] patterns from the response text and returns cleaned text plus a list of Quick Reply action items. Labels are capped at 20 chars, data is uncapped. Max 13 items (LINE limit). attach_quick_reply adds quickReply.items to any LINE message object. telegram_keyboard_to_quick_reply converts Telegram inline keyboard format (2D array with text+callback_data) to LINE Quick Reply flat list — used during onboarding so the same OnboardingReply works on both channels.
inventory_notes: Quick reply items trigger postback events; the _handle_generic_postback path forwards them to the agent as '[User tapped button: "<data>"]'.

user_story: As a LINE subscriber, I want to quote-reply to an assistant message and have the assistant understand which message I'm replying to so that conversations are contextually accurate.
expected_behaviour:
_extract_line_reply_context checks message.quotedMessageId on inbound events. If present, looks up LineOutboundMessage by (tenant, line_message_id). If found with text_excerpt: prepends '[Replying to: "<excerpt up to 200 chars>"]

'. If found but excerpt is empty (row predates feature): prepends '[Replying to an earlier message of yours]

'. If not found (pruned, >30 days old): returns empty string (silently drops context). This prefix is inserted before the user's message_text in the PendingMessage payload. LineOutboundMessage rows are written by _record_line_outbound on every successful push/reply send (bulk_create with ignore_conflicts). Rows are pruned probabilistically (1% chance per insert, 30-day window).
inventory_notes: LINE only sends the ID of the quoted message, not its content (unlike Telegram which inlines reply_to_message.text). This store is LINE-specific and required to resolve the context. 30-day pruning means references to messages older than 30 days show the 'earlier message' fallback.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

user_story: As the system, I want to deduplicate LINE webhook redeliveries so that a LINE at-least-once delivery never produces duplicate assistant replies.
expected_behaviour: claim_inbound_event is called at the top of _handle_event with event_key='line:<webhookEventId>'. Uses get_or_create on ProcessedInboundEvent.event_key (UNIQUE constraint). Returns True (process) if first sighting, False (skip) if duplicate. Race-safe: concurrent daemon threads for the same event_key will lose the IntegrityError and return False. Fail-open: any unexpected exception returns True (never drop a real message). If event has no webhookEventId, processes without dedup (fail-open). Probabilistic pruning: 1% of successful claims delete up to 500 rows older than 3 days.
inventory_notes: 3-day retention is comfortably longer than LINE's redelivery window. The UNIQUE constraint is the actual dedup; get_or_create is the race-safe wrapper.

user_story: As a LINE subscriber waiting for a response, I want to see a typing indicator so that I know my message is being processed.
expected_behaviour: _show_loading posts to POST /v2/bot/chat/loading/start with chatId=line_user_id and loadingSeconds=60. Fires and forgets — timeout is 3 seconds, exceptions are silently swallowed. Called twice for voice messages (once at receipt, once after Whisper transcription). Called once for text/sticker messages before the loading animation. The animation auto-clears when the actual response message is sent via Push/Reply API.
inventory_notes: Non-critical path — fire-and-forget with full exception suppression. Maximum 60 seconds of loading animation.

user_story: As an OpenClaw container running a cron job, I want to push a proactive message to the subscriber's LINE so that scheduled check-ins and reports are delivered.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/router/<tenant_id>/send/ — internal-auth only (X-NBHD-Internal-Key + X-NBHD-Tenant-Id). Validates internal key. Resolves tenant. Blocks delivery for non-ACTIVE tenants (returns 200 with status=blocked). Resolves channel via resolve_user_channel: prefers preferred_channel if linked, falls back to any linked channel. Returns 422 if neither channel is linked. Rate limit: max 20 messages per hour per tenant (in-memory, resets on restart). Validates message (max 8192 chars). Rehydrates PII. Logs ASCII chart leaks. For LINE channel: calls _send_via_line which builds Flex bubble with quick replies, extracts buttons, sends in batches of 5 via LINE Push API. Trips monthly quota gate on 429. Records LineOutboundMessage. On success: calls record_proactive_outbound to persist ProactiveOutbound row for thread-continuity context. Returns 200 with status=sent.
dependencies: LINE Messaging API Push (LINE_CHANNEL_ACCESS_TOKEN), Supabase/Postgres (ProactiveOutbound, LineOutboundMessage, Tenant, User), apps/integrations (internal auth), apps/router/line_quota (_maybe_trip_monthly_quota)
inventory_notes: Rate limit is in-memory and resets on process restart — not durable across Azure Container App restarts. parse_mode field exists in serializer but is only used for the Telegram path; LINE always builds Flex regardless.

feature: LINE Push monthly quota polling and state management
user_story: As the platform operator, I want the system to automatically monitor LINE Push quota daily so that I'm warned before the monthly cap is hit and affected users are notified when it is.
expected_behaviour: poll_line_quota_task (QStash daily cron) calls refresh_quota_state which polls LINE's /v2/bot/message/quota (limit) and /v2/bot/message/quota/consumption (used). Updates the singleton LineQuotaState (pk=1) row. Detects three transitions: 'entered_pre_warn' (usage crossed 90% threshold upward), 'exhausted' (used >= limit, was not previously exhausted), 'recovered' (was exhausted, now under cap). On any transition, enqueues dispatch_line_quota_handler via QStash. dispatch_line_quota_handler_task calls dispatch_for_current_state which runs all three idempotent handlers. Poll failure (network or non-2xx) is logged and returns polled=False without changing state. Uncapped plans ('none' type from LINE) use sentinel limit of 10,000,000 so ratios never trip thresholds.
dependencies: LINE Messaging API (/v2/bot/message/quota, /v2/bot/message/quota/consumption), Supabase/Postgres (LineQuotaState), QStash (dispatch_line_quota_handler task)
inventory_notes: LineQuotaState is a singleton (pk=1, save enforced). Recovery clears both line_quota_exhausted_at and line_quota_pre_warn_sent_at so the next calendar month gets a fresh pre-warn event. Both endpoints are free and don't count against the Push cap.

feature: LINE Push quota exhaustion live tripwire
user_story: As the platform, I want the system to detect LINE quota exhaustion immediately when a Push call returns 429 so that users are notified without waiting up to 24 hours for the next poll.
expected_behaviour: _maybe_trip_monthly_quota is called on every non-success LINE Push response. is_monthly_limit_429 checks status_code == 429 AND 'monthly limit' substring in body text. If true, mark_quota_exhausted_from_429 sets LineQuotaState.line_quota_exhausted_at = now and adjusts line_quota_used = line_quota_limit (if known). Returns True only on the first transition (idempotent). On first transition, publishes 'dispatch_line_quota_handler' task via QStash. Errors in QStash publish are logged and swallowed — the next daily poll will re-dispatch for the same event.
inventory_notes: Called from both the webhook Push path and cron delivery Push path. Transient 429s (rate-limit, not monthly cap) are not tripped — the 'monthly limit' substring check differentiates them.

feature: LINE quota exhaustion fan-out — user notification and channel flip
user_story: As the platform, I want affected LINE subscribers to be automatically notified and optionally switched to Telegram when LINE Push is exhausted so that they don't silently stop receiving messages.
expected_behaviour: handle_exhausted (idempotent, guards on line_quota_exhausted_notified_at >= line_quota_exhausted_at). Queries all User rows with preferred_channel='line', line_user_id set, and active tenant. For each user with email: if telegram_chat_id is set → flips preferred_channel to 'telegram', sets preferences.channel_flipped_by_quota=True, sends 'moved to Telegram' email via send_mail. If no telegram_chat_id → sends 'LINE only — connect Telegram to keep receiving' email. Per-user errors are logged and skipped (one bad user record doesn't block the fleet). Sets line_quota_exhausted_notified_at = now after completion. Returns audit dict {flipped, emailed_line_only, errors}.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (User, Tenant, LineQuotaState), Django email (send_mail), email templates in apps/router/templates/email/line_quota/
inventory_notes: Channel flip happens BEFORE the email send so any proactive send between flip and email already routes to Telegram. Users without email addresses are skipped entirely (no flip, no notification). Email templates: user_flipped_to_telegram_*, user_line_only_affected_*.

feature: LINE quota recovery fan-out — LINE available again email
user_story: As a subscriber whose channel was auto-flipped to Telegram, I want to receive an email when LINE is available again so that I can switch back if I prefer LINE.
expected_behaviour: handle_recovered (idempotent, guards on line_quota_recovered_notified_at >= line_quota_exhausted_notified_at). Skips if still exhausted or no prior exhaustion event. Queries User rows with preferences.channel_flipped_by_quota=True and active tenant. For each: sends 'LINE is back — want to switch back?' email with settings_url link. Clears channel_flipped_by_quota preference flag AFTER successful email (failed sends don't clear the flag so retry is possible). Sets line_quota_recovered_notified_at = now after loop. Does NOT auto-flip channel back — user decides via dashboard.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (User, LineQuotaState), Django email (send_mail), email templates (user_line_recovered_*)
inventory_notes: Recovery doesn't auto-flip back — intentional UX decision. Flag is cleared per-user after each successful email send so partial failures retry only the missed users.

feature: LINE quota 90% pre-warn email to platform owner
user_story: As the platform operator, I want to receive an email warning when LINE Push usage crosses 90% so that I can plan ahead for the monthly cap.
expected_behaviour: handle_pre_warn (idempotent, guards on line_quota_pre_warn_sent_at is not None). Skips if usage dropped back below 90% (transition was transient). Reads PLATFORM_OWNER_EMAIL from settings; skips with warning log if not set. Renders email via templates (pre_warn_owner_subject.txt, pre_warn_owner_body.txt) with used/limit/used_pct context. Sends via Django send_mail. Sets line_quota_pre_warn_sent_at = now. Returns True only when email was sent. The pre_warn_sent_at is cleared on recovery so the next month's crossing fires again.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (LineQuotaState), Django email (send_mail, PLATFORM_OWNER_EMAIL setting), templates pre_warn_owner_*
inventory_notes: One email per calendar month per crossing (cleared on recovery). If PLATFORM_OWNER_EMAIL is not configured, pre-warn silently skips.

feature: LINE quota status — preferred channel selector gate
user_story: As a subscriber setting my preferred channel, I want LINE to be blocked if the monthly Push cap is exhausted so that I'm not switched to a broken channel.
expected_behaviour: PATCH /api/v1/tenants/preferred-channel/ with {'preferred_channel': 'line'}. Validates channel is 'telegram' or 'line'. Checks that the requested channel is actually linked (LINE requires line_user_id, Telegram requires telegram_chat_id). For LINE: calls is_quota_exhausted() (one DB lookup on LineQuotaState singleton). If exhausted: returns 409 with error='LINE messaging is at its monthly cap right now. It will be selectable again at the start of next month.' and code='line_quota_exhausted'. Otherwise saves preferred_channel and returns 200 with the new channel. Requires IsAuthenticated.
inventory_notes: Frontend also disables the LINE radio client-side when quota is exhausted; this is the server-side enforcement in case the UI is bypassed.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

feature: LINE link token generation — QR code and deep link
user_story: As a subscriber who wants to connect LINE, I want to generate a QR code and deep link so that I can quickly link my LINE account by scanning the code in the dashboard.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/tenants/line/generate-link/ — requires IsAuthenticated. If user already has line_user_id set, returns 400 'LINE already linked. Unlink first to reconnect.' Otherwise: generates a 32-byte URL-safe random token via secrets.token_urlsafe. Creates LineLinkToken with expires_at = now + 15 minutes. Generates deep link: https://line.me/R/oaMessage/<LINE_BOT_ID>/?link_<token>. Generates QR code as PNG → base64 data URL using qrcode library. Returns JSON with deep_link, qr_code (data URL), and expires_at (ISO 8601).
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (LineLinkToken), qrcode Python library (lazy import), LINE_BOT_ID setting
inventory_notes: Token TTL is 15 minutes. The qrcode library is lazy-imported (only needed here). LINE_BOT_ID must be configured (e.g. '@nbhd-united') or the deep link will be malformed.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to unlink my LINE account from the dashboard so that LINE messages stop being processed.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/tenants/line/unlink/ — requires IsAuthenticated. Calls unlink_line: if user.line_user_id is None, returns False → 400 'LINE not linked.' Otherwise clears line_user_id and line_display_name, saves with update_fields. If preferred_channel was 'line', resets to 'telegram', saves. Returns 200 {success: True}. Does NOT send any message to the LINE user (they may have already blocked the bot). Does NOT affect LineLinkToken rows.
inventory_notes: Preferred channel is reset to 'telegram' unconditionally even if Telegram is not linked. Existing ProactiveOutbound rows referencing the old line_user_id remain but become orphaned.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

feature: LINE link status and quota check
user_story: As a subscriber viewing my settings, I want to see whether LINE is linked and whether it is currently available so that I can make an informed channel selection.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/tenants/line/status/ — requires IsAuthenticated. Returns JSON with linked (bool), line_display_name (if linked), and quota object: {exhausted: bool, checked_at: ISO8601|null, exhausted_at: ISO8601|null}. Reads LineQuotaState singleton (one DB lookup). No side effects.
inventory_notes: Frontend uses quota.exhausted to disable the LINE radio in the channel selector.

feature: LINE proactive context surfacing for thread continuity
user_story: As the system, I want to prepend recent proactive outbound messages to a subscriber's LINE message envelope so that the agent can thread replies to prior cron-initiated conversations.
expected_behaviour: surface_proactive_context is called in _forward_to_container before building the PendingMessage payload. Queries ProactiveOutbound for (tenant, channel='line', line_user_id) within the last 24 hours (configurable window_hours). Limits to 3 rows. Filters out consumed rows older than 5 minutes (follow-up window). Marks first-time-surfaced rows consumed_at = now in a transaction. Renders rows oldest-first as '[earlier-from-you <timestamp> job=<name>:\n<message>\n]' blocks. If any row has parsed_items (numbered/bulleted list), prepends a [thread-rule: …] guidance block instructing the agent to map reply paragraphs by index. Returns empty string if nothing to surface. The surfaced block is prepended before datetime context in the forwarded text.
inventory_notes: ProactiveOutbound is written by CronDeliveryView after each successful LINE push. Consumed rows stay surfaced for 5 more minutes to handle 'thanks, then the real reply' patterns. The parsed_items field extracts bullet/numbered list items for structured thread guidance.

feature: LINE conversation turn capture for cron context digest
user_story: As the system, I want to persist LINE conversation turns to Postgres so that isolated cron sessions can see what was discussed today and avoid reporting 'quiet day' on active conversation days.
expected_behaviour: _capture_conversation_turn is called by _drain_line_batch after a successful delivery. Writes a ConversationTurn row with channel='line', channel_user_id=line_user_id, local_date (tenant-local calendar date), user_text (joined from batch), and reply_text (PII-rehydrated, marker-stripped). Probabilistic pruning: 1% chance per write deletes rows older than 35 days. The ConversationTurn table is read by conversation_capture to render the 'Conversation so far' digest section in USER.md, which is auto-loaded by OpenClaw on every session including isolated cron sessions.
inventory_notes: Only LINE and Telegram turns are stored here. iOS/web turns are already persisted in AppChatMessage and the digest reads that table separately. 35-day self-pruning without a dedicated cron.

feature: LINE message buffering and hibernated container wake
user_story: As a LINE subscriber whose assistant is hibernated, I want my messages to be buffered and the container woken so that I receive a reply once it restarts.
expected_behaviour: When _handle_message detects a live tenant but the container is hibernated (tenant.hibernated_at is set), calls handle_hibernated_message(tenant, 'line', event, text). This creates a BufferedMessage row (channel='line', payload=LINE event dict, user_text=raw text). Updates last_message_at. If no existing undelivered buffered messages (fresh wake): calls wake_hibernated_tenant, returns ACK_FRESH → webhook sends 'waking up' short bubble. If existing messages are older than 5 minutes (stall): clears hibernated_at, calls wake_hibernated_tenant again, returns ACK_RECONNECT → sends 'reconnecting' bubble. If existing messages within 5 minutes (in-flight wake): returns SILENT → no ack message. After wake, a QStash deliver_buffered_messages task forwards buffered messages using relay_ai_response_to_line.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (BufferedMessage, Tenant), apps/orchestrator/hibernation (wake_hibernated_tenant), QStash (deliver_buffered_messages task), LINE Messaging API Push
inventory_notes: BufferedMessage.delivery_in_flight_until provides a soft lease to prevent duplicate delivery by concurrent QStash retries of deliver_buffered_messages. The stall threshold is 5 minutes.

feature: LINE onboarding flow for new subscribers
user_story: As a new LINE subscriber, I want to complete a guided setup flow so that my assistant knows my name, language, and timezone before I start chatting.
expected_behaviour: For tenants where not onboarding_complete or onboarding_step == 0, _handle_message routes through onboarding. Non-text messages (stickers, voice) during onboarding are rejected with a short bubble 'please type your answer'. Text messages are passed to get_onboarding_response(tenant, text, telegram_lang='') — LINE always passes empty string for language since LINE webhooks don't include a language_code. Onboarding replies rendered via _send_onboarding_reply: builds short_bubble Flex, attaches Quick Reply buttons by converting the Telegram keyboard format via telegram_keyboard_to_quick_reply. Country and timezone selection uses postback events (tz_country:*, tz_zone:*) handled by _handle_onboarding_postback → handle_onboarding_callback. needs_reintroduction triggers reset of onboarding_step to 0 for users with stale onboarding data.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (Tenant model), LINE Messaging API Push
inventory_notes: LINE doesn't provide a user language code at the webhook level (unlike Telegram's message.from.language_code), so the language question is always shown. Timezone postback buttons are the primary UX for timezone selection.
error_detail: Confirmed. In _handle_message, the audio branch (lines 913-945) runs unconditionally and early: it shows a loading indicator (924), makes the paid OpenAI Whisper API call via _transcribe_line_audio (925 — downloads audio + POSTs to whisper-1, typically 5-10s), then re-shows loading (933). Only later, at the onboarding gate (lines 1092-1099), does the code reject any non-text message during onboarding with "please type your answer."
