inventory_notes: Must use EmailTokenObtainPairSerializer.get_token() not RefreshToken.for_user() — the latter omits pw_iat and JWTAuthenticationWithRLS would reject the fresh token for users who have a non-null password_last_changed_at.

user_story: As a subscriber or an external app (e.g. YardTalk), I want to authenticate API requests using a long-lived PAT so that I can access the API without session-based JWT tokens.
expected_behaviour: PersonalAccessTokenAuthentication (DEFAULT_AUTHENTICATION_CLASSES[0], checked first) intercepts Authorization headers starting with 'Bearer pat_'. SHA-256 hashes the raw token for DB lookup. Fetches PersonalAccessToken with select_related('user','user__tenant'). Returns 401 if not found. Checks is_valid: revoked_at not null → 401 'Token has been revoked.'; expires_at exceeded → 401 'Token has expired.' On valid token, sets RLS context, stamps last_used_at (throttled to once per minute via update vs save), and stashes the PAT on request.auth_pat for downstream scope checks. Returns (user, pat).
inventory_notes: PAT auth runs before JWT auth in the auth chain. last_used_at update is rate-throttled to once/minute per token to avoid a cross-region write on every request.

user_story: As the platform, I want PAT-authenticated requests to be limited to their declared scopes so that a token issued for session ingestion cannot access unrelated endpoints.
expected_behaviour: HasPATScope (and subclasses HasSessionsWriteScope, HasSessionsReadScope) checks request.auth_pat. If auth_pat is None (JWT-authed request), the scope check passes through — JWT sessions have full access. If auth_pat is set, the required_scope must be in pat.scopes. ALLOWED_PAT_SCOPES currently contains only 'sessions:write' and 'sessions:read'. Views using these permission classes return 403 if the PAT lacks the required scope.
inventory_notes: JWT-authenticated requests bypass scope checks entirely. Only two scopes exist. Scopes are stored as a JSON list on the PersonalAccessToken row.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to create a named personal access token so that external apps can authenticate on my behalf.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/auth/tokens/create/ requires IsAuthenticated (JWT). Rate-limited by UserPATMintHourThrottle (10/hour per user). Accepts {name, scopes (optional, defaults to ['sessions:write','sessions:read']), expires_in_days (optional, 1-365)}. Validates scopes against ALLOWED_PAT_SCOPES — invalid scopes return 400. Generates a cryptographically random token (secrets.token_urlsafe(32) prefixed with 'pat_'), stores only the SHA-256 hash and first-8-char prefix. Returns {id, name, token (raw, shown ONCE), token_prefix, scopes, expires_at, created_at, warning} with 201. Raw token is never stored.
inventory_notes: Raw token is shown exactly once in the response. Token format: 'pat_' + base64url(32 random bytes). Hash stored is SHA-256 of the full 'pat_...' string.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to list my active personal access tokens so that I can see what apps have access and when they were last used.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/auth/tokens/ requires IsAuthenticated (JWT). Returns all non-revoked (revoked_at__isnull=True) PersonalAccessTokens for request.user. Serialized fields: id, name, token_prefix, scopes, last_used_at, expires_at, revoked_at, created_at. Does not return the raw token (stored only at creation). Returns 200 with a list.
inventory_notes: Filter is revoked_at__isnull=True so expired-but-not-revoked tokens still appear.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to revoke a personal access token so that an app can no longer authenticate using it.
expected_behaviour: DELETE /api/v1/auth/tokens/<uuid:token_id>/ requires IsAuthenticated (JWT). Looks up PersonalAccessToken by id and user (ownership check) — not found returns 404. If already revoked (revoked_at is not None) returns 400 'Token already revoked.' Otherwise stamps revoked_at=now() via save(update_fields=['revoked_at']). Returns 204 No Content. After revocation, PersonalAccessTokenAuthentication rejects any request bearing this token with 'Token has been revoked.'
inventory_notes: Ownership enforced by user=request.user filter. No admin override path on this endpoint.

user_story: As a subscriber completing sign-up on the web, I want the SPA to mint a short-lived authorization code so that the iOS app can exchange it for a JWT pair without re-entering credentials.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/auth/authorize/ requires IsAuthenticated (JWT). Rate-limited by AuthorizeMintMinuteThrottle (30/minute per user). Accepts {code_challenge (S256 base64url), code_challenge_method ('S256'), redirect_uri}. Validates: method must be 'S256'; code_challenge must be non-empty and match base64url alphabet (_B64URL_RE); redirect_uri must be in settings.AUTH_ALLOWED_REDIRECT_URIS (default ['nbhd://auth/callback']). On failure returns 400 {error: 'invalid_request'}. On success generates a 32-byte cryptographically random code, stores only the SHA-256 hash plus the code_challenge in OAuthAuthorizationCode row with TTL from AUTH_EXCHANGE_CODE_TTL_SECONDS (default 300s). Returns {code: <raw_code>} with 200.
inventory_notes: Only S256 method supported — plain not allowed. redirect_uri whitelist is in AUTH_ALLOWED_REDIRECT_URIS env var. The raw code travels only in the JSON response body; the hash is stored.

user_story: As the iOS app, I want to exchange a PKCE authorization code and code_verifier for a JWT pair so that the user is signed into the app without entering a password.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/auth/exchange/ is AllowAny. Rate-limited by ExchangeMinuteThrottle (30/minute per client IP via X-Forwarded-For). Accepts {code, code_verifier, redirect_uri}. Missing fields → 400 {error: invalid_grant}. Looks up OAuthAuthorizationCode by SHA-256(code); not found → 400 invalid_grant (logged). Inside a SELECT FOR UPDATE atomic transaction: checks consumed_at is None and now() < expires_at; redirect_uri matched via hmac.compare_digest (constant-time); PKCE verified by re-deriving S256(verifier) and constant-time comparing to stored code_challenge. User must be active. Code is marked consumed_at=now() inside the lock. Returns {access, refresh} minted via EmailTokenObtainPairSerializer.get_token() (carries pw_iat) with 200. ALL failures return identical 400 {error: invalid_grant}.
inventory_notes: select_for_update prevents double-spend race. All failure reasons logged server-side only, never surfaced to caller. pw_iat claim required (not RefreshToken.for_user).

user_story: As the platform, I want every password change to automatically bump password_last_changed_at so that all outstanding JWTs are invalidated without needing a token blacklist.
expected_behaviour: User.set_password() overrides AbstractUser.set_password() to call super() then set self.password_last_changed_at = timezone.now(). Similarly User.set_unusable_password() calls super() and sets the same stamp. This means the stamp is bumped on: admin password changes, password reset confirm, createsuperuser, and the rotate_all_passwords management command. The change in memory is committed only when the caller explicitly saves — callers must include 'password_last_changed_at' in update_fields to avoid silently dropping the bump.
inventory_notes: The PasswordResetConfirmView and rotate_all_passwords explicitly include 'password_last_changed_at' in update_fields. Legacy users (pw_iat=0 tokens) are only rejected if password_last_changed_at is non-null.

user_story: As the platform, I want every request to set and clear Postgres session-level RLS variables so that row-level security policies are enforced for the duration of each request.
expected_behaviour: TenantContextMiddleware.process_request() sets thread-local _tenant_context.tenant and calls set_rls_context(tenant_id, user_id) if user is authenticated and has a tenant. set_rls_context() issues a single SELECT SET_CONFIG(...) statement bundling all variables to minimize cross-region round trips. process_response() clears the context and calls reset_rls_context(), which issues another SET_CONFIG reset only when _tenant_context.rls_dirty=True (skipping unauthenticated/404 responses to avoid unnecessary DB connections). UserTimezoneMiddleware activates the user's IANA timezone for the request lifecycle.
inventory_notes: rls_dirty flag optimization avoids opening a DB connection on every response for unauthenticated requests. JWTAuthenticationWithRLS also calls set_rls_context after auth — this may set vars twice on authenticated requests, but is idempotent.

user_story: As an admin/operator, I want to force-rotate all users' passwords and email them reset links so that a security event results in every session being invalidated fleet-wide.
expected_behaviour: python manage.py rotate_all_passwords --reason=<string> [--dry-run] [--since=<ISO datetime>]. Excludes the PLATFORM_OWNER_EMAIL user. Iterates all users skipping: those with no email, those whose password_last_changed_at >= --since (idempotency). For each eligible user: calls user.set_unusable_password() (bumps password_last_changed_at, invalidates all JWTs), saves with update_fields, generates a uid+token, sends a reset email using privacy_rotation_2026/email_1_* templates. Email send failures are logged but do not stop iteration. Prints rotated/skipped/failed counts. Idempotent: re-running with the same --since skips already-rotated users.
inventory_notes: Also callable via QStash as rotate_all_passwords_task() with hardcoded 'june-2026-privacy-hygiene' reason. PLATFORM_OWNER_EMAIL must be set or operator locks themselves out.
error_detail: Real. Password is rotated and committed at line 107 (set_unusable_password bumps password_last_changed_at via the model override at models.py:109-118, invalidating all of that user's JWTs) BEFORE the reset email is attempted at line 115. The rotated counter increments only on email success (line 116); on email failure only email_failed increments (line 118) and the user is left locked out. Because the idempotency key IS password_last_changed_at, re-running with the same --since (the documented recovery, docstring lines 12-13) sees the bumped stamp at line 96 and SKIPS that user, so the reset email is never auto-resent. The user is locked out (no session, no reset link) with no automated recovery; manual only, which the code itself admits is unbuilt (line 132: --resend-only is a future enhancement). OVERSTATED sub-claim: the 'summary undercounts/misleads the operator' part is weaker than stated -- email_failed IS printed with a loud warning and an explicit 'check logs and manually resend' instruction (lines 128-134), so the operator is told the follow-up count and is not actually misled. Also note the QStash task path (tasks.py rotate_all_passwords_task) passes no --since, so an automatic re-fire uses a fresh now() cutoff that would re-rotate/re-email the failed user; the silent-skip lockout specifically bites the documented manual same---since retry. No test covers the email-failure-then-retry path (test_idempotent_on_rerun is happy-path only). Net: genuine lockout/data-integrity gap for users hit by a transient email failure during the campaign.

user_story: As the platform, I want to send a Day-0 welcome email after a tenant is provisioned so that new subscribers know how to connect Telegram and get started.
expected_behaviour: send_welcome_email(tenant) in apps/tenants/emails.py is called during provisioning. Idempotent: if tenant.welcome_email_sent_at is already set, returns False immediately. If user has no email address, skips and returns False. Checks LINE quota availability via LineQuotaState; if exhausted, uses 'Telegram' instead of 'Telegram or LINE' in the CTA to avoid offering a capped channel. Sends multipart HTML+text via Mailgun using welcome_subject/body templates. On confirmed send, stamps tenant.welcome_email_sent_at and saves. Exceptions are caught and logged (never bubbles to caller).
dependencies: Mailgun (via Django email backend), Supabase/Postgres, apps/router/models.py:LineQuotaState
inventory_notes: Non-critical — caller (orchestrator provisioning flow) must not roll back tenant state if this fails. welcome_email_sent_at is the idempotency stamp.

user_story: As a subscriber who received a promotional email, I want to click a signed redemption link so that my trial period is extended without needing to log in.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/tenants/promos/redeem/?code=<campaign_code>&token=<hmac_signed> is AllowAny. Validates: both params present; PromoCampaign exists for code; campaign not past valid_until; HMAC token (Django Signer with salt 'nbhd.promos.v1') verifies and campaign code inside token matches URL param; User and Tenant exist. Active Stripe subscribers are redirected with status=active_subscription. Already-redeemed (unique_together IntegrityError) redirects with status=already. On success: inserts PromoRedemption row first (outside atomic block), then in atomic block extends trial_ends_at, sets is_trial=True, sets status=ACTIVE. Always 302-redirects to {FRONTEND_URL}/promo/redeemed?status=<outcome>.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres, Mailgun (send side via send_promo_campaign command)
inventory_notes: Double-click idempotency via unique_together (campaign, user) — IntegrityError is caught and treated as 'already'. Redemption row inserted outside atomic() to avoid poisoning a parent transaction on IntegrityError.

user_story: As the platform, I want PAT-authenticated API calls to be rate-limited per token so that a single misbehaving app cannot exhaust server or billing budgets.
expected_behaviour: PATSessionIngestMinuteThrottle (60/minute) and PATSessionIngestDayThrottle (5000/day) are keyed by PAT id (not user). Non-PAT (JWT) requests return None from get_cache_key(), bypassing throttle. UserPATMintHourThrottle (10/hour) is applied at PAT creation (PATCreateView), keyed by user id. ExchangeMinuteThrottle (30/minute) is keyed per client IP, honours X-Forwarded-For. AuthorizeMintMinuteThrottle (30/minute) is keyed per user id. Throttle rates are backed by Django cache (Redis).
inventory_notes: Blast radius of a leaked/abused PAT is limited to 5000 requests/day rather than unlimited. Revoking the PAT immediately stops all further auth.

user_story: As the platform, I want expired Telegram link tokens to be purged regularly so that stale rows do not accumulate in the database.
expected_behaviour: cleanup_expired_telegram_tokens() in apps/tenants/tasks.py deletes all TelegramLinkToken rows where expires_at < now() - 1 hour. Called via QStash cron. Returns a string with count of deleted rows for audit logging. No side effects beyond the DELETE.
inventory_notes: TelegramLinkToken is a short-lived QR-pairing token, not a JWT. Separate model from PersonalAccessToken.

user_story: As an admin/operator, I want to view, search, and manage User and Tenant records in the Django admin so that I can investigate issues and perform manual interventions.
expected_behaviour: UserAdmin extends BaseUserAdmin with Telegram fields (telegram_chat_id, telegram_user_id, telegram_username, display_name, language, preferences) exposed as an extra fieldset. List display shows username, display_name, telegram_chat_id, is_active. TenantAdmin shows user, status, model_tier, is_budget_exempt, container_id, messages_today, messages_this_month, created_at. Filterable by status/model_tier/is_budget_exempt. Searchable by user__username/display_name/container_id. id, created_at, updated_at, provisioned_at are read-only. Standard Django admin auth (session-based) required.
inventory_notes: Admin does not enforce tenant-scoped RLS — admin users access all rows. Password rotation via admin triggers set_password override and bumps password_last_changed_at.

user_story: As the platform, I want to send a Day-0 welcome email to each newly provisioned subscriber so that they know their assistant is ready and how to connect a messaging channel.
expected_behaviour: Called from apps/orchestrator/services.py at provisioning step 4c after the optional Telegram welcome message is sent. Reads Tenant.welcome_email_sent_at as an idempotency guard — if already non-null, returns False immediately without sending. Skips silently if User.email is blank. Checks LineQuotaState.get().is_exhausted to decide channel_label: 'Telegram or LINE' when quota is available, 'Telegram' otherwise. Renders email/welcome_subject.txt (two subject variants based on telegram_connected), email/welcome_body.txt, and email/welcome_body.html with context: display_name, telegram_connected, settings_url ({FRONTEND_URL}/settings/integrations), video_url (from WELCOME_VIDEO_URL setting, optional), line_quota_available, channel_label. Sends via Django send_mail (DEFAULT_FROM_EMAIL). On success stamps Tenant.welcome_email_sent_at via update_fields=['welcome_email_sent_at','updated_at']. Exceptions are caught and logged; callers must not roll back provisioning on failure. Returns True if sent, False on any skip or failure.
dependencies: Mailgun (via Django email backend, DEFAULT_FROM_EMAIL), apps/router/models.py:LineQuotaState, Supabase/Postgres (Tenant row update)
inventory_notes: Subject line branches on User.telegram_chat_id: 'Welcome — your assistant just said hi' vs 'Welcome — pick a channel to start chatting'. LINE P.S. postscript only rendered when Telegram is connected AND LINE quota is not exhausted. Optional video_url block gated on WELCOME_VIDEO_URL setting. Fails-closed on LINE quota lookup (omits P.S. rather than risk 429). Provisioning retries are safe because of the idempotency stamp.

user_story: As the platform, I want to record the exact timestamp of a subscriber's first-ever inbound message so that I can measure onboarding activation rates.
expected_behaviour: Called inside apps/router/pending_queue.py:enqueue_message_for_tenant after every PendingMessage creation. Uses a conditional UPDATE: Tenant.objects.filter(id=tenant.id, first_message_at__isnull=True).update(first_message_at=timezone.now()). This means only the first call on a never-messaged tenant writes the stamp; all subsequent calls are no-ops at the DB level. Works across all channels (telegram, line, app) because it's at the PendingMessage chokepoint. No API surface; purely a side effect of enqueueing a message.
inventory_notes: Race-safe via the conditional UPDATE — two concurrent first messages don't overwrite each other. The stamp is never bumped again after the first write. Used to measure onboarding drop-off cohort.

user_story: As the OpenClaw container, I want to mark a feature welcome as delivered after successfully sending the in-chat welcome message so that the deploy-time backfill can skip already-served tenants.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/tenants/runtime/<tenant_id>/welcomes/<feature>/. Requires X-NBHD-Internal-Key and X-NBHD-Tenant-Id headers matching the per-tenant internal_api_key and URL tenant_id; returns 401 on auth failure. Valid feature values: 'fuel', 'finance', 'core' (case-insensitive strip); returns 400 with error='unknown_feature' and 'valid' list for anything else. Returns 404 if tenant not found. On success sets Tenant.welcomes_sent[feature] = ISO-8601 timestamp now via update_fields=['welcomes_sent','updated_at']. Idempotent — re-calls overwrite the timestamp without error. Returns 200 JSON {feature, sent_at}. Triggering this save also fires the agenda_signals post_save handler which mirrors the welcomes_sent transition into AgendaEngagement.FEATURE_INTRO as COMPLETED (on_commit).
dependencies: apps/integrations/internal_auth.py:validate_internal_runtime_request, Supabase/Postgres (Tenant row update), apps/tenants/agenda_signals.py (post_save mirror to AgendaEngagement)
inventory_notes: Uses AllowAny DRF permission class; authorization is entirely in the internal-key validation. AgendaEngagement mirror fires via transaction.on_commit so it doesn't fire inside test TestCase transactions — only in TransactionTestCase.

user_story: As the platform, I want to automatically record an AgendaEngagement COMPLETED event when a per-feature welcome is delivered so that the engagement renderer can suppress repeat onboarding prompts consistently.
expected_behaviour: Wired via pre_save + @receiver(post_save) on Tenant in apps/tenants/agenda_signals.py. pre_save captures the prior welcomes_sent dict from DB into _PRE_SAVE_WELCOMES keyed by tenant PK. post_save diffs current vs prior: for each key that transitioned from absent/null to a timestamp, schedules mark_surfaced (kind=FEATURE_INTRO, item_id=feature, signal='welcome_delivered') + mark_state (state=COMPLETED) via transaction.on_commit. Idempotent: only transitions (null → timestamp) trigger the mirror; stable non-null values on subsequent saves are no-ops. Does not fire inside TestCase (on_commit deferred).
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (AgendaEngagement writes via apps/tenants/agenda_service.py), Django signal framework
inventory_notes: Uses _PRE_SAVE_WELCOMES module-level dict — not safe for multiprocessing without reset; transient signals under WSGI/multi-worker are fine because each request has one pre+post cycle. The mirror is best-effort; exceptions are logged, not re-raised.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

feature: Promo campaign creation and email fan-out (send_promo_campaign management command)
user_story: As an operator, I want to create a promotional campaign and email per-user HMAC-signed redemption links to eligible subscribers so that I can offer trial extensions for retention campaigns.
expected_behaviour: management command: python manage.py send_promo_campaign --code <slug> --kind trial_extension --days <int> --valid-until <ISO8601> [--dry-run]. Audience filter: Users with a tenant AND (status=ACTIVE + is_trial=True OR status=SUSPENDED + stripe_subscription_id=''), excluding blank email and PLATFORM_OWNER_EMAIL. --dry-run prints audience without creating the row or sending. Creates PromoCampaign via get_or_create(code=code) — first run wins the audience snapshot (audience_snapshot JSON with user_ids + captured_at_count), subsequent re-runs reuse the same row and email everyone again (idempotent send; double-redemption is blocked at the view layer). For each user, calls make_promo_token(campaign_code, user_id) then builds {FRONTEND_URL}/promo/redeem?code=<code>&token=<signed> and sends using email/privacy_rotation_2026/email_2_{subject,body}.txt/.html templates with context {display_name, promo_url, valid_until}. Per-user email failures are caught, logged, and counted; the command continues to remaining users.
dependencies: Mailgun (via Django email backend), Supabase/Postgres (PromoCampaign create/get, User/Tenant queries), apps/tenants/promo_signing.py:make_promo_token, QStash (dispatch via cron/views TASK_MAP)
inventory_notes: send_promo_campaign_task() is a QStash-dispatched wrapper hardcoding code='privacy-zdr-2026', days=14, valid_until='2026-06-24T00:00:00+00:00' — designed to fire once. The command itself is reusable for future campaigns with different parameters. Re-running the command with the same --code does NOT widen the audience snapshot but DOES re-email the original audience (redemption idempotency is enforced by the view, not the command).

feature: Promo HMAC token signing and verification
user_story: As the platform, I want to sign per-user redemption tokens for promo campaigns so that only the intended recipient can redeem the trial extension from their email inbox without needing to be logged in.
expected_behaviour: apps/tenants/promo_signing.py exports make_promo_token(campaign_code, user_id) and verify_promo_token(expected_campaign_code, token). make_promo_token uses Django Signer(salt='nbhd.promos.v1').sign('{code}:{user_id}') — deterministic for the same inputs so re-sends produce identical tokens. verify_promo_token unsigns the token, splits on ':', and checks that the embedded campaign_code matches expected_campaign_code; returns user_id string on success, None on BadSignature, mismatched code, or malformed payload. Expiry is NOT enforced in the token — the campaign's valid_until on the PromoCampaign row is the deadline.
inventory_notes: Salt 'nbhd.promos.v1' scopes promo tokens to prevent substitution with other signed payloads in the codebase. Token is stable across re-sends of the same campaign for the same user — useful for partial retry after Mailgun outage. Mismatched campaign code is treated as forgery rather than silent pass.

user_story: As a subscriber who received a promo email, I want to click the redemption link to extend my trial so that I can continue using my assistant without subscribing immediately.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/tenants/promos/redeem/?code=<campaign_code>&token=<signed_token> — unauthenticated (AllowAny). Validation chain: (1) missing code or token → 302 to {FRONTEND_URL}/promo/redeemed?status=invalid; (2) campaign not found → invalid; (3) timezone.now() >= campaign.valid_until → 302 status=expired; (4) verify_promo_token fails → invalid; (5) user not found → invalid; (6) user has no tenant → records PromoRedemption(outcome=NO_TENANT) → invalid; (7) tenant has stripe_subscription_id → records PromoRedemption(outcome=ALREADY_SUBSCRIBED) → 302 status=active_subscription. Success path: base = max(now, tenant.trial_ends_at if future else now); new_trial_ends_at = base + timedelta(days=campaign.extension_days). Inserts PromoRedemption row OUTSIDE atomic block first (unique_together(campaign,user) catches concurrent double-clicks → returns 302 status=already without updating tenant). Then wraps tenant update in atomic: sets trial_ends_at, is_trial=True, status=ACTIVE. On tenant update failure: deletes the redemption row to allow retry, returns status=invalid. On success: 302 status=success.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (PromoCampaign lookup, PromoRedemption insert, Tenant update), apps/tenants/promo_signing.py:verify_promo_token, Next.js frontend /promo/redeemed route (redirect target)
inventory_notes: Insert-first pattern (redemption row outside atomic, tenant update inside) avoids broken Django savepoint state when catching IntegrityError inside an active atomic block. Paying subscribers (stripe_subscription_id non-empty) are blocked even if they made it past the audience filter in the send command — is_trial is not re-set for them.

user_story: As an operator, I want to send a rendered preview of any campaign email to my own inbox before a fleet-wide send so that I can visually verify the HTML renders correctly across email clients.
expected_behaviour: management command: python manage.py preview_email --kind <1|2> --to <email> [--display-name <name>]. kind=1 renders email/privacy_rotation_2026/email_1_{subject,body}.{txt,html} with sample reset_url 'https://neighborhoodunited.org/reset-password?uid=PREVIEW&token=PREVIEW'; kind=2 renders email/privacy_rotation_2026/email_2_{subject,body}.{txt,html} with sample promo_url and valid_until=2026-06-24. Prepends '[PREVIEW]' to subject. Sends via send_mail to the --to address. Invalid kind raises CommandError (only 1 and 2 accepted). Also available as a QStash task (preview_email_task) callable with kind, to, display_name kwargs — allows operator to trigger a preview remotely without container exec.
dependencies: Mailgun (via Django email backend), QStash (optional remote dispatch)
inventory_notes: preview_email_task validates kind in (1, 2) and raises ValueError for invalid values — QStash will retry on ValueError unless the task is idempotent-flagged. The sample promo_url in kind=2 preview points to the live redeem flow but the token 'PREVIEW' won't pass HMAC verification.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

feature: Fleet-wide password rotation with reset email (privacy campaign)
user_story: As an operator running a privacy hygiene campaign, I want to force-rotate every subscriber's password and email them a reset link so that users must set a fresh password and all outstanding JWTs are invalidated.
expected_behaviour: management command: python manage.py rotate_all_passwords --reason=<string> [--dry-run] [--since <ISO8601>]. Excludes PLATFORM_OWNER_EMAIL user from rotation (warns if not set). For each remaining User: skips if no email, skips if password_last_changed_at >= --since cutoff (idempotency on retry). Calls user.set_unusable_password() (which bumps password_last_changed_at via model override and therefore invalidates all outstanding JWTs for that user via the pw_iat JWT claim), saves with update_fields=['password','password_last_changed_at']. Then calls _send_reset_email: generates Django default_token_generator.make_token + urlsafe_base64_encode(user.pk) and renders email/privacy_rotation_2026/email_1_{subject,body}.{txt,html} with reset_url={FRONTEND_URL}/reset-password?uid=&token=. Per-user email failures are logged but do NOT prevent password rotation (user is locked out until manual resend). --dry-run prints would-rotate list without any mutations. Also registered in QStash TASK_MAP as 'rotate_all_passwords' wrapping rotate_all_passwords_task().
dependencies: Mailgun (via Django email backend), Supabase/Postgres (User.password + password_last_changed_at update), Django default_token_generator, QStash (optional dispatch)
inventory_notes: CRITICAL: if email send fails after the password rotation, the user is locked out with no usable password and no reset link — they need a manual resend. The --since cutoff defaults to now() at command start so a single run rotates everyone; subsequent runs with the same --since value are idempotent. Users with no email address are skipped (rotated users with email only).
error_detail: The mechanism is real. set_unusable_password() (line 106) bumps password_last_changed_at and the save persists it (line 107) BEFORE the email try-block. On email failure, email_failed is incremented and rotated is not (lines 117-122), but the password row is already mutated. The skip check (lines 96-98) compares password_last_changed_at >= since_dt and routes such users to skipped_already on re-run. So if the operator re-runs with a --since at or before a failed user's rotation time, that user is never re-emailed and stays with an unusable password and no reset link, locked out until manual intervention. There is no --resend-only flag (acknowledged as a future enhancement at line 132). However, severity is lower than the QA verdict's medium: this is fully documented behavior (docstring lines 10-13 and 63-66; ERROR warning printed at lines 128-134; inventory_notes), not a hidden bug. A documented workaround exists: re-running with an explicit --since EARLIER than the failure time (e.g. the original campaign start, the usage shown at line 19) causes failed users' password_last_changed_at to not satisfy >= since, so they fall through to rotation and re-email. The footgun only bites when the operator relied on the default --since=now() on the first run and then re-runs without reusing that exact timestamp. Real but narrow operational ergonomics gap with a self-documented recovery path, not a silent data-integrity defect. When it does bite, affected subscribers cannot log in (unusable password) and never receive a fresh reset link via the naive retry.

feature: Privacy rotation campaign email 1 (password reset template)
user_story: As a subscriber who received the privacy rotation email, I want to click the reset link so that I can set a new password and regain access to the dashboard.
expected_behaviour: Template set: email/privacy_rotation_2026/email_1_{subject,body}.txt + email_1_body.html. Subject: 'Action required: reset your NBHD United password'. Context vars: display_name, reset_url. The reset_url points to {FRONTEND_URL}/reset-password?uid=<b64>&token=<django_token> where the token is from default_token_generator.make_token(user) on the user's newly set unusable password hash. The token auto-invalidates when the user sets a new password (hash changes) or after PASSWORD_RESET_TIMEOUT (Django default 3 days). Rendered only by the rotate_all_passwords command (not the self-service forgot-password flow, which uses email/password_reset_body.html).
inventory_notes: Dark-themed Constellation aesthetic HTML with Outlook VML fallback for the CTA button and gradient headline. Separate from the self-service password reset email (email/password_reset_body.html) which has different template structure and context.

feature: Privacy rotation campaign email 2 (promo trial extension template)
user_story: As a subscriber targeted by a retention campaign, I want to receive a promotional email with a trial extension offer so that I can redeem extra free days.
expected_behaviour: Template set: email/privacy_rotation_2026/email_2_{subject,body}.txt + email_2_body.html. Subject: 'Your data stays yours — and 14 days, on us'. Context vars: display_name, promo_url ({FRONTEND_URL}/promo/redeem?code=&token=), valid_until (rendered as 'Good through ...' deadline). Used by both send_promo_campaign management command and the preview_email kind=2 command. The valid_until is passed from the PromoCampaign.valid_until field so copy stays synchronized with actual deadline.
inventory_notes: The valid_until template variable was introduced to fix a copy-drift bug where the original templates hardcoded 'June 6, 2026' and went stale as the campaign deadline was extended.

user_story: As a subscriber who forgot their password, I want to request a password reset email so that I can regain access to my dashboard.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/auth/password-reset/request/ with body {email}. AllowAny. Rate-limited: 3 requests per email per hour (key pwd_reset_email:<email>) AND 5 requests per IP per hour (key pwd_reset_ip:<IP>, from X-Forwarded-For or REMOTE_ADDR). Both buckets checked before any DB work; returns HTTP 429 on either limit breach. Timing-safe: always returns 200 {detail: '...'} regardless of whether the email exists, is active, or fails — account enumeration is prevented. If user exists and is_active: generates uid=urlsafe_base64_encode(user.pk) + token=default_token_generator.make_token(user), calls _send_password_reset_email which renders email/password_reset_{subject,body}.txt + password_reset_body.html with context {user, reset_url, display_name} and sends via Django send_mail. Email send failures are caught and logged; the 200 response is still returned. Cache unavailability fails-open on rate limiting (logs warning, proceeds).
dependencies: Mailgun (via Django email backend), Redis/cache (rate-limit counters), Supabase/Postgres (User lookup), Django default_token_generator
inventory_notes: Rate limit window is 1 hour (PASSWORD_RESET_RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_SECONDS=3600). Cache failure fails-open (does not block the request) to avoid locking real users out during a Redis blip. The reset link points to {FRONTEND_URL}/reset-password?uid=&token= handled by PasswordResetConfirmView.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

user_story: As a subscriber who clicked a password reset link, I want to set a new password and be automatically signed in so that I land on the dashboard without a second login step.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/auth/password-reset/confirm/ with body {uid, token, new_password}. AllowAny. Returns 400 if any of uid, token, new_password is missing. Decodes uid via urlsafe_base64_decode; 400 if user not found or uid malformed. Validates token via default_token_generator.check_token(user, token) — tokens auto-invalidate on password change and after PASSWORD_RESET_TIMEOUT (default 3 days); 400 with 'Reset link is invalid or has expired.' if invalid. Validates new_password via Django validate_password(new_password, user=user) — returns 400 with joined validation messages on failure. Calls user.set_password(new_password) which bumps password_last_changed_at (model override). Saves with update_fields=['password','password_last_changed_at'] — MUST include password_last_changed_at to persist the stamp. Mints a fresh JWT pair via EmailTokenObtainPairSerializer.get_token(user) (which includes the pw_iat claim) and returns 200 {access, refresh}.
dependencies: Supabase/Postgres (User password update), Django default_token_generator, apps/tenants/serializers.py:EmailTokenObtainPairSerializer (pw_iat JWT claim)
inventory_notes: Must use EmailTokenObtainPairSerializer.get_token (not RefreshToken.for_user) to mint the post-reset JWT, otherwise the pw_iat claim is absent and JWTAuthenticationWithRLS immediately rejects the brand-new token for users with a non-null password_last_changed_at. update_fields MUST include password_last_changed_at — omitting it silently drops the stamp which defeats force-logout.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to receive a clear, branded password reset email so that I can identify the link and click it confidently.
expected_behaviour: Template set: email/password_reset_subject.txt (subject: 'Reset your NBHD United password'), email/password_reset_body.txt, email/password_reset_body.html. Context vars: user (User model instance), reset_url ({FRONTEND_URL}/reset-password?uid=&token=), display_name. Rendered only by the self-service PasswordResetRequestView flow (not the fleet rotate_all_passwords command which uses the privacy_rotation_2026/email_1 templates). Dark-themed Constellation aesthetic HTML with NBHD star icon.
inventory_notes: Separate template set from privacy_rotation_2026/email_1 — different structure and no display_name from campaign context. The html template uses autoescape on and a minimal dark card layout.

user_story: As an operator, I want a database record for each promotional campaign with a unique code, kind, extension_days, valid_until deadline, and audience snapshot so that redemptions can be validated and audited.
expected_behaviour: apps/tenants/promo_models.py:PromoCampaign — table 'promo_campaigns'. Fields: id (UUID PK), code (SlugField max_length=64, unique), kind (TextChoices: TRIAL_EXTENSION), extension_days (PositiveSmallIntegerField), valid_until (DateTimeField), audience_snapshot (JSONField, shape {user_ids: [...], captured_at_count: int}), created_at (auto). Ordered by -created_at. The code field is used in redemption URLs and token signing — must be URL-safe. The audience_snapshot is written once at campaign creation time by the send_promo_campaign command (first-run wins semantics via get_or_create); re-runs don't overwrite it.
inventory_notes: Only one Kind value exists (TRIAL_EXTENSION) — model docstring notes future: STRIPE_COUPON, FEATURE_UNLOCK. The unique code constraint is load-bearing for the get_or_create idempotency in the send command.

user_story: As the platform, I want to record each promo redemption attempt with its outcome and the resulting trial_ends_at so that double-redemptions are blocked at the database layer and the audit trail is queryable.
expected_behaviour: apps/tenants/promo_models.py:PromoRedemption — table 'promo_redemptions'. Fields: id (UUID PK), campaign (FK PromoCampaign), user (FK AUTH_USER_MODEL), redeemed_at (auto), outcome (TextChoices: EXTENDED, ALREADY_SUBSCRIBED, NO_TENANT), new_trial_ends_at (DateTimeField nullable). unique_together=[('campaign','user')] is the load-bearing double-click guard — IntegrityError on the second insert is caught by redeem_promo and returns 'already' status. Index on (user, -redeemed_at). Outcome NO_TENANT and ALREADY_SUBSCRIBED rows are recorded for audit even though the trial is not extended.
inventory_notes: _record_redemption wraps the insert in transaction.atomic() (savepoint) to contain IntegrityError without poisoning a parent transaction — critical in test environments where the outer TestCase transaction is already open.

user_story: As the platform, I want to track per-feature welcome delivery timestamps on each tenant so that deploy-time backfills can skip already-served tenants and retry only those where the welcome failed silently.
expected_behaviour: Tenant.welcomes_sent is a JSONField (default={}) storing per-feature ISO-8601 timestamps, e.g. {'fuel': '2026-05-07T...', 'finance': '...', 'core': '...'}. Set by RuntimeWelcomeMarkView (POST from container after successful welcome send) and also read/written by apps/fuel/views.py and apps/core/views.py when scheduling the welcome cron (they may pre-populate the key if the cron is already registered). The deploy-time backfill skips tenants whose welcomes_sent[feature] is already set. Cleared implicitly only by explicit removal — not cleared on monthly resets. Changes to this field trigger the agenda_signals post_save handler which mirrors transitions into AgendaEngagement.
entry_points: apps/tenants/models.py:Tenant.welcomes_sent ; apps/tenants/runtime_views.py:RuntimeWelcomeMarkView ; apps/fuel/views.py (fuel welcome cron scheduling) ; apps/core/views.py (core welcome cron scheduling)
inventory_notes: Valid feature keys are 'fuel', 'finance', 'core' (enforced by RuntimeWelcomeMarkView). The JSON field persists across months and container restarts. The backfill logic lives in the respective feature's task/view, not in tenants.

feature: Tenant quota warning and exhaustion email stamps
user_story: As the platform, I want to track when quota warning and exhaustion emails were last sent to each tenant so that I can prevent duplicate notifications when usage hovers near the threshold.
expected_behaviour: Tenant model carries two DateTimeField idempotency markers: cost_warn_sent_at (90%-of-cap warning email) and cost_exhausted_email_sent_at (cap-exhausted email). Both are null until the corresponding email fires; both are cleared to None by reset_monthly_counters (apps/tenants/services.py) at the start of each billing month. The actual email-sending logic lives in apps/router/billing_quota_handlers.py:fire_threshold_emails_if_crossed and apps/billing/management/commands/reconcile_openrouter_spend.py, not in the tenants app itself — these fields are the storage layer only.
inventory_notes: These are storage/idempotency fields only — the actual email template rendering and sending happens in apps/router and apps/billing. The monthly counter reset (clear-to-null) ensures the first over-threshold usage each billing month always triggers a fresh notification.

user_story: As an anonymous user, I want to create an account with email and password so that I can access the NBHD United platform.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/auth/signup/ accepts {email, password, display_name?, invite_code?}. Email and password are required (400 if missing). If PREVIEW_ACCESS_KEY is set, invite_code must match exactly (403 otherwise). Duplicate email returns 409. On success, creates User with username=email, hashes password, sets display_name; returns {access, refresh} JWT pair with 201. No Tenant is created here — onboarding is a separate step. Password is hashed by Django's create_user; password_last_changed_at is NOT set at signup (only set by set_password/set_unusable_password).
inventory_notes: RefreshToken.for_user is used here (not EmailTokenObtainPairSerializer), so the access token does NOT carry the pw_iat claim. This means JWTAuthenticationWithRLS will accept it even for users with a non-null password_last_changed_at, because pw_iat=0 is only rejected when password_last_changed_at is non-null.
error_detail: Confirmed. SignupView.post() (apps/tenants/auth_views.py:187-227) goes from the required-fields check straight to User.objects.create_user(password=password, ...) and never calls validate_password(), even though validate_password is imported (line 7) and the project configures a full AUTH_PASSWORD_VALIDATORS set (config/settings/base.py:105-110: MinimumLength, CommonPassword, Numeric, UserAttributeSimilarity). The sibling PasswordResetConfirmView DOES enforce these validators (line 153) before set_password, creating a genuine inconsistency: an attacker/user POSTing directly to /api/v1/auth/signup/ can register with a password as weak as a single character, all-numeric, or a common word like 'password'. The only strength gate on signup is client-side (frontend/app/signup/page.tsx:30, password.length < 8), trivially bypassed by hitting the API directly. The dedup/token half of the claim is accurate but benign and intended: create_user routes through Django's _create_user -> make_password (not the custom set_password override), so password_last_changed_at stays null at signup; the JWT validator treats a null stamp as 'never rotated' and accepts the RefreshToken.for_user token (which lacks pw_iat) -- documented intended behaviour per the feature record and models.py:80-91, not a defect. Impact is low: weak passwords only weaken the account holder's own login (easier brute-force/credential-stuffing of one self-owned account), with no platform-wide blast radius, and the normal browser path is gated client-side. The real defect is the missing server-side strength enforcement on signup, inconsistent with the reset flow and the configured validators.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to log in with my email and password so that I get a JWT pair to authenticate subsequent requests.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/auth/login/ delegates to TokenObtainPairView backed by EmailTokenObtainPairSerializer. Accepts {email, password}. Authenticates against email (not username). Returns {access, refresh}. The access token carries a pw_iat claim (unix timestamp of user.password_last_changed_at, or 0). 401 if credentials invalid or user inactive. The serializer overrides the default username_field to 'email'.
inventory_notes: pw_iat=0 when the user has never had a password rotation. JWTAuthenticationWithRLS only rejects if token pw_iat < current password_last_changed_at — legacy users (pw_iat=0, password_last_changed_at=null) are always accepted.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to refresh my access token so that I stay logged in without re-entering credentials.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/auth/refresh/ is the stock simplejwt TokenRefreshView. Accepts {refresh} and returns {access}. Refresh tokens are blacklisted on logout. Force-logout: if the access token's pw_iat < password_last_changed_at, the new access token will also fail on next use because the middleware rechecks on every authenticated request.
inventory_notes: Refresh tokens are blacklisted by LogoutView; using a blacklisted refresh token returns 401.

feature: JWT-authenticated request with RLS context + pw_iat force-logout
user_story: As a subscriber, I want every authenticated API call to enforce my session freshness and tenant isolation so that my data is secure.
expected_behaviour: JWTAuthenticationWithRLS wraps simplejwt. On every request: decodes Bearer token, loads User+Tenant via select_related, checks user.is_active, then compares token pw_iat against user.password_last_changed_at. If pw_iat < password_last_changed_at, raises 401 with code=password_rotated. On success, sets thread-local tenant context and Postgres session vars (app.tenant_id, app.user_id) in a single round-trip via set_rls_context(). TenantContextMiddleware clears context on response. UserTimezoneMiddleware activates user's IANA timezone for each request.
inventory_notes: RLS session vars are set in a single SELECT to avoid multiple cross-region round trips. reset_rls_context() skips the DB call if rls_dirty flag is False — optimization for unauthenticated 404s.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to log out so that my refresh token is invalidated and my session ends.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/auth/logout/ requires IsAuthenticated. Accepts {refresh}; 400 if missing. Calls RefreshToken(refresh_token).blacklist() via simplejwt token blacklist. Returns 204 on success. Invalid token returns 400.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to retrieve my user and tenant details in one call so that the dashboard can render my account state.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/auth/me/ requires IsAuthenticated. Returns UserSerializer data (id, username, email, display_name, language, timezone, telegram_chat_id, telegram_username, line_user_id, line_display_name, preferred_channel, location fields) plus nested TenantSerializer under 'tenant' key. Tenant may be null if onboarding not done. Response is cached 60s per user via @tenant_cache(ttl=60, tag='tenant').

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to read my tenant record so that I can see my plan, usage, and container status.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/tenants/ and GET /api/v1/tenants/<id>/ via ReadOnlyModelViewSet. Scoped to request.user.tenant only — cross-tenant access is impossible. GET /api/v1/tenants/me/ action also available (cached 60s). TenantSerializer includes computed fields: has_active_subscription, trial_days_remaining, platform_budget_exceeded (reads MonthlyBudget), gravity_available, effective_model, free_model_offer. All fields are read-only. 404 if user has no tenant.
inventory_notes: TenantSerializer exposes finance_enabled, fuel_enabled, core_enabled, byo_models_enabled as read-only flags. Gravity availability is gated by GRAVITY_ENABLED env var regardless of stored flag.

user_story: As a subscriber completing signup, I want to submit my profile details so that a tenant and OpenClaw container are created and provisioning begins.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/tenants/onboard/ requires IsAuthenticated. Accepts {display_name, language, timezone, agent_persona}; all optional with defaults. Timezone is validated against IANA database. agent_persona must be in PERSONAS dict. If user already has a tenant in PENDING/PROVISIONING status, returns 409. If tenant exists in any other state, also 409. On success: saves display_name/language/timezone/agent_persona to User; creates Tenant with is_trial=True, trial_started_at=now, trial_ends_at=max(now+7d, 2026-03-31T23:59:59Z), status=PROVISIONING, model_tier=STARTER; calls seed_default_templates_for_tenant; publishes 'provision_tenant' QStash task. If QStash publish fails, status reverts to PENDING and 503 is returned. Returns TenantSerializer(tenant).data with 201.
inventory_notes: Trial end date has a hardcoded March 2026 promotion floor — comment says 'TODO: revert to timedelta(days=7)'. This is stale code that will give every new user a trial extending to at least March 2026.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

user_story: As a subscriber waiting for my assistant to be set up, I want to poll provisioning status so that the UI can show readiness.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/tenants/provisioning-status/ requires IsAuthenticated. Returns {tenant_id, user_id, status, container_id, container_fqdn, has_container_id, has_container_fqdn, provisioned_at, created_at, updated_at, ready}. ready=true only when status=active AND container_id AND container_fqdn are all set. 404 if no tenant.
inventory_notes: No caching — intended to be polled during provisioning flow.

user_story: As a subscriber whose provisioning got stuck, I want to retry it so that setup can complete.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/tenants/retry-provisioning/ requires IsAuthenticated. 404 if no tenant. Returns 200 if already active+ready. Returns 400 if status is SUSPENDED/DEPROVISIONING/DELETED. Enforces 90-second cooldown: if status=PROVISIONING and updated_at < 90s ago, returns 429 with retry_after_seconds. Otherwise sets status=PROVISIONING and publishes 'provision_tenant' QStash task; 202 on success. 503 if QStash publish fails (reverts to PENDING).
inventory_notes: Cooldown is based on updated_at not on a dedicated retry counter, so any save to the tenant in the past 90s will artificially extend it.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to read and update my profile (timezone, location, language, preferred_channel) so that the assistant knows my context.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/tenants/profile/ returns UserSerializer data (all profile fields, read-only: id, username, email, telegram/line IDs). PATCH /api/v1/tenants/profile/ accepts any subset of writable fields: display_name, language, timezone (IANA validation), preferred_channel, location_city, location_lat (-90 to 90), location_lon (-180 to 180). On timezone change: bumps pending_config_version, calls update_tenant_config (or defers if hibernated), then iterates all cron.list jobs and calls cron.update to sync the tz field on each. On location change: bumps pending_config_version and calls update_tenant_config. Returns {…fields, applied: 'ok'|'pending'}.
inventory_notes: Timezone sync iterates ALL existing cron jobs and patches each one — this is a live gateway call, not a config regen. If the container is hibernated, apply_or_defer_gateway_call defers all calls and applied='pending'.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see available agent personas so that I can choose one during onboarding or settings.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/tenants/personas/ requires IsAuthenticated. Calls list_personas() from apps/orchestrator/personas. Response cached 300s per user via @tenant_cache. Returns list of persona objects.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to change my agent's persona so that the assistant's tone matches my preference.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/tenants/preferences/ returns {agent_persona} from user.preferences (cached 120s). PATCH /api/v1/tenants/preferences/ accepts {agent_persona}. Validates against PERSONAS dict (400 for unknown). Saves to user.preferences JSONField. If tenant is ACTIVE, bumps pending_config_version. Returns {agent_persona}.
inventory_notes: Only bumps pending config — does NOT trigger immediate update_tenant_config call unlike profile changes.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to manually refresh my assistant's configuration so that setting changes apply immediately without waiting for the next cron cycle.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/tenants/refresh-config/ returns {can_refresh, last_refreshed, cooldown_seconds=300, status, has_pending_update, container_image_tag, latest_image_tag, image_outdated}. POST /api/v1/tenants/refresh-config/ requires tenant.status=ACTIVE (409 if PENDING/PROVISIONING, 400 otherwise). Enforces 5-minute cooldown on config_refreshed_at (429 if too soon). Calls update_tenant_config(tenant_id) from orchestrator; on success sets config_refreshed_at=now and config_version=pending_config_version. Returns {detail, last_refreshed}. 502 if update_tenant_config raises.
dependencies: apps/orchestrator (update_tenant_config), Azure Container Apps (file share write), OpenClaw container
inventory_notes: image_outdated is True when OPENCLAW_IMAGE_TAG setting != tenant.container_image_tag and neither is 'latest'.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to configure my assistant's daily check-in window and feature tips so that proactive messages happen at convenient times.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/tenants/heartbeat/ returns {enabled, start_hour, window_hours, feature_tips}. PATCH /api/v1/tenants/heartbeat/ accepts {enabled (bool), start_hour (0-23), window_hours (1-6, max 6), feature_tips (bool)}; all optional. Saves changed fields, runs full_clean() (validates heartbeat_window_hours <= 6). If tenant is ACTIVE: bumps pending_config_version, calls apply_or_defer_gateway_call(update_tenant_config). If enabled or start_hour changed: also calls apply_or_defer_gateway_call(sync_heartbeat_cron). Returns {enabled, start_hour, window_hours, feature_tips, applied: 'ok'|'pending'}.
inventory_notes: window_hours validated at both serializer level (max_value=6) and model clean(). Not writing window_hours via PATCH is intentional — only enabled, start_hour, and feature_tips are settable here.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to delete my account so that my data is removed from the platform.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/tenants/delete-account/ requires IsAuthenticated. Body must contain {"confirm": "DELETE"} (400 otherwise). Idempotent: if pending_deletion already True, returns 200 with existing schedule. With active Stripe subscription: calls stripe.Subscription.modify(cancel_at_period_end=True), sets pending_deletion=True and deletion_scheduled_at=period_end. Stale subscription IDs are cleared. Without active subscription: immediately calls _do_hard_delete() which deprovisions the Azure container and calls user.delete(). Returns {scheduled, deletion_scheduled_at, detail}.
inventory_notes: Hard-delete triggered by webhook (customer.subscription.deleted) for subscription users; immediate for free/trial users. Deprovision failure during hard-delete is logged but not fatal — user record is still deleted.

user_story: As a subscriber who requested deletion but changed my mind, I want to cancel the deletion so that my account remains active.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/tenants/cancel-deletion/ requires IsAuthenticated. Returns 400 if no pending_deletion. If stripe_subscription_id is set: calls stripe.Subscription.modify(cancel_at_period_end=False). Stale subscription IDs are cleared silently. Sets pending_deletion=False, deletion_scheduled_at=None. Returns 200 with {detail: 'Deletion cancelled.'}.
inventory_notes: Stripe errors that are not 'missing subscription' are logged as warnings but do not block the DB update — the deletion is cancelled even if Stripe call fails.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to switch my assistant's primary AI model so that I can use a different model within my tier.
expected_behaviour: PATCH /api/v1/tenants/settings/preferred-model/ requires IsAuthenticated. Body: {preferred_model: string or ''}. Validates model_id against _get_allowed_models(tenant) which combines tier base models + live free offer + BYO extras (400 if not in set). Empty string clears to tier default. Saves preferred_model, calls bump_pending_config(), calls _enqueue_immediate_apply() (publishes 'apply_single_tenant_config' QStash task with idempotency key, skipped if hibernated). Returns {preferred_model, model_tier}.
inventory_notes: Immediate apply enqueue is fire-and-forget — failure is logged but not raised. The hourly cron is the safety net.
error_detail: PreferredModelView.patch() (apps/tenants/views.py:769-770) accesses request.user.tenant with no guard. Tenant.user is a OneToOneField with related_name="tenant" (models.py:142), so a user with no tenant raises User.tenant.RelatedObjectDoesNotExist (a subclass of Tenant.DoesNotExist / ObjectDoesNotExist) on attribute access. DRF's default exception handler does NOT convert ObjectDoesNotExist to a 404 (only Http404/PermissionDenied/APIException subclasses are handled), so it propagates as an unhandled HTTP 500. Every sibling view in the same file that touches request.user.tenant wraps it in try/except Tenant.DoesNotExist returning a 404 (ProvisioningStatusView 137-139, RetryProvisioningView 170-172, RefreshConfigView 268-270, AvailableModelsView 860-861, plus the EntityRegistry/PIIDenylist views); OnboardTenantView (line 58) even gates on hasattr(request.user, "tenant"), proving tenant-less authenticated users are an explicitly expected state (pre-onboarding, or after tenant hard-delete). So an authenticated pre-onboarding user, or one whose tenant was deleted, who sends PATCH /settings/preferred-model/ receives a 500 instead of a clean 404. The defect is genuine but the trigger is narrow: the settings UI is normally only reachable post-onboarding, so realistic triggers are a direct API caller, an iOS client racing the onboarding/provision flow, or the deleted-tenant edge case. The identical unguarded pattern also exists at TaskModelPreferencesView.patch() (line 899), confirming this is a consistency miss rather than intended behaviour.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see which AI models are available on my plan so that I can make an informed model selection.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/tenants/settings/available-models/ requires IsAuthenticated. 404 if no tenant. Returns {models: [{id, label, input_per_million, output_per_million}], preferred_model, model_tier, task_model_preferences, task_slugs}. Models are the union of tier base + live free offer + BYO extras, sorted alphabetically by label. Rates from billing.constants.MODEL_RATES.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to assign different AI models to different scheduled tasks so that I can optimize cost and quality per task type.
expected_behaviour: PATCH /api/v1/tenants/settings/task-model-preferences/ requires IsAuthenticated. Body: {task_model_preferences: {slug: model_id, ...}}. Valid slugs: heartbeat, morning_briefing, evening_checkin, week_review, background_tasks (400 for others). model_id validated against allowed models (400 if not allowed). Empty string clears a slug. Merges into existing preferences. Saves, bumps pending_config, enqueues immediate apply. Returns {task_model_preferences}.
error_detail: Confirmed. TaskModelPreferencesView.patch (apps/tenants/views.py:899) and PreferredModelView.patch (line 770) access request.user.tenant with no try/except guard. Every other tenant-scoped view in the file (TenantViewSet, ProvisioningStatusView, RefreshConfigView, HeartbeatConfigView, AvailableModelsView at 858-865, EntityRegistry and PIIDenylist views) wraps the same access in try/except Tenant.DoesNotExist and returns a clean 404 ("No tenant found. Complete onboarding first."). An authenticated-but-tenant-less user is a real, reachable state: Tenant has a OneToOneField to User (models.py:142, related_name="tenant"), and OnboardTenantView (lines 49-58) is the step that creates the tenant, explicitly handling the no-tenant case via hasattr(request.user,"tenant") — so a registered user holding a valid JWT before completing onboarding has no tenant. For that user, request.user.tenant raises RelatedObjectDoesNotExist (a subclass of Tenant.DoesNotExist), which is unhandled, so DRF maps it to HTTP 500 instead of the intended 404. User impact is narrow: a pre-onboarding user (or any client hitting these endpoints outside the normal post-onboarding flow) on the task-model / preferred-model write path gets a 500 instead of a graceful 404. No data corruption, no cross-tenant leakage, no security exposure — purely an inconsistent error-handling/status-code defect on an edge state the rest of the module already handles. Note: the claim names TaskModelPreferencesView; PreferredModelView.patch shares the identical unguarded access.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to link my Telegram account to my NBHD United user so that I can chat with my assistant through Telegram.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/tenants/telegram/generate-link/ requires IsAuthenticated. Returns 400 if already linked (user.telegram_user_id set). Generates a 32-byte urlsafe token, creates TelegramLinkToken with expires_at=now+15min. Returns {deep_link: https://t.me/<BOT>?start=<token>, qr_code: data:image/png;base64,..., expires_at}. Linking completes when user sends /start <token> to the Telegram bot — handled by telegram_service.process_start_token(), which: validates token not expired/used, links telegram_user_id/chat_id/username to User, marks token used, triggers update_tenant_config via QStash if tenant is ACTIVE.
dependencies: apps/router (Telegram bot webhook processes /start), QStash (update_tenant_config), qrcode library
inventory_notes: Tokens expire in 15 minutes. process_start_token is called from the router/Telegram bot webhook, not from this API. A cron task (cleanup_expired_telegram_tokens) purges tokens older than 1 hour.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to unlink my Telegram account so that I can reconnect it or remove the channel.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/tenants/telegram/unlink/ requires IsAuthenticated. Returns 400 if not linked. Clears telegram_user_id, telegram_chat_id, telegram_username on User. If preferred_channel was 'telegram' and LINE is linked, switches preferred_channel to 'line'. Calls invalidate_cache(old_chat_id) from apps/router. Returns {success: True}.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to check if my Telegram account is linked so that I can see the current connection state.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/tenants/telegram/status/ requires IsAuthenticated. Returns {linked: true, telegram_username, telegram_chat_id} if linked, {linked: false} otherwise.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to link my LINE account so that I can chat with my assistant through LINE.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/tenants/line/generate-link/ requires IsAuthenticated. Returns 400 if already linked (user.line_user_id set). Delegates to apps/router/line_service.generate_link_token(). Returns {deep_link, qr_code, expires_at}. Linking completes via LINE webhook (separate flow in apps/router/line_urls).

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to unlink my LINE account so that I can remove the channel.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/tenants/line/unlink/ requires IsAuthenticated. Returns 400 if not linked. Delegates to apps/router/line_service.unlink_line(). Returns {success: True}.
test_result: flagged then cleared by adversarial review

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see my LINE link status and whether LINE messaging is at capacity so that I can decide which channel to use.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/tenants/line/status/ requires IsAuthenticated. Returns apps/router/line_service.get_line_status(user) merged with {quota: {exhausted, checked_at, exhausted_at}} from LineQuotaState.get(). The quota exhausted flag lets the frontend disable the LINE channel selector.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to set my preferred messaging channel (Telegram or LINE) so that proactive messages go to the right app.
expected_behaviour: PATCH /api/v1/tenants/line/preferred-channel/ requires IsAuthenticated. Accepts {preferred_channel: 'telegram'|'line'}. 400 for invalid value. 400 if LINE selected but user.line_user_id not set. 400 if Telegram selected but user.telegram_chat_id not set. If LINE selected: checks is_quota_exhausted() — if exhausted, returns 409 with code=line_quota_exhausted. Saves user.preferred_channel. Returns {preferred_channel, message}.
inventory_notes: Server-side quota gate enforced even if the frontend UI already disables the LINE option.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see all the PII placeholders detected in my chats so that I can review and correct the name bindings.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/tenants/settings/entity-registry/ requires IsAuthenticated. Iterates tenant.pii_entity_map via iter_normalized() (coerces legacy string-shaped entries to dict). Returns {entries: [{placeholder, name, relationship, notes, updated_at}]} sorted by placeholder. 404 if no tenant.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to edit the name, relationship, or notes for a PII placeholder so that rehydration uses the correct context.
expected_behaviour: PATCH /api/v1/tenants/settings/entity-registry/<placeholder>/ requires IsAuthenticated. 404 if placeholder not in pii_entity_map. Accepts {name (max 200 chars), relationship (max 80 chars), notes (max 500 chars)}; any subset. Validates types and lengths (400). Coerces current entry, applies patch fields, stamps updated_at. Saves via Tenant.objects.filter(pk=).update() (no full save). Returns {placeholder, name, relationship, notes, updated_at}.
inventory_notes: Uses filter().update() to avoid racing with concurrent saves on other fields.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to remove a PII placeholder entry so that it no longer appears in my privacy settings.
expected_behaviour: DELETE /api/v1/tenants/settings/entity-registry/<placeholder>/ requires IsAuthenticated. 404 if placeholder not in pii_entity_map. Removes the key from entity_map dict. Updates via Tenant.objects.filter(pk=).update(). Returns 204.
inventory_notes: Deletion does NOT update the denylist — rehydration of existing workspace file references still works via the entity_map entry being gone (they will no longer be rehydrated).

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see words I've marked as non-PII so that I can review my redaction exclusions.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/tenants/settings/pii-denylist/ requires IsAuthenticated. Returns {entries: [{key, reason, decided_at}]} sorted by key. Entries come from tenant.pii_denylist JSONField. 404 if no tenant.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to mark a word as non-PII so that it is never redacted in my messages.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/tenants/settings/pii-denylist/ requires IsAuthenticated. Body: {name: string}. 400 if not a string, empty, or >200 chars. Calls normalize_denylist_key(name) — 400 if canonicalizes to empty. Adds key to tenant.pii_denylist with {reason: 'manual', decided_at: now}. Saves via filter().update(). Returns {key, reason, decided_at} with 201.
inventory_notes: Adding to denylist does NOT remove the entity_map entry — rehydration of existing placeholders still works; only new redaction is suppressed.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to bulk-add multiple names to the PII denylist in one request so that the operation completes quickly.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/tenants/settings/pii-denylist/bulk/ requires IsAuthenticated. Body: {names: [string]}. 400 if names is not a list. 400 if batch >1000. Iterates entries: skips non-strings, empty (after strip), >200 chars, or entries that canonicalize to empty — each skipped entry recorded with reason. Valid entries added to denylist in one filter().update(). Returns {added: [keys], skipped: [{name, reason}]} with 200.
inventory_notes: URL order matters: 'bulk/' must be registered before '<str:key>/' in urls.py to prevent 'bulk' being captured as a key for PIIDenylistItemView.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to remove a word from my PII denylist so that the redactor can protect it again.
expected_behaviour: DELETE /api/v1/tenants/settings/pii-denylist/<key>/ requires IsAuthenticated. 404 if key not in denylist. Removes entry, saves via filter().update(). Returns 204.
inventory_notes: Removal re-enables redaction for that canonical key on future messages.

user_story: As a user receiving a promotional email, I want to redeem a trial extension link so that I get additional free access time.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/tenants/promos/redeem/?code=<campaign>&token=<hmac> is unauthenticated (AllowAny). Validates code and token params (redirects to /promo/redeemed?status=invalid if missing). Looks up PromoCampaign by code (invalid if not found). Checks valid_until (expired if past). Verifies HMAC token via promo_signing.verify_promo_token() (invalid if bad). Looks up User by decoded user_id (invalid if not found). If user has no tenant: records outcome=no_tenant, redirects invalid. If tenant has active Stripe subscription: records outcome=already_subscribed, redirects active_subscription. Otherwise: extends trial (base=max(now, existing trial_ends_at) + extension_days), inserts PromoRedemption row (unique_together constraint handles double-click idempotency — redirects already on IntegrityError), updates Tenant.trial_ends_at + is_trial=True + status=ACTIVE. Redirects to /promo/redeemed?status=success.
inventory_notes: PromoRedemption insert is done OUTSIDE the tenant update transaction to use the unique_together constraint as the race guard. Tenant update failure rolls back the redemption row so retries can re-attempt.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to create a long-lived API token so that external apps like YardTalk can authenticate to my account.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/auth/tokens/create/ requires IsAuthenticated. Throttled: 10 PATs per hour per user (UserPATMintHourThrottle). Body: {name (max 255, required), scopes (optional list, default ['sessions:write','sessions:read']), expires_in_days (optional, 1-365)}. Validates scopes against ALLOWED_PAT_SCOPES {'sessions:write','sessions:read'} (400 for unknown). Generates 32-byte urlsafe secret prefixed 'pat_'; stores SHA-256 hash and 8-char prefix. Returns {id, name, token (raw, shown once), token_prefix, scopes, expires_at, created_at, warning} with 201.
inventory_notes: Raw token shown exactly once and not stored. Caller must save it securely.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see my active PATs so that I can manage which apps have access.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/auth/tokens/ requires IsAuthenticated. Returns all non-revoked PATs for the authenticated user (revoked_at__isnull=True). Fields: id, name, token_prefix, scopes, last_used_at, expires_at, revoked_at, created_at.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to revoke a PAT so that the associated app loses access.
expected_behaviour: DELETE /api/v1/auth/tokens/<token_id>/ requires IsAuthenticated. Scoped to request.user (cross-user revocation impossible). 404 if not found. 400 if already revoked. Sets revoked_at=now. Returns 204.

user_story: As an external app using a PAT, I want my requests to be authenticated and scope-checked so that only authorized operations succeed.
expected_behaviour: PersonalAccessTokenAuthentication checks for 'Authorization: Bearer pat_<secret>' header. Hashes the token (SHA-256), looks up PersonalAccessToken by hash (401 if not found). Checks is_valid (not revoked, not expired) — 401 with specific message. Sets RLS context (tenant_id + user_id). Stamps last_used_at at most once per 60 seconds. Attaches pat to request.auth_pat for downstream scope checks. HasPATScope permission class checks required_scope in pat.scopes; JWT-authed requests bypass scope checks.
inventory_notes: PAT auth only tries if header starts with 'Bearer pat_' — falls through to JWT if not. last_used_at throttled to 60s to avoid cross-region write on every request.

user_story: As a subscriber who forgot their password, I want to request a reset link via email so that I can regain access.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/auth/password-reset/request/ is unauthenticated (AllowAny). Accepts {email}. Rate-limited per-IP (5/hour) and per-email (3/hour) via Django cache; 429 if either limit exceeded. Always returns 200 {detail: 'If an account exists...'} regardless of whether email exists (prevents user enumeration). If user found and is_active: generates uid (base64 user PK) + token (Django default_token_generator HMAC); sends password_reset_subject/body.txt/html templates via Django email backend. Email send failure is logged but not surfaced.
dependencies: Mailgun (email delivery via Django email backend), Django cache (rate limiting)
inventory_notes: Rate limit window is 1 hour. Fail-open on cache unavailability — rate limit is bypassed not blocked when Redis/cache is down.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to set a new password via a reset link so that I can log back in.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/auth/password-reset/confirm/ is unauthenticated (AllowAny). Accepts {uid, token, new_password}. 400 if any missing. Decodes uid (base64 user PK), looks up User (400 if invalid/not found). Checks default_token_generator.check_token() — 400 if invalid or expired (3-day default timeout). Validates new_password against Django password validators (400 with joined error messages). Calls user.set_password() (bumps password_last_changed_at), saves {password, password_last_changed_at}. Mints JWT pair via EmailTokenObtainPairSerializer.get_token() (carries pw_iat). Returns {access, refresh} 200.
inventory_notes: Must use EmailTokenObtainPairSerializer.get_token() not RefreshToken.for_user() — the latter omits pw_iat, causing immediate rejection for users with non-null password_last_changed_at.

user_story: As the billing system, I want to suspend a tenant on subscription cancellation and resume on re-subscription so that access matches payment status.
expected_behaviour: Tenant.status transitions are driven by Stripe webhooks in apps/billing/views.py (not exposed directly in tenants app). Status choices: pending, provisioning, active, suspended, deprovisioning, deleted. Active tenants must have status=active. Tenant.has_entitlement property returns True if stripe_subscription_id is set OR is_trial is True and trial_ends_at > now. Tenant.entitled_active() classmethod returns the filtered queryset used by crons and reports.
inventory_notes: Actual status transitions are in apps/billing/views.py stripe webhook handler and apps/orchestrator (provision/deprovision_tenant). The tenants app provides the model and properties.
error_detail: Real asymmetry confirmed. has_entitlement (apps/tenants/models.py:721-727) is positive logic: True only if stripe_subscription_id is set OR a valid unexpired trial exists. entitled_active() (apps/tenants/models.py:729-742) is negative logic: it returns ALL ACTIVE tenants with a container and only excludes the narrow tuple (is_trial=True AND trial_ends_at<=now AND empty stripe_subscription_id). A ghost tenant (is_trial=False, status=ACTIVE, no stripe_subscription_id, not budget-exempt) has is_trial=False, so the exclude clause does not match and it IS included in the queryset, even though has_entitlement returns False for it. The sibling sweep _unentitled_active_tenants() (apps/cron/views.py:651-674) was deliberately hardened against exactly this ghost class (its docstring cites ~17 production ghosts accruing LLM cost since 2026-04-15 after is_trial was flipped to False without a SUSPENDED transition), but entitled_active() was never updated to mirror that fix. entitled_active() gates cron seeding (apps/cron/views.py:523), force-reseed-crons (594), and admin broadcast (1268), so a ghost keeps receiving config-apply and cron-seeding cycles until the once-daily expire_trials sweep (apps/cron/views.py:741) suspends it. Real but minor and self-correcting: bounded by the daily sweep cadence (max ~1 day of wasted cron seeds/config applies for an unentitled tenant). One correction to the claim's framing: ghosts do NOT arise at account creation (new tenants start is_trial=True per apps/tenants/views.py:90); they only arise from a later is_trial flip without status moving to SUSPENDED. Net effect is a sub-1-day wasted-cost/correctness leak, not a security or access defect.

user_story: As the system, I want to reset daily message counters at midnight UTC so that the per-day limits refresh.
expected_behaviour: reset_daily_counters_task() calls reset_daily_counters() which does Tenant.objects.filter(messages_today__gt=0).update(messages_today=0). Returns count of tenants updated. Triggered via QStash cron at midnight UTC.

user_story: As the system, I want to reset monthly usage counters on the 1st of the month so that subscribers get a fresh usage allocation each month.
expected_behaviour: reset_monthly_counters_task() calls reset_monthly_counters() which updates all tenants with messages_this_month>0: sets messages_this_month=0, tokens_this_month=0, estimated_cost_this_month=0. Also clears cost_warn_sent_at and cost_exhausted_email_sent_at on ALL tenants (unconditional). Does NOT reset purchased_credit (persists across months). Returns count of tenants with messages updated.
inventory_notes: purchased_credit is intentionally excluded — prepaid credit persists. The quota-email idempotency markers are cleared unconditionally (all tenants, not just those with usage) so fresh notifications can send in the new month.

user_story: As the system, I want to purge expired Telegram link tokens so that the database doesn't accumulate stale records.
expected_behaviour: cleanup_expired_telegram_tokens() deletes TelegramLinkToken rows where expires_at < now - 1 hour. Returns a string with the count deleted. Triggered via QStash cron.

user_story: As an operator, I want to force-rotate all user passwords and send reset emails so that user sessions are invalidated for a security hygiene event.
expected_behaviour: rotate_all_passwords management command: iterates all users except PLATFORM_OWNER_EMAIL. Skips users with no email or whose password_last_changed_at >= --since (idempotency).
