For each user: calls set_unusable_password() (bumps password_last_changed_at → invalidates all JWTs), saves, generates reset token, sends email (privacy_rotation_2026 template). Reports rotated/skipped/failed counts. --dry-run mode available. rotate_all_passwords_task() wraps the command for QStash dispatch.
dependencies: Mailgun (email delivery), Django cache (not used here), QStash (task dispatch)
inventory_notes: Email failure leaves the user with a rotated (unusable) password but no reset link — they are locked out until manual resend. PLATFORM_OWNER_EMAIL env var must be set or operator will be locked out.
error_detail:
Confirmed against the code. set_unusable_password() is saved unconditionally at line 107, locking the user out immediately. The rotated counter is incremented only at line 116 after a successful email send; on email failure (lines 117-118) only email_failed is bumped. The operator summary headline "Rotated + emailed: {rotated}" (line 125) therefore EXCLUDES users who were rotated-but-not-emailed. The true locked-out count is rotated + email_failed, which is never shown as a single number. During a partial Mailgun outage, an operator who reads only the green success line will under-count how many users are locked out without a reset link.

This is real but minor, not a silent failure: the command logs each failure per-user with "rotated, locked out until manual resend" (lines 119-122) and prints a distinct red ERROR line "Email failed (rotated but no reset link sent): {email_failed}" (lines 128-134). All the information is present; the defect is only that the blast radius is split across two counters and the headline "Rotated + emailed" can be misread as the total locked-out figure. Hence low severity / ux category.

user_story: As an operator, I want to send a promotional email campaign to eligible subscribers so that they receive trial extension offers.
expected_behaviour: send_promo_campaign management command: requires --code (campaign slug), --kind, --days, --valid_until. Looks up or creates PromoCampaign. Filters audience (non-paying, active tenants). For each user: generates HMAC-signed per-user token via promo_signing, sends email with redemption URL. Records audience snapshot on PromoCampaign. send_promo_campaign_task() wraps with hardcoded 'privacy-zdr-2026' campaign constants for QStash dispatch.

user_story: As an operator, I want to bump pending config versions for all active tenants so that their OpenClaw configs are picked up on the next apply cycle.
expected_behaviour: bump_pending_configs management command: updates all active tenants with container_id set — sets pending_config_version = config_version + 1. bump_config_version management command: accepts optional --tenant UUID. Without --tenant: bumps pending_config_version + 1 for all ACTIVE tenants. With --tenant: targets a single tenant. Both emit SUCCESS with count.
inventory_notes: bump_pending_configs uses F('config_version')+1 (relative to current); bump_config_version uses F('pending_config_version')+1 (relative to current pending). These are subtly different operations.

user_story: As an operator running migrations, I want to disable Row-Level Security on newly created tables so that the application can read and write without RLS blocking queries.
expected_behaviour: disable_rls management command: queries pg_tables for tables in non-system schemas where rowsecurity=true AND tableowner=current_user. Runs ALTER TABLE ... DISABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY for each. Skips Supabase internal tables (not owned by current_user). Reports count.
inventory_notes: Only affects tables owned by the Django DB user — Supabase internal tables (owned by 'postgres') are skipped. In prod, Django tables are owned by 'postgres' so this command may be a no-op in production (see MEMORY.md: RLS relock is effectively a no-op in prod).

user_story: As an operator, I want to see a tenant's current local time so that I can debug timezone-related issues.
expected_behaviour: tenant_local_now management command: takes tenant_id UUID argument. Looks up Tenant + user, resolves IANA timezone (falls back to UTC on invalid). Prints tenant_id, timezone, utc_time (ISO), local_time (ISO), local_date, local_time_hhmm, then 'ok'. CommandError if tenant not found.

user_story: As the OpenClaw runtime, I want to mark a welcome feature as sent so that the deploy-time backfill skips already-served tenants.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/tenants/runtime/<tenant_id>/welcomes/<feature>/ is AllowAny but requires X-NBHD-Internal-Key + X-NBHD-Tenant-Id headers (validated by validate_internal_runtime_request — 401 on failure). Valid features: fuel, finance, core (400 for others). Sets tenant.welcomes_sent[feature]=ISO timestamp. Idempotent (re-calls overwrite). Returns {feature, sent_at}. 404 if tenant not found.
inventory_notes: AllowAny permission class is intentional — auth is via the internal API key in the header, not a JWT.

user_story: As the OpenClaw runtime, I want to record engagement signals for agenda threads so that the assistant can prioritize which topics to surface.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/tenants/runtime/<tenant_id>/agenda/<kind>/<item_id>/ is AllowAny + internal API key auth. Valid kinds from AgendaEngagement.Kind. Body: {action: surfaced|abandoned|completed|active|dormant|defer, signal?: string, until?: ISO-8601}. Defer action requires 'until' field (400 if missing or invalid datetime). Maps action to service calls: mark_surfaced(), defer_until(), or mark_state()+record_signal(). Returns {kind, item_id, action}.

user_story: As the OpenClaw runtime, I want to record a future-aware commitment so that the assistant can follow up on it later.
expected_behaviour: POST /api/v1/tenants/runtime/<tenant_id>/commitments/ is AllowAny + internal API key auth. Body: {about: string (required), surface_after: ISO-8601 (required), why: string}. Validates about and surface_after (400 if missing; 400 if surface_after invalid datetime). Calls record_commitment() which uses SHA-256 hash of about-text as item_id (idempotent — same about-text reuses the row). Creates AgendaEngagement with kind=ASSISTANT_COMMITMENT. Returns {kind, item_id, surface_after}. 404 if tenant not found.

user_story: As the OpenClaw runtime, I want to read and switch the tenant's preferred AI model so that the assistant can fulfill a user's in-chat model change request.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/v1/tenants/runtime/<tenant_id>/preferred-model/ — internal auth required. Returns {preferred_model, applied_model, model_tier, allowed_models: [{model_id, alias}]}. POST /api/v1/tenants/runtime/<tenant_id>/preferred-model/ — internal auth required. Body: {model_id: string}. If model_id not in _get_allowed_models, returns 400 with allowed_models in body. Saves preferred_model, bumps pending_config, enqueues immediate apply. Returns {updated: True, ...state}. Empty model_id clears to tier default.
inventory_notes: Does NOT include free-offer models in the allowed set (uses TIER_MODEL_CONFIGS + _byo_model_extras only, unlike consumer PreferredModelView which also calls offer_model_entry). Minor inconsistency.
error_detail: Confirmed code-level inconsistency. RuntimePreferredModelView.post() gates the incoming model_id against views._get_allowed_models(), which merges offer_model_entry() (the limited-time free-offer model, e.g. nemotron free, when the promo is active) into the allowed set. But the GET response and every POST response body are built by _state() (runtime_views.py:206-221), which only merges TIER_MODEL_CONFIGS + _byo_model_extras() and does NOT call offer_model_entry(). Net effect during an active promo: the runtime can successfully POST the free-offer model (switch applies, config bumps), yet that model never appears in any allowed_models list returned by GET or POST. Impact is minimal/cosmetic: the switch itself succeeds with no error, and the free-offer model is the auto-selected default during the promo, so the user can always reach it. The only consequence is a discovery gap — an agent that enumerates GET's allowed_models to decide options wouldn't list a model that is actually settable (and already running). Manifests only while the promo is live and self-resolves when it ends. Matches the auditor's own inventory note ('Minor inconsistency'); the consumer-side PreferredModelView's parity gate correctly uses _get_allowed_models.

user_story: As the usage accounting system, I want to compute a tenant's effective budget and whether they can still spend so that the AI gateway can enforce cost limits.
expected_behaviour: Tenant.effective_token_budget: returns monthly_token_budget if >0, else TIER_TOKEN_BUDGETS[model_tier] (5M for STARTER). Tenant.effective_cost_budget: returns monthly_cost_budget if >0, else TIER_COST_BUDGETS[model_tier] ($5 for STARTER). Tenant.is_over_budget: True if estimated_cost_this_month >= effective_cost_budget (0 budget = unlimited). Tenant.has_spendable_budget: True if is_budget_exempt OR NOT is_over_budget OR purchased_credit > 0. Quota-email idempotency markers (cost_warn_sent_at, cost_exhausted_email_sent_at) cleared monthly by reset_monthly_counters.
inventory_notes: purchased_credit extends spending beyond the included allowance but does not affect is_over_budget or effective_cost_budget — those metrics retain their 'included cap' semantics for email notifications.

user_story: As the system, I want to signal that a tenant's config needs regenerating so that the orchestrator can pick it up on the next cycle.
expected_behaviour: Tenant.bump_pending_config() increments pending_config_version by 1 and saves. When pending_config_version > config_version the orchestrator's apply_pending_configs cron picks up the tenant and runs update_tenant_config. config_version is bumped to pending_config_version on successful apply. config_refreshed_at is stamped. The gap (pending>config) is visible to the frontend via has_pending_update in RefreshConfigView.

user_story: As a newly provisioned subscriber, I want to receive a welcome email so that I know how to connect my messaging app and get started.
expected_behaviour: send_welcome_email(tenant) is idempotent via tenant.welcome_email_sent_at — returns False immediately if already sent. Requires user.email. Checks LINE quota (fails closed — omits LINE mention if quota check fails). Renders welcome_subject.txt, welcome_body.txt/.html with {display_name, telegram_connected, settings_url, video_url, line_quota_available, channel_label}. Sends via Django email backend. Stamps welcome_email_sent_at on success. Called from provisioning flow in apps/orchestrator.
inventory_notes: preview_email management command (and preview_email_task QStash wrapper) lets operators render and send test versions of this email without a real provisioning flow.

feature: Agent persona + PII context injection into USER.md
user_story: As the system, I want to inject the tenant's profile, timezone, and PII placeholder rules into the agent's system prompt so that the assistant knows who it's talking to.
expected_behaviour: envelope.py registers two sections via register_section: 'profile' (order=10, always enabled, refresh_on=User) renders display_name, timezone, preferred_channel, language, location_city as bullet lines (suppresses defaults like 'Friend'/'UTC'/'en'). 'privacy_placeholders' (order=11) renders placeholder handling rules + identity context (one line per entity_map entry with relationship or notes). Sections are rendered into USER.md by the orchestrator on each config regeneration.
inventory_notes: Identity context sub-section only renders entries that have relationship or notes set — entries with only a name are not injected.

feature: Tenant creation via Telegram bot (service layer)
user_story: As a Telegram user starting the bot for the first time, I want a tenant and user account to be created automatically so that I can start using my assistant without a web signup.
expected_behaviour: services.create_tenant() takes display_name, telegram_chat_id, telegram_user_id, telegram_username, language. Raises ValueError if user already exists for that telegram_chat_id. Creates User (username=tg_<chat_id>) and Tenant (status=PENDING, key_vault_prefix=tenants-<user_id>) atomically. Seeds journal templates and documents. Does NOT provision the container — provisioning is triggered by billing. Called from the Telegram bot webhook layer when a new user sends a message.
inventory_notes: Catch-and-rethrow for IntegrityError on telegram_chat_id ensures clean ValueError surfaces regardless of Django ORM variant.

user_story: As the system, I want to track when a container was hibernated and why it was woken so that message delivery logic can apply the right grace window.
expected_behaviour: Tenant.hibernated_at is set by the hibernation cron when a container is idle-stopped. Tenant.cron_wake_at is set when a container is woken for a scheduled cron job (not user message) — triggers a shorter 30-minute idle window before re-hibernating. Tenant.last_wake_at is set on any wake from hibernation — the message drain uses this as a 'still booting' grace window to retry delivery without burning delivery attempts. Views check hibernated_at before calling _enqueue_immediate_apply (skips if hibernated).
inventory_notes: These fields are set by apps/orchestrator/hibernation.py — the tenants app only defines the model fields.
